description:
THARKAD ABLAZE
Seat of power for the Lyran people since 2407, the frozen shores of Tharkad have sparkled with the might of the merchant princes of House Steiner for centuries.

Then came the Jihad.

Jihad Turning Points: Tharkad fleshes out the details of the conflict surrounding this pivotal world published in various Jihad Plot Sourcebooks. Additionally, players can join one of the myriad of forces fighting for control--and survival--of House Steiner's seat of power.

This campaign series uses the Chaos Campaign rules and gives players the option of fighting individual battles, following a campaign arc, inserting it into their own campaigns and even create their own scenarios with optional rules to give their games a ˆ_Ñ??Tharkad flavor.ˆ? The ebook includes a detailed map of the world of Tharkad, a run down of the forces involved in the conflict, and several tracks that players can use to run simple lance-on-lance battles, or stage up to a world-wide invasion. Only the players' imaginations are the limits.

SIAN ABLAZE!
The most important world in the Capellan Confederation, the verdant and serene capital has been the seat of the Chancellor's power for centuries.

Then came the Jihad. And more damaging, the betrayal of one of Liao's own blood.

Jihad Turning Points: Sian fleshes out the details of the conflicts that swirl across this pivotal world published in various Jihad Plot Sourcebooks. Additionally, players can join one of the myriad of forces fighting for control--and survival--of the center of House Liao's heart and soul.

This campaign series uses the Chaos Campaign rules and gives players the option of fighting individual battles, following a campaign arc, inserting it into their own campaigns and even create their own scenarios with optional rules to give their games a ˆ_Ñ??Sian flavor.ˆ? The ebook includes a detailed map of the world of Sian, a run down of the forces involved in the conflict, and several tracks that players can use to run simple lance-on-lance battles, or stage up to a world-wide invasion. Only the players' imaginations are the limits.

ATREUS CAST DOWN
Despite centuries of unrest, the Free Worlds League (formed in 2271) is the oldest of all the Great Houses. Its capital, Atreus, colonized during humankind's initial exodus to the stars, is held as the ideal of the democracy prized by the people of the League.

Then came the Jihad.

Jihad Turning Points: Atreus fleshes out the details of the conflict surrounding this pivotal world published in various Jihad Plot Sourcebooks. Additionally, players can join one of the myriad of forces fighting for control--and survival--of the Free Worlds League's capital world.

This campaign series uses the Chaos Campaign rules and gives players the option of fighting individual battles, following a campaign arc, inserting it into their own campaigns and even create their own scenarios with optional rules to give their games a ˆ_Ñ??Atreus flavor.ˆ? The ebook includes a detailed map of the world of Atreus, a run down of the forces involved in the conflict, and several tracks that players can use to run simple lance-on-lance battles, or stage up to a world-wide invasion. Only the players' imaginations are the limits.

A promo pack for Silver. It is a pack of 2 identical cards. This pack has 2 cards with value zero to replace the zero cards in a set of Silver cards.

The card has a value of 0, and says, " When faceup at scoring: save one other card in your village for the next round."

If you have a Leader faceup at scoring, save one other card in your village for the next round.

At the beginning of the next round, before you view your two cards, replace any card you were dealt with the kept card (facedown), letting your opponents know what the saved card is and where you've placed it. Shuffle the replaced card back into the deck without viewing it.

Ever wanted to play Fluxx and Ro-Sham-Bo at the same time?  Well now you can, insert the Sham-Bo pack into any Fluxx deck and the 3 Keepers, 3 Goals, and 1 Action card will let you be on your way to new interactions in the Fluxx game of your choice.

Dieron. Prefecture capital, District headquarters, a lynchpin resource and industrial world for the mighty Draconis Combine.

One of the Word of Blake's major successes at the onset of the Jihad, they would not let go of Dieron without a fight.

Jihad Turning Points: Dieron fleshes out all the details of the conflict surrounding this pivotal world published in various Jihad Plot Sourcebooks. Additionally, players can join one of the myriad of units fighting for ultimate control of one of the Dragon's precious resource worlds. The Word of Blake has turned the world into a major stumbling block, centered around the ancient and massive Fortress Dieron.

This campaign series uses the Chaos Campaign rules and gives players the option of fighting individual battles, following a campaign arc, inserting it into their own campaigns and even creating their own scenarios with optional rules to give their games a "Dieronese flavor." This PDF-exclusive book includes a detailed map of Dieron, a comprehensive list of the forces involved on Dieron during its nine-year occupation, and several Tracks for players can use to run simple lance-on-lance battles or stage a complete world-wide invasion. The only limit is the players' imagination.
16 new cards for use in any Star Trek Fluxx title.  This expansion adds Captain Jonathan Archer from the television series Star Trek: Enterprise to any copy of Star Trek Fluxx.

boardgame: BattleTech: Operational Turning Points – The Red Corsair
description:
In 3055, an unknown pirate menace threatened several Lyran border worlds. Known only as the ˆ_Ñ??Red Corsair,ˆ? this brutal pirate leader broke several militias and preyed upon its victims with no mercy. In response to ComStar's pleadings, forces from Clan Wolf and the famed mercenary Kell Hounds pursued the mysterious pirate band across several Lyran worlds to a climactic showdown deep in Clan Wolf space.

Operational Turning Points: Red Corsair opens up the battle for players to join in the fight (as detailed in Michael Stackpole's novel Natural Selection) using the successful Turning Points PDF exclusive format. Return to a time when tensions were high along the Lyran-Falcon border, and one wrong move might spark another interstellar war!

This campaign series uses the Chaos Campaign rules and gives players the option of fighting individual Missions and Touchpoints, following a campaign arc, and inserting it into their own campaigns. This PDF exclusive includes detailed information on several of the worlds affected by the Red Corsair, a rundown of the forces involved in the conflict, an overview of the historical campaign, and several tools and tracks that players can use to run historical characters and battles from this time period. Only the players' imaginations are the limits.
Star Trek Fluxx welcomes 16 new cards that introduce Porthos and Spot to the card game.  This expansion can be used with any or all of the Star Trek Fluxx games.

Wolfs' Dragoons, one of the Inner Sphere's most enigmatic mercenary units in the early thirty-first century. The Draconis Combine Mustered Soldiery, one of the most honorable and ruthless military forces among the Great Houses. On the tiny planet of Misery, the culmination of political plotting, jealousy, and fanaticism played itself out to a bloody, exhaustive end.
The extension of the stubborn wills of Jaime Wolf and Takashi Kurita, Misery became a bloody milestone in the history of the Dragoons and a shocking embarrassment to the Dragon's throne. Forever after, Misery became synonymous to both forces as a symbol of pain, dishonor, and unshakable devotion.

Historical Turning Points: Misery returns to an earlier time in BattleTech history, when the mysterious Dragoons were manipulated into a life-or-death battle by the jealousy of a Kuritan warlord, and nearly paid the ultimate price just to survive.

This campaign series uses the Chaos Campaign rules and gives players the option of fighting individual battles, following a campaign arc, inserting it into their own campaigns and even create their own scenarios. This PDF exclusive includes a detailed map of the world of Misery, a run down of the forces involved in the conflict, and several tracks that players can use to run simple lance-on-lance battles, or stage the complete, desperate battle for survival. Only the players' imaginations are the limits.

boardgame: BattleTech: Third League Turning Points – Free Taiw...St. Ives
description:
Taiw...St. Ives Free

In 3226, revolutionaries have freed Taiw...St. Ives from the oppression of the Third League. But are the people of Taiw...St. Ives really free? Or are they just trading on oppressor for another? With the Third League unlikely to stand for treason, Taiw...St. Ives will be the center of the universe for a brief period.

Turning Points: Taiw...St. Ives fleshes out the details of the conflict surrounding this pivotal world published in various Third League Plot Sourcebooks. Additionally, players can join one of the myriad of forces fighting for control--and survival--of this instrumental world.

This campaign series uses the Chaos Campaign rules and gives players the option of fighting individual battles, following a campaign arc, inserting it into their own campaigns and even create their own scenarios with optional rules to give their games a ˆ_Ñ??Taiw...St. Ives flavor.ˆ? The ebook includes a detailed map of the world of Taiw...St. Ives, a run down of the forces involved in the conflict, and several tracks that players can use to run simple lance-on-lance battles, or stage up to a world-wide invasion. Only the players' imaginations are the limits.

Aerospace BattleMat was made available exclusively at GenCon 2021.

It is a double-sized, double-sided 36” x 22" neoprene map with an aerospace theme. Side A is Aerospace #1 and Side B is Planetary Invasion #1.

Every Warrior can make the pilgrimage back to the Clan Homeworlds to fight on sacred land. This neoprene map is a double-sized 22" x 36" battlefield. One half of a giant "Circle of Equals" for Grand Melee battles on the first side, while the reverse side is the Bloody Basin, Lechenka District (from the Battletech novel Prince of Havoc). A rules booklet for the maps is also included.

Strana Mechty Battlemat was an unlockable stretch goal for the 2019 Clan Invasion crowdfunding campaign, which ran from 18 July 2019 to 18 August 2019.

Be kind and rewind to the 80s and 90s with this radical expansion pack for the award-winning game Telestrations, featuring decades-inspired words and phrases that will have you sketching out the tunes, fads, and fashion from the end of the 20th century. Double-sided cards let you easily flip between the two eras and add “da bomb” new sayings to your existing Telestrations game! *This is not a standalone game.

From the back cover:

Calvary Incoming!
In the heat and terror of battle, as enemy troops force you back and defeat seems at hand, nothing brings a smile like the sight of reinforcements!

Catalyst Game Labs offers a full line of high-quality, fully assembled plastic miniatures for BattleTech. But when you're looking for a quick and easy opposing force to face on your gaming table, Reinforcements: Clan Invasion provides the bevy of additional units you need.

This box includes nearly 200 full-color, high-quality stand-ups, covering all of the redesigned 'Mechs and Elementals from the hugely successful BattleTech: Clan Invasion crowdfunding campaign. Also included are 248 record sheets covering each 'Mech's primary variants and configurations. A double-sized map features the first-ever depictions of the Plain of Curtains and the Great Gash on the world of Twycross, two of the key battlefields of the Clan Invasion. Finally, a die-cut sheet of Support Units and Diabolis storm tokens - with corresponding rules - bring Twycross to life on your tables.

The Reinforcements: Clan Invasion box is a supplement to the A Game of Armored Combat and Clan Invasion boxes, as well as Total Warfare which are required to use this set.

Contents:
• 9 punchboards of nearly 200 additional 'Mech standees.
• 248 Record Sheets contained in 3 Booklets.
• One double-sided 36″ x 22″ game-map
  - Plain of Curtains
  - Great Gash
• Punchboard of Support Units and Diabolis storm tokens.
• 3 page rule booklet for the maps / tokens.

boardgame: BattleTech: Alpha Strike – Dropships and Small Craft Cards
description: BattleTech: Alpha Strike Cards – DropShips & Small Craft provides players with unit cards to bring these units to your Alpha Strike or BattleForce games. Featuring dropships & small craft from across the range of technical readouts and other sourcebooks, this product contains nearly 200 dropships & small craft. Note that DropShips & Small Craft are playable in standard Alpha Strike games based on ground and atmospheric combat, Alpha Strike space combat requires the Alpha Strike Companion.

boardgame: BattleTech: Alpha Strike – Warships and Jumpships Cards
description: BattleTech: Alpha Strike Cards – WarShips & JumpShips provides players with unit cards to bring these units to your Alpha Strike or BattleForce games. Featuring WarShips & JumpShips from across the range of Technical Readouts and other sourcebooks, this product contains over 140 WarShips & JumpShips. Note that Alpha Strike space combat requires the Alpha Strike Companion in addition to the Alpha Strike: Commander’s Edition rulebook.

boardgame: BattleTech: Experimental Technical Readout – Caveat Emptor
description:
After Gray Monday, the Republic has descended into a new Dark Age, ploughshares are being turned back into swords, and the jackals are feeding on the confusion. The desperate need for weapons of new militaries arising everywhere sees mundane machines strapped with outlandish weapons, flamboyant arena ’Mechs returning to the battlefield, and renowned manufacturers engaging in uncharacteristic experimental gambles. It is a seller’s market; anything goes and no price is too high. Caveat emptor—the dust will settle quicker on your new acquisition’s battlefield debut than on the transaction that got you there. You won’t have time for buyer’s remorse.

Experimental Technical Readout: Caveat Emptor samples production and prototype machines that have earnt infamy after Gray Monday as inefficient monstrosities sold to the highest bidder. Each unit featured in this book incorporates some of the new technologies that debuted in Tactical Operations (or quirks found in the BattleMech Manual)—now employed on battlefields of brush wars on former Republic worlds. Statistics and Record Sheets are included for 13 new advanced and experimental BattleMechs, ready for play in advanced BattleTech games.

MonsDRAWsity: Robots is the next thematic expansion for MonsDRAWsity, adding 50 more monsters to the game, new variant rules, and a 10th player board! Will you be able to memorize, describe, and draw these magnificent machines, or will your wires get crossed?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Vampire! A Retro Horror Card Game
description:
A SUMMER NIGHT IN 1957. ANYTOWN, USA.You are a group of small town Locals. But one of you is secretly the Vampire... and you're thirsty for blood.

Together, the Locals must identify the Vampire, reduce it to 1 hit point, and kill it with a Wooden Stake card. But they have to be careful: the Vampire is working to turn them against each other. And if they kill the wrong player, the Vampire will raise them from the dead as a Vampire Fledgling!

Inspired by The Twilight Zone and a real childhood nightmare, The Vampire! pairs the drama of the hidden role genre with the crunch of traditional card games.

[Description by the publisher.]

The Masterpiece Edition expands the game into a 5 or 6 player game.  It may also be used in a standard 2-4 player game with modification of the envelopes.

Masterpiece cards can be used in conjunction with standard cards to complete standard Melds or on their own to complete Masterpiece melds providing ways to score even more points.

Contents:

     14 Masterpiece cards
     1 Rules sheet w/Masterpiece Meld Guide


This expansion was available as an add-on during the Kickstarter campaign and later through the publisher website.

boardgame: Renaissance Wars: Kickstarter Exclusive Stretch Goal Cards
description:
A set of 11 cards that were made available as exclusives to Kickstarter backers.  They are mixed in for use with the standard cards in the set:

Contents:
- 1 Money in the Bank card
- 2 Populace cards
- 2 Immediate Intrusion cards
- 2 Immediate Event cards
- 4 Personal Action cards

Carefully utilize the every-player-shared main deck cards and take down the other Kings with your own King and Equipment!     
         
Have your Bear attack an opponent’s King once you’ve broken all of that King’s shields to win!     
There are 3 Win conditions:    
 
     Hit an opponent's King once with a Bear when the opponent does not control any Shields or Bears of their own.     
     Drawing the last card.     
     Control the all Muse-a-Bears: John, Dhruv, and Matt.     



During their turn, players play Bear, King, Shield, and equipment cards take down the other kings.

1 to 3 Players
Deckbuilding - Dice Management - Strategy - Solitary - Cooperative - Competitive

This game incorporates into the world of Experior, not only the Dark Epic Fantasy, already present in other games of the line, but also includes Science Fiction from different evolutionary paradigms, telekinetic developments, steampunk technology, Cyberpunk, worlds of almighty beings, anomalies, mutations and aberrations.

Starting from an asymmetric deck of 10 basic cards, each player will throw basic dice, exchange the cards in their hand for more dice, use their abilities or enhance other abilities already triggered. The combinations of use are infinite and these will depend on what the dice dictate, being able to also get new cards, buy improvements or travel through the portals, taking them to uncertain destinations. There you can fight or find objects or more fissures that will allow you to continue traveling.

In solo mode, the player must close a number of portals, while the automa will take time away from the game. The player has an initial life, if the automa consumes the time or the player's life reaches zero, the player loses.
In competitive mode, it will be a race between players to see who manages to close the portals first.

—description from the designer


1 a 3 Jugadores
Deckbuilding - Gestión de Dados - Estrategia - Solitario - Cooperativo - Competitivo

Los manipuladores de energía  pueden cerrar las fisuras, entrada de los Mitos a nuestro mundo. Para esto deben viajar a través de ellas como portales, llevándolos a destinos desconocidos entre los distintos planos de realidad y sus diferentes líneas temporales.

Este juego incorpora al mundo de Experior, no solo la Fantasía Épica Oscura, ya presente en otros juegos de la linea, sino que incluye la Ciencia Ficción desde distintos paradigmas evolutivos, desarrollos telequinéticos, tecnología steampunk, Ciberpunk, mundos de seres todopoderosos, anomalías, mutaciones y aberraciones.

Partiendo de un mazo asimétrico de 10 cartas básicas, cada jugador arrojara dados básicos, podrá usar las cartas en su mano para canjear por mas dados o usar sus habilidades si puede pagarlas o potenciar otras habilidades ya disparadas. Las combinaciones de uso son infinitas y estas dependerán de lo que los dados dicten, pudiendo aparte conseguir nuevas cartas, comprando mejoras o viajar por los portales, llevándolo a destinos inciertos. Ahí podrá pelear o encontrar objetos o mas fisuras que le permitirán seguir viajando.

En modo solitario, el jugador debe cerrar una cantidad de portales, mientras el automa ira restando tiempo de juego. El jugador tiene una vida inicial, si el automa consume el tiempo o la vida del jugador llega a cero, el jugador pierde. 
En modo competitivo, será una carrera entre los jugadores por ver quien logra cerrar primero los portales.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

Plan, position, and win in this puzzle game of speed and strategy!

In PicTwist: National Park, images of America’s greatest treasures are placed upside down, sideways, and out of order. Put your planning skills to the test with Action Cards to race against other players and reconstruct the natural wonders. Be the first one to fix your park photo to win!

Plan, position, and win in this puzzle game of speed and strategy!

In PicTwist: Bob Ross, the scenic landscapes from The Joy of Painting have been scrambled up, appearing upside down, sideways, and out of order. Put your planning skills to the test with Action Cards to race against other players and reconstruct his works of art. Be the first one to correctly restore your painting to win!

Euro Champions is a card game for 2 to 6 players who have an eye for dominate European football.

In Euro Champions, the tradition and fortune of European clubs will motivate your actions in every round. Your goal is to conquer the most Points Shields. For this players need to make a combination of cards that have enough stars of tradition or fortune to conquer each shield.

In addition, players can also collect cards of clubs from the same country or region and acquire valuable characters to help them or harm their opponents.

And to make everything even more fun and interactive, there's the "Penalty card" that will leave everyone's heart in their mouths!

An exciting and strategic game without being heavy, for 2 to 6 players, to be played with friends, family and find out who is capable of dominating European football!

Window Pains is a light 2-4 player strategy game where you are a craftsmen building a stained glass window, and your opponents are lowly interns messing it up.

Players add cards, partially overlapping the cards already on the table, and score points for the colors connected to rosettes as the play area slowly grows. At the end of the game, score bonus points for your secret objective and tally points to find the winner!

An eventful journey through the 4 seasons. Almost 150 events can influence this trip. Who will manage to get the most points despite all the hurdles?

Players roll dice to move around the board and pick a card from the stack color coded to match the spot they have stopped in.  Each card presents a hurdle and assigns points.

The first person to land on the last square by an exact roll of the dice wins.

TuHao is a simple, quick game designed to capture the traditional climbing game experience for two. Tuhao is the Chinese colloquial term for the new class of crass rich folks who have arisen during the past several decades in China.

A game is three hands long. All three hands are dealt to each player at the beginning, but not revealed until each round begins. In each round, the aim is get rid of all your cards first by playing combos. Each time a player “passes” because they are unable or unwilling to play a higher combo, that passing player purloins two cards from the previous played combo to re-use next hand--one card for them and the other for their opponent.

As players start each new round they combine the cards dealt at the beginning with the “tribute” cards chosen or allotted from the previous round.  At the end of each round players score for the number of cards left in their opponent’s hand, and at the end of the three rounds the winner is the player with the highest total points.

Part of the two-player climbing trilogy: Tuhao, Eat or Run, Hedges.

This game is called Murder Mystery. The player becomes one of the characters in the story and searches for the culprit of the incident that occurs in the story. Since the criminal is also one of the players, the purpose is that the player who became the criminal does not know that he is the criminal.
Each player has his or her own purpose other than searching for the criminal or not knowing it, and each player aims to achieve multiple goals and win the points obtained by achieving them.

"Zero Ni Makoto" is a fictional story about the Edo period in Japan. Do you know the "Shinsengumi" who played an active part as a police in the Edo period? The Shinsengumi had units from No.1 to No.10, and systematically guarded the town of Kyoto. However, there was a "No. 0" that did not remain in history in the Shinsengumi.

"Zero Ni Makoto" is a story of the success and tragedy of Shinsengumi No. 0.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ロナエナ ―厄災のギフト (Ronaena: Gift of Disaster)
description:
This game is called Murder Mystery. The player becomes one of the characters in the story and searches for the culprit of the incident that occurs in the story. Since the criminal is also one of the players, the purpose is that the player who became the criminal does not know that he is the criminal.
Each player has his or her own purpose other than searching for the criminal or not knowing it, and each player aims to achieve multiple goals and win the points obtained by achieving them.

"Ronaena -Gift of Disaster-" is a story about searching for the murderer of the murder case that occurs at the research school facility "Ronaena" in the near future in 2025.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 消えたパンツと空飛ぶサカナ (Kieta Pantsu To Soratobu SAKANA)
description:
This game is called Murder Mystery. The player becomes one of the characters in the story and searches for the culprit of the incident that occurs in the story. Since the criminal is also one of the players, the purpose is that the player who became the criminal does not know that he is the criminal.
Each player has his or her own purpose other than searching for the criminal or not knowing it, and each player aims to achieve multiple goals and win the points obtained by achieving them.

"Kieta Pantsu To Soratobu SAKANA -Lost Panties And Fish Fly In The Sky-" is a Murder mystery where five friends living in a share house search for the whereabouts of their lost panties. This story has a shocking impact on everyone.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 六花と灰色の夢見草 (Ricca To Haiiro No Tsukimisou)
description:
This game is called Murder Mystery. The player becomes one of the characters in the story and searches for the culprit of the incident that occurs in the story. Since the criminal is also one of the players, the purpose is that the player who became the criminal does not know that he is the criminal.
Each player has his or her own purpose other than searching for the criminal or not knowing it, and each player aims to achieve multiple goals and win the points obtained by achieving them.

"Ricca To Haiiro No Tsukimisou -Ricca and Gray Cherry Blossoms-" is the first in a series of Murder mystery that seeks out the true criminal by clearing the suspicion of the girl detective "Ricca" who is always the suspect in the case.

Find out the truth about the fall and death of her students in Ricca's high school.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 六花と日輪草のベーカリー (Ricca To Himawari No Bakery)
description:
This game is called Murder Mystery. The player becomes one of the characters in the story and searches for the culprit of the incident that occurs in the story. Since the criminal is also one of the players, the purpose is that the player who became the criminal does not know that he is the criminal.
Each player has his or her own purpose other than searching for the criminal or not knowing it, and each player aims to achieve multiple goals and win the points obtained by achieving them.

"Ricca To Himawari No Bakery -Ricca and Bakery of Sunflower-" is the first in a series of Murder mystery that clears the suspicion of the girl detective "Ricca" who is always the suspect in the case and searches for the true criminal.

We will explore the truth of the owner's death, set in the bakery "Sunflower" where Ricca works.

—description from the designer

Naivety and ingenuity are shared.

Una Que Pensamos Todes is a game of associations. A game that proposes to discover the corners of our minds that we share with the rest. Once per round, a person will turn over a card from the Context Deck and a card from the Trigger deck at the same time. Each player will then have one minute to think or write down a concept or idea that associates both cards. After the minute we will see how many people put the same thing as us and that will be our score. After ten rounds, whoever has scored the most points will be the person who wins.

Rainbow Galaxy is a very unique track in the sense that there are multiple tracks to choose from, each with slightly different lengths and spaces to land on. This track will keep you coming back as you discover which path is ideal for you!

The first new racer is Sonico Oxblade! Instead of driving a normal racekart, Sonico found the fastest Ostrich alive and trained him until he was fast enough to keep up with the fastest of racekarts!

The the second new racer is Fabio! Originally a bodybuilder, Fabio was told that for whatever reason, he just looked like he should be a racer. Only time will tell if he can live up to everyone's racing expectations of him!

—description from the publisher

Includes 1 additional track and 2 more racers.

The Battle of Tukayyid campaign book brings the pivotal conflict of the Clan Invasion to life in stunning grandeur. But the Tukayyid experience is too exciting to be contained in a single volume, and this Supplemental expands upon the options within that book.

Two sections dig further into BattleTech lore, allowing players to use the full range of mapsheets and record sheets published to date, while a Battlefield Support: Combined Arms Worksheet makes those rules easier to use.

Additionally, over two hundred record sheets cover every ’Mech included in the Battle of Tukayyid campaign book. Finally, a Strategic BattleForce expansion includes the rules, maps, and Formation Record Sheets to use the rules in Interstellar Operations to play out the Battle of Tukayyid with large-scale games.

Back in town is an expansion for Saloon Showdown, that adds "skills" to all the characters in the game, for greater depth of gameplay and replayability!

The ranks of the gunslingers in the base game are also joined by the Sheriff and the Farrier, two very special characters. The Sheriff wears the real Sheriff's star, and makes extra bucks if she manages to keep it to herself. The Ferrier is a powerful character who can interrupt the game (almost) any time, by throwing his hammer to other players, in order to collect their bounties.

—description from the publisher

Play Paula Deming (from Things Get Dicey) in any Final Girl Scenario!
Anytime you would roll only 1 die, roll 2 instead. If a single number result is needed, you choose which die to use.

The players have to reach the safe (Franz Jäger Berlin) in the center of the game board. To do this they need certain objects, that are provided by the green cards. The card "geheime Zonenzahl" will be needed by every player. The players move on the board from the edge to the center by playing the matching object card to enter a space. Movement is one space in any direction, orthogonal or diagonal adjacent. If the player has no suitable card, he may roll a die and exchange cards according to the result of the roll, instead of moving his piece.

After their move the players draw a red adversary card and all players have to follow the instructions according to the shown adversary, if applicable according to the colored frame of space they are standing in.

The first player to reach the safe and has a "geheime Zonenzahl" card wins the game.

—user summary

Hanamikoji: Kickstarter Goodies 2021 are the stretch goals unlocked during the Kickstarter campaign of Hanamikoji: Geisha's Road launched on October 26, 2021.

The goodies include:
7 Geisha Alternate Art Promo Cards

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim – The Adventure Game: From the Ashes Expansion
description: A new threat arises from the ashes, a powerful dragon is terrorizing the holds, meanwhile ghosts of your fallen companions haunt your every step. Add two major storylines to your existing game, new Dungeon and Treasure cards, special Armour Sets and more.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim – The Adventure Game: Dawnguard Expansion
description: Expand your adventures with 4 more heroes and their miniatures, a new 3 chapter campaign based on the Dawnguard storyline, and many more hours of gameplay.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

description:
In this fast-paced game, players must solve a word or dexterity challenge before the bomb explodes! In addition to word challenges similar to the classic 'Pass the Bomb' game, this Family edition for 3 to 8 players adds simple skill/dexterity challenges like catching a ball in a cup or quickly 'building' a worm out of puzzle pieces. The word challenge categories are 'Word Acrobat', 'Rhyming Artist', and 'Typical' (thematically-related words). The 'bomb' is an electronic ticking timer that can 'explode' with no warning during the game.

—user summary

Compete with other players to create seamless connections across the San Francisco Bay Area, and build a dream transit system for the Bay Area.

In 30-60 minutes of game play, 2-5 players compete to create an integrated transit network to help Bay Area residents and visitors travel seamlessly around the region.

Strategically place your homes, jobs and activity centers to create a more Seamless Bay Area. Multiply your impact by cutting transfer delays and installing dedicated bus lanes. The player who builds the most connections wins!

Initially setup with the current situation of city and transit delays, then use dice to pick where to put housing. Each turn the player has ways to remove transfer delays or to place more homes, jobs, or activities. Score is based on how closely connected those homes, jobs, and activities are, not just to one player's set.

Postaw na klocka is a board game in which players try to construct the building shown on the card as quickly as possible. Some of them are individual challenges, but most of them require you to join teams temporarily with the players indicated by the card. You should demonstrate not only dexterity here, but also good communication and perceptiveness, because the buildings must be constructed according to the pattern and color. The fastest player or team gets one point each.

When a player's pawn stands on a special field, he must draw a difficulty card. From that moment on, he must play a given grasping technique, e.g. using only his thumbs or not bending his elbow.

Have you ever wanted to understand why healthcare expenses are one of the leading causes of bankruptcies in the US in a fun educational way that also lets you destroy your friends? No? Well too bad, this game exists.

The goal of the game is to avoid getting the Bankruptcy cards. You have everything in your arsenal from GoFundMe campaigns, private equity buyouts, and PBMs to make sure you're the last one to go bankrupt. Discover all the insane ways the healthcare system ends up making you pay while getting distracted by the cute illustrations that describe them. Non-US citizens will not understand this game at all.

—description from the designer

Count Dracula is dead for the second and final time, and unfortunately, he didn't leave a will. As one of his favorite vampiric students, you believe that you should be first in line to inherit. However, all the other acolytes feel the same way! Connive to collect the best mementos for yourself, splitting and sharing Count Dracula's possessions so you get the lion's share of the loot and the bat's share of the glory.

Each round, you'll draw 4 cards from the deck, containing some of Dracula’s belongings that need to be divided up. Then, you'll split those cards into two piles between you and one of your neighbors.

They'll choose one of the piles and give the other one back to you, while you do the same thing with the opposite neighbor. Try to split the piles so that you get the cards you need while preventing your opponents from getting the stuff they want!

Your inheritance is made up of all the cards in front of you, and will grow each round. After placing all the cards you receive that turn into your inheritance, you can sacrifice a single card. Sacrificing a card removes it from your inheritance, but can win you Dracula’s Favor from beyond the grave.

The game ends after 6 rounds, shown on the Central Board in the center of the table. The player with the most Vampire Points (VPs) wins the game, ascending to the highest echelons of vampiric society!

—description from the designer

Set of 8 cards available in the English/Polish edition of Dwergar.

4 cards have simply art on them and don't have a function in the game. The other 4 are extra event cards marked 61 - 64:

61. Each player gets 3 gold
62. Each player can light up all his/her furnaces
63. Each player gets 2 stone or 1 iron
64. Each player gets one new furnaces (if available)

—user summary

Fiesta de Los Juegos is a special edition adapted from Fiesta de los Muertos. Instead of finding deceased celebrities, you'll have to find board game names. This very limited edition celebrates the board game industry and was sold at the French game event Festival des Jeux de Cannes 2022.

Each player chooses a word to describe their starting board game themed card. But beware, this word will pass from hand to hand, and change little by little. Indeed, each turn players will read the clue word written by their neighbour, erase it and write another word related to that clue for the next player around the table. Looking at the final words after they have been passed and altered four times and the starting cards, will everyone be able to find the right connections?

Bonus Card for "Catan - das Duell"
Text on card "Auslage: An ein Hügelland. Sie starten mit 1 Wein. Beim Ereignis Ertragreiches Jahr drehen sie den Weinberg (falls möglich) 1 Stufe höher. Drehen Sie den Weinberg beim Ereignis Fahrender Händler 1 Stufe tiefer, erhalten Sie 1 beliebigen Rohstoff. Bedingung: Kloster"

boardgame: Catan: Das Duell – Sonderkarte 2021: 25 Jahre CATAN-Kartenspiel
description:
Bonus Card for "Catan: Das Duel".
Text on card: "Sie dürfen eine große Festhalle für 2 Rohstoffe weniger bauen. Haben Sie bereits eine Große Festhalle vor dem Ausspielen dieser Karte gebaut, erhalten Sie 2 der bezahlten Rohstoffe zurück."

boardgame: Die Legenden von Andor: Magische Helden – Die Magie von Choranat
description:
Single card expansion for Die Legenden von Andor: Magische Helden.

Die Magie von Choranat gives bonus magic to the heroes.

boardgame: Grim(m) World: First Steps – The Feather, the Stone and the Frog
description: The Feather, the Stone and the Frog is the third mini expansion for Grim(m) World - First Steps. This expansion consists of 9 cards and includes two new rumors and a new level 1 quest, which you can add to your game.

boardgame: Too Many Bones: 40 Caves in Daelore
description:
Specifically designed for Too Many Bones: Unbreakable.
Mix up your Too Many Bones: Unbreakable games with new encounters and Baddies.

40 encounter cards
15 baddie chips

boardgame: BattleTech: MechWarrior Pilot Deck – Warriors of Kerensky I
description: This deck consists of 55 Pilot Cards detailing characters from across the Clans. One side of the each card has the standard Pilot Card entry; the other side depicts a character illustration commissioned for one of the Clan Invasion Kickstarter backers. Each pilot's name, callsign, and quote was also based on details submitted by Clan Invasion Kickstarter backers.

A set of three promo cards for the game "Sen".  Ujawnijcie (Reveal)

3 cards with the same image and text:  Ujawnijcie 1  with a value of 5

The player that uses the card can use it as a normal card or use its special action: the player reveals 1 card from his/her dream, all other players reveal a card lying in the same place in their dream, then the revealed cards are turned down.

Boat Canasta is the fast-moving card game where teams score points by melding cards (combining at least 3 or more cards of the same rank). The goal of the game is for each team to create at least one Canasta, or meld of seven cards, in each round. Teams work in unison, and their luck can change depending on how the cards fall. Point values are printed directly on the cards for easy scoring.

To score the most points by creating Melds, Canastas  and earning bonuses. The first team to reach 10,000 points wins the game. If both teams reach a score of  10,000 points in the same round, then the team with the most points wins. For a shorter game, players can  agree on a lower point total before play begins, say 5,000 points.

boardgame: The Captain Is Dead: The Problem with Priggles
description:
Nobody knows what Priggles are or where they came from. What is known is that they don’t like the aliens. Or maybe it’s the aliens that don’t like them? Either way, who cares? The important thing is that they can be used against the aliens, and that makes them really handy. Unfortunately, they’re clingy little things, and once they get their cute little hooks in you, it can be really hard to shake them off...

This microexpansion for The Captain is Dead adds Priggles!  Shuffle these little critters into the skill deck. You may draw one, and you can't get rid of it.  But when you have three of them, you can discard all three to get rid of aliens on the ship!

Pack contains 9 double-sided MechWarrior pilot Cards, for a total of 18 named MechWarriors.

• Yurika Suzume/Mackenzie Cross
• Robert Otek/Tracy Maxwell Kent
• Scotty Rosek/Sawako Maki
• Garrett Cedeno/Cole
• Odilon Rodriguez/Jorma Mercer
• Jillian Snuka/Audrey
• Jevanrod Ward/Rauf Icaza
• Trent Leroux/Geertruida
• Reshondra Descatris/Vaiana

A Clan Invasion Kickstarter exclusive, Card Pack I was known as MechWarrior Pilots II during the crowdfunding campaign.

Pack contains 9 double-sided MechWarrior pilot Cards, for a total of 18 named MechWarriors.

• Maconaquea Akerlund/Okubo Henderson
• Dieter Shackle/David Kendal
• Zaïre Danielson/Zhong Shui
• Maxim Vasiliev/Arthur Rutherford
• Vyna/Garo
• Hela/Magnus
• Stephania/Elvin
• Ivan/Bestla
• Polina Vickers/Eluris

A Clan Invasion Kickstarter exclusive, Card Pack II was known as MechWarrior Pilots III during the crowdfunding campaign.

boardgame: Devil Bunny versus the Entire Galaxy
description:
You are intrepid actuaries collecting butterflies of a specific color at a circus. Devil Bunny loves butterflies, and whenever he sees a big enough stack of them, he jumps on that stack, scattering them everywhere. Who will be the first to save enough butterflies, before Devil Bunny destroys the Galaxy?

Each player chooses a color of butterflies to catch. Now you roll the dice. 1-5 and you’re in the clear. You can add more butterflies to the map, or guide the ones already there back to your circus tent. The color of the top token of each stack determines who guides the rest on the path back, and you can only move as far as there are tokens in the stack. But be careful! Roll a 6 and Devil Bunny hops on your stack and scatters your butterflies to the winds! First to collect three wins!

boardgame: BattleTech: MechWarrior Pilot Deck – Champions of the Inner Sphere I
description: This deck consists of 55 Pilot Cards detailing characters from across the Inner Sphere. One side of the each card has the standard Pilot Card entry; the other side depicts a character illustration commissioned for one of the Kickstarter backers. Each pilot's name, nickname and quote was also based on detail submitted by Kickstarter backers.

boardgame: BattleTech: MechWarrior Pilot Deck – Mercenaries of the Inner Sphere II
description: This deck consists of 55 Pilot Cards detailing characters from across the Inner Sphere. One side of the each card has the standard Pilot Card entry; the other side depicts a character illustration commissioned for one of the Kickstarter backers. Each pilot's name, nickname and quote was also based on detail submitted by Kickstarter backers.

boardgame: BattleTech: MechWarrior Pilot Deck – Heroes of the Clan Invasion
description:
This deck consists of 55 Pilot Cards detailing characters from across the Inner Sphere. One side of the each card has the standard Pilot Card entry; the other side depicts a character illustration commissioned for one of the Kickstarter backers. Each pilot's name, nickname and quote was also based on detail submitted by Kickstarter backers.

This deck was originally listed as Mechwarrior Pilot Deck: Battle of Tukayyid during the crowdfunding campaign.

boardgame: Ура, это гриб! (Hooray! It's a mushroom!)
description:
In Ура, это гриб! ("Hooray! It's a mushroom!") players find themselves in a magical forest and, like real mushroom pickers, try to find as good mushrooms as possible.

On your turn, you may take any visible card. It is at this moment that you need to be as careful as possible, because you can often understand from the card back which mushroom or plant is hiding on the back. It can be a valuable mushroom or berry or a poisonous mushroom can be caught, which will take away victory points from you at the end of the game, or it can be nothing at all.

Once all the cards have been picked up, points are totalled and the player with the most points wins.


Настольная игра Ура, это гриб! – это игра на внимательность для самых маленьких игроков (впрочем, это не значит, что взрослым будет скучно или неинтересно). В ней игроки попадают в волшебный лес и, как настоящие грибники, пытаются найти как можно хороших грибов.Подготовить «Ура, это гриб!» к игре очень просто: нужно разложить карты рубашкой вверх посреди стола. Как именно они лежат не важно – в лесу тоже ничего не бывает аккуратно, а горка карт в этой игре и правда выглядит как лесная поляна.

В свой ход в настольной игре «Ура, это гриб!» игрок может взять любую видимую карту. Именно в этот момент нужно быть максимально внимательным, ведь по рубашке часто можно понять, какой гриб или растение скрывается на обороте. Это может быть ценный гриб или ягода, может попасться ядовитый гриб, который отнимет у вас победные очки в конце игры, а может и вовсе ничего не попасться.

Настольная игра «Ура, это гриб!» – это простая и увлекательная игра, которая помогает развивать внимательность и мелкую моторику. Правила объясняются всего за минуту, а сыграть в неё можно везде – лишь бы хватило места разложить карты.

While the Great Houses of the Inner Sphere decimated each other over the shattered remains of the Star League and slid into the technological dark age of the Succession Wars, the Clans of Kerensky entered into a golden era of prosperity. Bereft of Nicholas Kerensky’s leadership, the Clans pushed their technological capabilities to their limits, developing devastating new weapons and technologies and flooding the battlefields of the Pentagon and Kerensky Cluster with powerful war machines as they trialed each other for supremacy.

Technical Readout: Golden Century unveils the weapons and technologies developed during a pivotal era in Clan history, when the Clans came of age and matured into what they are today. Technical Readout: Golden Century debuts the production and custom machines used by the Children of Kerensky in the early twenty-ninth and thirtieth centuries. Statistics and Record Sheets are included for 29 new advanced and experimental BattleMechs, OmniMechs, vehicles, aerospace, battle armor and WarShip units ready for play. In addition, a complete section covers the experimental designs introduced by the nascent Clans immediately following the Pentagon campaign.

King Saul is a 2-5 player diplomatic-economic-wargame wherein battles, cards, cities, diplomacy & harvests are all  important keys to victory.

In King Saul: Will Saul defeat the Ammonites and be annointed King at Gilgal? Will Saul listen to Samuel, and be able to pass the Kingdom onto Jonathan? Once again, will Saul kill his thousands, and David his thousands upon thousands? What role will Doeg the Edomite play? Who will marry Saul’s daughter Michal? What will be the fate of Ahimelech, Ishbaal, and the Priests of Nob?

As the Ammonite King Nahash will you gouge out the eye of Israelite at Jabesh-Gilead? Will disunity in the Israeli command structure result in a weakened Israel? If Saul hunts after David, will that then be your choice time to attack? Will Saul be vexed by an evil spirit, and consult the witch at Endor? Will the Amelekites once again be victorious at the battle of Mount Gilboa?
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Lords of Ragnarok: Seas of Aegir
description:
The Aegir monument introduces Snekkar ships. Each player will receive four such boats and will be able to put them on the board in the spot with their Drakkar and then move them on the same rules as their Drakkar (dividing the movement between their Snekkar and/or their Drakkar). The Aegir monument replaces either Thor or Odin from the base game, and Nagflar will replace Loki as an alternate boss.

Contents: 
- Aegir monument with associated help and artifact cards 
- 20 Snekkar ship models 
- Nagflar monster model with monster card

- description from the publisher

Take a trip around the world and get to know some of the most amazing and unique sights on the planet! On your turn, try to recognize famous wonders and place them on the right continents. If you don’t guess correctly, the clues will help you find the right answer!

This Fact-Packed educational game tests your knowledge and takes you on a trip. Name the manmade and natural wonders on each card. Then guess their location or purpose to win the cards and score points. The player with the most points wins.

Contains 195 wonders from all corners of the world, and a map marked with their locations.

Note: Reimplements an earlier 2004 game of the same name, Wonders of the World.

A Card Game for the Normal Mormon This is essentially a edgy Mormon version of Cards against Humanity. Or in a more Mormon speaking way, a edgy version of Apples to Apples.

Draw a tan card, everybody picks one black card from their hand and you pick the best answer.  First to 5 points wins.

Roll a die at the beginning of your turn, if you roll a 6, draw a card and preform it's action. you can also try to inflict a Lucifer card on another player.
family: Religious: Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints

description:
This is a set of two additional Luminaries, Leonardo da Vinci and Johannes Gutenberg.  Each Luminary comes with its own player mat, card, and portrait token.  They may be integrated into the base game without modification to the rules.

This set was only available by pledging at the Renaissance pledge level or higher during the kickstarter.

An expansion for both Mixtape Massacre and Mixtape Massacre: Escape From Tall Oaks.

When Mr. Beckman and his friends went out for the annual Tall Oaks Hunt, they never came back. Two days later, a search party found him in the woods, covered in the blood of his friends, rambling about a myth from Tall Oaks lore. He quickly became the prime suspect. Now Kimmy and friends must work together to prove the existence of the Myth and save her father.

To dispel gossip and rumors, the Mayor called in esteemed big game hunter, Duke Morrison. Morrison was given two directives: prove or disprove the Myths’s existence and rid Tall Oaks of their slasher problem once and for all. He gladly accepted.

This expansion take the Scare character and gives it more than just a character and a couple cards.

The Scare booster is a playable character in Mixtape Massacre. In the Hallow's Eve expansion The Scare serves as the main adversary. And as you can tell by the picture above.

boardgame: Mixtape Massacre: Snack Attack Killer Pack
description: Adds 3 new characters to the game.  Delicious movie snacks have turned into killing fiends.  The Kernal, Frank and Bubbles are on the rampage!

In Kingyo, you have to catch as many Kingyo (goldfishes) as possible, matching the colors of the dice.

The Kingyo cards are laid out around the 5 dice. Players can divide themselves into teams as they wish. Each player takes a Bowl card to place the Kingyo cards they will catch.

One player rolls the 5 dice, then all players simultaneously try to catch the cards with Kingyo that match the colors of the dice, announcing the colors of the Kingyo caught.

Some cards have bonuses in addition to the Kingyo:
If you catch a Dragonfly, roll all 5 dice again.
If you catch a Hornet, each opponent must discard a card from their bowl.
If you catch a Scoop, you can immediately take any card.

When you think it's no longer possible to catch Kingyo cards with colors that match the dice, catch one of the special "Medaka" cards.  The game is over for you, but other players can continue to catch cards if they find any. Be careful because there is one less Medaka card than the number of players...

At the end of the game, players score 1 point for each Kingyo and 4 points for the Medaka card in their bowl. In a team game, only the team's bowl with the lowest score will count.

- description from designers

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar (Third Edition): Order Battletome – Stormcast Eternals
description:
Stormcast Eternals are the mightiest warriors of Order. Each forged in Azyr by Sigmar's hand, these noble warriors strike out into the realms to do battle. Riding coruscating thunderbolts, they arrive on the field of war with an ear-splitting thunderclap. The forces of the Stormcast Eternals are the mighty hammer that crushes the forces of Chaos and the shield that protects the helpless citizens of the realms from the myriad encroaching evils. Armed with weapons blessed with celestial energy, these immortal demigods' fury and faith knows no bounds.

In this book, you'll discover how the Stormcast Eternals, mortal heroes chosen by the God-King to be Reforged into his foremost warriors, fought to bring hope to the war-ravaged Mortal Realms. Battletome: Stormcast Eternals is also packed with all the rules you need to create your own force of Order, from the Stormcasts' finest champions to the ancient draconic lords.

This 184-page hardback book contains:


     A wealth of lore about the Stormcast Eternals covering everything from the Realmgate Wars to the Era of the Beast and the Dawnbringer Crusades, complete with stunning illustrations
     Background information on every Stormhost and unit available to the Stormcast Eternals
     A showcase of expertly painted Citadel miniatures and an in-depth painting guide covering a variety of Stormhosts
     76 warscrolls covering every Stormcast Eternals unit, including three faction-specific endless spells
     Rules for both Scions of the Storm and Stormkeep armies, as well battle traits, spell lores, mount traits, and holy commands
     Brand new Path to Glory rules, including rules for Reforging, bespoke Stormcast Eternals territories, four quests, two battle plans, 15 warscroll battalions, and more
     Pitched battle profiles with points for matched play games
     And more!


—description from the publisher

I.G.A.T is a modern tactical military tabletop solo (or cooperative) game.

The player select 5 soldiers with equipment, weapons, secondary weapons, accessories, helmet etc....
After this he can go on operations for help local police in Austria for town operation, kidnapping, bank attack etc...

The game has no phase, player make an action and IA make reaction. In the buildings the player need to discover contact point. The contact point become situation, enemy, civilian and others many events (dogs attack, electricity cut...)

The player must take care of alert, if alert is high, situation become more dangerous.

Campaign system for wounds, levelling items and high customise weapons.

Like in the video game swat4, you need to score points for win the mission and gain xp for levelling soldiers.

—description from the designer

In Dino Dino you are a dinosaur caretaker. Build your own park, collect food and place as many dinosaurs as possible in an ever expanding world! Does your collection ultimately score the most?

Dino Dino is played over several rounds in which each player takes separate turns. During a turn, the player can choose one of the following 2 actions.

Action 1: Place Land Tile
Action 2: Place Dino

Action 1 will be performed the most. During this action, the player takes 1 of the face up land tiles. This is then placed on its existing land. This way, your own park will expand in size. As soon as you place the tile you get food. There are 3 types of food: plants, fish and meat. When the same food icons touch each other you create a chain. This way you get extra food.

If a player has enough food, action 2 can be performed. Every dinosaur has some kind of food that they want to eat, you need a certain amount of this food to be able to place them in your park. The dinosaurs give you points for a certain food type at the end of the game. This depends on the number of food icons surrounding the dinosaur in the park.

The game is over when all the land tiles have been placed. The player with the highest score wins the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: The Card Game – The Dark of Mirkwood
description:
Your Fellowship of heroes have narrowly escaped from their ordeal in Dol Guldur. After a brief stay in Lórien, your party is on its way back to Thranduil when they see smoke billowing up from the eaves of Mirkwood. The black plumes lead you to the smoldering remains of a Woodman village, with bodies strewn about and black-feathered arrows sticking from their backs. This is clearly the work of Goblins, and the pained gasps of a dying Woodman tells you that the survivors were taken by the fiends to their lair within the forest. You swear an oath to rescue them if you can, or avenge them if you cannot, and so you draw your weapons and follow the trail into the woods.

This standalone scenario pack contains two adventures: The Oath and The Caves of Nibin-dûm. These scenarios were originally released as part of the Limited Collector’s Edition for The Lord of the Rings LCG. Now, with the inclusion of new boon and burden cards and the onset of the Revised Core Set, you and your fellow heroes can take on these two challenging scenarios as their own two-part adventure or as an extension of the core set’s Mirkwood Paths campaign. Will you track down the Goblins and rescue the captives? Or will you become lost in the dark of Mirkwood?

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)
Game: The Lord of the Rings – The Card Game (LCG)

description:
The players take turns to roll and move. They start at space 1 (Lindau). The first to reach space 55 (Bern) wins the game. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

—user summary

The Aeronautica Imperialis: Wrath of Angels boxed set includes everything you need to play exciting games of aerial combat between brutal Adeptus Astartes and sleek Asuryani aircraft.

The set includes the following Citadel multipart plastic miniatures, each supplied with an Aeronautica Imperialis base:

3x Adeptus Astartes Xiphon Interceptors
2x Adeptus Astartes Storm Eagles
3x Asuryani Nightwings
3x Asuryani Phoenix Bombers

The set also includes a 38-page rulebook featuring the following information:

The full Aeronautica Imperialis game rules, including how to build your force using Squadron Lists, perform Ace Manoeuvres, field different classes of aircraft, and unleash ground defences. Learn how to manoeuvre your aircraft, adjust their altitude, and change speed while avoiding the risks of stalling and breaking up. There are even rules for special manoeuvres including landing, taxiing, and taking off.


     The Dogfight scenario, as well as optional rules for fighting at night or in bad weather conditions.  
     Squadron Lists, providing details of all of the aircraft in the box as well as their weapon options and special rules.
     A reference page showing the 8 different manoeuvres aircraft can take, as well as a 2-page quick-reference guide containing the key game rules. 
     Wrath of Angels Starter Scenarios Booklet (including 3 additional scenarios to play)
     Duble-sided Area of Engagement game mat (depicting two aerial war zones)
     Quick-reference cards (containing the key game rules)
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A Picture is Worth a Thousand Words

Imagym is a game which consists in drawing words your opponent will try to figure out and in figuring out the words your opponent will have drawn. In order to line up your tokens on the game board and to win the game, you will have to use your imagination as well as your sense of observation and of strategy.

In turn, each opponent picks an indicator card and a 'difficulty' card, examines them, finds the word he/she has to draw on the game board and then places the cards, face down, in front of him/her. Each opponent draws his/her word on a sheet of his/her drawing pad. When all drawings are completed, each opponent shows his/her drawing to the others, long enough for them to try and figure out the word drawn. You get tokens for figuring out opponent word drawings and when they figure out your drawings.

The Winner is the player or the team which is the first to line up 4 tokens on the game board, either horizontally, vertically or diagonally.

Every seven summers the druid games take place. In them, druids from different areas use their skills to command creatures in tactical confrontations. (No animals or legendary creatures are harmed in the games)

Druid Games is a simple card game (played with a 54 card deck) in which each player will play combinations of cards across five terrains. The druid that is victorious in three or more of them is proclaimed the winner of that edition of the Druid Games. Different creatures have specific characteristics (fly, swim, walk) that might limit the terrains in which they can be played. Additionally special cards allows some 'druid magic' to take place. Yes, you can give wings to a walrus to have a flying walrus.
Gameplay:
Each turn a player will place a creature card on one of the five terrains and, if desired, play special cards to produce 'druid magic' effects. The game will finish after all creature cards have been played (15 turns). The winner will be the druid that is victorious in three or more terrains.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: British vs Pirates: Volume 2 – Lost Treasure of Dantes
description:
This PDF download is a single player voyage where you will find 4 Chapters of rich storyline that will test your might in ship combat vs the evil NPC (non player control). As you complete each Chapter you'll receive SPECIAL CARDS for that faction that may be used in other game modes!

All of this content is digital and printable, or you may simply reference the PDF while you play.  Requires British vs Pirates Volume 2.

Dawning Horror is an expansion for Four against the Great Old Ones. It contains 5 programmed adventures, each featuring an Investigator and a team of three Helpers. The adventures are:

     The Horror of the Mine
     Howl of the Mutated
     Night Tour
     Return to Antarctica
     The Inheritance


This expansion is a prequel to Four against the Great Old ones, since the heroes are peeking beyond the veil of sanity and normality for the first time, and advantages gained in Dawning Horror can be transferred to the main game in the form of Upgraded Investigators. It can be played with Investigators from the base game, Carcosa Rising, or from two brand new classes: the Archaeologist and the Redeemed Cultist.

It's Halloween, and it's time to go trick-or-treating!

The aim of the game is to get as much candy as you can, while running away from grumpy neighbors. The game consists of 13 dice — 12 white (treat) dice and 1 orange (trick) die. There are 6 different kinds of candy to collect that fall into 3 types; chocolate, lollipop or gummy.

A player's turn involves repeating two phases — rolling and collecting — until all the dice are set aside. When the dice are rolled, you must first set aside all Grumps. You can then choose any number of Runs to set aside, keeping in mind if there are more Grumps than Runs at the end of your turn, you get 0 points. You then choose any amount of one kind of candy to collect, each worth one point. On the orange die, a Double Trouble must be set aside and requires two Runs to escape. Tricks are worth nothing and can be re-rolled. If you roll a Mystery Candy, you choose at the end of your turn which candy it represents, as different combinations of collected candy get you bonus points!

The first person to get 23 points triggers the end of the game, and the person with the most points at the end of that round wins.

—description from the publisher

Die Baumeister von Köln is a board game for two to four players set in medieval Cologne, Germany. The theme is the construction of the city's 12 large Romanesque churches. Players must use their two carts each to bring building stones from the city gates to the construction sites on a historical city map. There they unload their cargo. Whoever has contributed the highest value of building stones to the construction of a building receives points upon its completion. At the beginning of each turn, dice are rolled to determine which building stone will be delivered to which city gate. The players try to block each other's paths in the city with their carts in order to be faster than the others. Encounter cards bring historical figures such as abbots, abbesses, mayors, thieves, etc. into play, allowing interaction with the other players.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Bad Aim isn’t your regular “sit around a table” adult board game. It’s a WTF-just-happened, unpredictable action game for adults where two teams race to shoot down targets that contain outrageous challenge cards. The first team to shoot down their targets wins, while the losing team is forced to complete the challenges left standing. Bottom line? Those with bad aim will be shamed. This adult party game will have you questioning your friends’ sanity, and quite possibly, all of humanity.

—description from the publisher

A game of collecting sets of photographs of famous Disney cartoon characters - Mickey Mouse, Pluto, Donald Duck and more - by exploring the Disney village. Players roll dice and try to move quickly and capture the right shot before their opponents do. Those with the sharpest tactics will prevail, and need to keep their wits about them.

Rainbow River is a grid-movement tableau-building game where players compete to get the most resources from an ever-changing board, with a unique setup for each game. Players play as explorers trying to piece together a cohesive map of the mystical rainbow river and fighting over map scraps.

You start with a randomized board and a unique Preference card. As the game develops, the board will shrink, and players will attempt to collect map tiles that will advance their river.

Build the longest river in order to secure victory!

—description from the designer

The Apalpador and his friends are organizing a big "magosto" so they need to go out into the woods to look for chestnuts. Who will get more? In this game for the whole family you must use your favorite characters on each round to be the player who collects the most chestnuts and get the title of Master Chestnut.

Each player will draw cards from the deck to collect as many chestnuts as possible before three hedgehogs appear among their collection. The cards show chestnuts or hedgehogs. The player draws cards until his turn is over, when the player decides to stop drawing them or three hedgehogs have appeared. In the first case, the player will choose one of those cards for his reserve; on the other hand, in the second, he will lose all the cards that he has obtained.
The player with the most chestnuts at the end of the game will be the winner of the game.

In this game emotions, tension, but also joy and well-being await you, just as you like it during real races. In the game you will start training the physical and mental qualities of your jumper. You will manage its energy, which will be important in racing. Of course, you will jump from a ski jumping hill with a realistic-looking jumper figure. 
As in real jumps, you will solve not only the length of the jump, but also the style and point evaluation of the judges. Your jumps will also be affected by the wind and also the equipment in which you will jump. 
The aim of the game is to place as best as possible in the individual races of the cup tour and become its overall winner.

At the beginning, each player slides the jumper's figure from the hill, and based on the distance determined by the rollers, we determine the order in which the player's cards are selected. Each of them have certain predispositions such as psyche, speed, technique, etc. Once we have chosen the player's card, we go to choose the flag of which nationality you want to represent. There is also a card draw on which hill we will jump. They are shuffled, then turn. So let's say it's going to be Japan, for example. This card is placed on the 3D hill and indicates to us what nation we jump in. Based on that, the home jumpers will benefit from a better meters rating simulating crowd support. And then the size of the hill is randomly selected by turning over the top card of the bridge and finding out that we are racing on the K90 hill, for example. Then we can go into the training of our ski jumper (players cards). Based on the different predispositions of individual jumpers, we can improve individual training skills, which happens in specialized rooms in gyms on the board and therefore we can choose which ability we want to improve. They are on special cards. This gains points on the player's card, but we lose time and energy while we train. And as we lose time, we gradually get closer to the day of the race, and as soon as we get to the seventh day, we can go jump from the hill. Before the race starts and we go jumping, we calculate the points we have gathered in training and then we will find it on the spinner table. According to the hill on which the race is held. And if we had the same nationality as the flag on the hill, we would get more bonus meters. Which is like fans support us. So, after entering the values, you can jump. Jumping is made by putting your plastic figure down from the hill. At the edge of the hill there are two rollers which are triggered by the plastic figure and there have plus and minus symbol and numbers. These rollers simulate so-called random weather factor such as the Wind, or the current mental state of the jumper, etc. If we jumped +1 point. We will write down all those things in a notebook and in the final evaluation of the jump, in addition to the points gained in training, the place where the jumper finished will also be recorded. We have different point areas. The possibility of falling is incorporated into the game. If the jumper does not reach the point area or fall, he will not get any points. But he also has another way to get points for style as in real, and these are the cards that represent the points we know from ski jumping. The points work by dealing 5 cards to the player, he does not look at them. Another five cards are dealt in front of him, and after turning the hourglass, which has a limit of 30 seconds, the player tries to find the best possible numbers in order to get the best points for style. After matching the cards, the highest and the lowest value is deleted, as it works in real races. The remaining three values ​​on the cards are summed up and written down in a notebook. All entered values ​​are then summed up on a special designed notebook so you don't have to remember all you data and the winner gets one of the trophies.

—description from the publisher

This is an easy dice game simulating a baseball game.
The outcome of the a dice roll with two dice determines the outcome of every at bat.

boardgame: Don't Quote Me Playing Cards: Sport Quotes
description:
Pentagonal cards with five suits.
Can be used to play traditional card games with the twist of a fifth suit.

Pied Piper Storyland is a roll and move children's game made in Australia by G.N. Raymond in Melbourne during the 1950's.

The game board consists of a concentric path beginning at the Gates of Storyland where they place a wooden token. Players roll the dice and begin their inward  spiral journey through Storyland where they become accustom to the 50 Nursery Rhyme and Fairy-tale characters encountered. Players must obey the directions when landing on a Nursery Rhyme character and there are detours on the path that must be followed. First player to reach home in the center of the board is greeted by "Old King Cole" and declared the winner.

—user summary

NätTrollz! is a party card game for 2 to 5 players (you can be more but then the choices are reduced slightly) from 10 years. In the game, you shoulder an online role with a single purpose - win or destroy as many discussion threads as possible on your particular social internet platform.

On your turn, players lay cards on the discard piles by matching the color icons in the upper corner.   A player wins a discussion thread by playing a picture of a Cute Kitten in the thread.

The game ends when all 5 cats have been played. The point values of cards are totalled and the player who has scored the most points when all five cat pictures have been played, wins the whole evening and can calmly log out with the keyboard intact. The cards you have left on hand at the end of the game give minus points, so you never want to let a good argument be lost!

Welcome to Häxjakten (the Witch Hunt)! A fast-paced race against the cauldron of destiny to decide who will be the witch master!

Häxjakten is a family game where you have to hunt for ingredients around all corners of the world to catch up with your opponents to brew your brews and become a witch master. But beware of the portal, monster attacks and the other witches who try to stand in your way.

Players roll the dice to move around the board and collect ingredient cards when landing on special spots.  They then have to move to the center of the board to brew their potion.  The first person to brew their potion, wins.

Häxjakten is created by Magnus Stenbenke from Växjö who runs the board game company Fungeon AB.

Räkna med monster ("Count with monsters") is a fun game where the children must match the right number of monster accessories with the right box in their monster nest. During the game, the children practice counting, recognizing numbers and also a lot of memory training. The first child to fill their card, wins.

Simple racing game with 15 illustrations of how to behave in an air raid.

Standard roll and move mechanics.

date from Main POost Wurzburg article

Take your warfare to a whole new level of excitement! Deploy under cover of exotic weather and severe planetary conditions. Surprise your enemy with your mastery of new battlefield tactics—from artillery to command-level comms to minefields! A daring commander can take advantage of any or all of these….if he’s brave enough to seize the moment!

Tactical Operations:Advanced Rules is the one-source reference for advanced rules that apply to on-world operations. It includes new movement and combat options.

Note: Together with Tactical Operations: Advanced Units & Equipment, the content of Tactical Operations: Advanced Rules made up the book, Tactical Operations. But in June of 2020 it was split in two, because it was deemed to be too long with over 400 pages.

boardgame: BattleTech: Tactical Operations – Advanced Units & Equipment
description:
Take your warfare to a whole new level of excitement! Deploy new forces and surprise your enemy with cutting-edge, prototype technologies! A daring commander can take advantage of any or all of these….if he’s brave enough to seize the moment!

Tactical Operations: Advanced Units & Equipment is the one-source reference for advanced units types and advanced technologies. It includes an extensive Advanced Weapons and Equipment section, and the rules for playing and constructing advanced Support Vehicles and Mobile Structures.

boardgame: Niko-Reise um die Welt: Tour de Roche
description:
A combination of travel-around-the-world and an exciting knowledge game, as specific questions must be answered in each continent in order to continue one's journey.

The players each choose one of the of the six starting places distributed on the continents and place the game piece of the corresponding color on it. The player rolls the red and black dice at the same time. They advance according to the black score. The number of points rolled in red indicates the number of questions (1-6) on the question card corresponding to the respective travel station (2-97). Correct answers allow the player to advance further according to the number of points rolled on the red dice. For an incorrect answer, he must move back as many stations as he last rolled the red die (e.g. two =question two=not answered= two back).

The first to reach the starting point again wins.

Im Tigerenten Club is a game based on the German TV-show Tigerenten Club. It is a roll-and-move game. Some of the fields that a player may land on have special events, and others trigger a question from question cards on everyday topics. The winner is the first to reach the finish line.

—user summary

English 3er-Memo is a Memory game in which players attempt to find triplets consisting of a picture, the English word for that picture, and the German word for that picture.

As in regular Memory, players take turns. Each turn, a player turns open three tiles. If they have a correct triplet, they keep the tiles. If not, they turn them face-down again.

When all tiles have been taken, the player with the most triplets wins the game.

—user summary

boardgame: Englisch für Kinder: Einfache Redewendungen – Erste Sätze
description:
The question cards of this quiz/learning game are taken from children's everyday situations. On the front of each card is the picture and the German phrase. On the back is the English name together with child-friendly phonetic transcription for learning the correct pronunciation. You roll the dice and move on the map, on a question field you may translate an idiom. Whoever gets it right gets a chip, a question field without chips is considered an empty field. The first player to collect 8 chips wins.

—user summary

On the playing field with the numbers 1-9 and the field "Cash box" chips are placed on 5 fields. The other fields remain free. Each player receives the same number of chips. The player whose turn it is rolls the dice and, if he has rolled an occupied square, may move on with this chip(s) until he reaches an empty square or the cash register and collects the winnings.
