boardgame: Jr. Rangerland Moth and Butterfly Blitz
description:
Description from the publisher: Roll the die and be the first to complete a butterfly or a moth. Watch out because your opponents can swipe your cards, but so can you. Each card includes fun butterfly facts.

In this card turning, set collecting game, a roll of the die determines the action you can take.

2014 Creative Child Magazine Preferred Choice Award

Imagine if there was an algorithm that WASN’T dumb…What if there was an algorithm that didn’t deliver what it thought you’d like, but what you didn’t even know you wanted? In short, an algorithm that harnessed the chaotic discovery of a Video Shop, where chance encounters and unanticipated juxtapositions on the shelf led to new associations and experiences?

Sadly, there is no algorithm that can do that. But now, there is a game that can.

Video Shop Algorithm revives the “cognitive congeniality” of the video library, where objects and people in space combine to create fresh connections and new knowledge about film and video – ever the currencies of contemporary culture.

Working together as a team, you are rewarded for the number and complexity of connections you can draw between an ever-expanding mind map of film titles. Without access to your smartphone, you must draw deep on your memory and stored knowledge, as well as build upon that of your fellow team mates to tease out unexpected connections and ford unbridgeable divides.

In Yggdrasus, each player becomes a nation and engages in a three-year battle against the Kaiju: Yggdrasus. Use your nation's unique abilities, strategies, and international commitments to achieve victory.

The first step is to send your army around the world and complete local missions. In this way, you can raise the level of your army, or build facilities and secure supplies to grow your nation. Increasing your charisma and gaining the support of international public opinion will bring you many benefits.

There are many ways to earn VP, but the best way to earn a lot of VP is to attack and defeat the Kaiju itself, or to accept and feed refugees.

The game ends after 12 turns, and the nation that has earned the most VP is the winner.

Dice management board game for 2 to 5 players.

Modify the results obtained by making your Robot's skills interact with the values of the dice and use the Programs to solve the most complicated situations.

This 12 tiles expansion contains 6 special park tiles and 6 new goal tiles.

In this 2-player variant of Roll to the Top!, the players start at opposite sides.
Instead of playing turns simultaneous, one player fills in first (the player who didn't roll the dice) and the other second.
Goal of the game is to fill the most spaces.

The sheet has 2 sides:

A Monopoly-like game based on a ironic reinterpretation of Turin.

Put on your high tops and your biggest bow to get ready for the brightest, most exciting Monopoly game ever! Join the party with a worldwide superstar as sweets, sparkles, and of course hair bows beam from the tabletop with Monopoly: JoJo Siwa.

Believe in yourself with a version of the classic game that's as playful and positive as the role model herself! The fun doesn't stop as you buy, sell, and trade locations named for all of JoJo's favorite hair bows, such as the colorful Unicorn Horn, super fancy Pink and Gold Pearl, or the starred and striped All-American Girl!

Move just like the Dance Moms performer with every turn using six custom tokens for all the tour essentials: choose the Cupcake to feel like a kid in a candy store, follow your D.R.E.A.M. with the Unicorn or Rainbow, strut the board with the High-Top Shoe, take BowBow for a walk, or grab the Microphone to shout it out wherever you are!

boardgame: Etherfields: Stretch Goals – Harpy & She-Wolf Campaigns
description: The Harpy Campaign adds 30+ hours of game play and exploration and the Harpy miniature.

Yammer is a game where you make words from letter cards in your hand and on the table to score the most points possible. The deck has 108 cards: 51 red letters, 51 orange letters, 2 wild cards, 2 2x multiplier cards, and 2 3x multiplier cards. It has rules for four games:

Yammer:  Make a new word from cards in the hand and/or the well, scoring the sum of the letter values, doubled if they are all the same color, and times any multiplier cards played. Cards from the deck are used to replenish the well first, and then the hand. The highest score when the deck runs out wins.

Yammer Solitaire: Yammer for one player, aiming to have a high score at the end, or the fewest cards unused.

Yammy: Gin Rummy with the Yammer deck.  Wild cards and multipliers are removed. Players start with 9-card hands, and on their turn they draw from the top of the draw or discard pile, and then discard a card. The round ends when one player can use their whole hand to spell words, each of which must be of the same color.  Play until winning a target number of rounds or reaching a target total score.

500 Yammy: 500 Rummy with the Yammer deck. Like Yammy, except that players may play single-color words during their turn, and/or add to existing words on the table.  They may also draw any card in the discard pile, but they must use that card to make a word immediately, and must draw all cards above that card in the pile.  A round ends when one person depletes their hand.  Play until reaching a target total score.

Will you be the first to set foot in Atlantis for thousands of years? Can you find and capture a live Yeti? Where is the hidden tomb of Alexander the Great? Only the most daring of pulp heroes can survive these adventures!

From 2-6 adventurers will play as pulp-era heroes and race from continent to continent, over oceans and seas, attempting to find priceless artifacts and mysterious locales - all in the name of fortune and glory!

Each turn, players will head towards a rumored artifact (card) on the map board, then have to deal with the Perils that protect it. Dodge deadly traps, defeat vicious guardians and overcome impossible obstacles using your stats of Brains, Brawn and Deftness with a simple d6 roll.

But don't go it alone; recruit stalwart partners and bring along useful equipment to augment your own heroic abilities. First hero to achieve 100 Glory points wins!
 Will you become a legendary adventurer or a simple footnote in history?

Not Another Fetch Quest!!! is a fast and fun party card game with a fantasy theme that pokes fun at one of the most annoyingly unnecessary elements in the RPG realm: fetch questing. Take the role of one of the Kingdom of Renfaire's most mundane of heroes and use your wits to conquer the quests before your rivals do.

Each player is represented by a character, which have unique stats and special abilities. Players take turns adding quest cards (with difficulty values that correspond to player stats) to the notice board until a player is challenged. When a player is challenged, they see if their stats (and loot, if needed) can overcome the challenges of the quests.

A player can win in one of two ways: if they win three challenges and get three success tokens, or if the player is the last one standing when all other players fail three challenges. It's suggested to play to two wins/losses if in a group of five or more. Also check out the other play modes for faster or more devious play styles.

Shepherds have led several flocks into a nearby valley to graze and breed.  They had hoped to keep the whole thing a secret.  Last night dragons were seen in the area and everyone knows they've come for the sheep and there is little anyone can do to stop them.

The Sheep Raid scenario turns the common strategies of Battle Skies on their head.  Flying high and fast won't help you collect any sheep.  Now you have to fly low to grab them and balance your need for speed with your ability to snatch sheep off the ground.  This time the battle isn't to the last dragon standing but to the last sheep found and the winner is the one who has the most sheep at the end.

Sheep Raid is a scenario for Battle Skies, in which the players are dragons attempting to collect the most sheep.  But beware, the shepherds have created decoy sheep filled with explosives, glue bombs, and other dangerous traps!

A scenario for Battle Skies.

A recent earthquake near the heart of the dwarven kingdoms opened up a path down into one of their deepest treasuries.  Vaults filled with gold, gems, and other valuables, a great many of which were stolen from the dragons they had driven out of the cave centuries before.  Legends have it that the dragon who lived in the cave fought to defend it until the bitter end.  It is said that in those last minutes he withdrew into himself and all that was left of him was a glowing crystal egg that has become known as a soul gem.  Perhaps out of some strange sense of dragon pride or out of personal greed you have decided to make the dangerous run through the caverns in hopes of grabbing the soul gem.

Dwarven Dive is an intense race through a series of defended caves.  The maps and defenses are set out as the game progresses to make for a different game each and every time it is played.  Which dragon will be the first to make it to the vault and retrieve the fabled soul gem?

On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The party who gets the most planes back to their airfield wins the game.

The Game was published before 1919, when the company still was named "Gebrüder Bing AG".

description:
The HEXAGON FILE, your new case, requires concentrated and precise investigation work. Puzzling traces and suspicious testimonials lead you to several places in Europe, where you need to solve six mysterious criminal cases, such as bomb attacks, diamond and painting robberies, gambling fraud, bloody fashion shows and even murder on the famous Orient Express.

Crime Tours - Hexagon Files is a game for fans of crime games and escape room games who want to solve complex criminal cases with real locations. The players have to find out 10 details per case: Who was the perpetrator? What was his motive? What tools did he use? And what exactly happened? However, before the cases can be solved, the players have to follow the perpetrators from one city to the next. In order to find out where and when the perpetrators will appear next, the players must carefully analyze the 50 case cards and solve puzzles. If the players need help, they can use clue cards. At the end of a case, the players take the solution card to check how many details they have correctly determined. They get one point for each correct detail. Finally, the evaluation of the investigative work takes place based on the total number of points achieved.

Safety Ranger Game is designed to allow children 9 – 12 years old to learn about unsafe situations and the behavior of suspicious persons who are dangerous in daily life through the Monster. Children will take the role of a Safety Ranger (a hero who can use child power, knowledge, wisdom and courage) With assistant from people around them to defeat these Monster.

The game is creatively designed with a way of playing that encourages children to think, plan and not focus on competition each other. But children need 100% cooperative to defeat the Monster. It encourages children to use what they have to create unity in cooperation and see opportunities is the people around them and turn that people into great helpers in daily life.

Players pick a card and get the resource shown on the card, planning action behind the scenes, 3...2....1. Players open the player scenes at the same time, see the result, defeat the monster or the monster still rampant in the next round..

The players will play for 5 Rounds to challenge their teamwork to gain victory points after defeating the monster for each round and accumulate the highest score in order to write the names in the Hall of Fame.

Game Developed by Facebook Page “Learning From Board Game” (Board game designers for learning in Thailand) Partner with Awakening Creative and funded in research and design by Partage through CPCR (The Center for the Protection of Children’s Foundation) in Thailand. Thai Edition for Universal Children’s Day.
publishers: The Center for the Protection of Children's Rights Foundation

description:
The quirky quiz sees you whiz through just five questions per card – and they’re not that hard!

So what’s the catch? Simple: you always have to give the answer from the question before… With hilarious results!

The box contains 2 cooperative games and a memory game that use an ingeniously simple scanning method to illuminate the human body. The "scanning device" is slid between a black background and one of 4 differently printed foils.   The "scanning device" consists of a handle and a white square that brightly highlights parts of the foil. These highlighted parts are now to be associated with individual plates that show exactly the same parts.

The winner of the memory game is the person who collects the most tiles.

The other two cooperative games are won together when all the tiles have been distributed.

boardgame: La Cucaracha!: Wargames rules for engagements with small forces during the Mexican Revolution

description:
Take turns building the barricade to keep the zombies at bay!

In this abstract dexterity game, you take on the roles of a group of survivors trying to escape a zombie apocalypse.

To keep the zombies at bay, each player needs to help build up the barricade with random pieces carefully stacked on each other. However, at times a player must also remove a part of the barricade to salvage parts for fixing the escape car.

The die adds luck to the situation which can land a player in the dreaded "blind stacking" event where they must depend on the other players' directions/clues to blindly place a barricade piece without toppling it all over. It only takes a handful of mistakes for the players to lose so keep a steady hand and focused mind. The barricade must not fall!

A game turn is comprised of 3 phases:

     Barricade Phase - roll the red D6 Action die & consult the Action table (add zombies or add to the barricade)
     Car Phase - roll the red D6 Action die & consult the Action table (add zombies or remove from the barricade)
     End Phase - zombie check & increase the black D8 Hour die


Players loose if there are 10 zombies at the barricade.
Players win if the D8 Hour die value exceeds 8.

Suroborous is an auction game in which the items you bid on will be worth points only if you bid the amounts shown on these items in future auctions — that is, a card may be worth 10 points, but it will show two circles on it, say 30 and 50. The card will not be worth 10 points unless you bid 30 and 50 in future auctions.

The economy is relatively closed with winning bids going to the auctioneer, and the auctioneer's bids going to the bank; however, if any player bids an amount shown on the card up for auction, that card receives a coin from the bank, covering that number.

When the game ends, players earn points for their completed cards, purple cards with special scoring criteria, star symbols on cards, and a bit for leftover money. Of note, players without at least one star symbol are eliminated. Otherwise, the player with the most points wins.

In Bohnanza, you plant, then harvest bean cards in order to earn coins. Each player starts with a hand of random bean cards, and each card has a number on it corresponding to the number of that type of bean in the deck. Unlike in most other card games, you can't rearrange the order of cards in hand, so you must use them in the order that you've picked them up from the deck — unless you can trade them to other players, which is the heart of the game.

On a turn, you must plant the first one or two cards in your hand into the "fields" in front of you. Each field can hold only one type of bean, so if you must plant a type of bean that's not in one of your fields, you must harvest a field to make room for the new arrival. This usually isn't good! Next, you reveal two cards from the deck, and you can then trade these cards as well as any card in your hand for cards from other players. You can even make future promises for cards received right now! After all the trading is complete — and all trades on a turn must involve the active player — you end your turn by drawing cards from the deck and placing them at the back of your hand.

When you harvest beans, you receive coins based on the number of bean cards in that field and the "beanometer" for that particular type of bean. Flip over 1-4 cards from that field to transform them into coins, then place the remainder of the cards in the discard pile. When the deck runs out, shuffle the discards, playing through the deck two more times. At the end of the game, everyone can harvest their fields, then whoever has earned the most coins wins.

Bohnanza: 25th Anniversary Edition is a special 25th anniversary edition of the game that contains a new bean type (Magpie Bean) and comes with three variant games, one of which will use a collectible coin packaged in the box.

description:
Aliens have descended into the earth’s atmosphere and seek to conquer its inhabitants. Armies around the world are sending out their personnel, weapons and ammunition in order to combat this invasion. Your military division is ready to receive instructions. You have to rely on a network of civilian and military communication to locate the UFOs. Time is of the essence and you must speed to the last spotted location, hopefully with enough ammunition to either shoot them down out of the sky or hunt the down aliens that are on the loose! THE FATE OF THE EARTH IS IN YOUR HANDS!

Inspired by Jon Meitling's Palm Island, Invasion is the hand held solo game where you try to shoot down the UFOs and then hunt down any surviving aliens. Rotate and store cards to increase your military’s ammunition, speed and efficiency. When you have enough, you can shoot down an UFO or hunt down a an alien. The further you progress, the more likely that UFOs or aliens will appear.

With “ON THE FLY” embedded variant technology, you can increase the challenge of the game or simply add variety to the game without having to add any cards. The variants are found in the top right corner. As head of a military division, your goal is to shoot down as many UFOs and hunt down as many aliens as possible At the end of the game, you will tally your score by the icons in the top left corner.

Start with a blank canvas. Then fill your streetscape with niche businesses of your choice, from craft pickleries to modern speakeasies. Will you construct a culinary empire? A nightlife epicenter? Or something else entirely, marked with your own brand of wit and wisdom? Over 100 unique businesses and a wide variety of game-changing “reality” cards.

On their turn a player rolls a custom die, taking the resulting action. Then they may build a property if they have one that is valid based on the size of their neighborhood.

Once any player has 10 properties, the end of the game is triggered and the person with the most Hip Points and Destination bonuses wins.

boardgame: Versus: The Gleaners vs Paris Street – Rainy Day
description:
A game with a fast paced mechanic based on puzzling in which players try to put in his/her turn as many pieces of his/her painting in an area delimited by a frame.

Each player has a different puzzle but one condition is always true: they both has the same cutting pattern. As there is not enough space for all the pieces of inside the frame, on his turn a player try to put as many pieces as he/she can in order to contribute the most to the whole puzzle, which will result a hybrid of both images.

The game ends when the frame is completed. The winner is the player who has put more pieces inside the frame.

boardgame: Rory's Story Cubes: My Neighbor Totoro
description: Create new stories featuring the characters from the animated film "My Neighbor Totoro" using a new set of 9 dice. Roll the dice and let your imagination wander.

This is an epic fantasy competitive game of strategy, adventure, resource management and Kingdom building, with a large world map, massive card decks, miniatures, modified dice rolling and lore driven.

All our tales are forgotten in the Sunset of All life, is what the people of Zamrock believe. Our Tale about Invasion takes us right to the Eye of Zamrock, or how people there call it - ‘the Eye’.

It is now nearing the end of the Eight Age after the Becoming and by surprise, the Demon King was slain while trying to set hold in the Eye. This left his younger brother Lorial unattained and a new King of Demon Realm. The New King's lust for vengeance and hatred against all life pushed him to force open a portal in the middle of the Eye at the Crystal Lake Iris.

It was in this dark hour that the almost forgotten prophecy was unearthed. It speaks of one Kingdom that will rise above all others and build a Crystal Tower that will shine the light which will destroy the invaders and cast its beam to discover the Truth that will save the Eye and free all.

But the the prophecy brought hope as much as the divide, for Kings’ ambitions extend beyond the current conflict, as each one hopes to emerge from the war as the strongest ruler, worthy of uniting Zamrock under a single banner.

There are many paths to victory, but not all lead to saving the world. Become the leader of one of Six Kingdoms of the Eye and balance your relationship with other players and your ulterior motives to become the leading Kingdom in order to attain the most victory points. Command your Kingdom where you build up your city, armies, equip your Hero through quests, and expand with your Emissary and fight Demon forces who have built a Portal in the iris of the world to destroy everything. There are five different areas in which your Kingdom can gain influence - Battle, Trade, Build, Quest and Colonization. Enough influence points allow you to acquire crystals needed to construct Crystal Tower.

Demons win if any of the Kingdoms fail to build their Crystal Tower to level three by the end of the 24th Turn of play. If the Tower is built, demon Portal closes and a celebratory Tournament is held. Kingdom with the most EOZ Medals, in the end, wins the game.

Be advised, straying too much from the path of stopping the invasion can make everything you do to expand your Kingdom futile.

—description from the designer

The objective of the game is to bank £1000 of your money as you try to overcome the many problems of life on benefits, getting a job and then hanging on to it!

On your turn, you will roll a 6-sided spot dice and then move your elected character piece around the board in a clockwise direction.

Each time you land or pass the Pay Day square, you will receive either your benefit or pay (if you have acquired a job during play) from the designated Banker.

After each throw of the die, the player will turn to the corresponding Flip Chart page heading and then roll the 20-sided spot die. The number showing will then be the number of the instruction to follow on the Flip Chart.
e.g.  Player lands on the Pink Elephant square, turn to PUBS chart and roll a 20-sided spot die, lets say it’s number 7, then follow the number 7 instruction.

State of emergency in Schlagerland! Everyone who was anyone in the industry had come together for the new edition of the "Schlager-Megaparade".

But shortly before the recording began, singer Janine Bayer was found dead. She was supposed to present her new No. 1 hit "Ich träum von mir heut Nacht" that evening.


         Story
    


State of emergency in Schlagerland! Everyone who was anyone in the industry had come together for the new edition of the "Schlager-Megaparade". The Christmas edition of Germany's most successful TV entertainment show was to be recorded in midsummer of all times. For it neither costs nor efforts were spared! Presenter and pop star Michael Prinz would again present a mix of well-known stars and promising newcomers. The show with snow cannons and a skating reindeer ballet should not only provide for Christmas mood in the living rooms of the spectators, but also strongly boost the record sales of the performing artists.

The recording began punctually at 6:30 p.m. in the sold-out Magdeburg Stadthalle. All the artists were eagerly awaiting their turn. But shortly after the show began, a shrill scream echoed through the hall: a production assistant had found the artist Janine Bayer dead in her dressing room. The singer, who had won the industry award "Der Goldene Gockel" a few weeks ago, was supposed to present her new No. 1 hit "Ich träum von mir heut Nacht" that evening.

Of course, you immediately called an ambulance and the police. But the emergency services that were called in could only determine Janine's death. Apparently, the victim was beaten to death with her own prize statue. While you now wait for your interrogation, you are left on your own with the question: What happened that day? How could this hit fest end so tragically?


         Players
    


4 female and 3 male roles (with 7 people). Male roles can be played by women and vice versa.

This includes the French Wars of Religion, the Dutch Revolt, the 30 Years War, and the English Civil War. Other conflict areas within this time period (say the 1560s through the 1650’s) should also play well with this system, but earlier conflicts (such as the Italian Wars) or later ones will be less and less representative the farther away from these dates you get.

Chronicles of Frost is a deck-building adventure game set in Æstemyr, a dark fantasy universe rich in its own lore, shared by Mistfall, Heart of the Mists and Shadowscape.

Start with two unique quests and a small deck, and improve your character by gaining new ability cards, items and allies. Work against your fellow real estate developers to make the city of Stockholm into a dense and tall version of its present state. Influence politics and policy to your advantage, increase population density by building crammed housing, close city airports and parks and, for all that’s holy, make sure the nightclubs are in your areas!

Each turn you play one card from your hand, either a scoring card which is secretly discarded or put aside to influence final scoring, or an event card which ads new action spaces to the map. When the back of this card is visible on the zombie pile, the sound of the engines frightens the active player's zombies: the active player recovers all his dice rolls for this turn.

Note: once the bikers are eliminated, discard this card. This card does not earn points.

Athanasia is a Scifi/Fantasy Competitive Skirmish Wargame where players engage in tactical conflict to secure the key to immortality.

Players mobilize and strategically position their characters, leverage their surroundings to increase their power, and battle their rivals as they venture towards victory.

Every character has a value that determines their health, movement, defense, and attack power as well as additional character abilities that increase their effectiveness on the battlefield.

Each turn, a player is allotted 3 activation points for their civilization which they can use in a variety of ways to increase their own power as well as decrease their rival's power as they carve their path towards victory.

In a given turn a player may: Move their characters across the battlefield based on their allowed movement, collect and activate items that spawn dynamically, activate obelisks to increase their movement, defense, and attack power, activate character abilities to weaken their rivals, and attack or steal items from adjacent rivals using a D20 die to simulate battle. As in any battle, strength in numbers always helps you come out on top. When attacking or defending from rivals, you get additional D20 roll or defense bonuses by positionally linking your characters together.

Securing the key to immortality first requires crafting it at the crafting table in the center of the battlefield using both the philosopher's stone and antimatter which are spawned throughout the game. The race is then on for a civilization to secure the key and activate it at the battlefield's exit point. The player that successfully activates the key at the exit point is the winner. Alternatively, you can win Athanasia by being the sole survivor after obliterating your rivals. Will you be the first to reach immortality?

—description from the publisher

Over 50 unique  illustrations and over 60 cards that all are functionally different! Lawnarchy is a turn-taking riddle game with lots of tactical thinking, changing objectives and interactive back and forth.

Each player owns a garden of 4 starting cards, only a fence separates you to your neighbor's garden. The rules are simple: Each turn you have three options and pick one card, but the possibilities are complex. 1) You convince the jury and pick a jury card from the examination board - these objectives constantly change! 2) You do some gardening, and replace a card with a garden card from the common "garden center" - same as the jury, all garden cards have different requirements! 3) You squabble with your neighbor by picking a squabble card. You can destroy their garden, steal their gnomes, or rearrange their garden. You have to figure out the best move - a wrong one and your neighbor will get ahead!

Players control one of two factions (the Cyto-Command or the Shadow-Force) who they will use to traverse treacherous jungle terrain, collect strategic resources, and construct powerful upgrades to their base that will deliver devastating blows to their opponent.

During the game, players will fight for control of the village, along with the other 4 unique locations around the board.

description:
'Chroni' is a card game where each card depicts a historical event, invention or discovery on both sides, with the year in which that event occurred, invention or discovery was made on only one side. Players take turns placing a card from their hand in a row on the table. After placing the card, the player reveals the date on it. If the card was placed correctly with the date in chronological order with all other cards on the table, the card stays in place; otherwise the card is removed from play and the player takes another card from the deck.

The first player to get rid of all his cards by placing them correctly wins. If multiple players go out in the same round, then everyone else is eliminated from play and each of those players are dealt one more card for another round of play. If only one player has no cards after a bonus round, he wins; otherwise play continues until a single player goes out.

The game was produced when the Hokey Pokey song and accompanying dance peaked in popularity as a music hall song and novelty dance in the mid-1940s in the UK and Australia.

boardgame: Interplanetary: A Giant Hop for Squeakind
description:
An automata-free solo experience about exploring and settling in one's solar system, with an entirely player-driven gameplay system and very few random elements.

--- Winner of the "most innovative mechanics" and other awards during BGG's 2021 P&P Solo Contest ---

Billions watched the live global broadcast of the first Squeak hopping on the Mun over 50 years ago. Now, the various nations on planet Tree have finally come together as the United Forests and have created a planet-wide space program to peacefully explore their solar system. You have been selected as Head of the Exploratory Branch of that program! Send probes into the void to reach distant moons and comets. Go where no Squeak has gone before and put two adorable little paws on another planet. Design and construct never-before seen spacecraft, purpose-made for your missions in interplanetary space. Peacefully explore the Squeak's solar system and establish research outposts on its many worlds and moons. Maybe, just maybe, make startling discoveries out on the final frontier and herald a new era for all of Squeakind!

This solo-game is an entirely player-driven experience, there are no turns and there is no opponent or random events. In 'Interplanetary', you are tackling a "campaign deck" that presents you with a semi-random list of goals you need to achieve, called "directives".
 After the initial setup, play consists of you picking an action from a (rather long) list, executing that action and then paying its cost by moving a cube along a numbered track. This cube tracks the available amount of actions to fulfill your current directive.
Each time the cube reaches the end of its track, the campaign deck gets updated and if you take too long, you might have failed your current directive. When the campaign deck runs out, you tally your score and if that score is better than your previous scores, you can rejoice!

In summary, this is a game of heavy action-economy optimization. You are trying to fulfill your goal with the most optimal sequence of actions while trying to improve your overall situation to have a better chance to also tackle the next directive.

—description from the designer

Conquer the sun platform with wits and logic and occupy it with the code of your own tribal colours. Those eliminated are dropped into the pyramid chamber. The winner obtains the eternal Maya gold. Discover new and fascinating strategies with every round.

Gone with the Beans (豆と共にあれ) is a flip-and-write game in which players take on the role of bean farmers who are acquiring and managing their resources while trying to grow their farm. The game includes fifty copies each of four different game boards, each with different set-ups, although the base system is the same. Players start in different provinces with different special abilities, and on each of of the game's fifteen turns, a character wanders the board, affecting where players can do what. Each turn, players gain some income, then have a number of actions from which to choose, such as building facilities or achieving milestones.

The game is strongly influenced by the author's favorite game, Terra Mystica.

boardgame: Gravetides: A Naval Fantasy Miniature Wargame
description:
Gravetides is a naval tactical miniature game of epic sea battles involving two or more fantasy fleets of magical and non-magical warships led by skilled captains and powerful sorcerers and manned by crews of ordinary humans and supernatural creatures.

Warships may face abyssal monsters or come across strange marine phenomenons and mysterious events caused by the wild mystical energies that traverse the drowning world of GRAVETIDES whose nature has been altered forever by the invasion of a magical parasite known as ‘The Defiler’.

Fleets can be supported in battle by small squadrons of winged creatures or flying machines while underwater vessels or sea creatures under the spell of sorcerers can strike at enemies from the deep.

Magic can wreak chaos and mayhem among enemy ranks or backfire unpredictably disrupting carefully laid battle plans.

Players can tailor their battle fleets selecting the number and types of warships, their individual special equipment and the quality and skills of their commanders and crews.

—description from the rulebook
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Postcard with map on one side, rules on the other including goal, set up and gameplay.

The card substitutes instead of a building card from base game. You use teh Microville card instead the regular/challenge building. You have to prepare the 4-card city as usual and put Microville next to it. You don't go to Microville with your Engineer, you build Microville with your regular build action. You score points for what you've built and for the sets of 3.

Quiz game based on Stadt Land Fluss - 55 question cards are placed in a stack with the theme side up, the letter tiles are shuffled face down and laid out. The player on the turn reveals a letter tile and all players simultaneously search for a term matching the card with the revealed initial letter. The first player to name a correct term gets the card, and the letter is removed from the game. If there are only 10 letters left in the middle, the player with the most cards wins.

—user summary

In Peter's orchard, the fruits are ripe and must be harvested. Each player must always have as many fruits in his basket as the open collection cards indicate, the type of fruit doesn't matter, but there must be no unripe fruits. Under the 12 collecting discs hide the most different numbers of pieces. Whoever's turn it is must choose one of the cards and may then turn over 2 unblocked discs - if the number of uncovered fruits is correct, you get the card, the discs are covered and the player moves 2 blocking discs. The player who has harvested 50 fruits wins.

—user summary

Play cards to manipulate the flock to your advantage.  When five or more “sheep” are face up, a player may end the game instead of playing a card.  Players score the sheep on their edge of the flock and the high score wins.

—user summary

The thrills of classic car rallying, from preparing the car and logistics, to the days on the road competing and assisting the car, in order to try to get it to the finish quicker than the others.

Classic Rally  is the board game that brings the classic and historic cars world to the tabletop. This game emulates the features, behavior and even problems of real life classic cars. At the same time, it simulates the main features of real life road rallying, like road books, checkpoints, weather changes, hazards, service areas, assistance cars, overtaking other cars on the road, etc.

This is a strategy game with a great component of logistics, just like in a real life car rally. Fuel and tires management play a great part on this game. Properly understanding the road book and going the right way is crucial to succeed in this game, as it is in real life car rallying.

This game has an initial setup phase, where each player contributes to the construction of the board, by tile placing road segments - this makes every game play unique.

Each player manages 2 cars (one competition car and one assistance car), having to select the features of the competition car and what each car will carry. There are dice to influence weather conditions and car malfunctions. There are cards with car features (like sport suspension), car accessories (like fog lights) and consumables (like fuel). The cars are moved by using fuel cards, depending on its feature and accessory cards and influenced by the weather conditions and malfunctions.

—description from the designer

Walking by the big city, you personify some 'porteños' early 20th century's characters: thief, policeman, 'mina' (woman), 'guapo' (man) or bartender.

The objective of the game is to travel through the city and collect money by avoiding the conflicts that arise when turning the wheel of destiny. The first player to deposit $ 5000.- in the bank, wins the game.

On their turn, players roll a die and move to the nearest square matching the color spot. When landing at a location, they spin the wheel of destiny and deal with the conflict.

A dice game combining strategy and luck in a race to the finish first!
—description from the publisher

Players take turns moving their pieces strategically to either land on beneficial spaces or to avoid potential hazards on a maze-like board in a race to see who can get to the finish line first.

—user summary

Each player must place letters on the board taking advantage of the prize boxes that will grant them credits (1, 2, or 3). The first player to get five credits is the winner. However, You can become a ghost and walk away from your goal or you can object to the last player's turn or call the word as completed - causing other players to lose credits or lives.

Avoid losing your three lives, because you will become the ghost.
Get five points to win the game.

In this game Hack!, 4 players try to hack each other's computers to steal private data and secrets, using some routers and USB cables. Each time one of them succeeds, he or she gets smarter and thus stronger. But be aware! When you get too smart, you'll surely meet more hurdles! The first player that manages to hack all computers wins the game.

Players layout a 6x6 grid of tiles face down and start the game with 5 tiles in their hand.  Players use or discard tiles from their hand to explore the boards and to turn, manipulate or block the route of networks as the board is developed.

A game based off the old childhood game of Connect-the-Dots, but played with single, double and triple-point squares to enhance a level of strategy Complete as many squares as possible by filling in all four sides of a square with pegs while trying to give your opponents as few squares as possible. The player with the most points (not necessarily the most squares) wins.

—description from the publisher

Spellcaster: The Frostgrave Magazine exists to bring new rules, scenarios, and gaming ideas for players of Frostgrave and Frostgrave: Ghost Archipelago.
This issue contains:

     The Diamonds of Elias Skull, a 3-scenario campaign for Ghost Archipelago.
     The Maze of Malcor Outtakes, including 1 scenario and 10 weird events.
     The Floating Hulk, a solo scenario for Ghost Archipelago.
     The Great Wyrm, a Frostgrave ‘big game’ campaign by Brent Sinclair, 3-scenarios leading to an encounter with a dragon!
     Rules for a new character, the Archer, in Barbarian Hack.
     Casting Roll Criticals in Frostgrave for Second Edition.
     16 Ulterior Motive Cards for Ghost Archipelago.


—description from the publisher

Reenact the fall of the Roman Empire in this grand campaign map. Hold the line as the Eastern or Western Roman Empire, supplant the empire with your kingdom as the tribal factions of the Sassanids, Franks and Goths, or trample all before you as the Huns. Each faction has unique victory conditions and ways to play. With numerous paths to victory, including variants, the game offers much replay-ability.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The 2nd Edition includes the Great Discoveries expansion and two promo cards (Stephen Hawking & Robert Hooke).

The middle of the 17th century was a period of great changes; with the advent of the scientific method came what we now call the Scientific Revolution. Many great scientists, with their theories and ideas, changed and shaped our perception of the universe: Galileo Galilei, Copernicus, Kepler, Bacon and, above all, Sir Isaac Newton.

In Newton, the players take the role of a young scientist who wants to become one of the great geniuses of this era. To reach their ultimate goal, they travel around Europe, visit universities and cities, study to discover new theories, build new tools and work to earn money.

The game is played over six rounds. Each round, every player plays five cards from their hand, and each played card allows the player to perform one of the many actions of the game. An action can have a variety of effects, which depend on the symbols on the board. At the end of the round, a player can take back all the cards except for one. One card has to remain on the board, which means that you give up one possibility of doing that action, but also that that very action will be carried out with greater strength. Fortunately, you can acquire new cards with additional powers to perform more actions.

After six rounds, you calculate your final score, and the player with the most VP wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Gump: Pes, který naučil lidi žít
description:
The family game Gump will tickle your cerebral threads and you will experience unforgettable adventures. During the game you are trying to make the dog Gump and and his human friend Bedřich meet again on the same tile before you run out of playing cards. The game contains a large number of traps to stop you.

Players win if the two are reunited.


Rodinná hra Gump pěkně potrápí vaše mozkové závity a zažijete nezapomenutelná dobrodružství. Při hře se snažíte, aby se psí kamarádi Gump a Bedřich opět setkali. Hra obsahuje velké množství nástrah a úskalí, která budete muset překonat a bude jistě bavit celou rodinu.

Can you draw a picture of. Scarecrow riding a horse in the jungle? Or a cowboy picking flowers on a pirate ship?

Put your artistic talents to the test with this crazy drawing game! Players battle it out to draw funny scenes based on topic cards for their team to guess.  Will you be able to guess the right answer or will you be a doodle dropout?

The team that guesses the most answers, wins!

Planet Box is a bluffing game from Japan. Players take turns placing numbered pieces into a sliding box. They are trying to bluff how many stars they are putting in. If if it is too many, and their bluff is called, the galaxy bursts and they must take back stars. The first player to clear their pool is the winner.
user summary

Publisher summary:
You are God. You are the Creator God.

This is the "star-stuffing game" played by the gods. You have to fill the mysterious PLANETBOX with stars in order and try to get rid of them before anyone else does. However, the box has a mass limit, and if you overfill it with stars, other players will spot you and penalise you.
It's a simple yet profound bluffing game where the key is to be able to spot when you have too many stars.

あなたは神です。創造神です。
これは神々がたしなむ「星詰めゲーム」。あなたは不思議な箱PLANETBOXに星を順番に入れていき、誰よりも早く手持ちの星を無くすことを目指します。ただ、この箱には質量制限があり、星を詰めすぎると他のプレイヤーに見破られてペナルティを受けてしまうかもしれません。
星の詰めすぎを見破れるかがカギとなるシンプルで奥深いブラフゲームです。
publisher's summary
publishers: Umeruma Games (ウメルマゲームズ)

description:
Kamelon is a bluffing game from Japan. A baker has stolen the shells from turtles to make Melonpan (a tasty snack that looks like a turtle shell). The turtles sneak around the bakery at night to try and get their shells back. If the baker can catch them, they lose.

Players move their turtles secretly around the tiles, face-down so their backs look like the other bun pieces. Arrows on each tile indicate where they can move too, and the tiles can be rotated. The baker can search when the shop is open, by turning over a bun. If a turtle can get across the shop to get their shell, and back to the exit in time without being found, they win.
user summary

A popular melonpan baker in town. In fact, it is a bad bakery that steals shells from the turtles behind the scenes and traffics them. One day, a turtle breaks into the bakery and steals the shell. But it is already time to open the shop. The only place he can hide is in the huge pile of melon pans! The turtle tries to escape by moving from melon pan to melon pan. But when you move, you give a clue to the shopkeeper. The clerk tries to guess where the turtle is hiding, and the turtle tries to deceive him with a false route.

街で人気のメロンパン屋。実は裏でカメからコウラを奪い、密売する悪いパン屋だった。ある日、コウラを奪われた一匹のカメがパン屋店に侵入。しかしもう店の開店時間だ。隠れられるのは、大量に並んだメロンパンの中のみ！メロンパンからメロンパンへ移動して脱出を目指すカメ。しかし移動は店員にヒントを与えてしまう。ヒントを頼りに隠れ場所を推理する店員と、偽の経路で欺こうとするカメの、頭脳系推理ブラフゲームです。
publisher's summary
publishers: Umeruma Games (ウメルマゲームズ)

description:
It’s a race to the center as you try to evade and eliminate your opponents throughout the maze. Move your pieces via various dice sequences and accumulate points to that help advance your Monarch.

Players start at the corner of the board and attempt to be the first to move their Monarch meeple to the center of the board or eliminate the opponent's Monarchs from the game. Players can capture their opponents' pieces to move their Monarch.

Submarine Boost Game, a simple roll-and-move game, is a Subway kids meal giveaway.  Each player rolls dice to move three submarines along a track, and the first player to get all their subs to the end wins.  If a submarine lands on an opponent's submarine, the player gets a second turn.

The game comes with punch-out paper pieces and game board that must be assembled.

Game-A-Saurus Rex is a playful little prehistoric plush! Rex comes stuffed with a soft blanket that doubles as a playmat along with plenty of pieces that can be used to play 10 different games!

Games include: Rock, Paper, Caveman; Egg Hatch Memory; Tic-Rock-Toe; Speedy Peppers; The Great Egg Race; The Dino Run; Egg Basket; The Rex Connect; Dino Fish; and Do You See What I See?

—description from the publisher

The players co-operate to empty a vault of gold before the time is up. The laser locks have to strategically be opened and closed according to dice rolls, so the carts can move between the vaults. The elevator also has to be moved up and down several times to get all the gold out. The same dice control both the locks and the elevator, so the most efficient use of the dice has to be quickly chosen upon.

When in turn, a player always rolls the two colour dice. A die can be used to open or close a matching laser lock on the track, or to move the elevator to the next level, if the colour matches. Further, the player can move their own cart along the tracks on their turn. They try to open a way to an opened vault with gold, and move there to load their cart. With a loaded cart they try to move to the elevator console in the middle of the board, and unload the gold off their cart there. When a cart is being moved, it can move freely along any tracks, until its way is blocked.

A free app needs to be downloaded for full play. The app functions as a timer, but also tells the players when their (non-player) partners on street level have managed to open a new vault. Gold can only be fetched from the opened vaults.

The elevator can only take 3 gold pallets at a time, and 12 pallets need to be brought to street level, before the time is up. If the players manage to bring up the 10th-12th pallets before the time is up, they win! If the 15 minutes are up before that, the players lose...

boardgame: ギリ、義理チョコ(Giri Giri Chocolate)
description:
Giri Giri Chocolate is a simple card game. People who got most chocolate will win, but people who got most amount of "Honmei Chocolate" will drop out from the game.

You give present cards to girls of your classmate. You choose a card from your hand and put it on the table, face down. People who shows smallest number start to give it to the girl. They have some restrictions respectively to put your cards.

After you give them gift, you pick up a card from a supply. You continue to doing it 4 times.

When Valentine's Day come, compare your present cards. People who give them most valuable present(biggest number) gains the "Honmei chocolate", and second highest present gains the "Giri Chocolate".

boardgame: Rommel's Last Stand: Italy 1943-44, What If?
description:
"Rommel’s Last Stand: Italy 1943-44, What If?" (RLS) is an low-complexity, two-player wargame simulating the possible events that could have unfolded during the first 30 days of this alternative history World War II campaign.

A complete game generally takes two to three hours to complete, and it is suitable for solitaire play. Each hex on the map equals 5 miles (8 km) from side to opposite side. Each full turn represents two weeks to two months, depending on the time of year. Every ground unit represents a regiment, brigade or division of about 2,500 to 12,000 men and/or 50 to 350 armored fighting vehicles.

published in Banzai Magazine No. 11
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: A.T.R.A.M (Advanced Reconnaissance and Attack Mission) is one of the first "Computer Aided Board Games". Players command military forces in a simulated NATO exercise against each other.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: BattleTech: Technical Readout – 3145 Free Worlds League
description:
Technical Readout: 3145 Free Worlds League introduces the wave of new battle armor, vehicle, BattleMech, and aerospace units appearing across the Inner Sphere in the Dark Age era. Featuring new and matured technology, and presented in this series of faction-specific PDFs, these combat units will add excitement and variety to any game table.

Each title includes Record Sheets for each featured unit and its variants.

Technical Readout: 3145 Federated Suns introduces the wave of new battle armor, vehicle, BattleMech, and aerospace units appearing across the Inner Sphere in the Dark Age era. Featuring new and matured technology, and presented in this series of faction-specific PDFs, these combat units will add excitement and variety to any game table. For use with BattleTech, Total Warfare, Tactical Operations, and Strategic Operations.

Each title includes Record Sheets for each featured unit and its variants.

Technical Readout: 3145 Draconis Combine introduces the wave of new battle armor, vehicle, BattleMech, and aerospace units appearing across the Inner Sphere in the Dark Age era. Featuring new and matured technology, and presented in this series of faction-specific PDFs, these combat units will add excitement and variety to any game table.

Each title includes Record Sheets for each featured unit and its variants.

Technical Readout: 3145 Capellan Confederation introduces the wave of new battle armor, vehicle, BattleMech, and aerospace units appearing across the Inner Sphere in the Dark Age era. Featuring new and matured technology, and presented in this series of faction-specific PDFs, these combat units will add excitement and variety to any game table.

Each title includes Record Sheets for each featured unit and its variants.

Technical Readout: 3145 Mercenaries introduces the wave of new battle armor, vehicle, BattleMech, and aerospace units appearing across the Inner Sphere in the Dark Age era. Featuring new and matured technology, and presented in this series of faction-specific PDFs, these combat units will add excitement and variety to any game table.

Each title includes Record Sheets for each featured unit and its variants.

The march of technology across BattleTech's eras is relentless, but some BattleMech designs never die. Each installment of this PDF-only series not only includes a brand new BattleMech or OmniMech, but also details Classic 'Mech designs from both the Inner Sphere and the Clans, now fully rebuilt with Dark Age technology (3085 and beyond). Allowing beloved units redesigned for the BattleTech: Clan Invasion crowd funding campaign to be competitive on "modern" battlefields, this series also includes in-universe development notes, battle histories, notable pilots, and record sheets for each 'Mech.

Wiz’N’Bang is a card-drafting, dice-rolling and tower-building game where players take on the role of a grumpy old wizard attempting to construct the perfect tower on the perfect hill. The only problem: every other wizard trying to do the same.

Players take turns drawing cards, building their tower, playing spells and moving their wizard, all with the intention of building a tower seven cards high and getting their wizard to the top.

Make use of tower cards such as Cannons, Dragons, Portals and more to construct your tower one floor at a time in the most offensive or defensive way you please. Use spells such as Fireball, Counter, Deadeye and more to defend your tower against the nefarious machinations of your enemies.

Simple in concept but with amazing depth, Wiz’N’Bang brings together open drafting, tower building, dice rolling, meeple management, action distribution and spell casting into a delightfully small package that won’t break the bank or hog the table.
A trivia game based on the TV series "Friends". The goal of the game is to be the first player or team to get 10 answers correct. Very simple concept with no board or pieces outside of 54 cards.

Zombies, Vampires, and Meteorites is an apocalyptic survival board game, with an engaging balance between luck and strategy. All players start off as Human and explore the board to be the first to survive 12 months of the Apocalypse. However, the Zombie City and Vampire Graveyard is out there and players who discover these locations become those fearsome horrors. And if that’s not enough to make you fret, then have fun dodging the pesky meteorites that continually change the course of the game!

At the start of a player’s turn, they roll the 6-sided Movement Dice and move that many spaces. If they land on an unexplored space, they pull at random a location and build the map. Locations can consist of Elements, Villages, Mountains, Zombie City, or Vampire Graveyard. Then, for a Human player, they decide how to use any collected Elements to work toward Survival Points or decide to attack another player before ending their turn. On a Zombie’s turn, they roll the movement dice, moving 1 less than the number rolled, but can control up to 6 Zombies in a single turn. Their turn ends when all movements and attacks are used.  For the Vampire, they roll the movement dice, moving 1 more than the number rolled, and decide whether to Suck Blood for Energy or Attack a player using Energy to obtain a Coffin. Their turn ends when all movements or attacks are used.

In the scenario a player rolls a SURVIVE! on the Movement Dice, they immediately roll the two Coordinate dice to determine where a Meteorite or Supply Crate lands on the board before concluding that player’s turn automatically.

The Game ends when a Player achieves the requirements to survive the apocalypse. For the Human, it’s collecting 12 months of Supplies (a combination of Elements, Supply Crates, and Defenses). For the Zombie, it’s Replicating in each Human Village and returning all 6 Zombies to the City. And the Vampire must obtain Blood Drops and all 5 Human Coffins before concluding their turn in the Graveyard.

A battle has broken out between Frogs, Knights, and all creatures of the Ribbit Kingdom.

Players battle around the board acquiring all different kinds of weapons, ranging from a measly fly-swatter to Bazooka to a swarm of flesh-eating beetles and eliminating other players.  As the playable area shrinks, players are forced in towards the middle until one is left standing as King of the Hill. Champion Royale. Winner, Winner, its Flies for Dinner!

This game combines components (25 cards, 27 dice, 27 cubes) from the first 3 Nano9Games - Empire, Railways and City Planner - and has both a solo and two player mode.

Players compete to build and upgrade buildings, create railways, move passengers to their destinations and produce goods in their factories in order to gain Capital and create Profit.

Empire Planners is strictly a rulebook. Players will need Empire, Railways and City Planner to play.

The Bunnies have hidden in their holes around the game board.

Each player receives three discs and tries to match the colors on their discs to the colors hidden on the bottom of the bunny playing pieces. The first player to find all their bunnies wins! Double-sided game board features two difficulty levels that progress as your child masters memorization and color recognition skills.

Fastrek is a cooperative game for kids 5 years and above that required teamwork and strategy to win. Play tested and reviewed by teachers, enjoyed by kids and loved by parents. This game can be played by 2 - 4 players.

In Fastrek, players roll their dice to move around the board.  Depending on the spot they land on, players may collect items, encounter monsters, pick an event card or move to another spot on the board.

To win, all players must get to camp before nightfall AND have all the necessary camping items to set up camp.

ハナニソム (Hananisomu) is a 2 player game from Japan where players compete to enjoy the cherry blossom viewing. In Japan, since the 8th century, it is custom to pass a pleasant afternoon in the spring, sitting under the cherry and plum trees, enjoying the blossoms while eating and drinking with your friends. This is known as Hanami, and is seen all across Japan as the blossoms open.

In this game, the two players are trying to create the best flower viewing experience for their friends. By laying out ground sheets under the trees, and providing food and drink, players score points. The most points wins the game.

Players take turns laying tiles in three colours, and putting markers for people on them. By controlling the largest areas connected to trees, and having the right type of people connected to the foods they like, a player can maximise their score.
user summary

This is a two-player game where players line up leisure sheets in a park full of cherry blossoms and the winner is the one who gets the best basho (spot under the trees) for the visitors. The player who gets the best basho for the visitors wins the game.

桜の咲く公園にレジャーシートを並べていき、訪れるお花見客により良いバショを取ってあげたプレイヤーが勝利する、2人用の陣取りゲームです。
自分のバショ = 陣地を、他のバショより大きくしたり、桜に隣接させたり、お花見客の好物を集めたりすることで得点が上がり、勝利に近づきます。
publisher's summary

ヴォーグ (VOGU) is a tile placement game from Japan about sneaker dealers (the game box is styled to look like a shoebox).

The game has a deck of cards showing sneakers in two colours (red/yellow, green, blue for example). Players are dealt a hand of cards, and on their turn either play a card to the table, or place a card in front of themselves and gain a matching colour token to multiply their end score.

Cards that are placed must match their neighbouring cards. As each card is placed, the popularity of the colours alters on a chart.

When the game ends, players have 4 cards left in total, in hand and in front of them. They multiply out the colours they hold plus tokens, against the colours' scores on the chart.

By placing a card in front, a player gains a multiplier, but shows the other players which colours they favour. Players must balance trying to improve colours place on the chance, against holding good scoring cards at the end.

The publisher includes a webtool to help calculate the end scores.
user summary

In the ever-changing fashion world, you are a sneaker dealer.
Place cards with colorful sneakers every turn and control the color trends by combining adjacent cards.
Read the ever-changing value of colors and the speculations of other players, and get around to get the highest value for your sneakers.

絶えず流行が移り変わるファッション界において、あなたはスニーカーを専門とするディーラーです。
毎ターン、カラフルなスニーカーが描かれたカードを配置して、隣り合ったカードの組み合わせで、色の流行を操作します。
刻々と変動する色の価値と他プレイヤーの思惑を読み、自分の持つスニーカーの価値が最も高くなるよう、上手く立ち回りましょう。
publisher's summary

boardgame: ワンダーワードワールド (Wonder Word World)
description:
ワンダーワードワールド (Wonder Word World) is a party game from Japan. Players are ambassadors from foreign nations. They take turns to memorise certain words, and then read a fairy tale out loud, but inserting their nation's words for key words in the story. The player giving the best and most dramatic reading is voted the winner.

The game includes booklets for five classic fairy tales, all in Japanese.
user summary

It is a memorization-type reading party game where you become ambassadors of countries with different languages ​​and take turns reading aloud while adding your own translations to the words that appear in fairy tales.

The player who remembers the strange words that increase as the story progresses and reads them aloud more steadily and dramatically than others wins.

変わった言語を持つ国々の大使となり、 おとぎ話に登場する単語に独自の訳をつけながら、 交代で朗読していく暗記系朗読パーティゲームです。
物語が進むにつれてどんどん増える、奇妙な単語を記憶して、 他の人よりよどみなく、そしてドラマチックに読み上げたプレイヤーが勝利します。
publisher's summary

Torch & Blade is a pen and paper, solitaire, dungeon delving game set in a sword and sorcery world. You control intrepid adventurers braving dark and perilous dungeons in an attempt to bring back precious loot and complete arduous quests. The game can be played as a chronicle, an endless chronicle, or a short play campaign. No matter what game mode you choose, you will play several individual sessions, called acts.

During each act you will guide an adventurer through a randomly generated dungeon. The playing time for a single act, once you are familiar with the rules, should be between 60 and 120 minutes.

During a chronicle you will control several characters, alternating among them. At any point you will only have 2 to 4 active characters to pick from, and they will all either succumb or successfully retire and get replaced by new adventurers. A full chronicle can last anywhere between 24 and 192 acts, mostly falling around the middle of those extremes.

A short play campaign lets you complete a full campaign in a shorter time frame. You will control a single character until they either succumb or successfully retire. In this game mode there are no overarching legacy elements, apart from the progress made by your character. A short campaign lasts between 1 and 8 acts.

An endless chronicle lets you keep the legacy element of the standard chronicle, but it doesn't have a fixed end. You can keep creating new characters without end and keep playing until you get bored!

Designed for the 2021 Solitaire Print and Play Contest.

In Cultistorm Face 2 Face one player takes on the role of the investigator, whereas the other player assumes the role of the cult leader.

The goal of the cult leader is to summon a Great Old One by holding dark rituals using a secret combination of elder signs to call forth and protect 4 cultists, whose job it is to complete the ritual.

The goal of the investigator is to prevent this by defeating the cultists that join the dark ritual each round. The investigator must keep on defeating them until there are not enough (fewer then 4) cultists left to complete the ritual. The investigator must deduce the elder signs connected to each cultist in order to defeat them.

A game consists of several rounds in which the cult leader and the investigator take alternating turns playing their cards.

If there are 4 cultists in play simultaneously, a Great Old One is placed at the end of the row. If the investigator cannot deduce all the remaining face-down ritual cards in his next turn right away (without revealing a card) the dark ritual is complete and the cult leader wins the game.

If the investigator manages to defeat 5 cultists by deducing all the ritual cards allocated to them, the cultists will not have enough power to summon their master and the investigator wins.

Face-2-Face was specifically designed with Essen Spiel ‘19 in mind.

Meet Pata TENSLIP, King POTATO, Pata TAPOIL and Rocky POTATO in crazy adventures! Four types of potatoes have crazy accessories : crowns, boxing gloves, slip ... or nothing ;).

Each player has three cards in hand and one card is placed on the table to start the game. One by one, place a card that has a common point with the last card placed on the table. Win the fold by placing a card on the potato which has something in common with the previous one but nothing in common with the starting potato.
The game ends when the deck is empty. The player with the most of potatoes wins the game !

There is also 5 variants to increase fun and challenge!

What a mess! They have searched all the backpacks and the bag of goodies that you took to eat tonight at the bonfire have disappeared! In its place you have found a strange map with the signature of the "King Micomicon".

If you want to recover your sweets and perhaps find legendary characters and ancient treasures, you will have to enter the magical Forest of the Micomicons. Let the adventure begin!

Our objective will be to cross the magical forest of the Micomicons while we advance through the letters on the way until we reach the King with the objects that he asks us for matching our letters.

Players roll dice to move and pick up cards to deliver to the King.  If they can't deliver to the king, they must move back 1plaec for each card in their hand.

In competitive mode, the first player who manages to reach the king and deliver the objects to him or, failing that, achieve what his roll of the dice indicates, will have won.

If we have played in cooperative mode, when one of the players reaches the king's card and they give him among all the objects he requests or get the action roll with the dice, they will have won.


¡Qué desastre! Han registrado todas las mochilas y la bolsa de chucherías que llevabais para comer esta noche en la hoguera han desaparecido! En su lugar habéis encontrado un extraño mapa con la firma del “Rey Micomicon”. Si queréis recuperar vuestras chuches y tal vez encontrar personajes de leyenda y antiguos tesoros , tendréis que adentraros en el Bosque mágico de los Micomicons. ¡Que comience la aventura! Así se nos presenta este juego que nos trae la editorial Enpeudejocedicions y que ha sido diseñado e ilustrado por Víctor Arenas.Nuestro objetivo será cruzar el bosque mágico de los Micomicons mientras avanzamos por las cartas de camino hasta llegar al Rey con los objetos que nos pide, en nuestras cartas.

El jugador robará una carta del mazo de cartas de objeto y podrá usar una de sus cartas de objeto de la mano dejándola sobre la mesa. Tras ver el resultado, llevará a cabo el efecto final de esa carta, que puede ser avanzar, retroceder o lo que se indique para finalizar su turno y dar paso al siguiente jugador.

En el modo competitivo, el primer jugador que consiga llegar hasta el rey y entregarle los objetos o en su defecto conseguir lo que su tirada de dados indica, habrá ganado.

Si hemos jugado en el modo cooperativo, cuando uno de los jugadores llegue a la carta del rey y le entreguen entre todos los objetos que solicita o consigan la tirada de acción con los dados, habrán ganado.

A judge draws cards to help inspire a fictional character. One card gives the first letter of the character's name, the other card gives the first letter of a profession, species, or other descriptor.

Then, 2 more cards are drawn. Everyone else thinks of a 2-word ‘gift’, starting with one letter from each card. Whilst people are thinking of gifts, the judge can improvise some details about the person.

Once more than half the people have decided, they all explain their gifts and then the judge picks a winner (the most useful gift for that character), to become the new judge. That player also gets the 4 cards used in the round.

At the end of the game, whoever has the most cards is the winner!

boardgame: Rainbow Rabbits: Friends Expansion – Frog & Lamb
description: The Frog and the Lamb join the rabbits in the base game. Increases the player count by two.

boardgame: The Drunken Idiots Present: A Night Out
description:
This three part board-style drinking game will take you through the pregame, adventures at the bar, and the drive home. Each section contains their own deck of cards based on real experiences. With up to 6 players A Night Out allows a realistic night out experience with your friends.

During their turn, a player will roll the die, move their game piece, and do what the space says but be careful! If you land on a check point, you could be sent to the drunk tank! The first player to make it to the home space wins!

Although designed with alcohol in mind, this game is compatible with non-alcoholic beverages so anyone can play!
The players take turns to roll and move. They start at space A (Arnsberg). The first to reach space E (Volkmarsen) wins the game. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

In the game there are 144 picture cards , each of which contains a drawn picture on the front and the written term and derivatives of it on the back. In turn, each player takes a picture card and reads the term to the next player. The next player must write it down. If he has written it down correctly, he receives the picture card. The first player to collect 7 picture cards wins the game. Additional spelling exercises are also included.

On 3 large picture boards, objects are depicted that have certain properties (color, shape, number). A rotary wheel is used to randomly select which property (e.g. "yellow") is being searched for. The first person to point to a matching object (e.g. "lemon") wins a point. Whoever has 5 points first wins the game.

—user summary

SAGA - Age of Hannibal is a Saga supplement that covers the three Punic Wars between Rome and Carthage that took place between 264 and 146 BC.
 This Universe includes six factions - some of which include multiple variants - each with its own Battle Board, history, Legendary Units, and special rules.

As well as these factions, you will find an array of mercenaries who can join your warband, as well as a system of Ruses which allow you to personalise your warband by granting specific advantages or chances to deal treacherous low blows to your enemies.

Finally, the supplement ends with Epic Saga, an entirely new way of playing Saga that allows you to play games with warbands of 12 to 18 points on each side. Now you can recreate the greatest battles of antiquity on your table! This game mode includes special rules and a unique method of setting up the game.

—description from the publisher

This first expansion for Interplanetary, titled Supply Drop, simply adds additional components which are added to the base game's pool of these components. Material of the following types is added:


     more directives cards, which increase the variety of the player's goals and thus the number of possible campaign deck configurations
     additional parts, allowing the player to construct new installations with fresh boons and to bestow new abilities to one's spacecraft
     additional outposts, increasing their number from 4 to 6, thus allowing for new tactics in a playthrough

Hide Your Nuts is a strategic, yet easy-to-learn card game that is bound to make you laugh. Take turns hiding your nuts until one player prevails! It is a fast-moving, fun, and easily portable game.  Perfect for traveling, a game night with friends, or a hilarious ice breaker for that first time meeting the in-laws.

On their turn, players use cards to hide their nuts or steal nuts from other players, as well as use weapons and the weather to turn the tide in your favor.
 2-3 Player Game: Be the first player to "hide" 15 nuts in your hiding spots
4-6 Player Game: Be the first player to "hide" 10 nuts in your hiding spots

Hide Your Nuts is intended for ages 13+. It is mostly kid friendly in design, but has a lot of adult humor.

boardgame: The Adventures of Robin Hood: Friar Tuck in Danger
description:
Have you successfully completed all the previous adventures of Robin Hood?

Four new tasks await Robin Hood and his companions in the expansion The Adventures of Robin Hood: Friar Tuck in Danger. Unknown warriors attack in the West, and old adversaries become bitter enemies once again. Friar Tuck will support the heroes with his special abilities.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

boardgame: Andor: The Family Fantasy Game – The Danger in the Shadows
description:
In this expansion to the base game, a dangerous beast, the Shadow Wardrak, is up to mischief in the lands of Andor. Without hesitation you set off on a quest to rid him from the lands once and for all! But before you start this huge task there is still a lot to do.

Throw yourselves into the fight against the Gors, help the residents of Andor and then defeat the evil Shadow Wardrak to save the land of Andor!

Andor: The Family Fantasy Game series features gameplay that is similar to the popular Legends of Andor, but more approachable to younger players, making it a great introduction to the entire Legends of Andor series of games.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Strategy card game.
Your goal is to accumulate as many points as possible by keeping the Virulence of the epidemic as low as possible, and through Missions carried out during the game. Draw and play humorous Development Cards to build your own strategy to resist the epidemic!

On their turn, players roll a dice to determine the evolution of the epidemic, then draw cards, and choose which cards to play to develop their country in accordance with their strategy. It is the intensity of the epidemic in a player's country that determines how many resources he/she will have to play cards during this turn.

Players try to accumulate points by developing certain abilities through playing cards. These abilities can help them slow the epidemic and reduce its intensity, or carry out missions. Both make the player gain points at the end of the game.

A game lasts 6 months, one month in the game being represented by a turn played by all players (so each player plays 6 turns). The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Kyojuu in the Dark is a strategic memory game about uniting a mythical island under your clan. Four clans make up the island: Rock, Water, Forest, and Earth.

In the darkness, quiet battles rage, with Lords, Warriors, Farmers, and even Children playing their part. Loyal as dogs, the towering Kyojuu fight alongside human masters. These mythical creatures protect their clans with unmatched strength. Remembering former times of peace, the Kyojuu are powerless in the presence of Children.

To start the game, each player places their 9 pieces face down within their designated 3x3 home area. Once placed onto the board, these pieces cannot be flipped over again except when attacking, being attacked, or by the special use of an action card. Each turn, players decide whether to roll and advance their pieces, battle another player, “pinch”, or instead play an action card. When attacking, players reveal the face of one of their pieces that is next to an opposing player's piece. The opponent then reveals their face, and the lower-ranked piece gets removed from the board. The only way to oust a Kyojuu from the board is to “pinch”, which is when two Children from any clan surround a Kyojuu. This move can be made at any point in the game. Through battles and card play, each player’s ultimate goal is to reach the center square of an opponent’s 3x3 square home territory. The first player that lands on the center square of an opponent’s territory is declared the winner.

boardgame: Star Trek: The Next Generation Collectible Dice Game – USS Enterprise NCC-1701-E Booster Set #5
description:
Expansion for the Star Trek: The Next Generation Collectible Dice Game – USS Enterprise NCC-1701-E Starter. 
Contains:
3xd8 Movement Impulse dice
3xd10 Weapon Phase dice
2xd12 Weapon Photon Torpedo dice
2xd12 Weapon Quantum Torpedo dice

Each booster set contains 10 new dice to expand your ship and rulebook with rules for multiplayer battles and explaining new dice effects.

The Buffalo Wings Expansion Kit expands coverage of the "Winter War" and "Continuation War" between Finland and the USSR, sometimes independent and then as part of WW2. The title comes from a plane -- the Brewster Buffalo -- which was woefully unsuccessful when used by US and British forces, but which the Finns adapted into a very useful fighter, which they nicknamed the "Sky Pearl." (Yeah, they liked it a lot!)

The original Buffalo Wings issue game is popular because it serves as an 'intro' game to the entire "Fighting Wings" series -- games from J.D. Webster that are among the most popular air battle board games of all time. This expansion kit allows folks who want to get deeper and into more complexity with their games to do so while still using the intro rules found in the base Buffalo Wings game itself.
