To that end, we've included more "Aircraft Data Cards" (the handy charts which capture everything that makes one plane different from another), plus the bombers in the expansion game now perform as individual planes (they are rather generic in the base game).

Best of all, the new map and ADC cards are still fully compatible with the entire Fighting Wings series of games!

boardgame: 秘密のオークションハウス (Secret Auction House)
description:
秘密のオークションハウス (Secret Auction House) is an auction game from Japan with some hidden elements. Players bid simultaneously with money cards in a series of auctions. But matching bids cancel each other (butting) in the manner of What the Heck?. So the auction can be won by a player with a low bid.

All bid cards are discarded, until the player exhausts their hand or spends a special token, to refill their hand. By careful choice, a player can know what cards other players have, and manage their own bids successfully.

Players spend cash when they win bids on auction items. Not running out of money too soon is a challenge.

Further, players have two secret cards of winning conditions they want to meet, such as winning different values or combos of items. For example, winning two pairs of items scores 5 bonus points, or a run of items valued 1-3 to get 3 bonus points.

At the end of the auction rounds, the player with the most points wins.
user summary

boardgame: Poetry for Neanderthals: More Cards Box 1
description:
More Cards Box 1 is the first expansion to Poetry for Neanderthals.

Poetry for Neanderthals is a competitive word-guessing game where you can only give clues by speaking in single syllables. So, instead of saying "broccoli," you'd say something like "green thing you eat for live long and have good health." If you mess up and use a big word, such as "vegetable," you get bopped on the head with a NO !Stick and you lose points.

The goal of the game is to score the most points by correctly interpreting words and phrases. The chosen Poet starts off the game with a Poetry Card and tries to get their teammates to say the listed word, using only words with one syllable within a 90-second time limit.

The box contains 500 new double sided cards. That’s 2000 new words and phrases to use with Poetry for Neanderthals.

2 player traditional card game in two phases. In the first phase play 13 tricks to draft a hand of cards for round two. The winner of a trick take a face up display card into their Showcase row, while the loser takes the top card of talon into their hand unseen. Then another display card is dealt. After thirteen tricks, in the second phase, the last thirteen tricks are played out exposed using the previously won cards in each's Showcase row.

There are several unique constraints that guide the flavor and strategy of the game: Phase 1 is played "All trump": a player has to follow suit but if they can't and play another suit that card automatically wins.  Only these ruffed tricks count as points in the first phase. In the second phase, the player with the least points in the first phase gets to choose trump (among any of the four suits, no-trump, or a repeat of the all-trump of the first phase).  All tricks count 1 point in the second phase. The other interesting constraint is that if a player leads a suit that the other player has in their showcase row, the other player must follow suit. Otherwise they may play any card.

FuertoRite (フエルトリテ) is a trick-taking card game from Japan. The game box shows a sail boat on Lake Ashi, which looks strangely familiar.

In FuertoRite, players follow normal trick-taking rules, but with some changes. Players draw more cards as they play, so ending with more hand cards than when they started.

Further, players draft cards with their neighbors during the game. This mix of drawing, drafting and hand management gives the game a different feel.

Scoring is based on which cards players win, but only the smallest set counts, so players must be careful to balance their winnings.

Specially designed for families with children ages 6 and up, Jooky Jooky is a game about an intrusive insect that players will delight in chasing through 4 different challenges: labyrinth, search & find, majority and alive.

Each player, armed with a swatter, must take a good look at the challenge card in play and be the first to correctly complete the challenge, either by swatting Jooky with their swatter or by swatting the card. The fastest player wins the card. The first player to collect 10 Cards win the game.

This game, which relies on speed, dexterity and observation skills, can easily be adapted to the age and abilities of its players.

Although Jooky Jooky may seem a bit childish at first glance, don’t be fooled, as both children and adults will have a great time! The whole family will enjoy playing this simple yet lively game.

Tourists do not count as an extra zombie card around the bus.

When they arrive, place 5 Injury tablets on the tourists. Remove one at the end of each turn.

As long as there is at least one patch left on the map, you can save these tourists by assigning them 4 action sides in a single turn. Win the card (place it under your hero).

As soon as there are no more pastilles on the map, tourists turn into zombies. They now count as an additional zombie card around the bus that you can no longer release or damage.

Note: if there is no more cheerleader to release, the tourists must also be released or wait for them to transform for the game to end.


Les touristes ne comptent pas comme une carte zombie supplémentaire autour du bus.A leur arrivée, placez 5 pastilles Blessures sur les touristes. Retirez-en une à la fin de chaque tour.

Tant qu'il reste au-moins une pastille sur la carte, vous pouvez sauvez ces touristes en leur affectant 4 faces actions en un seul tour. Remportez la carte (placez-la sous votre héros).

Dès qu'il n'y a plus de pastille sur la carte, les touristes se transforment en zombies. Ils comptent désormais comme une carte zombie supplémentaire autour du bus que vous ne pouvez plus ni libérer ni blesser.

Note : s'il n'y a plus de cheerleader à libérer, il faut aussi libérer les touristes ou attendre qu'ils se transforment pour que la partie prenne fin.

boardgame: Alfred the Great: The London Campaign, 885 AD (Volume 5)
description:
Alfred in this period commenced both the re-structuring of his Army and his Burh building program in earnest.

In 885 a Viking army that had been active in Frankia sailed and attacked Rochester, a pivotal Town/Burh with its confluence of roads and river running thru it. The Vikings of East Anglia, under Alfred’s longtime nemesis Guthrum, now saw an opportunity with their Viking Allies to strike back at their Saxon antagonists.

Lundene had been held as a defensive base by the Vikings since 871AD; by reinforcing the existing walls in the old Roman town they commanded a strategic asset which controlled the Thames River from both trading and military perspectives. If Alfred was to achieve his goal of an Anglo- Saxon England than this would have to rectified.

Kings are of great importance in this series of games. When they are leading the warriors in combat your moral and thereby fighting ability is increased, without them it is weakened. Of course, the loss of a King in battle can also mean the loss of your cause. However, in this era risks must be taken to take and hold the crown. Saddle your horse and grab your sword for the King has called you to war…for God and Wessex!

Each copy of Alfred the Great vol. 5: The London Campaign is composed of the following:

Two 11 by 17 hex map sheets.
Two sheets of 267 (total) single-sided, un-mounted counters.
Two Battle Boards.
Two Supply Tracks.
Nine pages of rules including designer notes and examples of play.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: Warrior Kings Campaign (High Flying Dice Games)

description: An operational design by Greg Porter covering the Nagorno-Karabahk War of 2020. You'll command formations of T-90 tanks, BMP-equipped mechanized infantry, drones, cruise missiles, and squadrons of attack aircraft as you simulate the war between Armenia and Azerbaijan, which took place in the fall of 2020. Units represent battalions grouped into brigades. Maneuver and attacks in the game are done via actions played from a deck of cards, and each pass through the deck represents 2 days and nights of fighting. A game of Caucasus Burning: The Nagorno-Karabahk War, 2020 will probably take a little less than 10 passes through the deck, so it will run a little faster than the actual war.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: This miniature, abbreviated version of Master Word was included as a promo in Game Trade Magazine #249. It contains two word cards and a QR code that links to an online rulebook.

boardgame: Field of War micro card game
description:
Field of War is an asymmetric game for 3 players. Quick to setup and play, but deep in strategy and escalating consequences. 2 players will fight over the territory playing command cards, recruiting troops and rolling dice to attack each other.  While a third player will try to farm his fields while dodging bombs in a worker placement and resource management style of game. Wait enough time, and the farm workers will raise against its oppressors.

On a turn, the farm player will use their workers to do an action of one of the available fields. Those actions allow him to gather resources, recruit more workers, build a barricade to peacefully trade with the surrounding troops. The army players however, will pick a command card from his hand to choose actions and march over the fields of the farmers, shoot any enemy rolling dice, recruit more troops or upgrade them.

Both sides will collide and at any time, the farmer player can decide to turn intro a revolutionary and fight the armies back.

The game ends when the farmers gather enough resources to pay for mercenaries. Or when the armies eliminate the opponents or troops arrive at the opponent's command base.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Animals need brave rescuers now. Defy a thousand dangers, help free the protected animals, take them back to the savannah and the primeval forest! You will have to deal with evil hunters along the way. You have to cross the Nile, which is teeming with wild hippos. You are flying over the barren Sahara. You can only count on the help of caravans. Who will be the first to bring back and feed all five animals?

Game rules: The object of the game is to return the animals to their habitats, the winner is the first to return five animals. They rescue an animal at the camp site by taking an animal card with them. They roll the dice, choosing their own route to get the water and food that will be right for the animal on the way, and then take it back to its natural habitat. When entering a caravan field, we get help cards, when entering a poaching field, we have to draw obstruction cards - unless we have handcuffs, in which case we can take the poach to the hunter.

Ja ci pokażę JUNIOR is a charades-based party game for teams of two or more players. It contains cards with items, animals, emotions and professions.

In a game, one of a team members take turns trying to get their teammates to guess as many words from the cards as possible.

There is way of showing in one side of the card and picture on the other side of the card. One player turns around top card and has to show or tell the word in a special way (i.e. only showing with no words or by using only sounds). If the team guesses they get the card as a point. If nobody can´t guess the word and time is up, next team plays.

When the preprepared deck runs out the team with the most points is the winner.

—description from the designer

The Campaign is a series of up to 5 games of Endangered. Each game must use a different species scenario. Winning a game makes the next game harder, while losing a game makes the next game easier!

Before each game, randomly draw one Campaign card (that hasn't already been used in this Campaign). Each card describes conditions dependent on whether you won or lost the previous game. After reading the condition, select your species Scenario and difficulty, and apply the condition.

Track your wins and losses. If you achieve 3 wins before you suffer 3 losses, your campaign is a success! If you suffer 3 losses first, sadly you have failed in your mission. Good luck!

The Endangered: Celebrity expansion introduces the power of publicity, getting your message out, and mobilizing the masses. Use podcasters, protests, and influence to tip the scale in your favor.

A new role is available for Endangered. The Celebrity mini-expansion comes with a role board, 14 cards, 3 custom dice, and 2 role powers. Change up your game by substituting a celebrity for a role in the core Endangered game. This expansion is contained in the Kickstarter version of Endangered: New Species but is also available separately.

Help Maëlys and Lucien to get out of the castle before midnight, by solving the puzzles in each room. Hurry, because the clock is ticking, but keep your eyes peeled for clues! Hours of play ahead to have fun all year round!

For 1 to 4 players. From 7 years old. A superb big game: 1 large castle to assemble, 180 puzzle cards, 9 envelopes, 1 red filter, 1 mirror card, 1 Maëlys and Lucien pawn, 1 magnifying glass, 1 clock, 1 hourglass, 1 notepad.

Elf King Red is a fastplay game using the Lucid Eye Engine, set in the Elf realms of the Isles of Eas, previously seen in The Red Book of The Elf King & Troll Wars. Ownership of those two books is not essential as this is a standalone product, but they contain lots of colourful background and setting information which some players may find useful. Elf King Red uses similar sized armies to Savage Core, which is around 7 figures or so, seven being a mystical number for the Elves. The game has been written to provide a playable experience with a low commitment entry point.

This free rulebook provides all the basic rules needed to play and includes stats for all of the Factions, otherwise known as Circles in Elf King Red.

So once again strap on your Faeblade of Starmetal, don your Glamourbound Armour and prepare your magick, for Red War and The Fall of All.

Obscure War is a tabletop miniature board game in which Warwulf Cabal and Vampyric Cabal fight for the control of Obscure Plane Domains. The game takes place in a contemporary Dark Fantasy universe with a rather spectacular mechanic of Power Cards that interact directly on the Game zone and affect the Characters in game.

You have 3 phases in a Turn : Activation, Power, and Dissolution.

During his/her activation, a Character (miniature) can perform 4 types of Optional actions : Power card Action, movement action, attack action, and control action. Each Cabal activates its Characters one after the other until the activation of all the 8 Characters deployed on the Game zone.

In the Power phase, each Cabal gains 1 Power Point for each Obscure Plane’s Domain it controls.

In the Dissipation phase, all the Power Cards are discarded and placed in the Discard.

To win Obscure War, a Cabal must have the highest Power point score after 3 Turns.

OBJECT: to fill up the two monsters you own by drawing the right animals.

Each player is the proud owner of 2 monsters! Greedy but difficult monsters, because they only feed on certain letters contained in the names of animals. To try to feed them, you have to draw a card. You then say the name of the animal shown on it. If this name contains one of the letters the monster likes, the monster can be fed. To be full up, it will need 15 calories, i.e. 15 points shown on the cards... Beware of the “indigestion” cards which can make you lose points. The first player to fill their 2 monsters up wins the game!

+++  2 difficulty levels so players can play at their own pace!

—description from the publisher

ウチューカイギ (Uchu Kaigi) is a trick-taking game from Japan, about an "interspace conference on Earth".

The players are trying to be selected as the ambassador for an alien conference on Earth. By providing entertainment for the delegates from seven different worlds, a player tries to curry favour. But if they give too much to one of the aliens, they will be penalised for favouritism. They will lose points, and their rivals may gain.

The game follows normal trick-taking rules, players following the colour lead. The cards shows an alien and the amount of hospitality the player is offering. Winning the trick gets the player the host spot and the cards go to their tableau.

Rounds end when a player exhausts their hand. The game end when somebody reaches enough points or enough rounds are played. The player with the most points wins.

However, the different alien races trigger effects, either when the card is played or scored. For example, a player might be forced out of a round, or lose points for having too many cards of one alien. This means players have to make more choices than simply playing high cards.
user summary

Tech City is a multi-player, semi-cooperative city building game for 1-6 players. The cards are tiles that are modular representing companies. Card game effects become available as the city expands. Service companies require multiple players to cooperate to jointly build. Each round players add companies to Tech City and players individually score points as they build companies based on pattern matching and board state. 

The Tech Deck is a modern deck of cards bringing playing cards into the 21st century. It is a game platform designed to support many different games. Each card in the Tech Deck has unique game effects to create unique game experiences. Also, see Tech Grid Poker. Cards represent companies in Tech City each with special effects that players can use.

Each round, players get to play a card representing a company to build up Tech City. They score points based on matching cards they play and the board state. Players then get to use effects on any card in Tech City to make each game experience unique and fun.

The game ends after 6 rounds of play. The player(s) with the most points wins the game.

Stack the Scoops is a an award winning ice-cream themed family card game kids and adults can enjoy together! Created to be both fun and educational, Stack the Scoops helps reinforce fundamental math skills while encouraging families to connect through games.

The premise is simple, kids and adults take turns trying to make ice cream sets by collecting a base, scoop, and topping. Different cards are worth different points. At the end of the game the player with the most points wins.

Kids love the easy playability and the fun images while the strategy plays on classic card game concepts making it recognizable and fun for adults.

You've just been hired as the new director of the CERN laboratory. Can you guide your team through six experiments and earn enough grant money to fund future research?

To celebrate the return of Pentaquark, Mike Mullins has designed a campaign mode for the original game.

It uses all Colliders – Pentaquark: Colliders (Kickstarter edition) and Pentaquark: Colliders (BGotMC edition) – (also included in the PNP if you don’t have them) and will keep you on your toes. As if the main game didn’t already do that.

boardgame: Balkan Wars: The First Balkan War
description:
Relive the First Balkan War in this exciting and quick playing game. Having recently lost Libyan and Adriatic territories to the Italians, the Ottoman Empire has shown its weakness. Serbia, Greece, and Bulgaria now see this as their opportunity to expand and claim the Balkans for themselves. Will you maintain the Empire and hold lands held since the 14th century for the Sultan? Or will you take your rightful lands from the sick man of Europe and secure your place on the Balkan peninsula?

Designed to play quickly and easily, The First Balkan War will allow you to easily teach younger audiences, and anyone knew to board gaming, how to play A&A style games. Move and recruit armies, rolls dice, and conquer the map. For those veterans of A&A, you already own all the pieces you need to play! Just buy the rondel packs and any relevant units you want for a more immersive experience. Test your strategies against each other and determine the fate of the future powder-keg of Europe!
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
6 Promo Cards for Hadrian's Wall + 1 rules card.

6 x Actor (1 per player deck)
Scores for completed performances:
2 = 1VP / 4 = 2VP / 6 = 3VP

New rules:
During Setup, each player adds their new Actor Card into their Draw Pile before shuffling. Rather than drawing 2 Cards each Year, players must now draw 3. They still choose 1 as their Path and 1 as their Prospect. The third, unchosen Card must be placed facedown on top of their Draw Pile, which they will see again in the next Year. In Year 6, the unchosen card can be discarded from the game entirely.

As pitcher and batter, you and your opponent try to outmatch each other. Just like the real thing! This is a cat and mouse contest between pitcher and batter. Both pitcher and batter actively choose either a black chip or white chip for each at-bat.  When both have chosen, reveal your choices at the same time.  If the colors match, then the batter is OUT.  If the colors do not match, the batter gets a HIT.  If it is a HIT use the "Snap Action Dice" and reference the chart to see what happened. PLAY BALL!!

The player with the most runs wins.
family: Series: Klix Pocket Travel Games (Tiger Electronics)

description:
レムレース (Lemures) is a horror game from Japan. The game can be played solitaire or as a two player co-operative game. The player is exploring a strange fantastic castle, helping souls trapped, and seeking their own way out.

The publisher says:
Is there a place for the soul to return here? The abyss is changing. You will always be exposed to the threat of unknown beings in the castle. Explore the interior of the old castle while rejecting "they".

Since the inside of the castle changes with each play, different developments will come every time. Co-operative play with two people is also possible.

During your exploration, you may find the souls of those who once lived in the castle. Such a soul is already a light in front of the wind, but if you give it power, it will bloom beautiful flowers. On the contrary, if you take power, it will soon die.

You cannot survive in the castle without strong power. Let's create a new technique from Reason and Instinct. There is some danger in learning the art of Instinct. The ending changes depending on whether you follow Reason or Instinct.

If you have been exploring the castle for a long time, you will be oppressed by karma. Karma is a dangerous being that undermines vitality and soul. Be careful, but be quick to explore. The deeper you go into the castle, the more memories of the past will come back ...
publisher's summary

boardgame: Family Feud Jumbo Card Game: Platinum Edition
description:
Survey says ... fun, in a big way! Challenge friends and family to give the top answers to survey questions. 
 --- Description from box

This promo pack adds 3 promo cards to Roll Player and can be used in combination with any of the Roll Player expansions.

Included cards: Cursed Ring, Ring of Charity, and the Cartographer backstory card.


         Note: The Cartographer backstory card is included inside every copy of Cartographers: A Roll Player Tale. These cards may not be purchased individually on the Thunderworks Games website, but Cursed Ring and Ring of Charity are available individually through the Boardgamegeek Store (as of 2023) if you already own the Cartographer card.
Expanded rules published in Space Gamer #34 (Dec. 1980) add new weapons and equipment as well as bases and a point system for design your own scenarios.

boardgame: Star Wars: Don't Upset The Wookiee!
description:
Celebrating some of Chewie's best scenes, this is a fast-paced card game where the Wookiee gets all the glory.

Play a card with an angrier Chewie than the last until he eventually loses it! Complete a set of Wookiees, be the first to lose your cards, or be the last one standing to win after three fun filled rounds!

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar (Third Edition): Destruction Battletome – Orruk Warclans
description:
The Orruks are Destruction incarnate. This vicious and primal race of natural-born killers loves nothing more than to wage unending war. Their society is built around conflict, and huge migratory invasions called Waaagh!s erupt from all corners of the Mortal Realms to drown the worlds of Order, Death, and Chaos in a green tide of violence. To face the Orruk Warclans is to face total annihilation, for their numbers are beyond counting, and they live solely for the thrill of bloody combat.

In this book, you'll discover the history of the Orruk Warclans, including their presence across the Mortal Realms and the societies that hold them together. As well as exploring the movements and motives of the orruk race, Battletome: Orruk Warclans also gives you insight into their spiritual connection to Gorkamorka, the primordial god of Destruction. The background information is complemented by a full suite of rules allowing you to field armies of Ironjawz, Bonesplitterz, and Kruleboyz, or to combine all three clans into one colossal Big Waaagh!.

This 136-page hardback book contains:

– A war chest of lore about the Orruk Warclans, their history, and their activities following the return of Kragnos
– Stunning art depicting the Ironjawz, Bonesplitterz, and the new Kruleboyz
– 37 warscrolls and pitched battle profiles for everything from sneaky Hobgrot Slittaz to the Earthquake God himself
– 15 warscroll battalions for Path to Glory narrative campaigns
– Allegiance abilities for every Orruk Warclans subfaction, including battle traits, command traits, and artefacts of power, as well as the Big Waaagh! that combines Ironjawz, Bonesplitterz, and Kruleboyz
– Content for matched play, including 5 grand strategies, 6 battle tactics, and 3 core battalions
– Path to Glory campaign rules for creating an Orruk Warclans warband
– A beautiful miniature showcase and painting guide for getting the most out of your Waaagh!
– Extensive bestiary information for the Orruk Warclans units, including the background of Kragnos, End of Empires

'— Description from the publisher'

boardgame: Hidden Games Junior: Das verschollene Schwert
description:
A brazen theft has the citizens of Rome in an uproar. The sword of the gladiator statue "Maximus", which stands for bravery and discipline, has been stolen. Alfredo, the guide and guard of the Colosseum, is suspected of having stolen the sword. He asks you for help and already has an idea how to track down the real culprit. Go to Rome, report to Alfredo and find the landmark of past times - the sword of Maximus.

Players embark on an exciting hunt for the culprit as a team, examining real evidence, analyzing interrogations and checking alibis. Players use tools like their cell phones and blur the line between game and reality.

Translated with www.DeepL.com/Translator (free version)

Promo card Given with the game at launch.
all "Name Cards" feature a real or fictional personality.

*Sherlock Holmes / Aretha Franklin
 *Pelé / Mulan
 *Cthulhu / Cleopatra
 *Gandhi / Mona Lisa
 *Walt Disney / Marie Curie

The second expansion for Interplanetary, titled Unity, enables cooperative gameplay, transforming the base game's solo experience into a two-player one.

The majority of the base game's rules as well as its general gameplay structure remains unchanged.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tough Calls: Dystopia – Emergencias y Desafíos
description:
This expansion for Tough Calls: Dystopia contains 60 new cards: 5 new scenarios, 30 new event cards, 10 emergency cards, 10 challenge cards and 5 faction cards.

Our colony will be confronted with extreme emergencies to which you will have to react quickly. Besides, you will be able to obtain extra votes if you solve the different challenges in the best possible way.

-description from developer

Spirit Fire is a non-linear, open-world board game system in which you explore compelling fantasy realms on an introspective journey of discovery, wonder, and mystery. Your choices matter, because instead of following a story, you ARE the story. Spirit Fire seeks to provide the greatest single player experience available, inspired by a love of TCGs, MMOs, rogue-lite-like loops, metroidvania map development and discovery, fantasy fiction, and — more than anything — a desire for something truly new and fresh.

The game’s system has two principal pieces: your character box, and realm boxes. With the first, you create your character, with whom you explore the open-world realm boxes. Across the realms, you tell your story in a campaign of your own creation, exploring where you want, pursuing the quests and ideals of your choosing, in an experience some have compared to an MMORPG (like World of Warcraft), but most agree defies easy classification.

You’ll explore a series of open-world maps, uncovering new locations and quests in worlds that evolve based on your discoveries and actions. As your character grows, you will craft your deck and bring it to bear against countless unique challenges in the game’s spiral bound book. Each quest or encounter card introduces variables that create a unique variation of one of the book’s dozens (and dozens) of challenge spreads, which vary wildly in style and function depending on the type of activity you are engaged in. Each challenge or activity you’re attempting (evading a threat, deciphering a lost language, negotiating with telepathic wolves, and so forth) recontextualizes your card play, giving you a constant stream of fresh opportunities for creative response without requiring you to learn a bunch of new mechanics all the time.

With drop-in / drop-out multiplayer (meaning seamless transitions between solo and cooperative play, if you’re a “never solo alone”-er), multiple open-world realms coming, and a plan for a continuous stream of quest packs that can by played in any realm, Spirit Fire is a deeply customizable, on-going experience.

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

boardgame: Alice the Train: The Board Game
description:
Alice the Train is a light-weight, pick-up and deliver board game that is based on the children's book of the same name. In this game, you play as trains with the mission of picking up animals and dropping them off at the zoo. You will build and manage your train cars based on how many animals each can hold while in the "vores" variant, separating the carnivorous tigers from the herbivores of the game. Collect stars when dropping off animals at the zoo with the winner being the player closest to 15 stars.

—description from the designer

Tex Willer is a very popular Italian comic book, started in 1948 and still running. Tex Willer, the main character, is a Texas Ranger. American Natives call him "Aquila della Notte" (Night Eagle).

Now he has his own Monopoly edition, too.

Includes a reprint of the classic story "Vendetta Indiana", by Giovanni Ticci

—user summary

The tower and its monsters stand between your ninja clan's rightful place of rule. You must use every fiber of cunning in this push your luck battle.

Draw seven weapon cards and use them to fight your way to the top. Be careful though, not having the right weapons means a long drop to the foot of the tower.

Weapons are made up of 3 colors (red,blue, and yellow) and 2 types (basic and legendary). The Blue Swords, Red Spears, and Yellow Magic Staffs. The legendary weapons also come with powers.

Each level of the tower has one of 7 different monster types. Discard the matching symbol weapons to advance. Your hand size will continue to shrink as you climb the tower thus making it much harder to have the right weapons.

Other clans are also battling their way to ensure they too are noticed. Play through three rounds of cumulating victory points. The player who is in last in between rounds receives the Mask of Shame - A Legendary Item that makes its holder immune, but at great cost to one's honor.

Will you climb your way into the Halls of Honor or be stained with the Shadows of Shame?

—description from the publisher

This set includes five expansion sets for The Devil's Level card game, totaling 144 cards. The Expansions are:

Not Safe for Work: 36 cards that let you steal from your boss and waste time. And you can mess up your customers and other people at work.

Legacy of Boodleheimer: Let's you use Boodleheimer's spirit to control stuff that happens in the game. It gives you cat powers and cat functions.

Sorry I'm Sorry: 36 cards that are about screwing up and breaking stuff all the time. Because it happens to me.

Foil Pack: KS exclusive Foil backed cards.

Admin Pack: KS exclusive pack of ultra-powerful cards designed by the Admin to sneak into your hand during play, unbalance the game, and make your friends mad.

A board game using cards from a traditional deck, Ace through 10 in four suits, and a 6X6 square grid, and colored pieces of two colors. Each square on the grid is labeled with a number representing all combination of products of the card ranks, e.g. 1 (1X1),2 (2X1),3, 4 (2X2, 4X1)..81 (9X9). Two players take turns play a card from their hands into either one of two rows, and then place a piece of their color into the location on the board matching the product of the two cards. For instance if a 3 is the top most card exposed and they play a 7 they could put a piece into square 21.  This continues until a player gets four pieces in a row or the other folds.

—user summary

Puffin Rock: Spin & Find game was developed to help children strengthen their memory and practice memory techniques. There are two ways of playing it - an easier and a more advanced mode.

The game has a spinner and tiles with characters. The characters are represented on the tiles multiple times in different environments and poses. The goal of the game is to find and get as many tiles as possible.

In the basic game mode, children first mark the back of the tiles. They can draw their unique markings, shapes and doodles that will help them remember what is on the other side. The tiles are then placed face-down. In the second part of the game they spin the spinner and find the character that the arrow points to among the face-down tiles. The player with the most tiles at the end of the game wins.

In the advanced game mode, extra tiles are added, with only some tiles marked, and additional rules apply that make the game even more challenging.

The rules include additional variants for a single player and cooperative gameplay.

—description from the publisher

A.T.R.: Ahora Todos Reímos is a lighter version of H.D.P.: Hasta Donde Puedas.

Play begins with a judge a.k.a. A.T.R. playing a black card with a question or fill-in-the-blank phrase from the top of the deck and showing it to all players. Each player holds a hand of ten white answer cards at the beginning of each round, and passes a card to the A.T.R., face-down, representing their answer to the question on the card. The A.T.R. determines which answer card is the funniest one in the context of the question or fill-in-the-blank card. The player who submitted the chosen card is given the question card to represent a point, and then the player to the left of the A.T.R. becomes the new A.T.R. for the next round. Play continues until a player collects 5 points.

El Gran Descamisado is a board game that combines strategy, general culture, consensus, chance, attention, verbal dexterity and mental agility. Through seven games that refer to the main milestones in the history of Peronism, an attempt will be made to obtain the seven "Peronist emblems" to become El Gran Descamisado.

To start the game, the players will concentrate their Peronist pawns at the “starting point” and will return there each time their game turn ends. In their corresponding turn, each partner must turn the historical clock. When the needle stops, it will indicate the game to be played, associated with a certain event in the history of Peronism. Each of these games may be individual, in pairs or in a group, as appropriate and will allow to win a Peronist emblem. This dynamic will be repeated until someone meets the ultimate goal of obtaining the 7 emblems.

"Pact of Loyalty": Game of pacts and agreements. Loyalty (and trust) ensures that both parties are favored. If ambition wins, it will become an all-or-nothing challenge.

"Trivia Compañera": Classic question and answer game about curious and historical data of Peronism.

"Choosing a candidate": Voting game where everyone chooses who they think best represents the topic of the slogan that summons them in the electoral act.

"The Renunciation": A game of strategy and chance, where three slogans must be answered blindly, accepting the consequences.

"Peronometer": Fun group game where attention and concentration will allow you to succeed in not stumbling in Peronist calls and gestures.

"La Censura": Classic game where you must guess a censored word from the clues and guidance of your partner, who will also be limited in not mentioning the prohibited words.

"Viva Perón": Verbal skill group game, showing creativity and ingenuity to create linguistic combinations against the clock.


El Gran Descamisado es un juego de mesa que combina estrategia, cultura general, consenso, azar, atención, destreza verbal y agilidad mental. A través de siete juegos que remiten a los principales hitos de la historia del peronismo, se intentará conseguir los siete “emblemas peronistas” para convertirse en EL GRAN DESCAMISADO. 

Para iniciar la partida los/las jugadores/as concentrarán sus peones peronistas en el “punto de inicio” y regresarán allí cada vez que finalice su turno de juego. En su turno correspondiente cada compañero o compañera deberá girar el reloj histórico. Cuando la aguja se detenga, ésta indicará el juego a desarrollar, asociado a determinado suceso de la historia del peronismo. Cada uno de estos juegos podrá ser individual, en duplas o grupal, según corresponda y permitirá ganar un emblema peronista. Se repetirá esta dinámica hasta que alguien cumpla el objetivo final de obtener los 7 emblemas.

Juegos: 
1)  1945: 17 de Octubre “Día de la Lealtad”. Juego: “Pacto de lealtad” Emblema: Escudo PJ
2)  1946: Primera Presidencia. Juego: “Trivia compañera” Emblema: Marcha Peronista
3)  1947: Voto femenino. Ley 13.010. Juego: “Eligiendo candidato/a”. Emblema: Carnet de afiliación
4)  1951: Cabildo Abierto del Justicialismo. Juego: “La Renuncia”. Emblema: Bombo peronista
5)  1952: Segunda Presidencia. Juego: “Peronómetro”. Emblema: Choripan y vino
6)  1955: Golpe de Estado y Proscripción. Juego: “La Censura” Emblema: No me olvides
7)  1973: Tercera Presidencia. Juego: “Viva Perón”. Emblema: Dedos en V

“Pacto de Lealtad”: Juego de pactos y acuerdos. La lealtad (y confianza) asegura que ambas partes salgan favorecidas. Si gana la ambición, se transformará en un desafío a todo o nada.

“Trivia Compañera”: Clásico juego de preguntas y respuestas sobre datos curiosos e históricos del peronismo.

“Eligiendo candidato/a”: Juego de votación donde todos y todas eligen a quien creen que mejor representa el tópico de la consigna que los convoca en el acto eleccionario.

“La Renuncia”: Juego de estrategia y azar, donde deberá responderse a ciegas tres consignas acatando las consecuencias.

“Peronómetro”: Divertido juego grupal donde la atención y concentración permitirá salir airoso/a de no trastabillar en los llamados y ademanes peronistas.

“La Censura”: Clásico juego donde se deberá adivinar una palabra censurada a partir de las pistas y orientaciones de su compañero o compañera, que también se encontrará limitado/a en no mencionar las palabras prohibidas.

“Viva Perón”: Juego grupal de destreza verbal, debiendo demostrar la creatividad e ingenio para crear combinaciones lingüísticas contrarreloj.

Square Play-on is a name re-defined based on the shape
This is also called Indian LUDO sometimes. It is one of the oldest board games extant. (Don’t know the Origins of the Game) It is played in many homes of India (particularly in the south part of India even today still being popular and known to everyone.  There are references to this game in some ancient Indian epics like the Mahabharata. 
The most common names are Chauka Bara (Kannada) Ashta Chamma (Telugu) and a list of various names and regions are given below 
Chouka Bara , Katte Mane ,Gatta Mane  - Kannada - Mysuru region
Chakaara or Chakka - Kannada - North Karnataka
Pakidakali , Kavidi Kali - Malayalam - Kerala region
Ashta Chamma - Telugu - Andhra Pradesh/Telangana
Daayam or Thaayam - Tamil - Tamil Nadu
Atthu, Kanna Kauri ,Chunga ,Cheeta ,Chanda —Hindi -Madhya Pradesh and central India region 
Champool/ Kach kangri - Marathi - Maharashtra
 Baji ,Chomal Ishto,Kaangi chaala  - Gujarati
Changa po - Rajasthan (source: Wikipedia)

This Game is a two- or four-player board game, Usually written by chalk on the floor as and when required. This game is an example of a fully observable positional system of pawns that has an element of chance introduced by the roll of cowry shells and an element of strategy (The strategy being the pawn the player decides to move after the roll ). While traditionally played with 4 or 6 cowry shells, Dice in particular Stick dice can also be used.
family: Traditional Games: Alquerque / Fanorona (Indian War Games)

description:
Vyapar is a game of trading using the random outcomes of dice and cards. It's a mid-level game for ages +10 and above as involves calculation and strategies to spend less and earn more with multiple trading opportunities to earn. 
The time limit of the game varies from 30 min to 90 min depending on the random outcome.

The synopsis of the game is similar to a stock market scenario where you will buy and sell Commodities of Gold silver and copper 
The game is played on the board where you will land on buy/sell or change rate or power lot multiples by the random outcome of the custom dice and based on the action you will do the activity 
There is a set goal to reach the end by having the minimum required quantity of certain commodities and above so that winner is declared. the fluctuation in the market makes you rich or bankruptcy.

The game mechanics are a race game kind of but with certain Rules like One cannot move earlier to the place before the other and there are chances that it will come back lose a turn kind of actions in it

This game is inspired by Rathayatra pulling of chariots and a few rules that Jagannatha Ratha cannot surpass the other 2 and Balabadara will start followed by Subhadra and Jagannatha ( here it is colored Ratha ).

This can be played solo and the design has been made to accommodate 8 players with their own path

The game consists of a central Dice tower, a path to move the chariots, 2 custom dice(one to the chariot and the other no of places forward or back), and action cards to change the course of the game.

The player movement is based on the Random outcome of Dice and Random action cards.

A game-based on card random pick to create a deck (set of Components) to form based on a Quest card or achieve Requirement as defined during the start of the game (combination of 108 water cards(sum)and 2 ingredients of each type)
The winner is the fastest to complete with the least possible deck set of the Ingredients

The game is played between 2-6 players.
It's a mid-level game for ages +8 and above as involves calculation and strategies to make sure you win with lesser cards and fastest to do the calculation and submit the set of cards
The time limit of the game varies from 10 min to 30 min depending on random outcome and level of gameplay.
There are various levels involved in this game to increase the complexity of calculations, increase the difficulty, and also to have killing strategies to make the opponent rebuild his complete deck thereby increasing the chance to win.

The game has 2 variation sets with 8 levels in the game from simple to advanced combination of ingredients

The advantage and limitation is both the calculations as you need to calculate the combination by mind mathematics(this majorly helps the children to do fast calculations of addition and multiplication).

Max 4 Players (solo variant to multiplayer variant)
the game has a setup of Tile laying (every time a new random Board is created using hexagon terrain tiles)and Actual movement of chasing elephants after on the terrain with the advantage of the terrain and the player character

Co-operative resource placement of the operational component
Co-operative actions to capture the wild elephant (3 wild elephants out of 4 elephants)
Random elephant movement (4 X 2 (custom dice) directional dice to work this mechanism on direction and no of places to move in that direction)
Placement of campsites (only in the first turn) and Capture mechanism by a player in subsequent rounds as actions by the player
Placement of capture mechanism by each player every round (excluding some terrain and based on terrain type.

Once all the player places the mechanism is done. a random player rolls the dice(Elephant movement dice) using Dice tower and based on the outcome the elephants move towards the indicated direction

If there is an obstacle it cannot move that direction(excluding a few obstacles and group movements)
The game continues until all or min 3 elephants move to Khedda or all campsites destroyed by the movement of elephants(Detailed rules on win is there in the rulebook)

A cooperative rescue operation game to safeguard the little naughty elephant to its family
this game is an extended edition of Operation Khedda and uses the same tiles and few characters to play

as earlier indicated the game is played on the same components of Operation khedda( Terrain Tiles and obstacles are the same) with certain changes to accommodate the fantasy theme of rescuing little Naughty Elephant. Here the player instead of capturing works on a clearing of a path for the elephant to join its heard from starting point to endpoint ( currently 2 sets of rules one with timed/ 24 rounds co-operative game and the other with one player as a ranger to capture and second player as a rescuer)
and win is the same Elephant rescued/captured or lost in the woods ( co-operative strategy).

A game based on the folklore of the start of the Hoysala kingdom.

Kannada folklore tells a tale of a young man, Sala, who saved his Jain guru, Sudatta, by striking dead a lion or tiger he encountered near the temple of the goddess Vasantika at Angadi. The word "strike" literally translates to "hoy" in Old Kannada, hence the name "Hoy-sala".

The game is played on terrain consisting of forest gurukul, school village, and farmland with the terrain of water stream pathways (a graphic of terrain and to consider a single terrain board or randomizer of terrain tiles work is in progress)

There will be the hero who is making a choice to consider the advice given by the guru, student, village lady, and a farmer based on the sighting of a lion (random cards) and based on the movement outcome of the dice/cards MP the sala can move to the desired location on the map and place obstacles (traps and fence mechanism)

A random Cards mechanism (a staged order) will provide the sighting of the lion after Sala movement and placement. there will 5 lion appearances and movements using the Dice and directional outcome-based of the card. If you are able to capture the lions before it hits the central temple Sala wins( co-operative team) else lost.

Dragons vs Ogres is a fantasy themed two-player card game. It is a speed/dexterity game where players compete to capture the most villagers. It has elements of the card games War and Old Maid.

In Dragons vs Ogres, players choose to play as either dragons or ogres and each have ten cards. They draw a hand of five cards to begin the game. A deck of forty villagers is placed at the center of the table. Players flip up a villager, one at a time. The fastest player to capture that villager scores one point. However, the card used to capture the villager must meet one of three conditions:

     Exceed the number
     Match the number
     Be the correct corresponding image


Depending on which condition a player uses to capture a villager, they might return the dragon or ogre card used to their hand, discard it and draw a new card, or shuffle their discard pile back into their deck and draw back up to five cards.

There are forty villagers and only twenty combined dragons and ogres. So each card must be used an average of two times in order to capture half the villagers. If one player runs out of cards, the other may keep playing until they are also out of cards. Players must be quick enough to beat their opponent to the villager but clever enough to make matches.

King's Color is a chess variant, played on a tri-colored hex-hex board.  Each king is confined to a 3x3 castle surrounded by a wall.  The other pieces are rooks or bishops, which promote to queens.   The big twist to this game is that when the king moves onto a different color, all the rooks become bishops, and all the bishops on the new color become rooks.

Capturing is limited to pieces inside the castle, which influences the game to be oriented around checkmate rather than attrition.

In Mimetix "s'envoie en l'air", in less than a minute, we have fun mimicking the spicy expressions of lovers ... of the French language! Get ready to "heat up" and "get it right", without necessarily "going to the pan"!
In less than a minute, act out and have your team guess as many phrases as possible. Easy ? Imagine miming literally “taking a rake” or “having a puck in the drawer”. The funniest in addition to the extravagant mimes? Fabulous responses from players pulling their hair out and tangling their brushes guessing the expression. You will find 2 rule variations, 3 difficulty levels of expressions and action cards to spice up the game even more!

boardgame: Pigeon Pigeon 2: Le Retour Du Bluff
description:
In this quiz game, make up false answers that are more likely than the real one. The more the opposing team bets on it, the more chips it loses, the closer you get to victory!

No more bluffing and more laughs with 440 amazing new cards!
Texto is back in a new version, playable alone or combined with the first version. You will discover 10 new themes to complete or renew your game.

Robots is a matching card game that invites you to observe, compare and focus your attention.

Goal of the game:
Be the first to get rid of all the cards in your hand. Each player will be able to get rid of their cards every time they find matches in eyes, mouth or nose with the card that is face up on the table.

To play between 2 or more, from the age of 5.

Both players have three soldiers; one player plays with black, the other with yellow. One of the players puts his soldiers upon the 3 blue spaces marked 1, the other puts them upon those marked 2. Each player alternately advances a soldier following the black lines in any direction, one space at a time.

In order to win it is necessary to advance so far as to be able to place your 3 soldiers on 3 spaces of the same color (blue or green) in such a manner that a triangle with specific properties is formed.

—user summary

A simple roll and move game with dinosaurs that accompanies the comic book "Kalle Anka & C: O" no. 42, October 18, 1993 (Kalle Anka is the Swedish name for Donald Duck). Roll the dice and move your game piece, read what is in the square. First in goal wins.

—user summary

A simple roll and move game included in the comic book "Kalle Anka & C:O" no. 13/14, March 28, 1994 (Kalle Anka is the Swedish name for Donald Duck). Roll the dice and move your game piece and read what is in the square. The goal is to collect the most Easter eggs before the finish line. The one with most eggs when everyone has reached the finish line wins.

—user summary

A simple roll and move game with Disney´s Pocahontas as the theme (included in the comic book "Kalle Anka & C: O" no. 49, December 4, 1995). Kalle Anka is the Swedish name for Donald Duck. Roll the dice and move your game piece to the finish line.

—user summary

In Skillmantics Connectors Mission space 1-4 players aim to build a rocket trail and defeat aliens.  Game can be played contributing to one main map or with each player trying to expand their own spaceship trails as much as possible.
Group layout.

Distribute 3 tiles to player
Place starting tile.  Tiles need to be placed connecting similar colors and asteroids may not be aimed towards planets.  Aliens must not face each other.
Players pick any tile from their hand to add to start.  If they cannot they draw from the tile bank.
First player to use all their tiles wins.

This game consists of a serious of 4 educational games where children learn more about themselves and their friends.

1. Ordering
This is a cooperative ordering game where all cards are put face up on the table. An adult asks the children to find all cards belinging to one category (eg. all cards with something eatable)

2. Sorting
This is a cooperative game where all children try to categorize cards one by one.

3. Taking
All cards are put face up on the table. An adult reads a story. As soon as one of the pieces on the cards is mentioned in the story, the kids should take the corresponding card. The fastest kid with the most cards wins.

4. Remembering
7 cards are put face up on the table. The remaining ones are put in the box. An adult reads a story. As soon as one of the pieces on the cards which are in the box is mentioned in the story, the kid shouts out. If it's correct and the card is in the box, the child gets one of the cards on the table. If it's wrong and the piece is on the table, the child has to put one of his cards back on the table.  
The kid with the best memory and therefore the most cards wins.

The game contains 3 games about the magic tree, its fruit, a caterpillar, a blackbird and a cat.

1. Classifying
This is a cooperative game where the wooden fruit and the animals should be classified and inserted into the game board.

2. Simple Dice Game
The children are taking turns in rolling the dice. When the dice shows one of the fruits on the tree, the child gets this fruit. When all fruits are taken from the magic tree, the child with the most fruits wins.

3. Searching
All puzzle pieces are put face down on the table. The children are taking turns in revealing the pieces. The kid who is the first one in recognizing any differences to the board game and finds the correct place, gets this piece. The child with the most pieces wins.

A simple game with Disney's squirrels Chip ´n´ Dale where the first in the squirrels nest wins. But you have to get the number in the next square with the dice to move to that square.

The game is included in the comic book "Musse Pigg & C:o", No. 9, September 1983 (Musse Pigg is the swedish name for Mickey Mouse).

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Call to Arms – Into the Dark
description:
The ancient Tombs and Darkened Caves of Skyrim are home to many treasures - and many dangers. Dare you venture into the dark to claim them? Take on the foes and threats that the world of The Elder Scrolls throws at you, where ancient enemies and fierce creatures the outcome is all up to you.

Into The Dark provides the core rules for random dungeon generation, and includes modules for Nordic Tombs and Wilderness Caves. Future releases will feature additional settings, such as Bandit Camps and Dwemer Ruins.

17-page random delve designer system to allow you to procedurally generate multi-level dungeons.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

description:
The game is based on the classic game of chance (scissors, stone, paper): Scissor beats paper. Paper beats stone. Stone beats scissor.
Every player selects one of the symbols on his board. When all players are done, the privacy shields are removed. All players calculate their scores and the next turn starts. As soon as one player scores 10 points, he wins.

—user summary

This is a short game before going to bed. 
The starting player rolls the dice and moves the sandman accordingly and puts a star on all the fields he passes by. Now it's the next player's turn who rolls the dice again and puts stars on the board. 
When all stars are on the board, the next round begins: The starting player rolls the dice again, but this time he turns all the stars face down. When all stars are face down, the job of the sandman is done and the kids can go to sleep.
The kid, whose stars are the last face up, wins.

—user summary

Stop & Shop is a shopping game. 
The player puts one of his dice tokens face down and moves his meeple. If the meeple is in front of a shop, he can go into the shop and buy as much as he can. He can put all the stuff he bought on his shopping card. As soon as he has fulfilled all the requested items on the shopping card, he can turn the shopping card face down and put the goods back in the supply. The player who is th first one to finish his shopping cards wins.

—user summary

A unique set of 10 additional cards that will expand your set of Timeline: Poland cards with events related to the history of Kashubia. Prepared in two languages ​​(on the one hand, the event cards are described in Polish, on the other - in Kashubian) and decorated with unique graphics, they are an interesting variety and allow us to expand our knowledge even more.

The set was created in cooperation with the Kashubian-Pomeranian Association.


Wyjątkowy zestaw 10 kart dodatkowych, które rozszerzą Twój zestaw kart do gry Timeline: Polska o wydarzenia związane z historią Kaszub. Przygotowane w dwóch językach (z jednej strony karty wydarzenie opisane jest po polsku, z drugiej - po kaszubsku) i ozdobione unikalnymi grafikami stanowią ciekawe urozmaicenie i pozwalają jeszcze bardziej poszerzyć naszą wiedzę.Zestaw powstał we współpracy ze Zrzeszeniem Kaszubsko-Pomorskim.

A game based on the traditional Korean card game Go Stop, with some differences. The game uses a modernized, more colorful Hanafuda deck. Flower Battle differs from Go Stop in the following ways:
1. Although 4 players may begin, only 3 players may play the main game; there is a pre-round elimination procedure in which player who "dies" may sell his/her Gold cards (called Bright cards in Go Stop) for points.
2. Go Stop decks have 4 or 5 joker/wild cards; Flower Battle has 3 wild cards that are doublers and a blank card for you to customize.
3. Flower Battle has options for scoring such as Wild Boar Hunt (players put their scoring sticks in a box; the player with the boar card wins the sticks) and Clear the Ground (if you collect the last 2 cards on the table, you also receive a plain card from each opponent's collection).
4. Point values differ slightly; in Go Stop, collecting all 5 brights typically gives you 15 points. In Flower Battle, you receive 10 points.

The original version comes with a deck of cards and a pouch of color counting sticks for keeping score.

A cat themed card game that is as simple to play like UNO but you can perform fast-paced combos with smooth interactive gameplay.

Each player starts out with 8-10 cards depending on the amount of people playing. The goal is to get rid of your hand as fast as possible but if someone goes out that won't necessarily end the game. Drawing cards is not a punishment. If another player plays a counter active card against the player that went out then that player won't win and that will give someone the opportunity to either stop that player or steal the win from that player.

The unique factor of this game creates simple interaction during any player's turn where the game still flows forward without ever stalling or back tracking which brings an enjoyable experience for everyone.

The possibilities can be endless and unpredictable. Every card was developed to never start out with a bricked hand, which will bring the quality to the game where no one ever feels as if they're at a disadvantage to win.

—description from the publisher

Steal is a fast and furious new letter game of cunning, treachery and outright theft!  A hugely exciting game from New Zealand, with all letter tiles contained in a handy mask-shaped fabric bag, allowing it to be played almost anywhere. Steal is suitable for 2-8 players of all ages, easy to learn but more difficult to master.

Play proceeds by flipping tiles (every 2-3 seconds), with players calling out and forming three+ letter words from the exposed letters. As the game proceeds, the aim then is to steal words from your opponents by adding one or more letters. So pin might be stolen as pain, and stolen again as inapt, then pliant, implant, platinum, manipulate, planetarium, perambulating etc....! All the while other words are of course being formed and stolen.

The set contains ten coloured STEAL letters which double the score of the word, so the highest scores are always obtained by assembling/stealing long words containing a coloured letter.  Play is further enhanced by the mask (joker) tile which can be any letter you want (& can change as the word is stolen).

Flipping stops while words are being assembled (with the last assembler being next to flip) but stealing does not. So often a word is stolen whilst it is being formed with the addition of an already-exposed new letter! Frustrating and hilarious at the same time, bringing out the competitive streak of everyone who plays. Once all letters are flipped and no more words can be made or stolen...the scores are counted up to find the winner.

—description from the designer

A Malaysian-made card game that incorporates strategy and competitive gameplay. Players accumulate trophy cards by winning battles. This addictive and fun game is guaranteed to light up your day. (description at back of box)

The game consists of trophy cards which are worth point values, and duel cards, which have duel points and are used to compete for the trophy cards. At setup, a number of stacks of trophy cards are laid out to match the number of players. Players are dealt 6 duel cards.

During play, players take turns to play duel cards next to the trophy stacks. Duel cards have different strengths and abilities. Once a third duel card is played next to a trophy stack, that stack is evaluated and the player with the highest duel card strength wins the stack. The stack is then replenished.

Duel cards come in different suits, and each type of trophy stack only accepts specific suits.

The game ends when the trophy deck runs out. For a shorter game, play to a fixed number of points instead.

Pax Viking promo pack 1

5 all new rule tiles - During setup you may choose or draw one to use in the game

5 new Saga tiles - Add one, some or all to a game as you prefer.

boardgame: The Last Day of Rose: A Malign Age
description:
The Last Day of Rose: A Malign Age is a cooperative card game for 3-5 players. Players will take on the roles of friends and relatives of Rose, a girl with severe depression and suicidal thoughts. Players will need to work together to the best of their abilities to accompany and care for Rose through multiple scenarios, each with their own unique challenges.

On a player’s turn, he or she must play a card from his/her hand which he/she has drawn from his/her character’s unique deck. All the cards have two kinds of effects to choose: a positive one might let player help with Rose’s wellbeing or add a more powerful card to their decks etc. A negative one will harm Rose’s mental state or add a harmful card to a player’s deck etc. All cards from the players’ hands must be played (or discarded) in order to complete a round, yet each player can only resolve a limited number of positive effects depending on the efforts they can spend.

The game is won if Rose's life is saved.

During 1989, the company Smarties, which makes chocolate candy, released a free board game in Sweden. The game was called the "Smarties game" and was available in stores for free if you bought a package of Smarties. The game also appeared for free in some comic books. Smarties used chocolate candy as game pieces and the winner got to eat all the game pieces. It's a simple roll and move game. First in goal wins. Each player start with 5 Smarties, during the game you can win or lose Smarties. If you down to zero Smarties you out of the game.

In the comic book "Kalle Anka & C: o", no. 21, 21 May 1984 was a simple roll and move game with circus theme (Kalle Anka is the Swedish name for Donald Duck). Cirkusspelet is a simple roll and move game where the first in goal wins. If there is a square with a number, read the instructions and follow them.

Flip a subject card in the pile, then flip a letter card from the other pile. Start the timer at 3 minutes. Everyone writes down words they can think of about the subject and the letter to collect points. The one with the most points after 5 rounds wins. The game was included in the comic magazin "Kalle Anka & C:0", nr. 8, 18 February 1991 (Kalle Anka is the swedish name for Donald Duck).

A game that was included in "Kalle Anka & C: o", no. 34, 22 August 1988 (Kalle Anka is the Swedish name for Donald Duck).

In Jakten på spökplumpen you have a round disc with white dots on it with a bowl of water next to it. Each round, dip a finger in the water bowl and rub on a white dot so you see the clue. Then everyone guesses where "Spökplumpen" is hiding. The one who guesses correctly wins and if no one guesses correctly, it is the next player's turn. This person dips a finger in the water bowl and rubs on a white dot to see the clue, then everyone guesses where "Spökplumpen" is hiding. This is how the game goes on until someone has guessed "Spökplumpen's" hiding place. Whoever guesses correctly wins.

A simple roll and move game with a Christmas theme that was included in "Kalle Anka & C: o", no. 52/53, 21 December 1992 (Kalle Anka is the Swedish name for Donald Duck). Each turn roll the die and move. If you end up in a red square with a number, read the instruction for that square and follow it. First in goal wins.

In Spöktåget (the Ghost Train), it is important to figure out which Disney passenger is in which ghost carriage and conquer as many passenger pieces as possible.

In turn, you choose a piece with a Disney passenger, put it in a ghost carriage and push it into one of the three ghost tunnels. When there are three ghost wagons in each tunnel, the game starts. Before the next turn player pushes a new ghost carriage into a tunnel, the player must guess which Disney passenger is in the ghost carriage coming out of that particular tunnel at the other end first. If the player guesses correctly, he gets the passenger piece and is allowed to continue. If the guess is wrong, the piece is put back in the pile of Disney passengers and the next player takes over. So the game continues. Guess which passenger comes out and pushes a new carriage into that tunnel. Lose and win pieces. The game is over when all ghost carriages are empty. The one with the most pieces wins.

A game with a ghost theme that was included in "Kalle Anka & C: o", no. 45, 8 November 1993 (Kalle Anka is the Swedish name for Donald Duck).

boardgame: サバンナラッシュ (Savannah Rush)
description:
サバンナラッシュ (Savannah Rush) is a placement game from Japan.

Will it be the elephant or the lion that rules the savannah? The giraffe? A monkey? Rats?
You are one of the gods of the savannah. Your job is to bring the animals to live there. Place your favourite animals in the best places!

This is a battle game where you have to place the animals on the map. The game is played with five different types of animals, and the aim is to place them in such a way that the two animals you are in charge of will have an advantage and you will score more victory points. Use the other animals to your advantage.

サバンナを制するのはゾウかライオンか？それともキリン？サル？ネズミ？
あなたはサバンナを司る神々のひとり。 動物たちをサバンナに住まわせるのが仕事。 贔屓の動物をより良い土地に住まわせよう！

マップ上に動物コマを配置して対戦する陣取りゲームです。
５種類の動物の特徴を活かして、自分が担当する2種類の動物が有利になるように巧みに配置し、より多く勝利点を獲得することを目指します。担当以外の動物もうまく利用しましょう。
publisher's summary
publishers: iop games (イオピーゲームズ)

description:
Starmine Rummy Pocket is a card game from Japan for one or two players. The players are trying to set off fireworks all at once until the finale with a Starmine. This is a firework trick which is made by arranging the sections.

Starmine Rummy Pocket is part of a series of pocket games for 1-2 players, produced by the publisher JUGAME STUDIO (ジュゲムスタジオ). Currently, there is no plan to produce a larger game of Starmine Rummy.
user summary

A lot of fireworks are shot off incessantly. The sound of the whistle and the smoke and the light and the roar which roars far above the expectation.

A starmine is a firework display that features a series of fireworks that are connected together. As a pyrotechnist, you aim to create the most memorable starmine of all.

The starmine consists of three columns (opening, main and finale). Place the firework card combinations in all three rows to complete the starmine. It is important to take your time to collect all the cards in order to create the best starmine, but the highlight of the starmine is the continuous firing! You can't just sit back and collect fireworks, because your rivals will be setting them off one after another. Try to complete your fireworks before the deck runs out and all the fireworks are set up.

フィナーレまで一気に花火を打ち上げろ。 役を揃えてつくる仕掛け花火。

多数の花火を絶え間なく打ち上げる。
鳴り続ける笛の音と立ち込める煙。そして予想よりもはるか上空に轟く光と轟音。

スターマインは、複数の花火を連結して打ち上げる、連射が特徴の仕掛け花火です。
あなたは花火職人として、誰よりも心に残るスターマインの打ち上げを目指します。

スターマインは、３つの列（オープニング、メイン、フィナーレ）で構成されます。
３つ全ての列に花火カードを組み合わせを配置してスターマインを完成させましょう。

より良いスターマインを完成させるには、じっくりカードを集めることが大切ですが、スターマインは連射が見どころ！ライバルも次々に花火を設置していくため、ゆっくりと花火を集めてばかりはいられません。 山札が尽き、全ての花火が設置されてしまう前に花火を完成させ、ライバルよりも印象的なスターマインを打ち上げましょう。
publisher's summary
publishers: JUGAME STUDIO (ジュゲムスタジオ)

description:
Galaxis: Stellar Frontiers is a scenario-based competitive sci-fi strategy game for 2-4 players where each scenario brings you into one of the many battles going on in a galactic-wide war. During the game you will build a thriving economy to fuel the battle while expanding your area of control with fleets of ships.

Gameplay:
In Galaxis: Stellar Frontiers, you assume the role of one of four unique galactic factions fighting for resources and territory. At the beginning of every turn, you collect resources from fields you control which you can spend on constructing Buildings on Planets, buying Ships, and researching Ship Upgrades to improve them. By moving your Ships into new fields, you expand your reach to get more resources and trigger Events which help you grow even faster. By growing your economy, you’re able to unlock new building tiers to bring the war the next level. Each faction follows their very own goals in the scenarios to gain Influence, and the one with the most influence points at the end wins the battle and one day, possibly the war.

Welcome to Castle, the game of fighting creatures and obstacles.

Start off with 40 points and see who will be the winner in the end! The first one who loses all their points, loses the game!

Players draft 1-2 cards to battle and inflict damage on each other. Object cards can increase or decrease a player's stamina. The first person to lose all 40 of their stamina points, loses the game.

—description from the designer

language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
WELCOME TO PLANET MARS!
It's the year 2090. Your spaceship has just landed on Mars. Captain Capability, Captain Courage, Captain Creativity, and Captain Compassion need to be able to train themselves on Mars to collect supplies in order to help them settle. But here's the twist - to help the astronauts survive, you are the Master Mind. Your astronaut needs to collect all the supplies and essentials to survive on Mars and you can use your predictive instincts to do so.

Building on the core game mechanics of Coder Mindz, this game adds set collection and learning about the planet Mara and space exploration to combine both AI and astronomy education. The idea is easy - play a board game, help the astronauts explore Mars, visit various places, collect necessary supplies, and a lot more. All in a fun and exciting Martian Adventure!

Learning Coding and AI concepts help you connect with real coding and AI.

Althing is the first expansion to the game Althing with 22 new cards. It includes a set of modules, complementary or independent, that will renew your games and the way you approach Althing, even in solo mode.

- Thorfinn legend (solo mode)
- Coward troll
- An epic under the sign of Hela : Add Hela cards under the step cards to add special effects to those steps
- Stridur and Bolvun : this module adds 2 new step cards with special effects.
- Loki, Tyr, Idunn are new player cards with new effects
- Jotünn : new vikking card with more options to trick your opponents

If you want to win, you still have to speed up your opponents in the race for the throne... But with this expansion you have new challenges to face, and new options to win!

Promo card that can be obtained during boardgames events. 
It can replace the basic Thing / Althing card.

It shows the coronation a little closer, but requires different conditions to be reached.

Thing - promo side : requires 3 gold, 3 wood, 2 bones
Althing - promo side : requires 3 gold, 3 wood, 4 bones

Promo card that can be obtained as a goodies when buying the game or picking up the game pre ordered in a game shop.
It can replace the basic Thing / Althing card, the last step of the race.

It represents a game shop in a viking village, and requires the same conditions than the Althing: promo card to be reached.

Thing - Butik side : requires 3 gold, 3 wood, 2 bones
Althing - Butik side : requires 3 gold, 3 wood, 4 bones

Ancient sorcery, dark omens and endless curses — you will become one of the adepts of the dark magic of the Egyptians or a follower of the ancient art of the Badly Dead Druids or an arcane sorcerer of Vudù rituals! Prepare yourselves for the biggest dispute of black magic ever. The Vudù tournament has just begun!

In Vudù, the players act as the most wicked and malicious sorcerers of all time, and they want to use their curses and black magic to make the lives of their enemies impossible to prevail over them!

But wait... many expansions came after Vudù... each one more cruel than the previous. So, to collect them all, you don't need a BIG box, you need a BAD box.

It contains the base box and these expansions:
Double Trouble
Ninja VS Pigmei
Zombi VS Barbari
Mostri VS Alieni

boardgame: Symposium: A Game About Asking Questions
description:
Symposium is a Descended From The Queen storytelling card game for 3-8 players, simulating a philosophical dinner party (or "symposium") in the classical style of the ancient Greek world and based on Plato's work of the same name, with some modifications to make it more accessible to a modern audience.

In the game you play guests at dinner party filled with interesting people: philosophers, poets, doctors, playwrights, and many others. All the best things in life are there: good food, wine, beautiful servants, and interesting conversation. You are a guest, and after you get comfortable your host makes a request of you: make a speech in praise of love. Every guest gives a speech, the contents of which are directed by a different metaphysical perspective. If your speech is the best one, the host will award you the relevant card.

After this initial round of play the role of host shifts, new perspectives are selected, and a new Question card is drawn by a new host; introducing the players to a variety of interesting questions. (What is is to be wise? What is courage? Why do I still love them?) Fresh speeches are given, and the card is awarded. Play continues in this manner until the You are drunk card is selected, at which point the game is over. The guest with the most cards at the end of the game not only wins, but is also the wisest.

From the publisher's website:

Disgruntled Decks is a card game for military service members and grizzled Veterans who enjoy laughing together about their time in the service. With a unique blend of descriptive language and military themes, Disgruntled Decks gives one more reason for service members and Veterans to talk about their shared experiences while having fun in the process. 
 We strongly believe that humor is healing, and some Veterans still have a good amount of healing left to do. We hope to provide some of that humor with Disgruntled Decks.
 Disgruntled Decks can be played by itself or used as an unofficial military Cards Against Humanity ® third party expansion.
 
Disgruntled Decks
The Original Card Game For Veterans.™
With the 2021: Extension Pack +, players will encounter 54 exclusive social media cards that give players the opportunity to keep their game current with events inspired by the year 2021.

boardgame: Do You Know Me? Expansion Pack
description: It’s about to get personal with the Do You Know Me? Expansion Pack! Find out how well you really know your friends with 180 new hilarious and oh-no-you-didn't questions to add to the Do You Know Me? Core game.

The second set of accessories in the Catan Goodies line (the first Catan Goodies set consisted of the Catanimals) features a set of three plush robber figures. These three figures are plush representations of “Teuber’s Robbers”: three characters named Chief, Lucky, and Bert, who together are the personifications of the robber in Catan. The robbers were originally envisioned to be hapless and ultimately harmless characters who are constantly being vilified by the people settling on Catan.

As with the Catanimals, the rules of a scenario using these plush figures was made available, although in this case the rules were only made available in the German language from the Catan website.

Rules: 
In a 3-4 player game, only two of the plush robbers are used.  In a 5-6 player game, all three of the plush robbers are used.

At the start of the game, none of the plush robbers are under the control of any player. A player may take control of one of the plush robbers if they have one of their hand cards stolen as a result of robber movement.

Once all of the plush robbers have been claimed, whenever a player has one of their hand cards stolen as a result of robber movement, the player having their card stolen may take a plush robber from the player stealing the card. If the player stealing the card does not have a plush robber, they may take a plush robber from any other player.

A player with control of one plush robber has their hand limit increased by three cards (they do not lose resources unless they have more than 10 cards, instead of the usual 7).
