The robber may not be moved to any hex where there is an adjacent settlement or city belonging to a player with control of two plush robbers, unless that player is in sole possession of the most victory points.

Turn-based strategy about running a space agency and exploring the solar system in the new space race. Each player takes on the role of one of the 5 space agencies, and in his turn can perform two of the 9 possible actions, such as developing the agency on the technology tree, launching a rocket, conducting research, placing buildings, or sabotage.

The game board is a model of the Solar System, consisting of 5 movable rings. On each ring there are successive objects of the Solar System, and the rings are divided into such numbers of squares, that rotating each of the rings by 1 square per round, the speed of planets and rockets on their orbits around the Sun is maintained according to Kepler's law!

Once all players have made their move, we rotate each ring and the positions of the planets and rockets change. This means that players, in order to optimize their missions and not waste valuable time, must learn to predict the future position of planets and start planning rocket launches in advance, just like real space agencies.

Development tree is divided into three branches: space tourism, base construction and sabotage. Players have to manage their budget on an ongoing basis, so they don't run out of funds at the crucial moment of the space race. In addition, each of the space agencies: USA, Russia, Europe, China and Japan, has its own unique bonus.

—description from the designer

Somethingly is the new, side-splittingly silly, party game from Toffee Hammer. Players perform Do This! actions in a ‘Somethingly’ way for other players to guess: Dance like you’re at a disco… suspiciously! Mime drinking tea… angrily! Shake hands with someone… mysteriously! Anyone who likes charades, will love Somethingly!

Included in the box are suggestions for three different types of gameplay: ‘Inclusively’ for families and mixed ability groups; ‘Rapidly’ for fast-paced team play; and ‘Competitively’ for individual players.

With 250 Somethingly cards and 150 Do This! Cards there are over 35,000 different possible combinations – making this a game that will last a lifetime. Cards are colour coded for difficulty
so it is easy to adapt for different groups.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Dead or Alive Game Supplement
description:
Travelling the desert roads and canyon trails is always a risky proposition with outlaws on the prowl. Whether bitter with mutation, risen from a shallow grave, or just looking to make an easy score, the highwaymen around Brimstone are ruthless in their greed for Dark Stone and gold.

This 15-card supplement adds a collection of all new Canyons Encounters and Canyons Artifacts for The Canyons World (found in the Shadows of Brimstone: Other Worlds – Blasted Wastes), as well as new Darkness cards. These cards are themed for finding items and encounters with lawless outlaws and highwaymen travelling the canyon valleys of the Southwest.

CARD LIST

     Outlaw Treasure Map ×2
     Highwayman's Holster
     Highwayman's Shotgun
     Gulch Rifle
     Bandit's Duster
     Life on the Run x2
     Nothing Personal
     Dark Stone Rage
     Stampede!
     "Not Yer Business"
     Outlaw Camp
     "Help! Help!"
     Shallow Graves

Os 3 Porquinhos ("The 3 Little Pigs") is the first volume in a series of fun games based on ancient tales passed down from generation to generation, later grouped into books by writers who became famous for such feats and came to be known as "Fairy Tales".
Here players have to band together to stop the wolf from taking down their houses. For that, they must think strategically and together, as it is a cooperative game.
Build the three houses as quickly as possible by putting an end to the wolf's plans will make the players win!

—description from the designer
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Three Little Pigs

description:
Christmas Rush is a family board game themed around Christmas, but playable all year round.

The object of the game is to find and wrap a number of Christmas presents (dealt randomly at the start of the game) and get them back to the Christmas tree. The presents are represented by tiles shuffled and located in pre-set areas on the playing board facedown.

Players move around the board using 2 dice and choose one dice for the number of moves they can make, and the second dice for the number of tiles they can look at once they have completed their move. Presents can be stolen by other players if they are not wrapped, and to wrap you need wrapping paper (collected from key spaces as you move) and then to roll a "Christmas tree" on the dice. Once all presents have been found and wrapped, play moves to the Holly Highway where the race is on back to the tree. Snowflake cards will help or hinder, and naughty elves will send you backwards.

—description from the publisher

Field Report 2765: AFFS describes the state of House Davion's Federated Suns just before the events described in Historical: Liberation of Terra (Part 1), including the overall military and logistical condition of one of the Great Houses that would eventually survive the collapse of the Star League and face a new age of endless war.

Field Report 2765: AFFS closely follows the format of Field Report: AFFS but with two new WarShip designs also included along with record sheets for each.

2765 Field Report: CCAF describes the state of House Liao's Capellan Confederation just before the events described in Historical: Liberation of Terra (Part 1), including the overall military and logistical condition of one of the Great Houses that would eventually survive the collapse of the Star League and face a new age of endless war.

Field Report 2765: CCAF closely follows the format of Field Report: CCAF but with two new WarShip designs also included along with record sheets for each.

Field Report 2765: DCMS describes the state of House Kurita's Draconis Combine just before the events described in Historical: Liberation of Terra (Part 1), including the overall military and logistical condition of one of the Great Houses that would eventually survive the collapse of the Star League and face a new age of endless war.

Field Report 2765: DCMS closely follows the format of Field Report: DCMS, but with two new WarShip designs with the respective record sheets.

Mind on the Movies is about creating movie titles and letting your friends pitch the ideas. You are either the writer that must create the movie idea using word cards or you become the producer giving the thumbs up or down for movie ideas. It has more customization and imagination than any popular voting game, but still very easy to pick up.

All One80 Games proceeds go to Campus One80 Ministries, a Christian leadership and outreach mission that serves the colleges of the US.

—description from the publisher

It's an abstract game similiar to Round Chess. The game is played in turns on a Circular board with one player playing as red and the other as green. Players move their pieces between spaces according to the rules of the piece being moved. There are seven different pieces. The goal is to be the first player to bring your Cardinal to Vatikan (center of the board) through the opponents half or to remove the opponents Cardinal.

Kids are waiting for board game elements. Will you help deliver them to their homes? Dice by trains, pawns by truck or maybe cards by ship? You decide what to load on the available vehicles. The person who does it most efficiently will be the winner.

The game combines fun with the development of a child's math skills: it teaches counting up to 20, orders of magnitude and simple fractions. The players' task is to fill their vehicles with game elements and deliver them to homes from all over the world.

boardgame: Jurassic Park: Bid to Win Trivia
description: Grab your Visitor’s pass and prove how “clever” you are by going head-to-head with other prehistoric adventure lovers with the officially licensed Jurassic Park: Bid to Win Trivia Game! Answer 600 questions that span Jurassic Park, The Lost World, and Jurassic Park 3 trilogy.

The Last Tank is a solo game simulating tank combat in the period of the invasion of the German army on the Moscow front in 1941.

Objective: The tank must survive until the end of the tenth turn. When it is destroyed, the game will be over.

To overcome the enemies you need your ingenuity as the captain of the tank, you will make decisions that are put into practice on the battlefield, this is achieved by defending your position and destroying all enemies, using both the cannon and the submachine guns. your tank.
 —description from the designer

You play as a squirrel and collect as many nuts as you can. Watch out, there are cars to hit you that make you lose your nuts. Plus, you can steal nuts, roll the dice and get more nuts, also skip players. The goal is to get a total of 60 nuts before anybody else.

It is an word game in a little mint tin box with an D20 letter dice and 50 sheets of paper with questions. It's simple to play and fits in every Pocket.
Short Shuffle all papers, lay it to the "?"-site. At your turn,flip the top card and role the dice. If you find the correct answer, get the card as Point. Then is the next player turn. The player with the most points wins.

Questions in all editions are in 5 languages.  The mint tin case and rules are language specific.
Rude and crude expansion pack for Gobby. Contains 50 phrase start and finish cards giving you 2500 new possible phrase combinations bringing the total up to 10,000 when added to the base game.

Two knights challenge each other in a tournament. A bidding system allow to win each turn. Winning two turns in a row means that the opponent is unsaddled: a knight on saddle has bonus on his bets against an unsaddled one. The player winning three turns more than the opponent wins the game.

—description from the designer

Published on History Kids, italian edition, issue #10.

FUTMES is The Most Ruthless Fútbol Board Game Ever. In this game you can live the experience of a soccer match full of unexpected events.

Your team will consist of one Coach, one Captain, three Football Players and one Stadium where you will play your home matches.
Use your cards and your strategic skills to defeat other teams and win the FUTMES LEAGUE.
Always pay attention to the skills of your team members, they could be the difference between a win and a defeat

In FUTMES the resources are called EVENT CARDS and are useful for controlling the unexpected events of the game.

Anything can happen during a FUTMES match flipping the CHANCE CARDS and find out if your goal was successful.

boardgame: Now or Never: Crafting Arzium Promo Set
description:
Two promo cards that were a physical reward for backing the Crafting Arzium film campaign by Zoom Out Media, which covered the design and development of Now or Never.

The additional cards are: Lava Flow (Shell Armor) and Junk Yard (Fate Magnet)

WISP is the new trick-taking card game that is recognisably different from some established trick-taking games. The core differences include the following aspects:


     You can compete as a team or individually.
     There are no "normal" cards that are hierarchically equal. So you can get into trick-taking incredibly quickly.
     It will always be dealt the same number of cards.
     The announcement of a trick to be taken is an inferior element, which exists only in the team variant.


Welcome to the world of mythical creatures and have fun dueling!

Field Report 2765: FWLM describes the state of House Marik's Free Worlds League just before the events described in Historical: Liberation of Terra (Part 1), including the overall military and logistical condition of one of the Great Houses that would eventually survive the collapse of the Star League and face a new age of endless war.

Field Report 2765: FWLM closely follows the format of Field Report: FWLM but with one new WarShip design also included along with record sheets for each of its variants.

Field Report 2765: LCAF describes the state of House Steiner's Lyran Commonwealth just before the events described in Historical: Liberation of Terra (Part 1), including the overall military and logistical condition of one of the Great Houses that would eventually survive the collapse of the Star League and face a new age of endless war.

Field Report 2765: LCAF closely follows the format of Field Report: LAAF but with one new WarShip design also included along with record sheets for each of its variants.

Field Report 2765: Periphery describes the state of the four great Periphery States just before the events described in Historical: Liberation of Terra (Part 1), including the overall military and logistical condition of the fringe powers--including the infamous Rim Worlds Republic, whose House Amaris would bring about the collapse of the Star League that would eventually plunge the rest of the Inner Sphere into a new age of endless war.

Tusky Joe's Snorkel Adventure is a 2-4 player light-medium family-friendly game about diving for treasures in the sea. The game features a nice blend of dice rolling, engine building and push your luck.

Over 7 rounds, players will be heading out on Tusky Joe's boat, strapping on their snorkels and diving gear and looking for lost treasures in the sea. On your turn, roll your dice and assign them to salvage cards that you'd like to recover. Beautiful treasures are there to be found and worth lots of delicious points. Don't like what you see? Move through the water using the brilliant side-scrolling sea bed.

Trade in your salvage at the shop to buy new gear which will help you in the water to achieve better dice results. Choose your strategy and tailor your equipment. Gain the most points by the end of the game and you're the winner!

—description from the publisher

To be the best at Roaarr Challenge, you will have to speak up and make sure you’re the first to be heard! Budding impressionists will need to fine-tune their best animal calls to win! This hilarious game for children from 4 years old is a game of speed where observation skills and concentration are key to success. Each player takes on the role of an animal: they can only express themselves through their cries. The aim of the game? Win the most cards by being the fastest to scream out whenever 2 identical cards appear on the table! Things are about to get loud!

boardgame: TKG ARENA: A Rift of Portals
description:
Establishing a portal to another world is a major milestone by any yardstick. The Constellars are often tasked with the conveyance of armies across realms during wars and this ability is crucial to their role.

Setting up and preparing for the ritual is the easy part, the real challenge comes in maintaining a delicate balance of the cosmic forces as one disrupts the fabric of space and time so that others may arrive at their intended destination safely.

TKG ARENA: A Rift of Portals is a game for 1 player.

Components required for this game:

WORLD TILES:    48
OINKS:    15 from each element for each player (Default)

Winning Conditions

The objective of the game is to stabilize the portal once it is set up and this is represented by fulfilling a number of conditions.

1.    The number of Oinks in the Inner Gate must be equal or more than the Oinks placed in the Outer Void.
2.    The number of elemental insignias placed on any linear sides of the Boundary must be equal.
3.    The elemental insignias on each tile must be matched by a placed Oink representing the same element.

Ending Condition

The game ends when all tiles are played and all unmatched Oinks on any tile on the Boundary are moved to the Outer Void. The player loses when the winning conditions are not achieved when the game ends.

Setup

A player starts the game by drawing randomly from a stack of world tiles to be placed in a 3 x 3 grid manner as below.

X        A    B    C        O
        D    I    E        
        F    G    H

1.    Shuffle the 48 world tiles into a stack. World tiles are referred to as tiles.

2.    Draw 3 tiles from the stack. From these 3 tiles, flip 1 over to the grid side to form the Outer Void (This is represented by the faced down tile O in the diagram), 1 to form the Inner Gate (This is represented by the faced down tile I in the diagram).

3.    Gain the Oinks matched by the elemental insignias of the last remaining tile and discard it, an area such as X can be designated for discarded tiles in the diagram.

All world tiles built are placed faced up with their elemental insignias visible. Tiles A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H surrounding tile I forms the Boundary. To fulfill winning condition 2,  the sum of the number of elemental insignias on the Tiles A, B, C; Tiles C,E, H; Tiles F,G, H and Tiles A, D, F are equal.

On each turn

Draw 3 tiles from the stack and the player must perform ONE of each of the following actions in the following order:

1.    Gain - Play a tile, gain the Oinks indicated on the elemental insignias and discard it. Put the Oinks gained in the Inner Gate.

2.    Build - Build a tile, if it is the first tile, place it adjacent to the Inner Gate, if it is not, it must be placed adjacent to another tile that has been built that bears at least one of its own elemental insignias. If the intended location is adjacent to two or more world tiles, then all adjacent world tiles must bear at least one matching elemental insignia between them.

If you are unable to or do not wish to build for any reason, gain the Oinks indicated on any one of the remaining tiles that has not been played and put them into the Outer Void. You can also replace any tile built with any tile in your hand but all Oinks placed on the built tile will be placed in the Outer Void.

3.    Move - Play a tile, you can move Oinks indicated on the elemental insignias of this tile from the Inner Gate to any built tile, or to move any Oink from a built tile to an adjacent built tile. If you are unable to or do not wish to move any of the Oinks indicated for any reason, gain the Oinks instead and put them into the Outer Void.

The player cannot skip any action and each action must be fulfilled in totality. E.g.  During a Move action, a tile with 3 Fire Insignias has been chosen but the player has only 1 Fire Oink in the Inner Gate. You may NOT move the existing 1 Fire Oink to any other tile, gain 2 Fire Oinks and then placed them in the Outer Void. 3 Fire Oinks should be gained and placed in the Outer Void.

Scoring

For each Oink placed in a tile that bears the same insignia, gain 1 Victory Point. The player with the most Victory Points wins the final victory if multiple players are competing. Ties are broken by the difference in Oinks between their Outer Void and Inner Gate. The player with the greater difference wins. If the differences are the same, the players shared a joint victory.

Variant

The game can be scaled up with a BOUNDARY with sides featuring more tiles. (E.g 4 or 5). The rest of the rules remain unchanged. In this case, players can choose to leave the space representing the Inner Gate empty instead of a faced down tile I. Players can choose to use other appropriate counters to represent Oinks during play.

Neimhaim is a 1 to 7 player cooperative game based on the popular saga of Nordic fantasy novels by Aranzazu Serrano Lorenzo. Players control brave heroes who must unite the peoples of the Forbidden Peninsula - the brave Kranyal warriors and the peaceful Djendel priests - before winter falls upon them. The games have an average duration of 45 minutes.

The objective of the game is to drive out the invaders and restore the Alliance in all villages. If you succeed... congratulations! You have all won!

Neimhaim is a fantasy literature saga with a Nordic setting, an epic saga enriched with the force of Viking myths and the mystery of Celtic culture. A unique and original universe endowed with an extensive ‘world building’ that includes illustrations, original designs and maps. Recommended for youth and adult audiences.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Carcassonne: The Signposts is a 12-tile expansion that can be mixed with the Carcassonne base game.

Each tile features, among other things, one or more road segments and two of three possible signposts, with the signposts indicating a left turn, a right turn, or a straight path. When a road that contains a signpost is scored, for each signpost you look at the next tile adjacent to the signpost, and if the road segment on that tile matches the signpost, then the signpost scores. For example, if the signpost shows a right turn, and the adjacent tile features a right turn (regardless of any other road segments), then the signpost scores.

If you score only one type of signpost, then each such signpost is worth 1 point; if you score two types, then they're each worth 2 points; and if you score three types, they're each worth 1 billion, I mean, 3 points.

At game's end, each signpost pointing in the correct direction on an unfinished road is worth 1 point.

King of 12 - Tournament Promo
King of 12 - Turnierspiel Promo
It is possible to play with up to 16 players.
Multiple games are needed. 
You play in different groups like you do in a normal game. Each winner within his or her group will the compete with the winner of the other group.

In Civilia, players place down overlapping cards to make groups of symbols and expand their civilization. The player that builds the largest group of a symbol becomes World Leader in that sector.

Exploit your superiority by planning and achieving to score points before your opponents take back the advantage! Score final points after the last card is drawn and the player with the most points wins.

Threees! is an abstract game with 81 tiles. There are 3 sets of 27 tiles. Each tile in a set has one of 3 different shapes, 3 different colours and 3 different quantities (number of shapes). The tiles are played directly on to a flat surface or table. There is an initial setup round to find the starting player and place some starting tiles on the table. No player scores in this setup round. All players then takes 3 tiles at random from the draw pile (or bag).

On their turn a player can either play their tiles or exchange them. To play, strategically place 1-3 tiles in a line adjacent to tiles already on the table, so making and/or extending line(s) with the existing tiles. The maximum number of tiles that can be in a line is 9.

Score 1 point for every tile in each line made or extended, so if a tile is in two lines it is counted twice. If the player has placed all 3 tiles, they receive a bonus of 3 points. The player then replenishes their hand up to three tiles. If the player had placed the ninth and last tile in a line (a "Niner") they get an extra go. If the player goes out (no tiles remaining in hand or in the draw pile), then they get a bonus of 3 points, play continues until every tile has been played.

The game ends when the draw pile is empty and every player has played all their tiles. The player with the highest score wins.

boardgame: C*NT: The Outrageous Four Letter Word Game
description:
C*NT is a four letter swear word dice game where the last player out is the C*NT! There are 4 lettered dice, each die has a wild card '*' on it. The included swear list score pad has 4 columns of 10 offensive four letter words, one column for each player. The words further down the list are more difficult to throw (statistically), C*NT at the bottom, being the hardest to throw.

In turns, each player bids to get control of the dice or passes. A bid consists of a number of throws and a swear word from the swear list. Each subsequent bid must be a fewer number of throws and/or a harder word to throw than the previous bid. When everyone else passes the last bidder wins control of the dice, they are now the Tosser.

The Tosser takes the dice and tries to throw the bid swear word within the stated number of tosses. If the word is thrown then the Tosser can cross off that word (or the first remaining word above it) from their swear list. If they have crossed off all their words, they go out, otherwise they start the next bidding round. If they did not throw their swear word, all the other players cross off their highest remaining swear word above and including the bid word, then the player to the left starts the next bidding round.

If at any time four asterisks are thrown then all the other player go out immediately. The last player "out" is a C*NT, everyone points at the C*NT and shouts "C*NT". The C*NT buys the next round of drinks, etc.

Ascet is a minimalist playing card game with roleplaying overtones that encourages diplomatic, mnemonic and maths skills with the use of simple mechanics that use cards, a d8 and a d10 dice (included in the deck as a card to cut, fold & paste). Inspired in the popular medieval theme of the ascetic monk that withdrew from society in search for enlightenment which has caught the imagination of master painters like Hieronymus Bosch, Salvador Dali, Albrecht Dürer, Max Ernst and Cezanne to name a few. Players will take turns to interpret an anchorite while the rest of the players take the role of demons seeking to lure him with 
Gameplay:

Each player will have 4 virtues: Compassion, Humility, Faith and Temperance. The Virtues have numerical values between 1 and 10. Additionally, players have in their hand temptation cards corresponding to the 4 virtues.

Players take turns playing their Ascet while the rest play the demons. Each demon presents a temptation card to the Ascet, and the Ascet chooses one of the presented temptations to overcome. They roll an 8 sided die for minor temptations and a 10 sided die for major temptations. If the result is equal or lower than the corresponding virtue, the Ascet overcomes the temptation and ascends one step in the Stairway to Enlightenment. Otherwise, the demon who proposed the temptation receives an additional card. Afterwards, all demons receive one card, regardless of success or failure.

if the roll was a 9, regardless of success or failure, the Ascet gets a Revalation, which may allow them to increase one of the virtues by 1.

The first player to ascend 5 steps on the Stairway to Enlightenment wins.

Push n Push is an abstract board game that combines elements of strategy and push-your-luck where players push each other's pawns to get rid of other players' pawns by pushing them out of the game board area.

The game is played in a way where players can move their pawns a distance from the tile that is determined from the number of dice they have rolled, player pieces can only move in a straight line (forward or backward) according to the direction of the arrow on their pawn (unless their pawns are pushed by another pawn). the player can push the opponent's pawn out of the game board area, the direction of the arrow of the pawn being pushed does not have to be the same as the pawn pushing it, a pawn can push more than one pawn. Players cannot push their own pawns out of the game board area.

The goal of the game is to push 9 (2 players) or 6 (4 players) opponent pawns off the game board area.

In the Crypto Card Game - NFT, we play the role of collectors of digital works of art and pictures from famous collections who are looking for the interesting NFTs for themselves to buy.

The place of competition for players in the game is the NTFs market and the players' task during the game is to buy this type of tokens from other players. The game is played according to the following rules.

All players reveal their own card at the same time and look for the same symbols and pictures on the cards with the rest of the players. The player who finds an identical picture on one of his opponents calls it loudly and takes its card back to his pile of cards.
Each picture on the game cards is original and represented in the real and virtual world of the NFT. To begin with, each player should recognize and correctly name all NFTs in the game. During the game, the names of the pictures can be given in full or shortened names.

The game ends as soon as one player collects all his opponents' cards and becomes the winner.

The Power of the Word is a board game where you can learn stories about the Lord Jesus and spend a great time with your family or friends.

The game is introduced by a story about people who are looking for treasures. They found the chest with fragments of priceless woven paintings depicting scenes from the Gospel. Now the treasure is in the hands of the players. They have beautiful, multi-colored cards with scenes from the Gospel in front of them, as well as pictures and words from the Bible.

On their turn, they decide which dice to roll and which cards to draw. They should do this in such a way as to create the highest scoring collections.

The winner is the one who scores the most points and reaches the finish line with his pawn the fastest.


Moc Słowa to gra planszowa, dzięki której można poznać opowieści o Panu Jezusie i spędzić doskonały czas z rodziną lub przyjaciółmi.Do gry wprowadza historia o ludziach, którzy poszukują skarbów. Odnaleźli skrzynię, a w niej fragmenty bezcennych, tkanych obrazów, ukazujących sceny z Ewangelii.
Teraz skarb znajduje się w rękach graczy. Mają przed sobą piękne, wielobarwne karty ze scenami z Ewangelii, a także obrazki i słowa z Biblii. W swojej turze decydują, którą kostką rzucić i które dobrać karty. Powinni to zrobić w taki sposób, by utworzyć najwyżej punktowane kolekcje.

Wygra ten, kto zdobędzie najwięcej punktów i najszybciej dotrze swoim pionkiem do mety.

Gra rozwija spostrzegawczość, myślenie strategiczne i przede wszystkim wiedzę biblijną.
Poznaj Biblię. Odkrywaj Moc Biblijnego Słowa.

Challenge: The earth is our Planet A. It is worth the effort to protect it, because there is no Planet B. Rising CO2 emission and a plastic torrent overwhelm our sensitive ecosystem; climate change and species extinction arise with all consequent changes. Strive against that and save our blue planet. In the process, ecological calamities and forever outdated political parties get in your way. Do not give in. We save the earth step by step - as long as everyone contributes.

Solution: By the use of 105 different cards about sustainability , the players face up with all kinds of environmentally friendly everyday items and ideas. With realistic tasks and practical ideas, the game throws a bridge into the real life and is thought-provoking. Playfully learning with a lot of fun plus making the world a bit better.

Description of the game: All players have to fulfil a secret task: either to collect certain thematic cards in their hand or to help the team Planet A get a collection in the middle of the table. Whoever accomplishes three tasks at first wins. Ecological calamity cards, environmental hero cards and environmental activist cards whirl the game and encourage discussions on sustainability.

—description from the publisher (translated)

A children's storytelling game, where players are presented with a basic narrative arc which informs them of what they need to accomplish. Each player has his own character with which he explores the map by opening new hexagon tiles and finding different items. These serve as inspiration and opportunity to tell a story and progress thorugh the game towards it narrative finale.

On a turn, a player rolls a die which tells him how many action points he gets to use. As an action, he can open a new tile, move to an adjacent tile, or pick up an item from the tile he is on. There's also a possibility he gets to use his special power. All this should be narrated as well. The game ends when all the story cards are completed, that is, a set amount of items in requested colours are collected. The players win together, I noted the game is cooperative. But I guess, there is no winning as such, as the game is about telling a story, there is no mechanical challenge.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Improve your wellness one opposite at a time.

Contents: 9 Category/Topic cards.

Players will be dealt a card which determines 3 categories as areas of wellness they have to improve.

Each round a topic and two categories will be determined.

Players will answer the question:

What is the [CATEGORY] opposite of [TOPIC]?

Players will reveal their answers in turns. If a player wrote your exact answer, you must pass for the round. Then players must vote for the best answer. Players will win points by voting for the same player, and for being the most voted on a category of their area of wellness. Then they repeat for the opposite category.

When all 8 topics are completed, game ends and the player with the highest score wins.

In Europa Tour, each player starts in Amsterdam and tries to complete their trip; East, West, South or North, through Europe as quick as possible.  The aim of the game is to make his journey through Europe as quickly as possible by adding the city cards of your route in the correct sequence to your route. Each route visits 16 European cities and requires 8 Visa cards at certain specific points of the route for border crossings. You win the game by being the first player to finish your route and end up back in Amsterdam.

The game starts with every player in Amsterdam, the other 96 cards of all four routes (16 city cards and 8 visa cards per route) are then shuffled and put into a grid formation on the table.

During their turn, a player flips 2 cards of their choice and if they fit, extend their trip by adding the cards to their line.

If one or both of the cards are part of your trip but not adjacent to the city you are in, you can safe the card(s) by switching it/them with another one (logically with a card in the grid close to you). At the end of your turn, you flip back all cards.

Some advancements of the routes require visa cards which need to be placed before you are able to advance, and the visa cards are shared between North & East and shared between South & West. Visa cards cannot be saved. The end point of Amsterdam can also not be saved.

This game can be summarized as a game of memory, where you can safe cards for later use and where their is a small competition for visa cards between players (South&West and North&East).
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
4th Street Basketball recreates the excitement of NBA or collegiate action by maximizing your team's talents against your opponent's. Use the driving, passing, defensive, rebounding, and shooting skills of your individually-rated players to magnify their team's productivity. Concepts include ball movement, defensive play (man-to-man, zone, press) and shooting in 3 different levels.

On their turn, each player rolls five color-coded six-sided dice that can determine a variety of actions: player match-ups, offensive play setups, and even the offensive plays themselves. As per the rules of standard NBA or collegiate basketball, the highest score at the end of the game wins.

boardgame: Montsoriu: the legend of Sant Joan
description:
Montsoriu: the legend of Sant Joan is a game of ingenuity, chance and strategy where you have to play your cards very well to get hold of the riches of Montsoriu. Getting the treasure will not be an easy task!

On each turn, layers pick and play 2 development cards. The first player to reach and activate four target cards, wins.



Montsoriu: la llegenda de Sant Joan és un joc d'enginy, atzar i estratègia on hauràs de jugar molt bé les teves cartes per fer-te amb les riqueses de Montsoriu. Aconseguir el tresor no serà tasca fàcil!

boardgame: Marvel HeroClix: Avengers Fantastic Four Empyre Miniatures Game
description:
Based on the earth-shattering Empyre event, this HeroClix miniatures game contains the Avengers, Fantastic Four, X-Men, and more!

Play up to 8 campaign scenarios for 2 players where results matter from session to session or sit down for a head to head single match of raw power! Each of the 10 beautiful ready to play figures comes with two different ways to play for hundreds of different combinations. Excellent point of entry for the first time miniatures player! Map components are thick double sided tiles and the box includes everything you need to play!

This boxed-set is a great way for 2 players to learn HeroClix! Existing players will definitely want to pick up this product since it includes tournament-worthy figures and interesting scenario gameplay!

The Four Kings of Clonedovia have one thing in common; their love of the jester. He travels from Kingdom to Kingdom amusing the kings...

However, something terrible has happened. Someone has attacked the Jester. Who would do such a thing? Who is guilty of the Knavel Assault?

Knavel Assault is a guessing game where each player takes a suit, chooses one card to be their "suspect", and then attempts to be the first to guess one of their opponents' suspects. They do this by playing cards from their own deck to make guesses while keeping track of the cards their opponents play in an attempt to make more educated guesses through process of elimination. The first person to guess an opponents card correctly gets a point, and a new round begins, with the first to score three points winning the game.

I arrive, I run, I fly!

No, you don't arrive, you don't run, you crawl. But it's perfect for this very original snail race where the slowest one wins. An ode to slowness which is good in our society where everything has become urgent. The color of your snail is secret.

On your turn, roll the 4 colored dice. Each dice allows you to move a snail if it is of the indicated color or if it is on a flower of the color of the dice.

Try to move the other players' snails without revealing yourself too much. If the other players spot you, they might make you finish first.

The player who finishes first, loses.

Freedom Rings is a game based on the idea that an economy is a community that can be free and fair to all. The richest don’t always win and victory is what you choose it to be. The game board is inspired by a thriving city; with the greatest land values in its heart, medium land values in the suburbs, and the lowest land values in the rural areas. Freedom Rings’ blends economics, ease of play, and short time to finish.

Each player receives the 7 goal cards and chooses a single goal card to secretly play.  The game is played with the 3 playing pieces, one for each of the three property rings. Before each turn, the player must choose which ring to play. The first time a player plays a ring, if it’s with a die roll, the playing piece starts at the Pay Interest Street.  The playing pieces move clockwise around the board. Every turn, except for the first turn and power plays, requires a die roll. The die roll isn’t to determine what property you claim, but to keep players moving around the board and to see if rent is due to another player.

When the game begins, all players have no money, no property, and no debt. Acquiring property is the way for a player to achieve their goal. The community is the bank, so players will volunteer to watch the flows of cash and debt as needed. Turn by turn, players accumulate properties. Tactics to win can include the following: acquiring properties after a die roll, purchasing other property, consolidating properties, trading acquired property for vacant property trading with other players.  Other tactics become available as the game’s economy grows and include earning the ability to change production point values, interest rates, and even your chosen goal. Only the wiliest will make the most of what is freely given in this game.

A player wins by achieving a goal chosen from seven goal cards available. Victory can be had with much wealth, or little wealth,  depending upon the chosen card. Players claim property squares on the board with every turn until they’ve acquired what’s needed as stated on their chosen goal card. Final scoring is calculated with the value for each property square, less the player’s debt.

Gather Your Party  is a 2-4 player tavern party game where you and your friends compete to gain the most Reputation in the tavern. Recruit Patrons, Send Drink Tokens, Complete Quests and avoid all perils of a night out at the tavern.

To win, players will have to manage how much Reputation they have, and how much they Drink.

Get four (4) Reputation, win the game. Get four (4) Drink tokens, get knocked out.

Players will take turns adding Patrons to their parties, completing Quests and avoid getting too drunk. Once you gain enough Reputation from completing Quests, you become a Drinking Legend and win the game.

Alternatively, you can drink your friends under-the-table and become the last player standing. There are many different Patrons and Events cards that give and take away Drinks, but essentially, if you hit your limit of 4 Drink Tokens, you are knocked out of the game.

Every round, players read aloud a card with subjective questions about Jewish culture such as "What's the most stereotypical sign of Jewish neuroticism?" and "What topic do you dread discussing with your relatives but know will come up?" Everyone holds up their A,B, C, or D voting cards to vote on their favorite answer.

Then someone from the majority opinion and each minority opinion gets 45 seconds or less to defend their answer. A re-vote occurs to see if anyone was convinced, and the answer is majority rule. Players all start with 10 points, and every time they disagree with the majority, they get a point deducted. The next round, players dive into another humorous question about Jewish culture or religion.

The player with the most points wins.

Operation RI is a print and play wargame of moderate complexity for one or two players, simulating the Japanese overland advance on Port Moresby in 1942 and the subsequent Allied counter-offensive.

Components (need to be printed, the counters require assembly):

Rulebook

Charts and tables

Set up cards

2 x maps (recommended print size 11" x 17" each)

1 x half sheet of double-sided unit counters and markers (74 combat units and 12 markers)

1 x half sheet of double-sided strength markers (88 strength markers)

Historical background and designer's notes

Comprehensive and illustrated examples of play

Scale: battalion and company

Map: point-to-point movement

Time frame: weekly turns

Solitaire suitability: very high

Design features: battle fatigue, jungle and mountain warfare, Allied air transport, Japanese bunker defences.

The Japanese have a chance to capture Port Moresby via the overland route using their seasoned infantry from the South Seas Force. The terrain will make it a difficult task. If they fail, the Allied forces will launch a counter-attack with the aim of driving the Japanese into the sea.

The system focuses on the effect prolonged periods of combat in difficult terrain had on combat units. Units which fight for too long without respite are vulnerable to counter-attack. Each side must manage their forces with respect to fatigue, which represents combat exhaustion and ammunition expenditure, and the inherent logistical difficulties this campaign posed.

Can the Japanese capture Port Moresby? In this simulation they can, but it is difficult.

Also challenging is the task of crushing the Japanese beachheads on the north coast.

Purchase via direct digital download at : https://www.wargamevault.com/product/379181/Operation-RI?src=hottest
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This compilation includes five expansions:

Crisis – Bases & Battleships
Crisis – Heroes
Crisis – Events
Crisis – Fleets & Fortresses
2014 Gen Con Gambit Set (with 20 cards)

Conveyor Rummy is a Rummy-style game for 2-4 players played entirely with dominoes.

The object of the game is to be the first player to get rid of all their dominoes by playing melds of runs and sums.

The game requires either a double-9 or a double-12 domino set to be played.

In the taberns of Aguacatata, mercenaries play a unique card game: Cardfrontation!

Each player controls two pawns. Move them to gather resources and craft weapons, equip these weapons on the left or right hand an attack your opponent to bring their life to 0!

Cardfrontation is a position based and resource management card combat game where two players compete in order to do the maximum damage to the opponent. Use strong weapons, powerful staff and tricky artifacts to win in this strategy driven game.

The Battleship cards were called "Fixing Other Games" cards, and I don't think that's the perfect title. We're not trying to make them better (although many will say that games like these need to be better), we're just trying to craft a different experience.

The Battleship cards, while tiny and dark, they make the game less of a seek and find and more of a seek and learn. And the Captains cut the length while giving cool bonus abilities.  Just a few small changes, but a very different experience.

Stretch goals that were reached during the 2020 Rush M.D. ICU Expansion Kickstarter campaign and consists of:


     105 Cards 
         6 Urinalysis Diagnosis Cards
         10 Scenario Cards
         4 Hospitalized Patient Cards
         Patient, Diagnosis, ECG Cards
    
     1 3D Cardboard Helicopter 
     10 Cardboard Tokens 
     1 Die 
     1 Rulebook

We had 4 Monopoly role cards to spice up everyone's favorite game (haha). Each has a whole bunch of new things going on. They have a special die roll, a starting corner / dollar amount, a special color where they gain extra money, and a big crazy special ability.

With Rush M.D. - Campaign Mode you will be able to play a series of interconnected scenarios of Rush M.D., reliving the memories of a young medical intern.

Stretch goals that were reached during the 2020 Rush M.D. ICU Expansion Kickstarter campaign and consists of: 

     1 Hourglass
     1 Die
     12 Cards
     1 Board 
     25 Cardboard Tokens 
     2 Cardboard Tiles
     1 Booklet

El Foc de la Gleva (The Clash at La Gleva) is a small and simple postcard game published as a freebie by SNAFU Design. It’s a 2-player game recreating a small clash during the Third Carlist War in Catalonia, around the city of Vic, in March 23rd, 1873.

With a mechanics similar to the "Storm over..." series, the Liberal player must clear the road from Vic to the barracks of Conanglell, while the Carlist player must pass at least two units to the city of Vic. Otherwise, victory is checked tallying Victory Points for occupied areas.

During six turns, players alternate activations of units in an area for movement or for combat, until all units have been activated or both players pass. Then a new turn begins.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: We made a cool little Scrabble variant that plays both competitively and cooperatively. I don't know if Bananagrams have points on their letters, but if they do, this would be great to slip inside that little banana case. Either way, you can challenge other players and press you luck with larger destination tiles, or you can lead your friends along to words that may be within reach. We also included our simple fix / rule change about using up to 1 blank (upside down) tile in a word. Other words can share the blank but the same blank replacement letter must work for both words.

The nuclear apocalypse has struck, leaving the world's culinary supply in shambles. Choose a Survivor to battle it out for new nuclear delicacies while wielding hilarious makeshift weapons fueled with ridiculous visual puns to combat your opponents.
 Hostile Hoarders is a game of war and self preservation for 2-5 players. Each survivor plays 4 weapon cards face down in front of them. Choose from one of your attacking weapon cards and pair it with an opponent's defending weapon card. Compare your attacking power to their defending power. The winner of the battle has prevailed and collects their reward. If the attacking player has conquered, they receive a meal that day, collecting one ration card. If the defender has bested the battle, they flip their defending weapon back face down and steal thee attacker’s weapon for their own, adding it to their hand.

Various instants can be added to aid in the skirmish.
When you have depleted all of your fellow survivor’s defense, go in for the kill. Attack a player with no facedown cards in front of them, and collect their survivor card as a reward. No food, only glory. When all other survivors are eliminated or a player has collected 10 ration points, you are the glutton of the apocalypse.

—description from the publisher

Zip Zap Zop is a unique combination of deck-building and trick-taking that allows players to play a trick-taking game where they decide what cards they get dealt. This new concept gives the agency and strategy of a deck-building game, while maintaining the quick-paced, tactical nature of a trick-taking game.

The theme is integrated throughout, with players taking on the role of comedians in an improv show. The card play has the players interacting with each other in an improv scene and the new cards they buy are suggestions from the audience for the next scene.

Zip Zap Zop’s trick-taking follows the rules of a standard trick-taker, with a few differences:


     You may be able to play more than 1 card to the trick.
     Some cards are modifiers that modify a played card.
     If a player ever plays the same number as the previous card, then that player may play an additional card to the trick. The played cards then add, forming a new number.
     After playing tricks, players will buy cards using the number of tricks they won as currency.

The object of this game is for the players, using dice and playing pieces, to travel on the gameboard from a Residence Square to the shopping locations shown on their shopping lists. The first player to return to his residence with travel cards for all locations on his shopping list wins the game!

Game board is map of Altoona, PA depicting roads, highways, places of interest, businesses and billboards. Businesses used this game as an advertisement. Came with coupons for businesses advertised.

—user summary

A trivia game inspired by the TV series FRIENDS. You spin the Wheel of Mayhem to go up the Ladder of Chance. You go past the Mud Hut, through the Rainbow Ring to get to the Golden Monkey. You yank his tail and BOOM! You're in Paradice Pond!

—description from the publisher

Pokémon battle arena game with fighting, rocks, ground, and water Pokémon. "Gotta scratch 'em all!"

In this scratch off game, two players scratch off challenge spaces revealing Pokémon, Team Rocket, treasures, et. Depending on the revealed icon, players get to scratch off other sections of the card. For example, a duel icon means that you will face off against your opponent in the arena. Each player scratches a space and scores points based on how close the icon is to the player's own power type. The first to reach 25 points is the winner. If all the spaces are scratched off before that happens, the player with the most points is the winner.

In solo mode, the player plays both sides to see who wins. There are 8 scratchee cards in the box.

Pokémon racing game with flying, dragons, fire, electric, and bug Pokémon. "Gotta scratch 'em all!"

In this scratch off game, two players scratch off a square on the track, revealing Pokémon, Team Rocket, treasures, etc. Depending on the revealed icon, players get to scratch off other sections of the card and potentially score points. Whoever collects the most points wins the game.

For solo games, the player plays both sides until a winner is determined.

The aim of the game is to score the most points by sending the puck into the opponent's goal.

Played facing each other, two players alternately hit the wooden puck with their mini hockey stick. A game is played in 3 rounds and the round ends when one player has accumulated 7 points. Each goal is worth 1 point

Tiki Toss: Adventure Island is a tropical tiki themed dexterity game combined with a traditional roll and move game in which up to 6 players race to be the first to return to the starting point after collecting a set of 5 different colored ring cards. Players earn ring cards at launching pad locations spread across the board. Once at one of these locations, players draw a corresponding ring card and successfully complete all instructions listed on the card. This usually requires players to make one or more successful tiki tosses. A tiki toss consists of a player swinging a metal ring, so that it catches on a hook attached to a tiki pole in the middle of the game board. As players move along paths to and from the different Launching Pads, they will encounter tiki heads that may help or hinder their progress.

—user summary

Your Village is in trouble with the Tiki Forces that be!  They are angry and demand 5 Precious Rings in order to spare the Village.  Two to Six Villagers are chosen to brave the dangers of Tiki Toss Adventure Island but there can only be one Hero! Can you be the first to collect the five rings (Gold, Silver, Ruby, Emerald, Coral) and return them to the village?

In order to collect the 5 rings, Players must embark on a journey through Quicksand Beach, The Isle of Fire, The Fourth Sister, The Ghost of the Galleon and Head Hunter Hill.  Along the way, Tiki Toss skills are essential! Although each player must beware of Sirens, Eruptions, being sentenced to the Pit and more, the biggest challenges can come from an opponent! You may be skipped, reversed or have a ring stolen by an opponent at any time! Trust no one. The stakes are high and the race back to the Village is on!

To be the Hero requires a balance of Luck and Skill!  The Dice may be on your side and you may draw the right cards, but do you have the skills of a Tiki Toss Master? Can you handle the pressure?

—description from the publisher

description:
Suipi is a classic Samoan card game played using a standard deck of cards, similar in play to "Casino" or "Scopa".

Play starts with four cards being laid face up in the middle of the table, and eight cards being dealt to each player. The goal of the game is to capture the most points by matching cards and building stacks of cards. While regional rules may slightly alter how the game is played, some standard rules apply.

To gather a card from the table, players play a card from their hand which matches a table card in number, or matches the sum of two table cards (if the player has a 9 in their hand, they can gather a 5 and 4 from the table for example). Additionally, cards can be stacked (built) and taken as a stack on the next turn, but only if the player has the stacked number in their hand (for example, a 7 could be built onto a 3 to create a 10, allowing a player with a 10 in their hand to gather the whole stack on their turn). Face cards have no value and can only be gathered using matching face cards.

Additionally, if a player plays a card which matches several cards, stacks, or sums of individual cards on the table, the player may take all those cards. For example, if a player plays a 10 and there is on the table a 10, a stack of 3 & 7, and individually a 6 and 4 the player may take all of those cards as their 10 matches the first individual card, the total stack number, and the sum of the individual 6 and 4 cards.

If a player can not gather a card, they must 'drop' a card onto the table from their hand. If all cards are removed from the table, the player who removed the last card immediately scores 1 point and proclaims 'Suipi!'. The other player must then drop a card to continue the round. "Suipi" is played over 3 rounds, with each round ending when both player's hands are exhausted. Cards on the table remain there between rounds. At the end of the 3rd round, when cards run out of players hands', any cards remaining on the table go to the player which completed the most recent match.

Points are scored per round as follows:
Most cards = 3 points
10 of Diamonds = 2 points
2 of Spades = 1 point
Most Spades = 1 point
Aces = 1 point each
Each Suipi = 1 point (scored during the rounds)


     Note: depending on the village the game is learned in, scoring may be slightly different

What does it take to be a victorious vendor of Plushie Pier? Selling lots of plushie sets, letting buyers haggle a bit, and boosting POMP! POMP is a local acronym for “place On My Pier.” It basically reflects the popularity, charisma, and overall esteem of your Craft.

Using the cards overlapping mechanic, player for prices of plushies on the pier for sale.
Each time you make a sale, you get a score card matching the icon and quantity of that set. If a player has sold their 8th Plushie, players complete the final round and score.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Horror Trivia Part II: Deeper Cuts
description:
150 game cards, each featuring two horror film trivia questions (with answers on the back) and one of five weapon icons in the corner.

A trivia question is read from the card and the currently active team attempts to answer. If successful, the next question is read and they attempt to answer it also. If both answers were correct, they collect the card. Play passes to the next team.
The first team that collects 3 different weapons wins the game!

It should be easy to name 3 Disney dogs - but can you do it under the pressure of 5 seconds twisting down, and with the other players staring at you, waiting for you to get flustered? Time's not on your side, so just say what comes to mind and risk ridiculous answers slipping out as time twirls down on the unique twisted timer! It's all in good fun with this fast-paced game where you have to "Just Spit It Out!"

Everyone’s favorite game meets everyone’s favorite show! In this hilarious game, you read a topic card and then write down your response. It can be anything that comes to mind—there’s no right or wrong answer! Then all the responses are read aloud, and you have to figure out who said what! You won’t believe the THINGS… the people you know will come up with! With the Schitt’s Creek Edition, explore your thoughts about the show and it’s hilarious characters and see what THINGS… you’ll say!

—description from the publisher

On a player's turn, they select a line and press down any number of bubbles on that line. The opponent then presses down any number of un-pressed bubbles in a line of their choice. The player left to press the last bubble on the board loses the round.

Argy Bargey is a new board game where you compete to create your own canal empire.

Each player must complete routes between towns and cities, competing against each other. Using tiles, you'll want your waterway to take the fastest possible route across the board.

Just like real canals, you'll need to use locks and tunnels to tackle hills. Junctions will be needed to connect other canals and create an efficient network.

With an approximate playing time of 45 minutes, 2 to 6 people can gather to do battle - canal style! Can you become the greatest canal tycoon of the 21st century?

This game was made by wholesale store "Makro" (or "Metro" in Germany) for their 25 year anniversary in the Netherlands. You play this trivia game with 2 to 4 teams of each 2 to 4 players in it. Every team put their pawn (a brain) on the start position. The first team to reach the finish wins.

During your turn you will pick the top card from the pile. On each card there are 2 themes, one on each side of the card, and you have 10 seconds to decide which side you want to play.

After choosing the side you want to play, you get 60 seconds to guess the words written on the card which belong to the theme on the top of the card (Dutch writers, capital cities, Makro employer titles, types of cheese). The team to the right of the playing team is the control team, which tallies how many correct answers are given.

The answers are written on the card but have a red coating over them which makes them almost invisible to read, the game also features a pair of sunglasses with red-tainted glasses which can see through the coating and can read the correct answers (this pair of sunglasses is for the control team).

If no correct answer is given in the first 30 seconds, the first answer is given by the control team as a hint, this answer does not count towards the score. After the 60 seconds are over, the playing team moves their pawn as many spots as their score and then the control team becomes the new playing team.

Furthermore, some spots on the board have a special action like "Go back 3 spots", which goes into effect immediately when a team lands on it, or "if you can at least 5 correct answers next turn, move one extra spot forward" or "Lose a turn", which goes into effect on their next turn. The board has 48 spaces, including the start and the finish space.

In this game, each team/players tries to collect the 5 different coloured chips in the game which correspond to the questions on the quiz cards.

All questions are questions about what happened in the Netherlands and the World between October 1995 and October 1996.

The player/teams who rolls the black side of the die starts the game.
On a team/player's turn, they roll the die (yellow, red, purple, blue, green and black).
If they roll the black side, their turn is forfeited.
Otherwise, they have to answer the question on the quiz card which corresponds to the colour they rolled.
Is their answer correct, they gain the chip in that colour.
Is their answer incorrect, the turn goes to the next player.
If a team/player rolls a colour they already have a chip in, they have to answer the question connected to that colour and if they are correct, they may roll again. (normally this isn't the case after a correct answer)
Their turn continues until they either roll the black side, when they answer a question incorrectly or when they obtain a new coloured chip.

In this Trivia game about the European Union and its member states, players try to answer questions correctly in order to continue their turn, but if they guess wrong, they might give their opponents an extra turn!

Every player starts on the starting spot.
The player who rolls the highest with the die starts the game.
In your turn, you roll the die and move that amount of places.
You can land on either: 

     a flag from a country, you then answer a question about the country who belongs to that flag
     a spot on the map of a country, you then answer a question about that country
     a Star, you then answer a question about the European Union


If you guess correctly, you get and extra turn and can roll again.
If you guess wrong, the next player can try to answer correctly, if any of the other players guess correctly, they save up an extra turn that they can use after their normal turn, but they do not move right now and the turn of the current player is over (unless they saved up an extra turn between their last turn and this one).
If no one answers the question correctly, the turn of the current player is over, unless that player has saved up extra turns between their last turn and this one.
You can save up at most 2 extra turns.

Questions from countries can be in their native language (Finnish, Swedish, Danish, English, German, French, Luxembourgian, Austrian) or they are in Dutch.

When a player reaches or goes over the finish spot, they have to answer one last question about the European Union to win the game. If they answer incorrectly they stay at their spot and get a new question about the European Union the next turn, if they guess correctly their next turn, they still win the game.

—user summary

Each player receives a set of dice, which instead of points have faces in different expressions. The players roll all the dice and place them on the matching starting squares. A special die specifies which die faces may be moved in that round. This die is changed each round. The six different dice faces also offer action possibilities. The winner is the first to draw his 6 dice with different expressions onto the target fields.

—user summary

The first player to return to his residence with travel cards for all locations on his shopping list wins the game!

Game board is map of Columbus, GA depicting roads, highways, places of interest, businesses and billboards. Businesses used this game as an advertisement. Came with coupons for businesses advertised.

A roulette variant with pig motifs on the bidding cards and on the roulette wheel. Each player has 4 cards in hand, with one suit and one pig position, he can bid up to four suits. Then the wheel is spun, for the winning color with matching pig position there is a winning pig and the bet card, for the right color with wrong pig there is a triple chance card, for the wrong color and the right pig there is a single chance card, but you have to give up the bet cards. If nothing is right, you give away all the cards you have bet. Whoever has a winning pig can challenge another owner and bets on a pig position, if he turns the right one, he gets the opponent's pig, otherwise he loses his own to the opponent. The first to own three winning pigs wins.
