boardgame: 5 Surprise Mini Brands!: Mini Market Dash! Game
description:
Get ready to dash! Race around the market using one of the Mini Brands Collectable movers, gathering all the Mini Brands you can. Complete your cart to collect them all!

—description from the back of the box

The game is based on a series of collectible miniatures based on actual brand name items, four of which are included to use as pawns representing the players. Players also receive three cards representing a shopping list, with three food items on each. On your turn, you roll a single die (d6) and move that amount around the board. If a space has a face-down token it is revealed, and if it is an item you need you can pick it up and put it on your list (it stays on the space if not). There are also a few "wild" tokens that can used for any item. Other spaces let you draw from a deck of cards with a variety of actions, including stealing tokens from an opponent's list. Complete a list and you get another, and the first player to complete three lists wins. Any token from a completed list is put in the center and can be collected if a player lands on an empty space; as such, the game picks up pace as it goes on as more items are readily available.

To play House of Cronies, each player’s Cabinet consists of up to 6 cronies; each carrying a set number of votes, plus an optional foreign ally. All cronies, besides the foreign allies, can be elected as president or vice-president. Other positions such as Finance, Justice, Service Delivery and Communications, can only be filled by specialists.

Two decks of cards are placed on the table: the main deck and the tactics deck. The main deck consists of influence, corruption and crony cards and the tactics deck contains only tactics cards. Players start with seven cards from the main deck and two tactic cards each. Any cronies or foreign ally in the initial hand must be placed in the cabinet immediately (and any crony or ally that can not be placed is discarded).

In each round of House of Cronies, five cards are drawn from the main deck and placed face up in the middle of the table. In turn order, players can either pick up an influence / corruption card OR bid influence, corruption or a combination of both for a crony. Players can only gain one card per round, so any player who outbids another forfeits their turn. Players that are outbid, get their turn and their influence / corruption bid card back.

The end of the game occurs when there are no cards left in the deck or fewer cards than needed to draw for a new round. A final round is played for players to buy and used tactics before the election takes place and the player with the most votes wins!

boardgame: Das große "Oh wie schön ist Panama" Spiel
description:
Each player is given a piece to roll to Panama and back as quickly as possible. On the way there are special fields on which you have to test your memory by revealing the matching tile to the field you have reached. If none of the 3 revealed tiles is correct, you have to travel back to the next special field. The first player to return home (after reaching Panama) wins the game.

—user summary

Schermstrijd is a family game. Children battle against their parents to gain more screentime! It's a simple card game in which you try to duplicate the colored wifi-code laid out in the center of the table. Sabotage your opponents and be the first to complete your code to win the game!

The game is played in 3 to 5 rounds (you can choose). Set a starting time and roll the dice to see how much screentime can be added or extracted. 
4 colored wifi-card are layed out in the center in the beginning of the round. The first player to duplicate the code wins the round.

During a turn you roll a dice to see what actions you may perform. This can vary from stealing a card to changing the code in the center. You play cards to complete your code or to prevent your opponents to complete theirs.

Schermstrijd the battle is a fun and easy family game to end discussions about, well... screentime. In only 30 minutes of offline fun it is settled. Do you dare to battle?

—description from the publisher

Galaktion: Messengers of the Void is a tactical co-op sci-fi story-driven dungeon crawler game for 2-5 players, with an option for solo play.

The game will take you to the far future of the Milky way, where a team of renegades are hired by a shadowy corporation to fix a messy experiment with galactic proportions.

Unique setting, armed with tight game rules, allows you to “learn-as-you-crawl” through the dungeon maps and master your strategy against the game. Randomly spawned enemies and skill based characters will allow you to choose from variety of skills and abilities to find your best game style cooperating with your team mates. In Galaktion: Messengers of the Void, the team play means survival.

—description from the designer

"The Different Game"

The game is promoted as "fast moving, surprising - different". It is played by four players on a 13x13 multi-coloured checkered board. On each side of the board, there is a role (producer, doctor, politician, and opportunist). One of these roles is assumed by each of the four players. Playing time is presented as being one to two hours. The board also states that the game is "a game of skill", played with "no dice or cards".

MAKALOT was published by Modern Games Company in Oaktown, Indiana. The copyright date is 1937, held by Roy A. Beard. A "patent pending" notation is shown on the game board.

boardgame: Waste Knights: Second Edition – Beyond the Horizon
description:
Beyond the Horizon is a massive expansion for Waste Knights: Second Edition, a postapocalyptic game of adventure and survival. Now is the time to leave the continent, embark upon a ship and sail to places you only know from legends and myths.

Explore a new map enabling you to visit Tasmania, Solomon Islands or New Zealand altered by the Apocalypse. Learn the truth behind the infamous Project X, while battling deadly sea monsters and experiencing strange weather phenomena.
Seek for fresh water – a new resource that will make the game even more realistic and challenging both for sea-farers and continent dwellers.

Play as one of new Knights armed with unique abilities and expand your base game using tens of new cards, tokens, and adventures to experience a setting of enormous proportions however you like – solo, cooperatively or competitively.

Choose your ship, set the course and see where the winds shall take you!

-description from publisher

A rampaging herd of dinosaurs are circling a peaceful cave village. Located far out in the dusty desert plains, the village's only defence against the prehistoric invaders are their very own Clyde, Charlie, Chester & Chuck. Rallying together throughout the night, can the brave team chase off the Caveosaurus Dinos?

A fast paced co-operative card game for 1 to 4 players with a game length of 15 minutes. On a players turn they will draw two cards from the deck and will choose to play one and discard the other. Cards will allow a players caveman to move around the village circle of caves, send in an enemy dino, rotate the attack spear or hopefully chase off a dino.

Once a day and night has been played the team of cavemen can add up their total score for points awarded for chasing dinos, caves protected and if they returned to their home caves.

—description from the designer

Sweets in Wonderland is a trick-taking game where players draft the conditions of each trick, aiming to achieve the most points.

Specifically, players draft if the trick will be must-follow or can't-follow for a randomly revealed un-dealt card, and if high cards will win or low cards.  Players may also play 1 or 2 cards on their turn.

The winner of a trick wins the chips used for drafting the round conditions, and the start player for the draft rotates.

At the end of the game, the player with the most points, determined through a set collection mechanic with the drafting chips, wins.

The Managers Expansion Pack comes with cards that add ten managers to the game.

Each of the Golazo Managers has 3 special abilities to give you an advantage over your opponent! Choose your manager at the start of the game.  You can only use each card once, so use them wisely!

Note: each of the 10 managers has their own special ability cards that are different from those of the other managers.

How will you build your Victory?

Slash & Spells is a  Hero Building Battleground. Embody your Legendary Hero and unlock your unique ability tokens using magical Relics across the realm. Command clever card play and take ever-evolving heroic actions.

Slash & Spells is an epic duel using action selection, variable player powers, and hero tech trees tied into your relic choices. Players control unique Heroes drawn into an epic battle during your turn you take 6 basic actions to grow in power, attack, move, play cards from your unique hero deck, and ultimately knock down your enemies by reducing their health to zero.

The battle takes place via attack actions and playing cards from your hand. Each card that resolves deals their base Dmg and card effects to their target. Once a card has been played its card effect becomes an available action for your hero to take.

Victory is yours when you claim the most Knockdowns by the end of rounds and can claim your true destiny!

-description from designer

Stampede is a set-collection auction game themed around stamp collecting.

On a player's turn, they reveal 1 to 4 cards from a prepared deck to auction.  The players then choose how many dice to roll.  The player who rolls the highest amount is the winner, but will pay the value of the second highest amount rolled.  The players pay their bids in dice, with each 6 coins (pips) they need to pay consuming 1 of their dice.

The end of the game is triggered once a certain card is revealed near the bottom of the deck.  The player with the most points from the cards they've collected wins.

Master Caramel's pastry shop is famous for the most delicious gingerbread. It gets so many orders that the master is no longer able to fulfill them on his own, so he asked you for help, which is a very responsible task! Each gingerbread must have the right shape, plenty of icing and the right sprinkles. Those of you, who deal with your tasks as quickly as possible, will surely fall into the favors of Master Caramel... and get a pot of icing to lick off! So get to work!

Pierniczki (The Gingerbread) is a game that combines the element of memory with matching shapes and colors, which is why it is perfect for the youngest children.

—description from the publisher

Cukiernia mistrza Karmela słynie z najpyszniejszych pierniczków. Spływa do niej tyle zamówień, że mistrz nie jest już w stanie ich samodzielnie zrealizować, więc poprosił Was o pomoc.To bardzo odpowiedzialne zadanie! Każdy pierniczek musi mieć odpowiedni kształt, dużo lukru i odpowiednią posypkę. To z Was, które najszybciej upora się ze swoimi zadaniami na pewno wkradnie się w łaski mistrza Karmela... i dostanie garnek lukru do wylizania! Zatem do dzieła!

Gra Pierniczki łączy w sobie element zapamiętywania, dopasowywania kształtów i kolorów dlatego idealnie sprawdzi się wśród najmłodszych dzieci.

—description from the publisher (Polish)

The world is new, and fledgling civilizations beseech you for aid. In this 2-4 player game, you will assume the role of a Greek God vying for control of the world and the future of your civilization. Wield divine power using asymmetric abilities and your customizable action deck. There are 13 Gods to choose from, providing a different challenge and experience each time you play.

As a God you exercise your will, represented by various cards and abilities, to build the world as you play. On your turn you will play cards and pay for their scalable effects by exhausting your civilization’s cities and temples.  New cities and temples can be built when your population grows, making your action cards more powerful and your turns more efficient.  You’ll be able to shape the shared world map as players choose which unique hex-shaped land tiles to add, giving the mortals new areas to grow into.  Each land has its own mix of geographic type, attack and defense bonus, population growth rate, potential build sites, movement cost, and location abilities.  No two worlds will look the same.   You will also guide your Hero and High Priestess to go on quests, use location abilities on land tiles, lead your people to new areas, or convert the non-believers. There are also 12 powerful mythological creatures that can be summoned, each with their own abilities to enhance your strategy.

As powerful as you are, the mortals are not entirely within your control as they have their own free will.  They will pray for various signs, protections, or resources, and answering these prayers will grant you a range of benefits in return.  In addition, at the end of each player's turn the population will move and grow in response to the Mortal die's result.

The game ends after 3 Ages of raising mountains, answering prayers, causing catastrophes, unlocking technologies, warring, and building cities, temples, and monuments. Victory points are awarded for area control of the world, your completed quests and world-building secret objectives, technological discoveries, military victories, Follower's morale, and other achievements. Each God also has their own way to earn VP.  The god with the most VP takes their rightful seat on Mount Olympus.

—description from the publisher

description:
Nowhere are the opposites of good and evil more evident than in politics. And in this board game.

Honest, committed and capable rulers oppose lying, cheating and scheming fellow citizens. In the struggle for power in the role of Chancellor / Chancelloress, some people will use any means. 
As a member of politics, you must assert yourself in this - often unfriendly - environment and also be able to deal with unfair means of competition in order to decide the question of the next Chancellor for yourself.
 Based on real political events, you will experience this tension and all these contradictions in Kanzleramt.
Your path to the chancellor's office is long and rocky, and it always leads through the Bundestag. It is important that you get as many members of your own party as possible into the Bundestag. Because in this parlor game they elect the chancellor at the end.

As in real life, the path to this position is practically never straightforward and is marked by both positive surprises and setbacks. Luck, skill, fighting spirit and a wide variety of political events influence the path of your party's politicians to the Bundestag.

—description from the publisher (translated)

On a map of Europe, you build track to fulfill your contracts.
On his turn, each of the players chooses one of 6 action phases and triggers it. All players act while the triggering player has an advantage (like in Puerto Rico). In the contract phase, you choose contracts that include a start city, a destination city and sometimes 1 or 2 in between. It says which type of locomotive you are allowed to use and which and how many wagons.

In the building phase, you connect any 2 cities by paying for the track you build. In the market phase you buy locomotives and wagons, in the auction phase you must offer at least one locomotive or wagon that is not yet part of a contract. In the reward phase, you get money for your connected cities and in the state subsidy phase you  receive a locomotive or wagon for free. At the end of a round all players receive income according to their partly or completely fulfilled contracts.

After a player has an agreed amount of fulfilled contracts, the game ends and you get points for fulfilled contracts, income and connected cities and minus points for contracts not at least partly fulfilled.

—user summary

HackBots is a strategic card game of sabotage for 2-5 players. The object of the game is to raise your opponents’ device risk and hack their devices before they hack you.

Each player lays out three Device Tokens in their playing area. Next, deal 4 cards face down to each player. Place the remaining deck face down to create the Draw Pile. Then, flip over the top card to start the Discard Pile.

On your turn, draw one card from the Draw Pile or Discard Pile. Play one card from your hand. You can ATTACK an opponent by raising the risk on one device, DEFEND one of your own devices by lowering or resetting the risk, or DISCARD one card if you cannot or do not want to play a card.

When a device’s risk reaches level 4, it may be hacked. Once a player has three hacked devices, that player cannot win the game. However, they remain in the game and continue to attack the remaining payers until there is a winner.

The last player remaining with at least one unhacked device wins the game!

boardgame: Stalingrad '42: Little Saturn / Winter Storm Expansion
description:
A small expansion for Mark Simonitch's Stalingrad '42 which adds an additional scenario covering the period from December 14th, 1942 through February 5th, 1943. 
The scenario portrays the Soviet Little Saturn offensive following up on Operation Uranus, against the German allies of Romania and Italy. The German forces attempt at the same time to break the Soviet encirclement of the 6th Army with Operation Winter Storm. Counters are provided for the Soviet Ostrogozhsk-Rossosh Operation against the Italians and Hungarians, the Voronezh-Kastornoe Operation against 2nd Army, and the final offensive against Stalingrad, Operation Ring.

Contents

    1 8-page Little Saturn/Winter Storm Booklet
    1 half-countersheet
    1 Axis Setup card
    1 Soviet Setup card
    1 Turn Record track with Reinforcements
    1 updated Stalingrad ‘42 Rules Booklet
    2 updated Stalingrad ‘42 Player Aid Cards

Sophie Scholl - 3 - Bis zum Ende deines nächsten Zuges darf niemand Karten aus der Hand ausspielen.

Coralon is the base trick-taking game for Crab Fragment Labs' Vines deck.  The Vines deck has eleven cards in five suits, including Ace (high) and four face cards: Knight, Bishop, Farmer, and Tar. Below these are the ranks 7 through 2. Games with the Vines deck include several trick-taking games for 3 to 6 players, including cutthroat and team games, based on familiar mechanics from games like Hearts, Pitch, and Euchre, with an abundant assortment of clever innovations.

Players bid on the number of tricks they will win, with the highest bidder leading and choosing trump.

If you make your bid, you score the number of tricks won and your opponents score nothing.

If you make over your bid you and your opponents all score the number of tricks won.

If you make under your bid you score nothing and your opponents score the number of tricks won.

Game ends when one player reaches a winning score (dependent on number of players).

Everyone on the internet has an opinion. Some more outlandish than others. In this family friendly trivia game there are 5 categories related to reviews left by people on the internet:

One Star Wonders - There are always winners and losers - these are the losers.

Mystery Basket - What's being described here?

Facts & Stats - Sort out the truth from the fake news.

Historical Howlers - Wild reviews from the days before the internet.

Fill in the Blanks - Identify the missing words to complete these reviews.

A sixth category "?" provides a little chaos and randomness with unique challenges provided on each card.

Players move around the board to each of the different category zones trying to collect a star card from each one by correctly answering a question.

First one to collect a star card from each category and make it back to the center of the board wins.

Age of Journey is a "slightly" different kind of worker-playing game in which you develop a forgotten village in the mountains. The game is based on the idea that workers (warriors) and resources (livestock) are two sides of the same coin. If you send a large number of people to raid an area, you will earn more money, but you will have fewer actions to perform. In addition, the dominance of the colours over the area makes the management of your warband very difficult. Use the sale market to sell your livestock at a high price and invest in various cards to get rich!

Worker pieces are double sided. On your turn, you choose a number of workers to send to a given space. One worker occupies the space, triggering it's  while the remaining workers flip over to become the depicted resources. These resources can then be sold on a variable market, allowing you to buy skills, more workers, and Estates, which are the main source of victory points.

The player with the most victory points after 3 rounds (short & solo games) or 4 rounds (long game) is the winner.

—user summary

description: This pack include 34 cards all based around characters from the YouTube series. It has 9 characters with their own actions, plus 8 foods, 2 toppings, 2 conditions, 2 bonus actions and 2 events.

boardgame: The Legend of Zelda Game Comic Part 1
description:
A very rare, limited release board game bundled and sold with candy by Bandai.

The players spin the wheel to figure out how much to move (1-3 spaces). Then, they move their token and defeat monsters (also by spinning the wheel) to collect weapon tokens.

Once someone has four weapon tokens, the game turns into the dungeon phase, wherein players race to get to the boss and defeat it, acquiring a triforce fragment. Then the game goes back to the overworld and the process is repeated.

Whoever has the most triforce pieces by the end of the fourth dungeon wins.
This pack has 28 new cards focused on expanding your food cards. It includes 2 new character and their actions, 12 foods, 3 toppings, 3 conditions, 3 bonus actions and 3 events.

A card management game with 3-5 players. Players change their positions in each turn so everyone will have a chance to be the boss, or the lowest-level employee. Players have to finish their task with their ability cards and get the merit. According to different position, players can change the situation by using their special ability card. For example, a lower level clerk may shift his job burden to his /her supervisor by using a Sick Leave card.

There are Ability card and Task card. Players pick and collect ability cards first in order to fulfill the condition of getting a task card.

After finishing a task card, player can get coins as reward. Player who first collects 3 black coin wins.
This pack has 28 new cards adding food to your game based on that famous footlong place. It has 2 characters and their actions, 12 foods, 3 toppings, 3 conditions, 3 bonus actions and 3 events

Introducing the King’s court! The characters in Cafe Chaos are in for a royally good time. These 8 new characters now feature a RANK (shield with number). This ranking is in addition to the character power, and ranked characters can be played mixed in with non-ranked characters.

Also included in the 34 new cards are 8 foods, 2 toppings, 2 conditions, 2 bonus actions and 4 events

Help climber Sam reach the yeti at the top of the mountain in this cooperative vocabulary game.

Sam needs your speed and your vocabulary to overcome obstacles and reach the top of the mountain. Roll the dice, find out on a map the challenge you will have to meet and each time you succeed, Sam can move a little further towards his goal.

Players pick a challenge card off the draw stack and roll a die to see how many words they must recite to meet the challenge.  If no one can pick a correct word, they must roll the die and move back that many spaces.

Who wins? Whichever team or player first gets Sam to the top of the mountain.


Un jeu de vocabulaire coopératif
Aidez l’alpiniste Sam à rejoindre le yéti au sommet de la montagne!Amateurs de jeu de mots, voilà votre chance de briller aussi en alpinisme avec Sam, qui a besoin de votre rapidité et de votre vocabulaire pour franchir les obstacles et atteindre le sommet de la montagne. Lancez le dé, découvrez sur une carte le défi que vous aurez à relever et chaque fois que vous réussissez, Sam peut avancer un peu plus vers son but.

Qui l’emporte? L’équipe ou le joueur qui, le premier, permettra à Sam d’atteindre le sommet de la montagne.

An extension of Magic: the Gathering 4th edition. Drawn from the first four Magic: the Gathering expansions. All cards in the set were from the Arabian Nights, Antiquities, Legends, and The Dark expansions (14 from Arabian Nights, 21 from Antiquities, 71 from Legends, and 19 from The Dark). Each card was white-bordered, while the cards in their original sets were black-bordered. Also, each card carried the expansion symbol from the set it was originally printed in.

The set was printed in English and Japanese only. The Japanese version was black-bordered, because as it was the first time those cards were printed in the language. A similar black-bordered set called Renaissance was released into German, French, and Italian markets. These contained a different array of cards.

Now the Heroes have been hired to find him and bring him back alive. But the dark tidings foretold in his recent stories have begun to bear an eerie resemblance to reality, building toward something sinister.

This limited edition, deluxe resin terrain piece depicts a massive eruption of stone and a swirling portal of darkness – the Hell Mouth, an all-new piece for Shadows of Brimstone, plus a new mission - Through the Mouth of Madness - themed for the new piece!

The Hellmouth acts as a gate for enemies or the void by doubling the number of encounters, attacks or growing dead on the exploration token and adding enemies to fight.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Challenge Pack #2 – Beast Unleashed
description:
Legend tells of a sinister beast that stalks the caves and canyons of Black Rock, a thriving mining town north from the ruins of Brimstone. Known as The Devil of Black Rock, they say it towers over normal Slashers and has grown mutated beyond recognition. With four razor-sharp scything arms, it eviscerates its prey, feeding on their souls as it shreds their flesh. Layered in a thick shell of light-refracting Void Chiton, The Devil of Black Rock phases through time and space with shimmering speed, warping in and out of reality as it strikes!

¡Yo No Tomo! (I do not drink!) is a hilarious card game that appeals to competitiveness and is designed for meetings with friends where drinks and the desire to have a good time are present.

The game challenges each team to get all the cards in their BAR before another does or before they have had enough to give up. In the attempt, each team must defend themselves with the cards they have in their possession to avoid getting drunk, while at the same time they can attack their opponents with cards that make them drink. This game is designed and optimized to be played in teams, but its optional individual version also works very well and is just as fun.

¡Yo No Tomo! is just as fun and competitive without alcohol, and for this it is recommended to draw the TRAGO cards.

Iwapelé is a set collection and pattern recognition game of African masks.

In their turn, players pick and discard a card, and make sets of 3, 4 or 5 cards, of the same community membership and geometric pattern, receiving points for each set.

The game ends at the fifth round or when a player reaches 500 points.

description: The 5 zoo area cards are laid out, the animal cards are added, all other cards are stacked face down, each player has 7 cards. Whoever's turn it is rolls the dice and moves the game piece, then he can lay or discard cards and draw new ones. Whoever completes a row with food, baby animals, scales with the animal keeper or regardless of cards laid with the veterinarian takes the row for victory points. When all the animals in an area are cleared, the player who has collected the most cards wins.

オルカコール (Orcas' Call) is a 2 player Japanese co-operative communication game. The players are orcas looking for a romantic relationship. But each Orca has a different personality, and they cannot use words. So they convey their feelings using sounds (calling cards).

Both players try to control their scoring on two feelings; crush and comfort. Each player will choose a calling card in secret and show it, and then match them an emotion card. The way the different calls relate, and how they are effected by emotions, make their feelings rise and fall.

As the players get to know each other, unexpected things can happen. The important thing is to keep their feelings together. Will they be united by a love deeper than the sea?

カードの組み合わせによって上下する「ときめき」と「やすらぎ」の２つの気持ちをコントロールしながら、お互いの仲を深めましょう。
時には思いもよらないハプニングも発生するかもしれません。

あなたたちは海よりも深い愛で結ばれることができるでしょうか？

Spectacular horse race with a minimum of 3 players and a maximum of 6. Before starting, the participants will decide how many races they will run per game. Each one receives an initial sum of money, with their objective being to obtain the highest capital at the end of the game. In Krev a Písek (Blood and Sand), players are gladiators who are trying to come out on top in the arena.

Each player starts the round with a hand of cards, and everyone secretly chooses and reveals a single card to serve as their starting attack value. In turn, each player plays a card to add to their attack, repel an opponent's attack with a defense card, or modify an attack or prevent a defense; alternatively, a player can pass to skip one turn. If all players are out of cards or pass in turn, the round ends, and whoever has the highest attack value wins. If, however, a player would increase their attack value to 5, they win the round immediately.

The round's winner receives Roman denarii, and whoever first collects 5 Roman denarii wins.

Your goal in Florist is to create bouquets of three flowers as whoever first creates five bouquets wins.

To set up, shuffle the twenty flower cards, deal four to each player, then lay out the remaining twelve cards in a row. Each player places their florist card next to one of the six cards in the middle of the row, then the game begins.

On a turn, play a card from your hand to move your florist in the market, then pick up the card adjacent to where you moved and leave the played card in its place. If you now have a bouquet in hand — three cards of the same value or type or three cards in a straight — score a bouquet by revealing one flower on your scoring card, then replace the three cards in the bouquet with the three cards at one end of the flower market.

If your florist lands on the same space as the opponent in the market, reveal all cards in your hand to see who has the higher value and wins a point — except that you don't count cards that match the revealed symbols on your opponent's scoring card.

Wild Assent is a miniatures board game with solo, player-versus-player, and cooperative modes. Battle your opponents in the gladiatorial arenas of the Silvestrem in Wild Assent’s skirmish mode. Hunt the dangerous creatures of the Wilds, by yourself or with friends, fighting not just for survival, but to keep your expedition running. Regardless of the mode you choose, Wild Assent offers strategic and varied combat, ensuring no two battles are the same.

The Lavon Rising Expansion is new content for Wild Assent that adds a 100-page decision-based narrative campaign book with multiple different adventures that give the feeling of a large scale narrative campaign (with impactful choices that affect the story and its conclusion) but without needing years to complete them. The Daughters of Draketh adds a new narrative-based campaign to Wild Assent.

War is brewing in the Wilds and the key to peace rests in the hands of the Daughters of Draketh, a secluded tribe of ashcarii dragon riders. Choose a side and adventure deeper into the Wilds than ever before, battling (or fighting alongside) new creatures and mercenaries.

boardgame: Conquest: The Last Argument of Kings – The Spires Abomination Expansion
description:
Abominations loom several man heights over the head of even the tallest mortal. Their gore slicked limbs spider out from its bulbous, distended abdomen and thorax. They are instruments of pure mayhem on the battlefield, scuttling forwards and reaping a bloody toll with their scythe like appendages, leaving only bloody ruin behind them. Survivors speak of a keening whine, just on the verge of hearing that pervades their memories, a sound that registers deeper than the cries of their fallen comrades. This is the only sound an Abomination can make, the only sound it is allowed to make: a keening howl to convey the depth of their misery and torment.

Abominations were not originally intended to serve as organic war engines. They were an advanced and cruel form of punishment, devised on a whim by the Sovereign. Ensconced deep within an Abomination lies the body of an Exile who has betrayed his trust or breached the most sacrosanct of Spire laws. Despite significant effort, even the Sovereign's most ardent opponents have not been able to come up with a more cruel punishment and have adopted this cruel torture for their worst enemies.

Invasive cerebral procedures impairs the cognitive function of the condemned, as chemical and hormonal treatments are applied to make them lose control of their Life-Binding gifts and forcibly bond them to a lower organism. Multiple organisms were proposed and tried but the humble ant was ultimately preferred. While more gruesome examples could be made, it was found that those Abominations bonded to other species were too intractable or self-destructive. The ant's instinctive hierarchical physiology somehow makes it more susceptible to Pheromonic command, forcing the body to respond to orders while allowing compromised psyche trapped within the hellish amalgam only enough control to wail its anguish even while enacting the will of its tormentors.

With easy assembly instructions, each of the 7 events have been minituarised to recapture the spectacle, and of course glamour, that is The Gladiators!

Hey all, Scott here! Evade taxes like never before with the Scott The Woz edition of the classic board game MONOPOLY! From the rules:
Introduction
This is a reworking of a two-player Euchre variant that I was taught about 35 years ago in Arnprior, Ontario (a small town near Ottawa). It appears to have been a local variation on Six Hand Almonte Euchre (which seems a reasonable conclusion considering Almonte and Arnprior are roughly 30km apart).

Object
Seventeen tricks are played each hand; the object is to win at least nine of them.
The Cards
A pack of 39 cards is used consisting of A K Q J 10 9 8 7 6 in four suits (hearts, diamonds, clubs and spades) plus three distinct jokers (called Bennies).
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
Doppelkopf for Two

From the rules:

Object
Twenty-three tricks are played each hand; certain cards are worth certain points; the object is to capture the most points. There are bonuses for catching your opponent’s Ace of trump and for winning the last trick with the Jack of Clubs.
The Cards
The 48 card deck is made from a double pack that has been shortened by removing cards below 9: so each of the four suits contain two each of A K Q J 10 9.

In most contracts, the cards rank, from high to low:

Trumps: ♥10, ♣Q, ♠Q, ♥Q, ♦Q, ♣J, ♠J, ♥J, ♦J, ♦A, ♦10, ♦K, ♦9 (26 cards)
Clubs:  ♣A, ♣10, ♣K, ♣9
Spades:  ♠A, ♠10, ♠K, ♠9
Hearts:  ♥A, ♥K, ♥9

The cards have values:

A = 11    10 = 10    K = 4    Q = 3     J = 2

making a total of 240 card points. The basic object of the game is to take more than half of these card points in tricks. The card points are used to determine which team has won the round - they are not the same as game points which are written on the score-sheet as a result of winning or losing.
family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
Nostalgix is the culmination of decades of trading card game experience from industry veterans. By blending our favorite mechanics from the long history of classic card games and introducing several more unique to Nostalgix we have created a game that feels familiar yet fresh.

In our debut Base Set, a worldwide event has trapped anyone who falls asleep in a shared lucid world populated by characters from fictional universes humans have invented over the years. Join Heroes like Vix of the Six and Doctor Corvus and clash Fighters like the powerful Booster Box Dragon, Avengaline, and Gametron against each other to reclaim 10 portal counters, return these characters to their rightful homes and restore the multiverse!

D-Spirits is a fast paced Trading Card Game. It is designed to mitigate the luck you find in TCGs. The game feature 2 deck, allowing you to draw the type of card you really need at any given moment during the game.

Each of the 160 original spirits have game-bending effects that make each game different.

Taking inspiration from other "monster catcher" card games, players (or "D-Tectives" as they are called) take turn summoning "D-Spirits" to playing field. The D-Spirits can then attack on another or their opponent, to inflict damage.

Uniquely, each D-Spirit, has an SP value & when a player's D-Spirit is defeated, that player loses that much SP. 
Each player starts with 5000 SP & the goal is to reduce your opponent's SP to 0.

The unholy hybrid of Euchre & Doppelkopf (for 2 players)
 Mr. Canoehead, a quintessentially Canadian superhero: on a canoeing trip in Algonquin Park, he was hit by lightning while portaging his aluminum canoe, which became permanently welded to his head. As a crime fighter, he would capture criminals by turning around so that the canoe knocked them over.

Object

family: Organizations: The Game Artisans of Canada

description:
That's Bananas is a bluffing party game for 3 to 8 players, where all but one bad apple in the bunch can share in the victory (must provide your own bad apples).

That's Bananas adds a fresh theme and a unique twist to the genre! Collect 4 identical fruit cards, or be unable to play when required, and you lose. Everyone else shares in the victory!

However, a player who calls a bluff correctly earns a Banana card. Banana cards can provide increasingly devastating disadvantages to the player of your choice. Use your Banana cards at the right time, and you can ensure a group victory (or a single loss, depending on how you want to look at it)!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Bah! Humbug!: A Twelve Days of Christmas Bluffing Game
description:
Mail your True Love one Christmas card each day—and be sure to boast to everyone else about it. In order to keep up with all the other suitors, you might have to lie about what you’ve sent. But lying is half the fun! Will you win by being the sneakiest liar, or by catching others in THEIR lies?

Bah! Humbug! is a bluffing and deduction card game that uses a special card deck based on the traditional "Twelve Days of Christmas" carol (12 x Drummers Drumming, 11 x Pipers Piping, etc.) plus a deck of 12 cards that form a circular wreath or calendar in the center of the table.

Each round, players are dealt a hand of Christmas cards. They then take turns secretly mailing 1  Christmas card to their True Love (playing a card face-down on the calendar). These cards are played in descending order, from 12 to 1. As each player boasts about what card they've sent, other players may shout, “Bah! Humbug!” to catch them in a lie. Lies that go unchallenged are awarded "coal" at the end of the round, while lies that are successfully caught earn the accuser 1 "holly berry" (or take away holly berries, if the accuser is wrong.)

A player can win Bah! Humbug! in either of two ways: by being the most successful liar (collecting the most coal tokens), or by being the best at catching other liars (collecting the most holly berries).
family: Theme: Mail / Stamps / The Post Office

description:
Friendship is green, and we are colorful? Yes!

In reMEmber, colors have to be assigned to words. Players should always connect their choice with a personal story. Everyone at the table tries to remember this story because in the end it's the WE that counts and what all players are able to remember together!

At the beginning of the game, all players receive a set of twelve different color cards. In each round, they assign a word to a color card and tell the group why they chose that color. At the end of the game, all players together try to recall everyone's colors and stories. For each color or story that's incorrect or not told, the team gets one minus point. Of course, no one is allowed to name their own color or story.

reMEmber is a new edition of Farben that is now played co-operatively and contains many new words.

—description from the publisher

The highest cart total gets to take on the $100,000 Super Sweep and be commemorated in the Aisle of Champions.

Includes: 1 scoreboard, 3 team answer boards, 1 host board, 20 game sheets, 1 host question category guide, 166 Big Sweep item cards, 100 Super Sweep cards, 20 shopping list cards, 6 inflatables cards, 4 dry-erase markers, 1 phone holder, 3 carts, 1 Aisle of Champions scoreboard, and rules.

description:
Welcome to the Dodgeball World Tour! This game is designed to create an experience akin to Vince Vaughn and Ben Stiller’s movie version of professional dodgeball, but presented as a touring road show, similar to professional wrestling. On the Dodgeball World Tour, an ever-evolving collection of teams travel from city to city, entertaining fans with a nightly program that features an escalating series of matches.

Put together your team, line up on the court, rush for the balls, roll dice to score hits on your opponents and the team with the last player standing wins. Advance on the tour, improve your TV ratings and become the Dodgeball World Tour champion.

Use 36 cards ranking AKQJT9876 in each suit, plus 3 Jokers distinguished as Red, Black, and White. Deal 13 cards each in ones.

The objective is to win tricks and enable your right-hand neighbour to win more than the player on your left. The player with the greatest number of tricks that matches these conditions, wins.

Dealer's left-hand neighbour leads to the first trick. You must follow suit if you can, but may otherwise play any card. The trick is taken by the highest card of the suit led, or by the highest trump if any are played, and the winner of each trick leads to the next.

You score the number of tricks you won multiplied by the number won by your right-hand neighbour.

Deal 52 cards (or 36 if three play. For 2 players, see below) ranking AKQJT9876(5432). Deal them all out in ones. If five play, discard the odd two.

The objective is to win tricks of higher rather than lower value. The player with the highest number of points, wins.

Dealer's left-hand neighbour leads to the first trick. You must follow suit if you can, but may otherwise play any card. The trick is taken by the highest card of the suit led and the winner of each trick leads to the next.

Each trick is worth a different value, with values ascending, then dropping in value, then ascending again over the course of 10-13 tricks (depending on player count).

At first sight Dividend looks and plays like an ordinary four-player trick-taking game. The difference is that when you win a trick you don’t necessarily win all four of its constituent cards – you may, instead, win three, two or only one of them, depending on your position at the beginning of the trick. Thus at the end of a round of 13 tricks you score not for how many tricks but for how many cards you won, which could be anything from under 7 to over 20.
 Four can play either solo or, if preferred, in two partnerships, with partners sitting opposite each other and playing alternately to each trick.

Object
To win tricks, especially by playing two cards with the greatest possible difference between them. For this purpose cards have numerical values from Ace = 1 up to King=13. Thus if the cards you play are King-Ace the difference between them is 12 (=13-1), while if they are equal the difference is zero.

Each trick must contain exactly one card of each suit.

アングレット (unGret) is a 2 player card game from Japan. The publisher calls it 'psychological warfare' and 'mind-reading'. Each player has four cards numbered 1 to 4 and some markers. The players choose only card to go face-down. They must deduce which of their opponents cards is face-down and can spend markers to expose remaining hand cards. The game is balanced by the cost of markers against the chance of success.

description:
ユビエイム (You Be Aim) is a 2 player flicking game from Japan. The publisher Protocraft focuses on games with miniatures of mechas (brutalist robots). Players have a crawling mecha each, and must move around the factory to achieve goals. The mecha has a hollow body and the player loads a cylinder into the mecha and then fires it across the board by flicking the back of the mecha.

The game board in You Be Aim is double-sided, so players can play two different games. In one game, Watari Usagi (わたりうさぎ), they aim to fire cylinders at the rabbit robots of their opponent. In the other game, Jitsuriki Sugoroku (実力すごろく), the players must move around the factory and fire at targets on the walls without hitting the rabbit robots.

Customers who reserved early for the game at the Tokyo Game Market got a free metal miniature rabbit robot.

Seventeen tricks are played each hand; certain cards are worth certain points; the main object of the hand is to capture 61+ points.

With Cracking, Recracking, Schneidering, and Schwartzing, the game value can progress from as low as a single measly point for an entire hand of play, or as much as 64(!) points for the same hand (if both players are doubling fools).

description:
This game is based on Phat, a Scottish member of the All Fours family (whose members include Pitch, Pedro, and Don).

Twenty-one tricks are played each hand; the object is to peg points on a Cribbage board for capturing valuable cards. The game end is triggered when someone pegs 180 (or more) points. The hand is completed and the player with the higher score wins.

The game is quite simple to teach as it uses standard must-follow trick-taking. To increase balance, one player will pick a trump suit and the other player will pick the rank order (which cards are high or low). What elevates the game is its distribution of card points among the ranks; in particular, trying to avoid giving up the high Phat points for capturing the middle-ranking 9s makes for some interesting and sometimes quite tense decisions.

June 27, 1898, Baler, Principe District, Luzon Island (Philippines). With the arrival of the Filipino insurgents from the Katipunan and in the midst of the Spanish-American War, a Spanish detachment made up of 53 soldiers and the local priest took refuge behind the walls of the town church, remaining completely isolated. Thus began one of the longest sieges in history that lasted 337 days, where the Spanish, despite suffering so many hardships, defended the last Spanish overseas position with great heroism. It would not be until June 2, 1899, when the act of capitulation was signed. Discover and enter a siege so unique to this exciting.

Heroes of Baler 
On April 27, the US fleet of the Asiatic Squadron, commanded by Commodore Dewey, left Hong Kong for the Philippines. On May 1, he reached Manila Bay and defeated the Spanish fleet, commanded by Admiral Montojo, resulting in its entirety being sunk. The Cavite arsenal and plaza surrendered to the Americans the next day. The Battle of Cavite marked the beginning of the end of the Spanish presence in the Philippines. On May 19, Aguinaldo returned to the Philippines on a US ship to lead the revolution, which spread throughout the island of Luzon. At the end of May, news reached Baler about the start of the war against the United States and the defeat of the Spanish fleet in Cavite. These were some of the last news received by land from abroad since the rebels prevented the passage of news through the mountains. The Spanish in Baler were completely cut off.

The Filipinos tried at all costs to demonstrate to the Spanish detachment that the war had ended, but despite the numerous attempts at parliament that occurred during the months of siege, the Spanish refused to capitulate and surrender the plaza outright.
After numerous attacks and attempts to take over the church by the Filipinos, it would not be until the morning of June 2 when Martín Cerezo began to reread the newspapers that Aguilar had left behind and those, he thought were Filipino forgeries in order to confuse them. for them to give up. In one of them, he found a small review that made him see that the newspapers were true, as well as his news: he realized that the war was over and that there was no point in continuing to fight. The review, within the section of military destinations, was the announcement of the new destination in Malaga of the lieutenant of the scale of the Infantry reserve Francisco Díaz Navarro. Martín Cerezo had coincided with the officer, of whom he was a close friend, before he was assigned to Cuba. Díaz had told him that at the end of the campaign he planned to request a transfer to the Andalusian capital, where his girlfriend and her family lived, so Martín Cerezo accepted the news as true and, by extension, all the newspapers and news that Aguilar had left for the that finally, that same day, the capitulation of the Spanish detachment took place.

THE GAME
Heroes of Baler is a tactical game where players will take control of one of the two available sides (Spanish or Filipino). Both sides are asymmetrical so that the victory conditions and the way of playing are different for each side. Throughout the game the players (besieger and besieged) will try to achieve victory by carrying out firefights, raids, artillery attacks, repairs, trench construction...
This is a game for 1 to 2 players with a game length within one and two hours. In the two-player game each player will take the role of besieger (Filipino) or besieged (Spanish). In the solitary mode, the player will take the control of the Spanish as besieged faction.
 Each game turn represents approximately two months of siege. The main panel represents the town of Baler with the church that the Spanish defended in the center and around it the Filipino bahays and trenches. In addition, the board shows two exclusive areas one for each player.
Based on an game system which mix hand management and action points, the turn begins with the initiative phase where each side will try to seize the initiative in turn order. Next, in turn order the players will play their event phase where they may play up to two event cards and may play support cards to power up the played event(s) if they meet the requirements. Once this phase is finished, the events phase takes place where the Spanish player will have to deal with the lack of food that triggers the beriberi epidemic and the desertions while the Filipino side will have to deal with the supply of supplies and from the turn 4 with E.E.U.U. The player with the initiative will proceed to perform a mandatory firefight phase. After this phase comes the action phase. In turn order, players will play the rest of the cards in their hand as action points. The player second in turn order will proceed with the second phase of mandatory shootouts. Finally, a maintenance phase is carried out where the players will check victory conditions, take enemy fog of war cards and decide whether to give them to the enemy in exchange for a common card or keep them in their discard and prevent the enemy from obtaining that card. Finally, the military units are replaced in empty guard posts and detachment area, leader check rolls are made, and the next turn is prepared finally.

Components:
• 1 game board
• 110 playing cards (55 in Spanish and 55 in English)
• 55 cards solitaire mode (27 in Spanish, 27 in English and 1 help card in both languagues)

• 2 rulebook (Spanish and English)
• 2 scenario book (Spanish and English)
• 10 six-sided dice (5 blue dice and 5 red dice)

description: Players are given dice with symbols and minus or plus points. The dice are rolled simultaneously. Whoever rolls a red emoji may place it in the center of the game (if there are not already 3). A die with a green emoji must be passed on to the next player. A round is over when there are 3 red emojis in the center. Then each player counts his current plus or minus points and gets paid chips accordingly. Otherwise, the players continue to roll the dice. Whoever has collected the most plus points after 7 rounds wins the game.

Bimbo the monkey starts on a black space on one narrow side of the game board. The two keepers start on two black spaces on the opposite side. The monkey moves diagonally forward or backward. The Keepers may only move diagonally forward.

To win the game, the monkey has to pass the keepers to escape. The keepers win, if they enclose the monkey, so it can't move anymore.

Offered for Essen Spiel visitors.

CONTENT: 1 Messe Tile

SETUP & RULES:
After the setup, just add Messe Tile into the Drafting Bag (Tiles Supply). Messe Tile is a restricted tile. During the game, the player is eligible to move their cube onto the Messe Tile as soon as they have captured at least one themed tile (blue or orange or green).

boardgame: 札束と溶鉱炉 (Satsutaba to Youkouro)
description:
札束と溶鉱炉 (Satsutaba to Youkouro) is a 2 player bluffing card game from Japan. The players take two roles; one is building a road and the other is building on the road side. The designer likens the game to Skull. The game title translates as "Bankrolls and Blast Furnaces". The player who most recently fell into a blast furnace gets to go first.

One player arranges five good tiles (bankrolls) and one bad tile (a blast furnace) in a face-down line. The other player turns them over from left to right until they want to stop.

The turning player gets a point for each good tile they turned over, a bonus two points if they stopped just before the bad tile, and no points at all if they turned over the bad tile.

The placing player gets a point for each good tile they placed before the bad tile (i.e. each one that the turning player had an opportunity to take), plus a bonus two points if the turning player turned over the bad tile.

Swap places and do it again, do all of that three times with double points in the last round, whoever has the most points wins.

七福廊 (Shichi Fukurou) is a 2 player card game from Japan. Players are trying to collect seven wise owls to win, or lose getting the five unhappy cats. The publisher says the English title is Seven Lucky Owls. The Japanese title is likely a reference to the Shichi Fukujin (七福神), the Seven Lucky Gods; replacing the word fukurou (梟; owl) with a clever combination of fuku (福; luck, fortune, blessing) + rou (廊; corridor, hall, tower).

Players choose two hand cards face-down and reveal them together. One gets to choose whether the high or low number will win, and the high card of that pair wins. That winner then gets to use the effect on their winning card.

Players build up a tableau of cards, and aim to get the seven different owls to win the game. Otherwise, they try to force the unhappy cats onto the opponent. If a player gets five cats, they lose.

Publisher's summary:
In this lightweight two-player set collection game, you win by collecting Seven Lucky Owls before the opponent. On the other hand, you lose if five or more Unlucky Cats gather in your hand. Each card with a character also has a number on it. The players take
turns to choose whether a higher- or lower-number card will win.

Following that, they each play one of the two cards from their hand face-down, and then flip to reveal them simultaneously. Compare the numbers on the cards, and the winner can use the effect of the card they played.

Some cards have effects such as reversing the numbers and achieving a come-from-behind victory. Who would manage to outwit their opponent to force Unlucky Cats onto them or collect Seven Lucky Owls?

相手より先に7種類のふくろう「七福廊」を集めましょう！手軽に遊べる２人用セットコレクションゲーム。プレイヤーは相手より先に７種類のふくろう「七福廊」を集めると勝ちとなります。反対に、不幸の猫「黒闇天」を５枚以上集めてしまうと負けてしまいます。
それぞれのキャラクターが描かれたカードには数字も書かれています。毎回片方のプレイヤーが、数字の大きい方が勝つか小さい方が勝つかを選択。お互い２枚の手札から１枚を勝負札として裏向きで場に出します。伏せたカードを同時にオープンし、大小比べに勝ったプレイヤーは、カードに書かれている効果を使うことができます。
カードの中には大小カードを反転する効果や大逆転が狙える効果のついたものもあります。
読み合いを制して「黒闇天」カードをうまく相手に押し付け、「七福廊」を早く集めるのはどちらのプレイヤーでしょうか？

Unconventional tabletop warfare!

Each player is dealt one HQ card, which has its own tactical advantage. Players are then dealt cards, on their turns they draw a card and play a card, attack or defense. Forge and break alliances, wars on all fronts, the table is your warzone!

Each HQ can within stand 20 points of damage before falling, the damage is inflicted by horseshoes, hand grenades, and nukes! There are cards to repair your damage, but remember, nuclear damage is not repairable! Once your HQ hits 20 points of damage, you are out of the game, last HQ standing wins.

boardgame: ペンタキューブマッチ (Penta Cube Match)
description:
ペンタキューブマッチ (Penta cube match) is a 2 player abstract game from Japan. Players are trying to fill a narrow space with pentacubes against the clock.

A pentacube is made from five cubes joined in various different patterns. There are two sets of matching cubes, one set for each player. At the beginning of the game, all the pentacubes are mixed up in a bag, and each player draws three. They take turns placing two of them on the board in any position, and keep the third as their hand.

The board is a simple 6*7 grid and the pentacubes must not go outside of the grid. Nor can they go any higher than two cubes.

The remaining cubes are tipped out in a pile, and players cannot touch them. One player starts and must pick a pentacube to place by sight alone in ten seconds, counted down by the opponent (who is allowed to count fast!). Once they have chosen their piece, they take it from the pile, and either place it or the hand pentacube, so that it fits on the board and goes no higher than 2 cubes.

Players alternate this way until one player can no longer place legally, and they lose.

The designer points out that players suffer in the back end of the game from poor choices made at the opening. Planning ahead is crucial to survival.

ペンギンセンス (Penguin Sense) is a push your luck game from Japan. As Emperor Penguins, players swim out to see in search of food for their young. But they risk being attacked by predators such as seals or orcos.

Each player takes turns to take a food chip, worth 1-4, and then flip the Sea Card underneath the chip. Each card shows some aspect of life at sea, some are good fortune, some are bad luck.

Players can choose when to give up feeding and return to shore, but they cannot go out again. The game ends when either all the penguins have gone back, or when a second orca appears, which means any penguin still at sea loses all their food.

Of the penguins that got back home, the one with the most food value is the winner.

The game cards includes information, so the game is good for educating children (who can read Japanese).

boardgame: お元気ですか？～紫陽花祭り～ (How are you? ～Hydrangea Festival~)
description:
'お元気ですか？～紫陽花祭り～ (Ogenkidesuka? ~Ajisai Matsuri~) (How are you? ～Hydrangea Festival) is a 2 player postcard game from Japan. "お元気ですか？" How Are You? is the name of the series of postcards being introduced. Hydrangea Festival is the first game and was produced in memory of the designer's grandfather. It is sold with 3 postcards, so you can mail a copy of the game to somebody you miss.

In the game, players are trying to have raindrops fall onto the petal of the Hydrangeas to change their colours. The raindrops are pink or blue (the player's colours), and the flowers are pink or blue or purple.

The flowers change colour in sequence: pink, purple, blue. So a blue raindrop on a pink flower turns it purple, and another then turns it blue. A blue drop onto a blue flower locks the colour in place. The same works in reverse for pink drops.

Players take turns placing flowers in the garden, and then raindrops in the clouds. As the drops fall, the flowers change colour until the clouds have passed. Players win by having more flowers in their colour at the end.

In Okupa tu també! (Let's squat!), each player is a group that lives in the neighborhood, but the scam of unbearable rents and multi-life mortgages has left you and your people homeless — or at least homeless until now because you have said enough is enough, and you are determined to find a place to live and build your project by whatever means necessary, facing if necessary judges, police, thugs, renters and vulture funds.

In the game you represent one of sic groups: Okupat Social Center, Housing Union, Casal Popular, PAH, Neighborhood Family, or even the Mafia. By drafting cards, you accumulate resources (money, tools, training, and networks) in order to enter and occupy one of the empty houses on the game board. To do this, you need to spend the resources that the housing cards demand. Once inside, you have to resist for as long as possible police and law enforcement in order to try to win the game.

But wait! You can't do it alone. If you want your project to survive thugs and speculators, you need the support of your teammates, either because they're good or because they have no choice. They will give you resources, they will help you to overcome the letters of order and law, and they will (sometimes) even give you their victory points. Resisting an eviction for as long as possible and helping your teammates earns you points, and that will be the key to victory.

•••

A les vostres mans estarà escollir un dels sis col·lectius: Centre Social Okupat, Sindicat d'Habitatge, Casal Popular, PAH, Família del barri o fins i tot, la Màfia.

A través d'una selecció de cartes en forma de draft. Haureu d'acumular diversos tipus de recursos (diners, eines, formació i xarxa) per poder entrar i okupar una de les cases buides que hi ha haurà sobre el taulell de joc. Per fer això, hauràs de gastar els recursos que les cartes de casa et demanin, només així el teu projecte okupat començarà a caminar.

Un cop dins, haureu de resistir el màxim temps possible a les forces de l'ordre i la llei, per tal d'intentar guanyar la partida.

Però ep! No ho podreu fer soles. Si voleu que el vostre projecte sobrevisqui a matons i especuladors, necessitareu el suport de les vostres companyes, ja sigui per les bones o perquè no els hi queda més remei. Elles et cediran recursos, t'ajudaran a superar les cartes d'ordre i llei i fins i tot et podran arribar a cedir els seus punts de victòria.

Resistint el màxim  temps possible a un desallotjament i ajudar als teus companys et farà sumar punts, i això serà la clau de la victòria.

Imagine a baseball game where you call all the shots, not just your team but your team, your league, your world! On-deck baseball is an immersive table-top baseball experience where you have the ability to create a fictional baseball world on your terms.

On-Deck Baseball is a simplified version of Strat-O-Matic that allows players to create a base of players that were not tied to reality, a fictional universe. Percentages are all based off of 10s therefore it made the most sense to use the D10 die (a 10 sided die) so a person can truly see a representation of the percentages as possible outcomes, incorporating the use of a rolling matrix, result are obtained by cross reference the rolls of 2 different die, in this case a red and blue die.

Players will roll dice, gather resource cards, form dynamic alliances, and create trading strategies to learn mRNA splicing - a complex biological process in a board game. The player who gets 10 Protein cards first wins the game!

Easy to play! No prior knowledge is needed.

In every living organism, DNA carries the genetic information. DNA is first transcribed into mRNA in order to decode the genetic information. Then, mRNA is translated into proteins. The genetic information is often interrupted by "junk" fragments (introns) in higher organisms, such as humans. Therefore, the initial transcribed mRNA contains both genetic fragments (exons) and "junk" fragments. Introns should be removed and exons should be joined in a process called mRNA splicing. ​The splicing mechanism was awarded the 1993 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine.

—description from the publisher

Honour and Arms is a fantasy adventure game played on a map of a small unnamed region. Each player controls a character; there are 16 characters supplied, differentiated by their values in six different traits, their special abilities, and their alignments.

On a turn a player rolls a die for their number of movement points and then moves from space to space on the map. In each new space a die is rolled to determine how many (if any) encounter cards need to be drawn. Encounters may be items which can be taken or may be beings which can be fought or bargained with, or in some cases taken on as minions. Fighting provide experience, either physical or psychic, and when a character has three experience of a type they must turn them in for increases in traits.

There are a number of fixed locations on the board with special rules in a separate adventure booklet, and in some cases special decks of encounter cards. There are also minor locations which have cards in the standard decks and are placed on the board once found.

There are rules for encumbrance, player vs. player combat, healing, etc. Players may band together as a group. Some activities are regulated by alignment, of which there are two kinds: good vs. evil and imperial vs. rebel. For example, good characters can't attack without provocation.

There are several ways to win the game, all dependent on alignment. For example, a good character can achieve a spiritual victory by entering the Nether Realm and defeating the Prince of Darkness, while a rebel character can achieve a political victory by finding the king in his keep and defeating him. Some ways require finding the five rings forming the Hand of Fate, but if a character wears all of them at once they may become bewitched by them, in which case they get special powers but then can only win by killing all the other characters. The rules suggest that for a shorter game a time limit is set and then if no one has achieved a victory condition, a scoring rubric is provided.

For an ordinary deck or a Scopa deck. For the first ten tricks, players compete to win display cards ("jewels") from the talon by winning tricks. Display cards are placed into a row in front of each player known as their showcase ("la vetrina"). Winning showcase cards gives you points, but at a cost: it if a player leads with a card matching a suit of a card in your showcase, you must follow suit for that trick. Moreover, cards from your showcase may only be used to follow, and your opponent sees your cards. For the second ten tricks, once the talon is gone, you play out your hand and showcase. Preparing  your evolving hand to win more points overall is a cerebral conundrum.

Some unique features include that in the first half it's easier to lose tricks than to win them (since phase-1 is played "all trump": any card of an off suit wins). Simultaneously, the showcase allows the leader to determine if their opponent must follow suit or not-- so for connoisseurs of trick games there are new interesting puzzles.

—description from the designer

The game of Inter-Rail is about traveling around Europe, by train, experience as much as possible, buy souvenirs to take home, and in that way get most points to win the game.

You do this by rolling the die, move around the board, and land on spaces to draw city or chance cards. This goes on for a specified amount of time, usually 1-2 hours, and when you get home to Start (Oslo), you draw point cards to see how valuable your purchased items are.

The player with most points wins the game.

—user summary

You and up to 3 frenemies are Managers on the climb up the brutal corporate ladder. Manage your team of familiar employees and win by completing projects OR by getting everyone else FIRED by HR instead!

The game starts with all Employee Cards being dealt to each Manager. Each Manager is then dealt 5 cards which be their hand. During a turn, a Manager can choose to play a Misconduct Card OR a Project Management Card OR an Action Card from their hand. At the end of a turn, Managers redraw to make sure they have 5 cards in their hand.

There are two ways of Winning HR.

The first is to eliminate all other Manager's Employees by serving them Warning Letters for Misconducts. When an Employee receives 3 Warning Letters, the Employee is Fired and placed in the Retrenched Pile. Some Misconduct Cards would deal 2 Warning Letters to specific types of Employees instead of 1.

The second way of winning the game is when a Manager successfully completes an entire Project Cycle by putting down all 4 stages of Project Management Cards.

boardgame: 私立ゴロ合ワセ大学 (Pun University)
description:
私立ゴロ合ワセ大学 (Shiritsu goro gō wase daigaku) (Pun University) is a party game from Japan. 私立ゴロ合ワセ大学 translates as Private Goro Goiwase University. Players are trying to pass the university entrance exam, which requires making witty puns.

Players take turns to be the examiner and select a topic from two cards. Once declared, the players write puns on their whiteboards, and get a few seconds more after one player stops.

The puns are read out, and the players award points to the best.

Then the examiner asks questions. Players have to pass the exam questions using the puns read out.

After a few rounds, the player with the most points is the winner.

This game is the second in a series of 'Nappy' games by the publisher.
user summary

The game contains two types of cards: levels (with numbers from 1 to 9) and balloons (with addition and subtraction operations; for example, a balloon card with a three has both "+3" and "- 3").

Each player starts with 6 cards after mixing the two decks, and one card of each type is placed in the center of the table. In turns, each player can throw all the level cards they want, as long as the number that appears on them is the same as the one on the level card on the table, or the result of applying any of the operations to said number of the balloon letter. You can also shoot balloon cards if it makes it possible to shoot another level card later.

The player who first runs out of cards in hand wins a star. The one with the most stars will win the game.

boardgame: 12星座のガールズギターリフダービー (The 12 Zodiac Signs Girls Guitar Riff Derby)
description:
12星座のガールズギターリフダービー (The 12 Zodiac Signs Girls Guitar Riff Derby) is a card game from Japan. The publisher says "A music competition for primary school children, with no psychological warfare. Let's get into the rock swamp!

The game is a tournament between 8 high school girls chosen from the 12 signs of the Zodiac. You might think that this is a beauty contest or a story about a prince who wants to become a princess, but it's not. It's a battle of rock guitar riffs.

The game is short and minimalistic, so all but the finals are decided in a "rock-paper-scissors" kind of way, with lots of draws. Until the final, you may not reveal which girl you are favouring.

In the final, you will have to play the Do-Re-Mi... on the card as if it were a guitar riff battle. （The final is a guitar riff competition. (The author has arranged the music, but there is no stave, so you will have to make corrections and add any missing notes.)

The game is very light and quick to play. Recommended for primary schools children. You can also play "Horoscope Detective Michika" using the same 16 cards to guess your opponent's 12 signs of the zodiac.

心理戦とか無い(精神年齢)小学生向き音楽系勝ち抜き戦。ロック沼にはめよう

この「12星座のガールズギターリフダービー」も12星座から選ばれた８人の少女によるトーナメント戦です。
少女たちによる対戦なので美人コンテストや王子様のプリンセス目指す話でしょと思われるかもしれませんが違います。ロックのギターリフを競う戦いとなります。
短時間でゲームが終わるミニマムゲームのため決勝以外は「引き分け多めのジャンケン」のような方法で勝負が決まります。決勝までどの少女を推してるか伏せても構いません。
決勝ではカードに記しているドレミ…をギターリフ合戦を繰り広げるように競います。（作者が編曲していますが五線譜がないので補正したり足りない音があれば加えたりしなければなりません⁈ヒントはその少女がリスペクトしているミュージシャンです）。
とても軽くスカッとプレイするゲームです。精神年齢小学生におススメです。
［同じカード16枚使って、相手の12星座を当てる「星占い探偵ミチカ」もプレイできます］
publisher's summary

Spin Fighters were die-cast metal top toys that were manufactured by Bandai in China in the mid 1990s (1993-1997).

The Spin Fighters tops were loaded into Launchers (Later, called "Power Launchers"), which were then tightly wound by fingertip, and subsequently released into the Spin Fighters Battle Arena (or, onto a flat surface). The tops battled (hitting against each other) until the last top spinning was the winner.

Spin Fighters tops were ~1 inch across, came in black and gold colors, and bore a prismatic sticker featuring a character from a TV show or video game. Usually, the black tops represented the "Bad Guys" and the gold tops represented the "Good Guys." Spin Fighters were sold two to a package: one gold and one black spin fighter. Power Launchers and Battle Arenas were available separately or bundled. Spin Fighters tops can be disassembled and worn on jackets, shirts, and hats, through any small button hole.

Production of Spin Fighter ceased around 1997. They are widely seen to be the forerunner to Beyblade, which began production in 1997.

Spin Fighters series included: World Wrestling Federation (WWF), Saban's Mighty Morphin Power Rangers, and Capcom's Street Fighter.

Doppeldoppel is a trick-taking game for 3 to 4 players about carrying souls down to a lower world.  However, if two souls descend at the same time, they will disappear!

The game uses an irregular deck of cards, with 1-3 cards in each of ranks 1-6 in each of 3 suits.  Depending upon the player count, some cards are played face down, and others face up, but it is a "must-follow" game, and the highest ranked card of the lead suit will win.  However, in keeping with the theme, if two or more cards of the same rank are played, they are removed from consideration.

The winner of the trick collects any remaining cards into a score pile.  All cards start at -1 points, but each rank has a unique scoring or play affect.  For instance, if you have at least three cards of rank 1, each of your rank 1 cards is worth +2 points instead of -1; each rank 5 card is worth +7 points if you have no rank 6 cards.

The game lasts a number of rounds equal to the number of players; at the end, the person with the most points wins.

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Call to Arms – Histories of the Empire Volume 1: The Stormcloak Rebellion
description:
Histories of the Empire, Volume 1: The Stormcloak Rebellion is a narrative driven expansion to The Elder Scroll: Call to Arms. Enabling its players to take part in pivotal events such as the search for the Jagged Crown and the Battle for Whiterun, culminating with the final push on the capitals of Solitude or Windhelm.

It includes a three-part campaign detailing the rise or fall of the Stormcloak Rebellion in their fight against the Imperial Legion. The first act details the skirmishes and rising tensions that lead to the Battle for Whiterun. The second places the player in command of their chosen faction, allowing them to tactically decide which holds to attack and gradually push their front lines toward the enemy’s capital. The third and final act entirely focuses on the siege of a faction’s capital, moving from the walls into the city itself and culminating in the hero’s facing each other for a final confrontation that will decide the fate of Skyrim.

Included within this book are new rules for setting your battles within any of the nine Holds of Skyrim. From Haafingar with its soaring peaks to The Rift and its many beast nests. These new Hold rules allow players to further expand on the environment mechanics presented in the core rules and experience more thematic battlefields.

Building and expanding on the existing rules for Elder Scrolls: Call to Arms, this book offers new and exciting experiences for fighting in Tamriel. Adding extra effects to environments, altering what equipment each faction can take and offering thematic bonuses based on the holds the player controls, these expanded rules add a new depth to the game so the player can truly control how their faction handles the war.

Also included are rules for an entirely new faction - Hold Guards. Included are Heroes, Followers and Adversary cards that can be used as allies or enemies in your struggle for dominance.
family: Video Game Theme: The Elder Scrolls

description:
DTwww is a trick-taking game without cards.  The game uses 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, and 20 sided dice in 3 suits and a wild suit.  Players draft the dice from a bag -with explicit rules' permission to feel for the number of sides they want, as long as it doesn't take too long- with some of the dice being public in front of a player screen, and others being behind it.

Once everyone has drafted their dice, they are rolled.  The game is "must-follow", but the lowest number played of the lead suit will typically win the trick, and the highest number played, regardless of suit, will lead to the next trick.

The winner takes their die and sets it in a special scoring cup; dice from the cup will be rolled at the end of the round to determine each player's rank, with higher rolls being awarded with more points.

The player with the most points after a number of rounds wins.
publishers: Tanuki Tsune Komuten (たぬきつね工務店)

description:
Nervos is a real-time puzzle game that combines elements of Ubongo with Ghost Blitz.

The game includes a stack of puzzle cards and 4 polyomino pieces.  Each puzzle card shows a shape that can be made using exactly 3 of the pieces.  A puzzle card is revealed and the players race, mentally, to determine which piece is not used.  Once they feel confident, they grab the piece, and then have a few seconds to "show their work" by completing the shape with the other pieces.

The games ends when a player successfully completes 4 cards; alternatively, players are eliminated if they incorrectly answer 3 cards.

VICKE is an abstract game where each player has a ship, sailing the seas, looking for the two islands of their opponent's that are hiding treasure.

On a player's turn, they can choose from three basic actions (or pass), and have an additional action if they choose.  The basic actions involve sailing their ship, creating a route, or checking for treasure.  The additional action allows a player to move their ship along the route they have created.

Players alternate turns until a player has discovered both of their opponent's treasures; the discovering player wins.
publishers: ピーナツデザイン (Peanuts Design)

boardgame: Doctor Quack: The Game of Pills and Ills
description:
"In a little village in Fairy Tale Land, there lived a friendly old doctor, who all his life wanted nothing more than to help people. Whenever a call came in, he grabbed his bag and rushed to the patient's bedside. Very often, however, he found that in his haste, he had brought the wrong cure. But did that stop him? No, he tried anyway, and, funny as it may seem, his patients would always get well."

Players each choose a color of playing piece, and place them on Doctor Quack's house in the center of the playing board, to start. Each player draws one Cure Card. Players spin to see who goes first.
