In Asteroid Field X, you play as one of four factions mining an inhospitable asteroid belt.  The object of the game is to build and upgrade mining infrastructure to both keep your colony alive and protected and to mine and sell as much precious ore as possible.  The faction that accumulates the most Creschins (money) from their sales will win the game.  However, there is an “X-factor” in play: buried somewhere in the uncharted regions of this asteroid field is a mysterious alien artifact that emits terrifying forms of energy.  It is of great value in this hyper-capitalist world, and of equally great danger.  If you unearth it and manage to transport it back to your base dome, you can sell it to the ore transport ships for an enormous profit.  But given the difficulty of handling it, someone else might steal it before you can accomplish this!

Asteroid Field X is a limited edition game by Zach Horton.  It is based upon a prototype he designed in 1992.  It functions as a prequel to Supernova, an origin story about a civilization’s extractavist excesses, when mining colonies supplied the raw materials to fuel a powerful but fragile empire—before Object X caused their home star to become unstable, leading to the desperate conditions of Supernova.

Players build and upgrade mining infrastructure in order to extract base metals (not very valuable, but needed to manufacture equipment), premium metals (the chief export of the mining colony, but also used to manufacture some items), and volatile compounds (substances needed to maintain your colony such as water and minerals).  Beyond strategizing between the payoffs of these substances, you need to maintain transportation networks to return your ore safely to your base dome.  You may build various other units to be used for sabotage, protection, or direct attacks.  Asteroid Field X makes extensive use of hidden movement, and ships can be equipped with cloaking devices to render their locations invisible to other players.  Many cargo cards offer special capabilities to players who develop and play them.  Some of these are played covertly and revealed anonymously, to often unpredictable results.  Combined, these mechanisms allow players to pursue secret campaigns of sabotage, openly hostile actions, and peaceful diplomacy, often all at once!

After all players have taken their turn, each round ends with a “diplomatic phase” in which players simultaneously manufacture new units, deploy covert cards that will be revealed in the next round, and decide how much ore to sell to the waiting merchant ships.  The base prices of metal ore fluctuate during the game, as they are tied to a semi-randomized timeline of market cards.  The final price that each player receives for their ore, however, is determined by how much total ore is dumped on the market that round by all players.  Thus betting and bluffing play a significant role, as players attempt to manipulate the market to favor their own interests. After 12 rounds (each represented by a Market card), the game ends, with the player who has received the most money declared the winner.

boardgame: The Roads of Europe (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
This mini expansion has been designed by Carcassonne.cat to celebrate the first European (Online) Team Championship. In doing so, we have encapsulated elements of the global Covid-19 pandemic such as the recommendation to use a protective mask to minimize the spread of the virus, and the need for self-isolation among those who may have been in contact with it.
Thus, the meeple with stars between the two roads depicted on the tile symbolizes the geographical context of the championship and the exceptional situation in Europe, and throughout the rest of the world.

Setup

The Roads of Europe tile should be mixed in with the other tiles as usual

How to use the tile

Following the basic rules for laying tiles, proceed as normal according to the following cases.

In 2-player games, promote a more competitive use

When a player draws this tile and places it such that it extends a road (or roads), any meeple(s) occupying these roads must immediately be removed from them without scoring. These meeples have been in contact with the virus and must go home to self-isolate! The active player must then place each removed meeple into separate incomplete and unoccupied cities of his choice, somewhere on the landscape. Meeples may not be moved into any city that is already occupied.
If there are not enough unoccupied cities available to accommodate all removed meeples separately, the active player chooses which meeples to place and which to return to their owner. After all removed meeples have either been moved to cities or returned to their owners, the active player is granted a bonus turn and must draw a new tile and place it according to the usual rules.
If the tile is placed on an unclaimed road (i.e. one without a meeple), it may not be claimed by the active player and no bonus turn is given.
The road may be extended on subsequent turns, but may not be claimed until such time that it is completed. The player that completes the road may choose to claim and score it, as per the usual rules, as long as they have at least one meeple in their supply.

With 3 or more players, promote a more playful and fun use -

The rules for games with more than two players follow the same rules as outlined above for games with two players, but with one exception: when the road is closed, all players who have not closed the road will lose as many points as there are tiles on the road.

Special cases


     While meeples may never be placed as thieves on any of the road segments on the special tile, the fields are safe from the virus and may be claimed according to the usual rules by a meeple as a farmer.



     There may be a situation where the placement of the special tile automatically closes a road. In that case, if there are meeples on it, they would return to their owners and the player who placed the tile would not be able to claim the road and the other players would not lose points either.



     In the case of playing with expansions, all the tiles with special effects would disappear as part of the road expansion before closing. In any case, the tokens could still be used as normal tokens. Example: The mini tunnel expansion contributes to the expansion of the road without adding any special effects, but the princess and the dragon or inns and cathedrals contain symbols that are canceled when they are part of the road.

boardgame: Flying Colors: Beat to Quarters, Vol II
description:
Beat to Quarters, Vol II (Serpents of the Seas) is an official expansion to Serpents of the Seas by GMT Games, LLC and requires a copy of that game to play.

One of the most common criticisms of the Flying Colors system is the clutter of status markers on the map and that they may detract from game play. This concern can be minimized somewhat by using status sheets and a handy pencil to keep track of things on paper. This, however, adds a separate record-keeping aspect that the design of the game goes out of its way to avoid.

Beat to Quarters provides the ultimate way by which to keep track of the status of individual ships through the use of individual tracking cards that are kept conveniently near the map and that are laid out to conveniently hold a variety of status markers such as Broadsides Fired, Sail State, Rigging Damage, and Hull Damage. The cards are designed to be printed at a standard 3.5 by 2.5 inch size so that they can be slipped into card protectors for extended usage.

Individual tracking cards are included for all 255 individually named ships included with Serpents of the Seas. But that’s not all!

As a bonus, Beat to Quarters also includes the print-and-play components for an entirely new way to play Serpents of the Seas. Drawing from games such as Naval War, Modern Naval Battles, and (in particular) Enemy in Sight, Beat to Quarters can be played with just the ship tracking cards, a special set of markers and 8.5 x 11 inch tracking board, and the status markers from Serpents of the Seas. The ship cards are organized into squadrons that maneuver and fire against the opposing fleet within a relatively small playing area. With Beat to Quarters, you can play the huge battles without the space requirements for several game maps.

NOTE: This volume of Beat to Quarters only includes marker sheets for the British, French, and American fleets. There are insufficient Spanish, Venetian, and East India Company ships to form a fleet, but these ships can be included in the fleets of the other nationalities as desired or added to the cards from the first volume of Beat to Quarters.

With Beat to Quarters, you can either enhance or completely change your enjoyment of Serpents of the Seas!
Card play modifications to Flying Colors.

—description from the publisher

description: In the Nordens Ark Familje-Quiz, everyone, regardless of age, can test their knowledge of animals, nature and the environment. The game includes children's questions for the youngest, junior questions from 8 years and senior questions from 15 years and up. The game is quick and easy. Roll the dice to decide your question. If you answer correctly, you can take an animal badge out of the "secret bag". If the animal on the badge is the same as on the question card, you may take another badge. Who, with the help of knowledge and a little luck, first manages to gather three identical animal badges to win?

The Olympian gods are up to their usual mischief, again!  In this Greek mythological game, you'll assume the hidden identity of an Olympian god. Zeus has granted you the ability to wield his Thunderbolts in a Tournament of the gods. You’ll capture mythological creatures as you build an army to command in an epic contest for fame, fortune and most of all, bragging rights in Mt. Olympus. In this mystically veiled bidding and battling game, the god that scores the most points wins.

This is a fun, fast paced action game that is played in three distinct phases, the capture, command and scoring phases. As part of the game, there is a hidden god role card that each player has assumed. Only the gods know when you'll reveal your god identity and how your divine power will be used to enhance your play and stifle others.

In the capture phase, using a bidding style mechanic, you’ll use your limited supply of Thunderbolt tokens to capture mythological creatures from around the table. Gathering information is a key element of this phase because each player will only be allowed to see the creature’s strengths on other players stands. Their own creature card will be on a stand facing away from them and therefore unknown. So, if you surrender, you’ll need to take the lowest strength creature you can see.

After the capture phase, each player will then command their army of mythological creatures one at a time to battle for treasures! This phase is an epic competition for various treasures (gold, armor, valor, temples and worshippers) that only interest the gods. Treasure cards have different point values that range between 1 - 10 and various symbols that are part of your set collections and end game scoring. But be aware that worshippers, the 9 and 10 point value treasure cards, don't have any symbols while the golden Apple, the 1 and 2 point value treasure cards, have a wild symbol that can be used in any one of your set collections.

After all the battling and treasure collecting is done, it’s time to move into the scoring phase and tally up your scores. Unused thunderbolts, treasure cards, set collections (combination of creatures and treasure cards) and any god card bonuses all count towards your grand total. The player, or Olympian god with the most points wins and gets bragging rights in Olympus... for now.

Cuistot Fury is a competitive team game for 2 to 9 players divided into 2 to 3 brigades of cooks. Their goal: to prepare the best recipes and serve all their customers, in a fierce time trial where every second is precious.

When it's your turn:

     Chose the recipe to prepare. The more complicated the recipe, the more points you get
     Cook as a team


Ingredients and ustensils cards are split between the cooks of the team. Coordinate to make recipes by stacking the cards on the cooking board in the relevant order, before the end of the hourglass!

     Serve the customers


The hourglass helps you put pressure on your competitors, since the faster you are, the less time they have to cook. 
Advanced rules can also add more fun in the competition, with combo mechanisms to serve several customers at the same time, funny challenges for your opponents, and special powers...

Talaref is a trivia game that revisits the 90s and 2000s

The goal is to accumulate the most "refs" (the points in the game) alone or in team, by answering correctly questions on TV shows, music, cinema, news, sports... related to the 90s and 2000s !

When it's your turn, roll the dice. 
If it's a category, answer the question to win the card and the associated "refs". The more difficult the question is, the more refs you get (from 1 to 3). 
The dice can also indicate a challenge. In this case, compete with your friends by proprosing an answer on the topic described on the card, one after the other. The winner can steal a card to its opponent :)

600 cards

boardgame: Darkstar: Tactical Starship Combat in the 26th Century
description: Darkstar is a tactical war game postulating naval combat in a “science faction” universe set five centuries into a troubled and uncertain future. Players take command of warships serving in the new “black water” navies of reborn empires of old, struggling for control of shipping lanes, resources, and colonies. Ships maneuver and fire in fast-paced combat, with survival not only of the players’ fleets at stake, but perhaps their nation and all of humanity as well.

Based on the popular BBC TV show of the 80's and 90's, be the first player to get all four of your Star Tokens around the board and back to your remote control.

Along the way, answer questions to double your move, or take on the "Hoofer Doofer".  Hoofer Doofer squares involve your opponents guessing a TV prgogramme via either an impression, charade or hummed theme tune.

Some tactics take place with up to two of your tokens on the board at any one time, as you have to decide whether to play it safe or risk gifting an opponent an extra move.

babaOrum is a board game that plunges you into the world of pastry. To win the clash of the chefs, take your chance and be strategic!

Each player is a baker with a special ability. At the beginning of the game, each player receives an order which is his secret objective and indicates the recipes he has to make to win the game.

Each player is also given face-up recipes that he or she can make.

When it's his turn to play, a player can: 

     First, validate a pastry he prepared in the previous turn if relevant
     Then, try to win recipe, ingredient or action cards, either by betting on luck, or by betting on strategy, or by answering a question about pastry
     Finally, he can play action cards from his hand, or prepare one of his recipes with the ingredient cards he has in his hand. 


The action cards spice up the game, by allowing to exchange or steal cards, to exchange the chefs' special abilities... In short, nothing is won in advance!

As soon as a player has finished preparing the recipes on his secret order, he wins the game!

In the Pleasant Nonsense expansion pack for the Sweet Existence game, a few things have changed from the Sweet Existence game. Instead of Existence Squares, you’ll have Nonsense Squares that are dreams with unique powers. Perishing is no more. The Consciousness Persists Square is hiding in your Nonsense Squares and prevents you from dreaming so you can’t win. Action Squares multiply chances of mishaps, while Sucrose Sweet Disks make for a sweeter victory. Each game includes 1 of 3 possible mysterious object tokens to track the Active Being. The first being to pay for all 3 Nonsense Squares in front of them wins! It's a marvelous, light-strategy party game for current and budding Strange Planet fans ages 13 and up. —description from the publisher

The Game of Gelato, also known as 'Il gioco del Gelato', is a card game about gaining ingredients to prepare a quality ice cream: fresh cream, milk, beet sugar, seasonal fruit… Recipe book in hand, the winner is whoever manages to please customers by guessing their favorite flavors.

The box is made up of 110 cards, which are then divided into Customer cards, Ingredient cards and Base Ingredient cards; there is no shortage of recipes and regulations. The deck of customer cards, marked with an ice cream symbol, are to be placed in the center of the table and are revealed during construction to form the "shop". In turn, each player decides whether to prepare an ice cream to serve the customer or to obtain the necessary ingredients. To prepare the ice cream, the player must discard the necessary ingredients from his hand by first checking the recipe in the cookbook. The “wild” cards can replace any ingredient. The more ice cream of customers' favorite flavors the player prepares, the more victory points he earns.

The player with the most points when the deck runs out, wins.

boardgame: Merchants Cove: The Entertainer Crossover Pack
description:
This expansion adds a new Character to play in Merchant's Cove - the Bard.  Assume the role of a fantastical Bard on a journey for fame and glory. Enjoy a unique tempo-dial mechanism in your quest to be the best.

Requires requires board and components from Bardwood Grove or Bardwood Grove: Spirits of the Grove

Tin Spin is a mix of physical skill, luck, and a bit of strategy. You will split your turns either rolling your die or spinning it, trying to spin longer than your fellow players. It’s a game that comes in a mint tin, making it super compact and easy to bring places, though you will need a table with a hard surface to play on (no playing on carpet, grass, etc…).

The game has 25 gold cubes. The first player to earn 5 of them wins.

You earn cubes using the seven unique dice. Six can go to players (it supports 2-6 players) and the seventh is used to swap dice after each player's turn. It’s rare that you’ll have the same die every turn.

Each die has its strengths and weaknesses, and physical skill at dice spinning makes a difference. It’s surprising how easy it is to learn how to spin dice though. Some dice are more likely to earn you a cube when rolled, some are better at spinning, and you choose each turn to roll or spin. Either way you end your turn by rolling the swap die and doing what it says to swap dice with other players.

Sichere Six is the second entry in the "Nein Nine" series of trick-taking card games, and it introduces Fate's Mercy and Mirth.  Players have the opportunity to stick their opponents with the painful penalty cards.  If they feel brave, they can try to collect all of the penalty cards and bring down their opponents' scores even more. Sichere Six begins as a free-for-all.  As the game progresses, temporary partnerships will form, backstabbery will ensue, and only one will escape Fate's game with the victory.

Over the course of no more than nine rounds, players will be trying to collect as many tricks as possible while avoiding dirty tricks that contain 9's.

During each round, the player who first collects a 9 becomes The Chosen and is granted Fate's Mercy. They get to exchange one card with the kitty.  If they are feeling brave enough, they can try to collect all of the 9's and trigger the special scoring known as Fate's Mirth.

The first player to earn 9 points wins the game.  If the game does reach the final round, the player with the highest score wins.

boardgame: Last Full Measure: The Battle of Gettysburg (Second Edition)
description:
A new edition of the first Last Full Measure title; Gettysburg with a larger map in size and area covered; new map art and colors; new charts and tables; and new counters.

The Last Full Measure Series is a collection of brigade-level wargames covering battles of the American Civil War. The games use regiments to represent the brigades on the map, and allow them to occupy multiple hexes. LFM is an intermediate rules set that includes cavalry charges, ranged artillery fire, and a simple command system, at a scale 250 yards per hex and 100 men per strength point.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Ramen — for some, it is a brutally delicious noodle soup originally from Japan. For its makers, ramen is the center of an intensely brutal fight in a mega-market. In the metropolis of Tokyo, cooks compete fiercely for the best ingredients so they can satisfy their customers' needs and desires.

In Ramen Chef, you are at the forefront of this battle. Play as one of up to four chefs who run their soup business with sophistication and brains in order to become Tokyo's greatest ramen chef. Your greatest weapon? Your smart head!

Ramen Chef features a literal flip of a classic memory game. You are trying to match ingredients from a 3x3 grid, and collect ingredients that match a secret objective. However, all of the ingredient tiles are double-sided, meaning you need to remember both sides of the tiles in front of you. On your turn, you must verbally state the ingredient you see BEFORE you flip, then flip the tile to reveal the opposite side. If after the second flip, your two tiles (on their opposite sides) now match, you are able to collect one of those tiles for your restaurant and work towards fulfilling your secret objective tile. The player with the most tiles — both ingredients and completed objective tiles— wins.

But be careful! If the opposites sides of your target tiles do not match, you collect nothing, and may leave a combination of tiles that your opponent can now use instead. Surprisingly tactical and shockingly difficult for only nine tiles, Ramen Chef is sure to leave you questioning your memory abilities and hungry for ramen!

—description from the publisher

Roll & Colour: Hearts is a two-player game, played on an A5 greeting card. Imagine giving a card that is also a ten-minute game! On the front of the card is a picture, while on the back the rules are laid out neatly.

The aim of the game is to color in the hearts in different colors. One player takes the inside of the hearts, while the other takes the outside. You score points for hearts that are not next to, i.e. that don't fall into the same row and column as, a heart of the same color. To play, lay the card flat on the table. Label the columns 1-3 and the rows 4-6. Next, choose a different color for each roll of the die, so 1 might be red, 2 might be orange, and so on.

On a turn, you roll three dice, then allocate one of the results to a column, one to a row, and one to a color, then fill in a blank heart in that column and row using that color. So if you roll 3, 4, 6, then you could fill a heart in column 3 and row 4 with color 6, or you could fill a heart in column 3 and row 6 with color 4. The game ends when all hearts are colored, and whoever has the most points wins.

In Let Sleeping Dons Lie your goal is to get out of your criminal life with enough loot for a fresh start before the Don wakes up.

As a member of the Don’s crime family, you begin with a hand of 7 cards and some loot. The Don is laid out sleeping in the middle of the play area. Based on how big your crime family is, will determine how much plunder there is to go around.

Each turn you draw a card from either the draw or discard piles.
Then Play a card onto the discard pile, following any actions that may be on the card. When you manage to pick up a total of 7 of one type of plunder, the round ends and the plunder is stashed into Loot bags. Then another round starts. You escape when you are the first to reach enough loot bags for a fresh start.

If the Don wakes up then the game is over and you have to get out with what you loot you can carry.

Karrier is a board game where 3-6 players compete. You need to clean up your dedicated area and reach the sacred altar to win. Get rid of all the trash in your land. Beware, some players can be sneaky. Secretly dump trash in other areas or throw it to opponents’ karriers! Blow others’ Karriers up! Form your own strategy and be the first to take all the honor and reward!

On their turn, players draw and play cards, load trash and move around the board.

The game ends when a player has achieved the goal or when the draw deck runs out of action cards.

The first player to clear the Trash token of their own territory and enter the Sacred Altar with an empty Karrier claims Victory.

A push your luck dice game of making real Cocktail and Appetizer recipes with cards of real recipes of cocktails and appetizers.

Take 3 random cards, roll the dice to try and roll the "Plate" or "Glass" to start your recipe card then start finding the ingredients you need to finish the recipe card, if you finish a card you can keep the points on the card or you can re-roll the unused dice, but if you don't roll an ingredient you can use you lose all the dice you've collected and no score collected and play passes to the next player.

Play continues until one a player reaches a score of 20.

The players form two teams and take turns to roll and move. Each team controls four pawns. They start at home at space 1 and have to reach the train station at space 35. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

The first team to get four pawns to the train station wins the game.

The game was published just after WWII. It came in an envelope and the meeples consisted of small aluminium cylinders, perhaps leftovers from other productions.

—user summary

You’re snuggled up warm and drowsy in your bed when it strikes: the need to use the bathroom. If you try to just clench down and ignore it, you could ruin your comfy cozy bed and have to wake up your parents. So you decide to navigate your way past spooky shadows and scary noises to relieve your need so you can get back to bed and finally fall to sleep.

Mission: Bathroom Run is a game for 2 or more players. The only thing required is 1 six-sided die per player and 1 deck of playing cards for every 2 players.  Draw a card, roll a die, and see if you make it to the bathroom!

—description from the designer

On each of 36 cards are 2 to 6 different animals. The cards are laid out face down. The player whose turn it is rolls the dice and tries to uncover a card with the animal on it. If the player succeeds, he may collect the cards that have been revealed so far, or continue rolling the dice and reveal another card. If the player does not succeed, all revealed cards must be turned over again and it is the next player's turn. The player who collects the most cards wins the game.

—user summary

Silk, the new Colt Express bandit…
… For whom you can choose or make up your own power!*

You can choose one of the three following special powers:

     Move power: Move 1 or 2 Cars, whether you are inside the train or on the roof.
     Dodge power: Give to the player of your choice the Neutral Bullet you get when you encounter a Lawman (Marshal or Shotgun) and then change positions as specified in the rulebook. 
     Block power: You block the rear door entrance. No other Character (Bandit or Marshal) can enter that Car through the rear door.


All players must agree on the power of Silk before choosing their Bandit.

—description from the publisher

The players set up their satellite dishes between the two planets with the spikes pointing at the double lines on their respective spaces. Then the players take turns rolling a die and rotating one of their dishes according to he result of the roll.
The first player to get to align the spikes in a straight line between the planets wins the game.

—user summary

boardgame: Untamed: Feral Factions – Spirit Strike expansion
description:
The first expansion to the fast-paced shuffle-building card battle game, Untamed Feral Factions: the Spirit Strike expansion adds the Snakes, Gorillas and Deer into the mix, adding new keywords and abilities, and enabling powerful new combos!

Moreover, the Squirrel and Platypus factions are also available to add even MORE variety to your Untamed Feral Factions game!

Content also available in Upgrade KIT: The Upgrade Kit includes the 3 new factions, plus all Spirit Strike Edition content: premium tokens, premium Quick Setup (vac)Tray, and all applicable Stretch Goals and Exclusives.

The original game is needed to to use the expansion or upgrade kit.

Super-Charge your Word Building But avoid the Fletter Fuse bombs!

In Fletter Fuse, 2-6 players have to fuse Single, Double and Triple letters together to make words. Everyone plays at the same time, so you have to think fast and act faster than your opponents if you want to win. The aim of the game is to make the most points.

Players toss cards with letters into a joint pool.  The first player to see a word of 5 letter or more, shouts the word out and collects the cards.  Once the deck runs out, the cards are added up and the player with the most points wins.

A book of 15 small games, some including boards, that were published in remembrance of game designer Piero Cioni.

"In June 2019, a great friend and colleague, Piero Cioni, left us prematurely, and many Italian authors wanted to create this work that, in addition to remembering and paying homage to him, wants to make a small contribution to the organization that has supported him in his latest days and which offers daily assistance to cancer patients and their families: the ANT Foundation.

“In this volume you will find Fifteen Games with Piero. Many times we have found ourselves around a table with Piero, exchanging ideas with him, and we would like to do it again. But Piero is no longer there and so we turned to his games, to the ones we loved most, "talking" with them to create new ones. They are therefore not Piero's games but they are, perhaps, the games that we could have designed together.".


“Nel Giugno del 2019 ci ha prematuramente lasciati un grande amico e collega, Piero Cioni, e molti autori italiani hanno voluto realizzare questa opera che, oltre a ricordarlo ed omaggiarlo, vuole dare un piccolo contributo all’organizzazione che lo ha supportato nei suoi ultimi giorni e che quotidianamente offre assistenza ai malati di tumore e alle loro famiglie: la Fondazione ANT,” scrivono nella prefazione i promotori dell’iniziativa.“In questo volume troverete Quindici Giochi con Piero. Tante volte ci siamo trovati intorno a un tavolo con Piero, a scambiarci idee con lui, e vorremmo farlo ancora. Ma Piero non c’è più e allora ci siamo rivolti ai suoi giochi, a quelli che abbiamo più amato, “parlando” con loro per crearne di nuovi. Non sono quindi giochi “di” Piero ma sono, forse, i giochi che avremmo potuto progettare insieme” concludono.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Once upon a time there was a country where justice reigned, there was no poverty and power was actually in the hands of a capable person. That land was Syntharia, which was ruled by King Rorik. After he died, the country sank into Chaos and a new king have to be found. So the people started a tournament for the best, strongest and wisest people of Syntharia, this tournament is called Chibi Army of Doom.

Chibi Army of Doom is a game in which you can choose one of ten different characters and then have to eliminate all other players in a battle royale tournament. Each character has its own deck consisting of 30 cards and thus individual skills. Tthere are a variety of skills that can be used on opposing turns to protect yourself or to prevent other players from their actions.

Each player chooses a character. Then all player places up to 5 Obstacles from the box on the Table.

Each player takes in turn and resolves all turn effects, draws cards, and takes actions and calculate damage. Each character has 20 life points and is kicked out of the game when it reaches 0. The winner is the last player standing.

Name Dropping is the frustratingly fun movie card game. Challenge your friends and family to hours of fast paced fun as you listen to clues to guess the titles of Hollywood's biggest films. Comes with over 300 movie trivia cards, featuring blockbusters, indie films, superhero movies, cult classics, musicals, and much more.

Each Name Dropping card comes with 6 clues, ranked hardest to easiest, that relate to a particular movie, clues include actors/actresses, a quote, and a fun trivia fact, to help you figure out the movie name. Each card also comes with a clue genre, which gives you a snapshot description of the movie, to help guide your guessing.

One team-mate reads the clues, while the rest try to guess the answer as quickly as possible.  If the team guesses correctly, they keep the card and add points.

boardgame: The Movie Movie Game: Video Game Movie Game
description: Video Game themed expansion to The Movie Movie Game.

boardgame: The Movie Movie Game: TV Show Movie-Game
description: TV Show themed expansion for The Movie Movie Game

ウィキッド・ラビリンス (The Wicked Labyrinth) is a bluffing card game from Japan, produced by the makers of The Wicked Forest and features the distinctive illustration of anime artist Gekidan Inu Curry (劇団イヌカレー).

The publisher says: 
You are one of the young men participating in a ritual and you enter a labyrinth inhabited by a witch with a bull's head. In order to escape, you must explore the labyrinth's "corridors" and collect the "soul fragments" of those who have entered the labyrinth in the past. However, depending on the "corridors" you explore, you may have to take in the "witch's stain" which is a negative point. You will have to explore many corridors to collect more soul shards than anyone else and escape the labyrinth.

Each year, only a select few are allowed to return from the ritual.
The chosen young people who have just come of age enter the labyrinth. The labyrinth is said to be inhabited by a witch with the head of a cow. Those who return are honoured as heroes. And the next day, some of those who had disappeared in the labyrinth would return. All of them have the gift of heroism and will later achieve some success.

The game features
Explore the corridors of the labyrinth and collect the "soul fragments"! Only the player who has more search pieces than anyone else can search.

However, only the player who holds more search pieces than anyone else will be able to search, and in addition to the search pieces, he can also hold the "sign of the witch", which is a negative point. If it is held by another player, the searching player must take back the negative witch's stain.

The value of the soul can be increased by collecting soul pieces of the same colour and leveling them up. So collect as many points as you can and win!

Game Overview
The player becomes one of the young people who participate in the ritual and explores the "labyrinth" where the witch with the head of a cow is said to live. In this game, you have to explore the labyrinth one corridor at a time to collect the soul shard tokens. In order to do so, the player has to reveal more pieces than anyone else in order to be entitled to explore the corridors. (The player who reveals the second most pieces also gets the right to explore.) However, depending on the corridor explored, the player may have to take a negative point, the "Witch's Stain". You will have to explore many corridors and collect soul shards tokens while avoiding them. With the collected soul fragment tokens, you can achieve the "oracle card" and get more points to escape the "labyrinth"!

あなたは儀式へ参加する若者の一人となって、牛の頭を持つ魔女が棲むという迷宮に入ります。脱出する為には迷宮の中にある「回廊」を探索して、過去に迷宮に入った者の『魂の欠片』を集めます。しかし、探索した「回廊」によってはマイナス点である「魔女の穢れ」を引き取る必要があります。それらを避けながら沢山の「回廊」を探索して誰よりも多くの『魂の欠片』を集め迷宮を脱出しましょう。

その儀式から帰還できる者は、毎年決まってヒトリだけ。
成人を迎えたばかりの選ばれし若者が迷宮へと入っていく。 その迷宮は牛の頭を持つ魔女が棲むという曰く付きのものである。 帰還した者は英雄として称えられる。 そして、翌日にはその迷宮で過去に消えた者も数名戻っている事があった。 それらはいずれも英雄としての才能を持ち、後に何らかの成功を収める事となる。

【ゲームの特徴】
・迷宮にある回廊を探索して、『魂の欠片』を集めよう！ただし、探索ができるのはだれよりも多く探索駒を握ったプレイヤーだけ。

・探索駒の他に、マイナス点である『魔女の気配』も一緒に握る事ができる。それが他のプレイヤーによって握られていたなら、探索を行うプレイヤーはマイナス点である魔女の穢れを引き取る必要がある。

・同色の『魂の欠片』を集めてレベルアップすることで魂の価値を上げる事ができる。そうして得点をたくさん集め、勝利せよ！

【ゲームの概要】
プレイヤーは儀式に参加する若者の一人となり、牛の頭を持つ魔女が棲むという「迷宮」を探索します。 このゲームでは「迷宮」にある「回廊」を１つずつ探索して『魂の欠片トークン』を集めます。その為には、誰よりも多くの『探索駒』を握って公開する事により、「回廊」を探索する権利を得ます。（２番目に多く公開したプレイヤーも探索の権利を得ます。） しかし、探索した「回廊」によってはマイナス点である「魔女の穢れ」を引き取る必要があります。それらを避けながら沢山の「回廊」を探索して『魂の欠片トークン』を集めましょう。集めた『魂の欠片トークン』によって『神託カード』を達成し、より多くの得点を獲得して「迷宮」を脱出しましょう
artists: Gekidan Inu Curry (劇団イヌカレー)

description:
An epic crossover for all backers in the Kickstarter campaign.

Kuxcoatl by artist Tom Babbey from Dragons of the Red Moon has been recreated in the style of Dodos Riding Dinos. This expansion contains a plastic miniature, a wooden meeple and their Dino Rider card.

boardgame: Dodos Riding Dinos: Mecha Chicken Dinosaur
description:
Chicken Nicholas joins the race with this mechanical dino. "Mecha Chicken Dimetrodon" is a crossover guest for Dodos riding Dinos and will be a machine that players can build in War for Chicken Island.we

This Expansion includes:
"Oby & The JJJ's" card as a Leader in War for Chicken Island.
Krakatoa & Cannons card as a runner for the Dodo Eggthrone in Dodos riding Dinos.
Mecha Chicken Dimetrodon miniature, compatible with 2 cards for both games, DrD and WFCI.
The Players start at the upper right and the lower left corner of the maze und take turns to move their mouse one space per turn. The first player to reach the center of the maze wins the game.

boardgame: The Towers of the Black Prince (fan expansion for Carcassonne)
description:
Édouard Plantagenêt, better known as the Black Prince (1330-1376), was the eldest son of Edward III of England and Philippa of Hainaut. The origin of his nickname is uncertain. Some associate it with the colour of his armour; others refer to his reputation for brutality on the battlefield.

The Black Prince's "ride" in 1355 was a devastating raid. In Carcassonne, the upper town (city) remains untouched, but the lower town disappears in flames. In Narbonne, the suburbs, devoid of defences, were destroyed. Retracing his steps, Édouard took the Chemin de Gascogne, set fire to Miremont, destroyed Carbonne, Gimont and returned to his Bordeaux lands without difficulty.

Here, the Black Prince's army is represented by a wooden black tower figure (a black chess rook). The black tower will move around the playing area like the dragon (see Expansion 3 - The Princess & the Dragon), destroying any towers it encounters. It will capture all the followers placed on them.

Towers cannot capture any follower placed on the same tile as the black tower. The black tower cannot be removed from the game even by the dragon.

Our addicting guess-the-gibberish game just had a baby, and it’s the Incohearent Fresh Phrases Expansion pack. With 180 new gibberish cards to be added to the Incohearent Core game, hilarious game nights are guaranteed. A rotating judge holds a card up so that only they can see the answer, but everyone else sees the Incohearent phrase. Everyone else reads the front of the card aloud. Whoever decodes the phrase first wins the card.

There are 4 large and 12 small cards, that allow 3 different kinds of play:

- Each player gets a large card. The small cards lie bottom up on the table. The players take turns turning up a card. If the card belongs to the player's large card, he keeps it. Otherwise the card is returned to the table. The first player to get all 3 small cards belonging to his large card wins the game.

- Trump game: A player calls a trump card, f.i. "living room". Now the other players add a fitting card. The last player who is able to do this, collects the played cards and call a new trump card. The player who collects the most card wins the game. This game is played only with the small cards.

- Terzett game: All small cards are dialed among the players, who take turns to draw cards from one another to collect trios. Each player gets a large card to act as a wild card. The player with the most trios wins.

The pictures on the cards illustrate the correct use of aerosol sprays.

デコニファ (Deconifer) is a co-operative dexterity game of stacking from Japan. The playing area is a large wooden board showing a fir tree with ribbons, which stands propped up at an angle (much like a stepladder). Players take turns placing toys and gifts on the tree, starting from the bottom and working up.

There are some horizontal lines across sections of the tree. If a gift is placed so that it crosses a line, decorative balls and ribbons can also be hung on the tree. The game ends when a placed object falls from the tree.

At the end of the game, players can work out a score and see how well they did in decorating the tree. If they are careful, they will complete decorating the tree with a Santa and a golden star at the top.

The designer has specialised in highly ornate games made using woodburning techniques. The game features a large number of intricately designed, hand-made pieces, brightly coloured to produce a highly attractive design. As a result, only a few copies are made, and sell for a high price (approx $400). 
user summary

Galaxy Junkers is a 2 to 5 player sci-fi strategy game. With excellent replay value, this innovative game is fun for the whole family! Add a new swashbuckling spaceship adventure to your board game collection, and begin your quest to see who can survive the dangers of deep space.

You are galaxy junkers searching for valuable space artifacts from the ruins of an ancient space station. Unfortunately, the wormhole portal collapses, and your ship's engines and power core are severely damaged. You are stranded in deep space. You must salvage the ancient space cubes, power crystals, and power core and use them to escape. Beware: other galaxy junkers are right behind.

Objective: To win, collect the space cubes and power core indicated on your Ship Diagnostic card, and be the first player to reach the center of the wormhole. You may also win by destroying all opposing ships.

Play Time: The play time varies depending on the number of players.

3 Players: 30 minutes, 4 Players: 60-90 minutes, 5 Players: 90-120 minutes

—description from the designer

Now you can play PRESS YOUR LUCK, the classic TV show, at home with your friends and family. Unfold the scoreboard, write your names, ask each other questions from the cards plus download the free Press Your Luck Appcessory to play the Electronic Big Board! But look out for those nasty Whammies. And speaking of Whammies, this edition includes a fantastic, collectable Plush Whammy!

The game lasts 3 rounds (2 for short game, 4 for long game).  Each round consists of the host reading four question cards to the players.  You will collect three spins if you answer the question correctly and are the first to lay down the answer card.  If you lay down the correct answer card and are not first, you still get one spin.  Zero spins for the wrong answer.  After the question round, you get to play the Big Board and spend your spins.  At any time, you can pass your spins to the next player and make them press their luck!

The winner is whoever survives the Whammys and makes the most money.

BONC! is The Battle Royale Of Card Games, it is a fun, intense, and strategic card game for kids and adults alike.

All players start with 5 lives each. Take turns playing one card face-up on the discard pile.

Try to BONC! the player right before you by matching the number of the card they played.
Or get fancy & use wild cards for special attacks, protection, & healing.

If you lose all your lives, you’re out of the game. The last one standing wins!

—description from the designer

In Anabid!, players compete to outspell, outbid and outwit their opponents to achieve the highest score.

After the custom 6- and 8-sided lettered dice are rolled, the first player states a word length and bids how many words that long they think they can spell.  Each subsequent bid must increase the number of words or the word length.

Each player has a scant few seconds to make a bid and each bid is an opportunity to drive the next player to unwisely overbid -- a core strategy.  The top bidder has a minute to fulfil his bid.

Words with greater lengths garner higher scores.  To counter Aunt Edna's dictionary memorization, however, the game includes a clever mechanism that enables a would-be thief to steal the bid score!  After each player has rolled three times (with the middle round in reverse order), the player with the highest score wins!

—description from the publisher

Card Game is a communal card game where the rules are only limited to the submission requirements. The end goal of the game is defined entirely by the players and the cards they decide to play as the game progresses.

A Player is/has: 1 Deck, a 4 card starting hand, and a zone to play cards. The cards tell the players everything else, and players must fill in the blanks.

This game requires you to play it before there is a clear end state, but as long as you follow the instructions of the cards, you will reach it. ~10% of cards contain win and loss scenarios. Normal gameplay revolves around setting up a zone in anticipation for a way to win, and all actions are done with the cards.

Players draw 1 card per turn, and play cards from their hand. [Places] are played in the player's respective zones, as are [People] and [Things]. [Things That Happen] are played, and then discarded.

1. Each card has a NAME.
2. Each card has a TYPE (Person, Place, Thing, or Thing That Happens).

Cards can have any optional keywords alongside their name and type. Common examples include: 
Hand, Deck, Zone, Space, Discard, HP, Damage, Can't, Point, Win, Lose, Friend, Draw, Move, Tie, Counter, Opponent, Turn, Player, You, Look, Stack, Game, Card, Flip, Effect, Gain, Choose, Facedown, Drift or any others you may think of.

Percentages of each card type are constantly changing with every submission and print run.

This is a collectable card game. All proxies and bootlegs of official "Card Game" cards are legally sanctioned and considered "genuine" cards for casual or constructed play. This includes cards that are altered in terms of effects, art, printing quality, or physical alterations and damage.

boardgame: Here to Slay: Fierce Panguardian Promo Card
description:
"At the beginning of your turn, you may switch The Fierce Panguardian between Guardian and Fighter. 
Each time you CHALLENGE another player's card, that player cannot play Modifier cards until the end of the turn." - Card Text

Are You Ready to Dare: Smeghead card game is here to transform every moment of boredom into an entertaining party game that you won’t get tired of! This game is the ideal combination of skill and luck, where the only purpose is to have fun while testing your courage with silly or brave challenges.

Endless Hours of Fun: You don’t need to be a mastermind to play this party game for kids, as the only requirement is to be open to challenges and tons of fun. Our game is based on silly, hilarious, or bold dares, perfect to break the monotony and share endless moments of fun and laughter with your friends.

For All the Family: If you are older than 14 and have a good sense of humor and passion for life, this card game is perfect for you! Gather your family, friends, or colleagues outside work and embark on an unforgettable experience full of tension, laughter, competition, and see if you are up to the challenge.

Party Ice Breaker: Whether you plan to meet with your friends on a night out or want to change the atmosphere in your house, the Smeghead adult card games are definitely the right choice. They are perfect for birthday parties, anniversaries, family reunions, trips, holidays, or other events.

Easy to Play: The Smeghead family card game contains 4 types of cards and an easy-to-read guide, which makes this game extremely easy to play. Daring, uneasy, and super fun, this game will get you hooked from the first hand, dragging you into endless rounds of fun and dares.

—description from the publisher

First, 15 picture cards are distributed according to the lotto rules (3 picture cards per tray). Then letter cards for the 4-letter words are called out. Since the letters are needed several times, speed is also important here in order to be the first to fill the board. Whoever manages to do this wins the game.

—user summary

A cooperative game about the rural school situation in Africa. 36 cards are laid out face down in a 6x6 grid. 
Players roll the dice and move one of 3 pieces across the grid, but may not jump over gaps. The card you leave is revealed.
On 5 cards there are obstacles to learning (e.g. lack of school fees), which have to be removed immediately after uncovering in order to win the game. To remove a learning obstacle, an already uncovered photo pair must be removed from the grid. 4 turtle cards (recognizable on the back) help when it gets tough on the playing field. 1 monkey card causes trouble and all cards are unrevealed.

—user summary

Warzone is the first new contract set for A Billion Suns. It allows players to use A Billion Suns to tell brutal stories of military might and system-shaking clashes of far-future weaponry.

A Billion Suns, played with the Core Systems contract set provided in the rulebook, is a game of cleverly and frugally assigning resources, telling stories of industrial espionage and resource capture.

The Warzone contract set changes the feel of A Billion Suns considerably. The Warzone contract set allows players to play an all-out war between their two corporations. The stories these new contracts tell are different, and you’ll find a host of new tactical questions to wrestle with.

—description from the publisher

Sports simulation that allows realistic Tournament Play and Season Play in a reasonable time scale, while still giving ALL the features that come in to effect during ATP / WTA Tennis seasons. You can play Singles or Doubles, Seasons, Tournaments, on-off matches. World Team Tennis, Davis Cup, Federation Cup, or Hopman Cup matches or Tournaments or any other Contests you wish.

The game comes with the options of playing a set-by-set mode, game-by-game mode or instant match results mode where the outcome is determined by the roll of a few dice. Men and women players are rated for each year to give realistic scores but still with the possibility of generating shock results.

The first Sportsreplays set release was the Australian Open 2012 Edition including updated ratings as at January 30th 2012.

Exclusive mini expansion for backers of the game during the Kickstarter campaign.
This expansion includes 2 new anomalies for ADELE and 4 new objects (2 copies of each) to make your game a little more different.

The new anomalies: Simply add these new tiles to the ones already in the game; just keep in mind they are not suitable for game modes where ADELE plays as an automaton.

Maintenance Robot and  Surprise Attack

The new objects:

Computer Virus, Portable Charger, PDA, Portable EMP:

You may just add the new objects to the common pool of objects but there is a risk that some vital objects will become too scarce, like the batteries or the space suits. So it is recommended removing some existing objects when using the new ones as follows:

Computer Virus - remove 1 battery and 1 Stimulants
PDA - remove 1 flashlight and 1 space suit
Portable charger - remove 1 flashlight and 1 pieces
Portable EMP - remove 1 teddy and 1 first aid kit
family: Organizations: Board Games Association of Creators in Spain (LUDO)

description:
The Last Gig is a board game that simulates your band life on the road: unforgettable concerts and poor food, merch to sell after each Live, and records to be recorded in the studio. But mostly many, many, MANY kilometers to travel with your partners!

There is only one way to beat your opponents: get on stage!

On your turn, roll the dice, stop in the tour spaces, play in awesome venues, do live sessions to get (or lose) Tour Points, spin the Rock Wheel to or buy new Merch cards that you can sell at your next Live session.

Race around, gather as much Tour Points as possible, and be the first to finish the game with your Last Gig!

Rhino 5 is a cooperative card-based crisis management board game for 2-4 players.

Players form the crew of an old mining spacecraft, caught in a deadly meteor storm after an immense catastrophe that has engulfed the solar system. Play cards and use special skills, as you work together to repair damage to your ship. All while simultaneously trying to avoid incoming meteors, which will continue hitting the ship until you reach and complete a hidden objective.

Play isolated scenarios, or take part in a solar-system spanning campaign to save the people of Earth and discover what caused the catastrophe in the first place.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Wookiee Warriors Expansion
description:
Add a formidable unit of powerful Wookiees to your Star Wars: Legion armies with the Wookiee Warriors Unit Expansion! This tall and commanding species boasts nearly unrivaled physical strength and ferocity, and now both the Galactic Republic and Rebel Alliance can make use of their formidable skills.

The Wookiee Warriors Unit Expansion puts six Wookiee Warrior miniatures that can be assembled and used in a variety of ways at your command, including as either a close-range unit carrying ryyk blades and Kashyyk pistols or a unit of Wookiee sharpshooters firing X1 Carbines. No matter how you assemble your miniatures, four upgrade cards invite you to field these units for both the Republic and Rebel factions. Additionally, a miniature representing a Wookiee Chieftain can enter the fight as a Republic commander and a fifth unit card and three command cards let them further influence your strategy. Finally, eight upgrade cards invite you to customize your units with Wookiees carrying an array of weaponry.

—description from the publisher

This is a strategic war game that uses real-world effects to make for a realistic experience. In this game, the players could build businesses, collect taxes and use this money to build an army to conquer the world. Here, any alliances formed for battle can be broken and trust can end up being limited. The costs and taxes can change with business cycles and trades between players are allowed.

At the start, each player starts off with two countries. The game will be conducted in rounds. Each round a player can choose to do an action with their cards, trade or even start wars with their resources. The player that gets a clear majority (more than half) of the countries under their control wins.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
In Eternal Palace, you are a noble family who has pledged to help the Emperor rebuild his palace left derelict for centuries so that you may gain his favor. You must send your team to collect resources and rebuild monuments. You will also honor the Emperor by painting a beautiful picture of his beloved gardens and palace — but others are trying to impress him, too, and only one will have the honor of being chosen as the Emperor's favorite.

In this game, your team of workers is represented by dice, and by placing them on the game board you contribute towards rebuilding the different parts of the Eternal Palace. Each location is reached based on dice rolls, but if others have gone to an otherwise inaccessible location, you may visit it too by paying fish, one of the resources in the game. Complete tasks first — or contribute more than your competitors to these monuments — to earn tokens reflecting your overall effort. Recruit new workers to your team, and use the painting pieces you receive as each location is unlocked to "paint" a record of your work, layer by layer.

Who will contribute the most to the reconstruction and gain the favor of the Emperor? Find out in this tense and highly interactive Eurogame!

Kickstarter Deluxe edition including:

     4 monument minis
     Labyrinth expansion (labyrinth board)
     Exports expansion (12 exports cards, export board)


Kickstarter pack:

     Composition expansion (11 painting piece goals)
     Alternate locations (5 alternate location tiles)

This booster adds 21 new cards to Totenstadt!

It's getting cold in the Totenstadt.
The zombies don't care about the cold, the mercenaries do.
So wrap up in warm clothes, light fire barrels, keep your balance on the slippery roads and free snowbound survivors.

But beware!
In every snow hill, a zombie can be waiting for your helping hand!

The cards can be easily mixed with the Totenstadt basic box and expand the missions with new possibilities and encounters!

Dieser Booster erweitert Totenstadt um 21 neue Karten!

Es wird kalt in der Totenstadt.
Den Zombies macht die Kälte nichts aus den Söldnern schon.
Daher hüllt euch in wärmende Kleidung, zündet Feuertonnen an, haltet das gleichgewicht auf den glatten Straßen und befreit eingeschneite Überlebende.

Aber vorsicht!
In jedem Schneehügel kann ein Zombie auf eure helfende Hand warten!

Die Karten können einfach mit der Totenstadt Basisbox vermischt werden und die Missionen mit neuen Möglichkeiten und Begegnungen erweitern!

boardgame: Hazardous Movement Scenario Pack 2: The Un-Civil Wars
description:
Hazardous Movement Scenario Pack 2: The Un-Civil Wars thematizes three very consequential civil conflicts that took place immediately before and after the Second World War: in Spain (1936-1939), Greece (1944-1949) and China (where civil war resumed in 1945 and ended in 1949).

The pack consists of 10 exciting, innovative ASL scenarios spanning these years. Civil wars, ironically, are very bitter affairs, a fact that is reflected in the combination of No Quarter, Hand-to-Hand Close Combat and Political Commissars (for the Communists) that makes for particularly violent scenarios, even by ASL standards.

—description from the publisher

It is a competitive game between 2 and 6 players. On their turn, each player must perform one of the 8 challenges, in a time of one minute. If they succeed, they win the token for that challenge. Whoever gets the 8 different tokens wins the game.

The 8 challenges are:

     Memory: remember a sequence of 8 cards with objects.
     Language: make words with 2, 3 and 4 letters.
     Translation: translate a Roman number to an Arabic number.
     Visual: alternately say the written word and its ink color.
     Identification: recognize which card is missing.
     Repetition: read a tongue twister and repeat it without mistakes.
     Logic: find the fourth card that is deduced by Logic.
     Deduction: a question is read and its relationship is deduced.


It is a game conceived, created and developed by neuroscience specialists dedicated to the study of the human brain. The objective is to provide a playful tool that helps improve mental functions.

More challenges from the Destreza Mental game.  This is a stand alone version - it does not need the original game to be played.

It is a competitive game between 2 and 6 players. On their turn, each player must perform one of the 8 challenges, in a time of one minute. If he succeeds, he wins the token for that challenge. Whoever gets the 8 different tokens wins the game.

The 8 challenges are:

     Memory: find out which is the missing province.
     Language: form a sentence with seven letters.
     Visuospatial: order mamushkas from largest to smallest.
     Calculation: do a mathematical calculation.
     Multitasking: place domino cards alternating the higher and lower numbers.
     Logic: place the twelve cards without touching consecutive cards.
     Inhibition: place cards but only one of its three characteristics matches.
     Social cognition: discover where the other player hides a card.

In the Chipmunk Choppers game, kids learn letters, matching, spelling and pattern recognition while having fun. The object of the game is to pick nuts off the tree to find the right letters or colors to match the pattern on the card. But it's not simply finger picking-players use a cool chomper mechanism to collect the nuts.

Be the first player to earn the most cards by gathering the correct acorns to spell words or complete patterns.

There are 3 ways to play Chipmunk Chompers™ game: Match & Spell, Complete the Pattern and Acorn Chomp.

Match & Spell - First player to complete 3 cards wins!
Complete the Pattern -  The first player to complete 5 cards wins!
Acorn Chomp - The player with the most acorns wins!

The object of Fir is to assemble the most legendary band of loggers in the Pacific Northwest. To win, players must shrewdly manage their timbermen and homesteads to earn the most myth points (mp) over five rounds.

During the game, you task your workers with all manner of awe-inspiring labor, from felling fir trees and driving logs to milling timber and stoking steam donkeys! To succeed, you must put your workers in harm's way. Bumps and bruises will take their toll and send many loggers to an early grave. But do not despair! Injured workers will enter trade buildings where they become foreman, freeing you up to recruit day laborers, flip flapjacks, or moonlight as an impresario.

Players who move timber along the supply chain and compete in logging games will earn valuable resources that will help them improve their homesteads. Will you tend to corn and chickens, build bunks and barns, or furnish the river with bridges and rocker boxes? Your chore list is as tall as the day is long! A trip to the trading post will pay huge dividends, while a visit to vaudeville might strand you on skid row.

So grab your axe and pike, muster your morale and might, then marvel your fellow timbermen with exploits of mythic proportions!
Emperor's Box contains all of the unlocked stretch goals (mostly components), including the Barbarians Solo module.

boardgame: Angry Birds Lunar Launcher and Planet Base Playset
description:
Angry Birds Lunar Launcher and Planet Base play set. Based on the best-selling phone app game, Angry Birds, kids can now play their favourite app game in real life! This set offers two different ways to play - you can mount the planet base on the wall (using the included 3M Command strip technology) or place it on a table. The gravity-defying blocks and pigs and a handheld launcher allow kids to build, launch and destroy like never before! Includes: handheld launcher, planet base station, mounting bracket, 3 popular Angry Birds, 3 pigs, 10 blocks and bonus pieces and 2 x 3M Command strips. Suitable for ages 5+.

Object:  To score points by knocking down pigs, blocks and bonus pieces to score the most points.Level 1•  Decide who will build the castle on the planet base and who will launch birds to    try and knock it down!•  Build a castle on the Planet Base.  You may design your castle any way you want,     but you must use ALL the blocks, pigs and bonus pieces.•  Launch the birds at the castle.  You get three tries, one for each bird.When launching a bird, hold the launcher in one hand, and then put your finger in the launcher ring to pull it back.  Then let go and watch it fly!

Any pig you knock off is worth 2 points, and any bonus piece you knock off is  worth one point. NOTE:  if the pig or bonus piece    is still attached to a block when you  knock  it off, it still counts.•  Add up your points, then switch roles so that the player who launched the birds now builds the castle, and the person who built the castle will now launch the birds.•  After both players have had a chance to score points, compare the results.  The player with the highest score wins the round.•  If there is a tie score, the round doesn’t count and you must play it over.•  First player to win 2 rounds is the winner!Level 2•  Level 2 is exactly the same game play as Level 1 but the scoring is different.  It’s a little more complicated but there’s also a      chance for more points!•  Just like in Level 1, you have 3 chances (1 for each bird) to knock things off.  But in Level 2, you also get points for knocking off    blocks!  Also, EVERYTHING is worth more points.•  Pigs are worth 75 points, Long Blocks are worth 50 points, Short Blocks are worth 25 points and Bonus Pieces are worth 10     points. •  Just like in Level 1, compare points to determine the winner of each round.   If there is a tie score, the round doesn’t count and    you must play it over.•  First player to win 2 rounds is the winner!

boardgame: Marvel Zombies: A Zombicide Game – Galactus the Devourer
description:
Take on Galactus in unique scenarios as he destroys the map. Stop him before there's nothing left... or devour him as a zombie and gain his might! Heroes will need to climb onto Galactus' huge world eating machine and fight through his defenses, gradually weakening him until he can be stopped (or devoured)! Playable in both Hero Mode and Zombie Mode, with unique features in both of them.

Standing over 60cm tall (over 23 inches) and with a base diameter of 45cm (almost 18 inches) Galactus and his world-consuming engine are brought to life in great detail, functioning as a unique play area for the new missions contained in this expansion. The expansion comes with both living and zombie versions of Galactus’ head and right arm, which can be easily swapped out to face Galactus in either Zombie Mode or Hero Mode.

In addition to Galactus specific scenarios, the Silver Surfer can also be played in other games in Hero or Zombie mode, or spawned as an additional challenge in missions in either mode.

-description from the publisher

This booster also introduces a new type of card, Stella cards, and a new Heroes of the Legends card, the objective of which is that influence reaches 20, regardless of type.

The 18 new Stella cards and the new Hero card completely change the way you play. You will have to be even more wary of your opponents.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In Supply and Demand, you play the leader of a country balancing the needs of production and consumption within your borders.

Unfortunately, the world does not have an unlimited surplus, so you will need to efficiently manage your resources to protect yourself from collapse and chart a new future.

Supply and Demand is designed for exclusively 3 players. Each round, players will receive production capacity through an "I Cut, You Choose" mechanism, and then will take turns selecting actions on an action matrix. Players produce goods and sell them across their territory to their opponents, which effectively functions as an attack.

The game ends when a global supply of points is depleted to the level of a player's supply of points. The game ends, and a winner is determined after repayment of loans.

Supply and Demand is a game of interconnection, and all that entails.

boardgame: The Magnificent: Dice Tower 2021 Promo Cards
description:
Each master card will have as a bonus that for 3 coins you can move the wagon 1 to 3 spaces and the card scoring is 2 VP for each pip of a certain die color.

Each master card will be for one specific color (as well wagon as die): green, orange or purple.

Originally available as part of Pack E from the Dice Tower 2021 Kickstarter campaign.

Explore the magical Filipino folklore of Lagim! Lagim is a one-of-a-kind fantasy card game inspired by Fillipino myths and folktales. Lagim offers a unique and action packed gaming experience made more immersive by its authentic roots and its gorgeous and evocative art. With so many exciting ways to play you deicde your fate-an enthralling game at every turn. Lagim invites you to a fantastic realm of monsters, mayhem, and magic - play the game and discover the thrilling stories and exciting encounters we've carefully woven for you.

No matter who you are and where you are from, an amazing adventure full of infinite possibilities awaits! In this game about strategy and luck. Lagim can be played by 3-6 players. Each player takes turns battling monsters and each while defending their bargyos. During each players turn they are reffered to as the active player. The active player is allowed to play 1 game action each turn and they cannot pass; this action depends on their current cards and strategy and their turn ends once an action has been performed or a battle has been concluded.

The game board is split into 2 realms the forest realm sits at the center of the play area. Each player starts with 3 souls. The last player alive with remaining souls is declared the winner.

—Description From The Publisher

The year is 2039. Against the background of the rest of exhausted Europe, Scandinavia seems quite prosperous: there is no war, or even hunger. Sweden, a small regional leader with a recently acquired nuclear arsenal, seems to be completely safe from any encroachments. Neighbors on the peninsula are reaching out to her for protection.

A rogue state, the Great British Republic, is stirring up the North Sea. Once proud frigates, and now pirate ships of the British scurry back and forth in search of prey. Threatening with missiles, they take the "passage fee" from every oncoming vessel. In a world where bank accounts have lost value, tribute is collected in goods: from tankers - oil, from trawlers - fish, and so on.

So far, no one has dared to resist the brazen sea racketeers, but there is a limit to everything. When the British Overprotector demanded offshore oil platforms from the Norwegian government, the King-Queen of Sweden stood up for the allied country, threatening nuclear retaliation...

—description from the publisher

Britain vs. Sweden
Naval and AirLand combat.
OGRE-like structured ships mechanic.
Nuclear weapons available for both opponents.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Forge your own star realm! Build up your military through shipping and trade, then use your mighty fleet of warships to protect what’s yours and take what isn’t!

Designed by Magic hall-of-famers Darwin Kastle and Rob Dougherty, Star Realms combines the fun of deckbuilding games with the interactivity of trading card game-style combat. As you play, use trade to add new ships and bases to your deck. When played, those ships and bases generate powerful effects, additional trade, and combat to attack your opponent and their bases. If you reduce your opponent’s score to zero, you win!

This Star Realms Box Set includes all of the cards from the Star Realms Core Set and Gambit Set, plus a game board and two additional starting decks, providing awesome gameplay for 2-4 players. And the Nemesis Beast and the Pirates of the Dark Star challenge cards provide additional solo and cooperative play options! Each box is a complete 4-player game containing rules and 162 cards.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Flamecraft: The Spice Tower and Flamestarter Statue
description:
The Spice Tower and Flamestarter Statue are additional shops for Flamecraft available via the Kickstarter and as a promo pack.

The Spice Tower is a wild goods shop with the power to either double a good you have or provide 5 goods of a type you don't have. The artwork is a reference to a well-known content producer and contains many board game puns in the background.

Flamestarter Statue is a high reputation shop that has the unique ability to allow players to gain dragons that have been previously placed.
—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Players draw cards from their hand to move across the board collecting asteroids and faults.

A round ends when all participants arrived at one of the stations or were disqualified due to malfunctions and left the game

After 3 rounds count the number of asteroids collected by the participant. The player with the most, wins.


Hebrew only:
התקדמו לאורך המסלול ואספו אסטרואידים, שלוש תקלות והחללית תתרסק. רק הראשון שיגיע לסיום המסלול ומי שיצליח לחזור לארץ בבטחה יקבל את הנקודות

The year is 2061. The Moon Base Development Project. Also known as Astronika.

It's been almost 100 years since the Apollo missions, when humans first set foot on the Moon. The history of the moon is about to be rewritten by mankind. With the development of science and technology, the Moon is no longer an impenetrable territory, but a new frontier within reach.

And it is no longer the nations of the past that are inviting mankind to the Moon. Instead, it is the international space companies, which transcend national boundaries.

Players are the project leaders of an International Space Company. Your mission is to select the "right people", assemble a team and lead the project to success.
Which company will establish a bridgehead in the new frontier of the Moon?

- A medium-weight board game with the theme of developing the moon
- Expansion & Reproduction x Engine Build x Area Majority
- Assemble a diverse crew and build your own team!

—description from the publisher (translated)
publishers: メリーアンドリューワークス(merry-andrew-works)

description:
Kickstarter exclusive mini-expansion for Icaion.

The Aberration is a special enemy unit that spawns after a considerable amount of Parasites gathers in one place. It is the evolution of a basic Parasite. It is hostile towards inferior Parasites, and embodies a direct threat to the Seekers as well. It is a real menace in lots of aspects since, when it steps in, here is what you are not allowed to do:

you cannot deploy Machines in its region 
    you cannot build a Wonder in its region
    you cannot extract Qoam in its region
    you cannot add Parasites to its region, harming players that follow a "parasite-hunting" strategy

When a Seeker manages to defeat the Aberration the miniature is removed from the game (for now) and points are scored by the Seeker.

Inspired by the most known dating apps, It's Llamatch! is an agile and easy-going card game where only the boldest llama will be able to find its perfect Llamatch! Because dating... is a game!

Scroll through different profiles, read their bios and have a laugh chatting with them. Don't forget to turn on notifications!
Hopefully you'll draw your favourite llama's attention.

Start by taking a Profile card that indicates what you are looking for in your Llamatch!: Sexiness, romance, awareness or coolness.

Display four llamatch! cards in front of each player, facing down.

Now explore your Llamatch! Each one gives you different values for each stat. 
Check if they fit with your preferences.
No? Next!
Yes? Then Slide right and play your cards right:

Use Bio and Chat cards to modify stats, change the rules of the game with each Notification and sabotage other players.

When any player is left with no llamatch! cards in the pile, the game ends.

Get the highest score so you and your date have the best chemistry. Only then we can really say It's Llamatch!

Summon Guardians is a 2-4 player war strategy board game.  It combines the spirit of fantasy and adventure with strategic castle warfare. Players join an epic, centuries-old war for the realm of Quadrenna. Here, four Kingdoms battle over a centrally located territory known as the Wastelands. The main Battlefield of this war is the intersection of these Kingdoms, where each struggles to prevent the other from achieving victory.

Through their Queens, the four Kingdoms summon Guardians from throughout time and around the galaxy in an attempt to gain control of the Wastelands. The Kingdoms’ main tools for winning the war are their two Guardians, each of which has unique strengths and weaknesses. Although the Guardians’ attitudes about being summoned vary, they all loyally serve their Kingdoms and find high honor in their duty.

Each turn consists of 3 phases, in this order: Move, Card, and Action. The Move and Action phases are optional, but the Card phase is required. Both Guardians act during each phase; you can choose the order in which your Guardians act.

A Kingdom achieves victory when it obtains ownership of the Wastelands. This is achieved one of three ways: kill all enemy Guardians, kill all enemy Kings, or take ownership of the Red Jewel Platform in the center by sacrificing 5 Red Jewels to it after your Kingdom is fully Upgraded.

boardgame: Crash of Thunder: Napoleon at Austerlitz 1805
description: "Crash of Thunder: Napoleon at Austerlitz 1805" is a fast and easy wargame of the Battle of Austerlitz in 1805.  One player commands the French Army of Napoleon and the other the Allied Army (Russian and Austrian).  Each side tries try to win the battle by inflicting casualties and breaking the morale of their opponent.  The combat abilities of each corps or formation as they historically performed in the battle are listed on its own Formation Card. Each Formation card represents a varying number of units that are placed on the map of the battlefield of Austerlitz.  There are also tactics cards that reflect actual events of the battle, that each side may use in order to increase their chances of success in each attack or defense.  The Game consists of each side taking turns by declaring an action to activate one card each turn.

description:
An easy and atmospheric board game with a little tactics for the whole family.

Bilberry Patch is an easy game with a little tactics for the whole family. The best pickers know that speed does not always equal a full basket when picking berries: the best patches reveal themselves to those who take their time in the forest!

Players roll dice and move around a circular board, collecting bilberries on spaces with special icons.  Action cards and magic branches add an extra element of excitement to the game. The object is to collect as much bilberries as possible during one round.

At the end, the players count their bilberries and the player with the biggest haul wins the game.

Includes rules in Finnish, Swedish and English.

A trick-taking card game.

First, draw cards from the deck, or from the face-up card next to it, until you reach the hand size limit. 
Then, the player must place at least 1 card from their hand onto the board. During your turn, you may play up to all cards in your hand. If you "Recycle" a card from a captured super trick you also play it, in addition to the cards from your hand.

Eventually you will run out of cards to draw from the deck - then players stop drawing but still must lay down at least 1 card per turn until their hands are empty. At this point, cards on each players side are automatically captured by those players. The cards left in the middle remain neutral. Count up your captured cards, and whoever has more is the winner.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Onitama: Steer and Lobster Promo Cards
description:
A set of 2 cards packaged together in Pack A of The Dice Tower 2021 Kickstarter campaign.

These cards provide new movement options for your pieces.

boardgame: Here to Slay: Howl of the Dead mini and promo card

If you Discard a Hero card, Destroy a Hero card.

boardgame: Here to Slay: Reigning King mini and promo card
