Reigning King:

Reigning King is a new mini in the Here to Slay Vinyl Mini Series which comes with a promo card.

In Colony X, you play the role of a space foreman working for a billion dollar corporation. Your mission? Establish a sustainable colony on a distant planet.

Colony X is a 2-4 player game featuring area control and colony building. Each turn players land one of their ships on a nearby planet to collect two resources from an ever-changing pool of resources or use one of their ships to visit an architect to construct a new building for their colony. But be warned, after construction future buildings will cost less for your opponents.

The game takes place over four rounds. At the end of each round, the player with the most ships on a planet gains its influence token which is needed to complete work orders and increase the value of certain buildings.

At the end of the final round, the player with the most Victory Points tallied from their buildings, influence, and completed work orders is the winner.

Students are disappearing in the Cuniculus Academy of Magic. Can you help rescue them from the shadow world? La Biblia is 2 player game of deduction and bluffing. Players try to guess the characters in each other's hand by asking a limited set of questions. The other player must tell the truth unless the Rabi card is in their hand, then they may answer with a bluff or lie.

Totally Medieval is a game where you control opposing Armies. As you move closer to your opponent you will find new terrain and different weather systems which may help or hinder your progress. When you meet your opponent's army, be ready to attack and/or defend yourself so you can stop or slow down their advances on your homeland. Your survival is based on your success in keeping your opponent from advancing. Your goal is take over their homeland before they take yours.

In the base game you will start as Viking or knights with other types of people to come later. Each type of person comes with historical aspect to each person on the team.

This game also comes with a great twist of the dice roll. In a game that would normally have a pair dice to roll and move your army's around you draw cards instead. These cards are call Math dice and they come in different levels of Math. Addition, Subtraction, Multiplication, division and Algebra and all the choices you could use. Each player would draw a card and have the opponent draw it solve it to get a bonus for each battle.

So for this game you will learn a little fun history and solve a few math problems. With the upgraded armies to miniatures you gain a great chess set.

—description from the publisher

The world is getting more and more connected. As an owner of a distribution company, it is up to you to make your company grow and deliver goods all over the world.

The Silk Route is a flip and write game where players have a distribution company. Deliver the appropriate goods to different cities and squares inside these cities, by drawing posts of your own. Right after, draw a poliomino in your company panel to represent the experience you got with each transaction.

Eventually, squares will have all their necessities covered. It's the moment to see how your goods increase their value! Use these values to score more points than any other player and have the most prestigious company by the end of the game!

—description from the designer

The Solar Sphere Expansions box contains the White Dwarf and Corporation Robots modules, and additional components to support a fifth player.

With the construction of Solar Sphere #1 underway, The Corporation has set its sights on a nearby White Dwarf. You can now choose to survey the White Dwarf to gain valuable resources and opportunities. The White Dwarf expansion adds a unique rondel mechanism, where you can chose to move a meeple up to the value of your die, or if you have upgraded the White Dwarf location, you can move two meeples for extra rewards!

The Corporate Robots expansion adds a set of cards and meeples that act as remote access to worker locations. Install the cards into your command centre and deploy the meeple robots to locations to fully upgrade that location's power! You can then access that location from your command centre.

A fifth player can also now join the table with all the components needed to do so.

Are you ready to expand the Solar Sphere?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Wer ist zuerst in der Schule?
description:
Each player moves his figure towards the school by rolling the dice (values 1 to 5). On green fields you may roll the dice again, on red fields you have to sit out. If you roll the teacher symbol with the dice, you move the teacher one space further. All players who reach the school before the teacher win the game.

—user summary

The game contains 105 question cards asking for the year of a certain event. Each player makes a guess and names a year. Whoever comes closest to the correct answer receives the card as a prize. The player who has collected the most cards after a predetermined number of questions wins the game.

—user summary

Not Not is the multiplayer adaptation of the eponymous video game by Alt Shift, a speed game combining orientation, logic and reflexes, based on two cognitive biases: multiple negations, as well as incongruent words (colors are written in another color than the one they designate).

It's up to you to filter out this irrelevant information that slows down your reaction time in order to tap as quickly as possible on one of the cards that meets the criteria generated by the dice and thus win it.

—description from the publisher

Rainbow: The Kaleidoscopic Flip and Colour Adventure!

Players use the colour and shape cards to fill in their grid in order to collect points and bonuses. Complete as many squares as you can or colour all of the rainbow, unicorn or unicorn shoes!

—description from the designer

A solitaire roll and write game created for the 10th Roll and Write contest.

Players roll 3d6 (red, blue & yellow) and use the values to lead both the fox and the rabbit along trails to find food. Dice can be combined to cross the primary, secondary and black numbers on the trails. Players can use powers to modify dice and can gain extra dice or powers during the game.

Meanwhile, at the end of each turn the Farmer AI sets a trap.

When both animals have eaten 3+ foods they can escape through the fence using the required values. Alternatively, once the Farmer AI sets his 13th trap, he will try to set it off using a different required value.

Whichever achieves the required values first (both animals OR the farmer)wins.

Tamar Rising is a BattleTech sourcebook providing full details of the events in the former Clan Jade Falcon Occupation Zone from 3151 to mid-3152. Included are a full historical summary of events in that region of space, personality and unit profiles of key players—many appearing here for the first time, and game information to bring it all to your BattleTech tabletop.

Note: Includes a Campaign Map, which with the physical book is a 26” x 38”, double-sized, laminated poster.

The Ghosts of Rocca is a worker-placement game for 2-5 players, ages 8 and up. As a player, you control two necromancer characters, moving them around a haunted castle in an effort to soothe the castle’s restless ghosts by bringing them the enchanted charms they crave. But beware! If you enter a room occupied by a rival necromancer, you are bound by honor to duel — and if you lose, your rival may take a charm from you!

In addition to worker-placement, the game features resource-management, area control and optional combat. Movement is accomplished by a combination of cards and custom dice. Come heed the call to honor and adventure!

—description from the publisher

King Frea - King of Weirdland - has decided to give one of his sons/daughters in marriage to the bravest knight in the kingdom.
Each player will assume the role of a knight and will compete with each other to get the Royal Consideration needed to ask the prince/princess in marriage.

The knights will have to manage their Energy, change the rules of the game with the right Spells, collect Gold and Reputation and complete the Challenges that fate will put on their path.
If the knight will be lucky enough, he will even have the honor of meeting the prince/princess in a Blind date, but only those who will be able to overcome the different challenges, adapting to the situation brought forth by the events, will receive the prince/princess’ hand!

—description from the designer
The Tricktakers Expansion Set features 7 more roles that can be used in the Tricktakers Base Game. The Expansion set was released at the Fall Tokyo Game Market 2021.

A Gigantik charades game for kids! It's a play of words!

The curtain is going up on Theatrix!

Theatrix is a game of charades developed by educators with children in mind. Theatrix gives your children the ability to use their imagination to act out what is on their card. Each card is divided into 4 categories: things we do, everyday things, things we use to move on and characters & creatures. When selecting a card, the child can use either the 400 words or the easily recognizable full-color illustrations, without the use of reading.

The Gigantic floor gameboard creates a fun atmosphere for playing Theatrix, allowing the child the freedom and space to "play" out their turn.

Theatrix is the game that allows parents to sit back and enjoy the show! (Popcorn not included.)

—description from the back of the box

The Players, the Games & All the Stats. 800 Questions & Answers

Was Shaq, Magic, or Sir Charles once known as the NBA's "Round Mound of Rebound"? What is baseball's Mendoza line? After School Sports  is a Brain Quest for outside the classroom, a feast of players, stats and famous moments. Baseball's M&M Boys. A "Tomato Can" and the Munchkin of Munich. Plus relay races and auto races, the balk and the "Fosbury Flop." It's not just sports--it's part of our culture.

Contains two decks. Can be used with the Brain Quest game.

boardgame: Brain Quest: After School Weird Stuff
description:
Strange, Gross and Unbelievable-But-True- Facts .800 Questions & Answers

Where is your sweet tooth? What part of its own body does the common toad swallow several times a year? After School Weird Stuff is a Brain Quest for outside the classroom, a feast of oddities, curiosities and the unbelievable-but-true. Why you can't catch cold in Siberia. The 69 1/2-year hiccup attack. Plus pogonologists and piranhas, mummies and shrunken heads. Because it's not just weird stuff--it's part of our knowledge.

Contains two decks. Can be used with the Brain Quest game.

The year is now 2071 and with the arrival of the Robotrons on earth, technology has advanced by great leaps and bounds aiding humanity in its struggles. However, Venom has turned this technology to their own nefarious purposes such as the creation of Cipher a gifted telepath who has been changed into the living brain of Venom Island, the creation of their airborne shock troops the Vultures, and much more!

But that is not all for dark things from man's ancient past are rising once more as the Master Vampires Lord and Lady Crimson wake from a 600-year slumber and now seek to carve a new their old domain. Fullmoon a brilliant LA Detective attacked and infected by a werewolf seeks out the aid of Freedom Squadron to help control the beast that now lurks in her soul.

To combat these new threats General Steel has implemented the Advance Training Program where you can now before your Recruitment Phase Retire Recruits and Commandos to upgrade them into new and better versions such as the Pilot, Heavy Gunner, or Spy! But think carefully because Recruits will give you more choices to upgrade to but is then placed in your discard pile. Where Commandos give you only one choice they are placed on top of your draw deck.

Originally delivered to Kickstarter backers of Verdant, this is a set of 16 Promo Cards, including 10 unique plant cards (2 each of 5 types) and 6 unique goal cards (2 each of 3 types).

With the addition of these cards, players will have new ways to play with the Potless Plants mini-expansion which adds completely new gameplay elements, as well as additional goal cards adding hundreds of new combinations of puzzles to the mix!

COMPONENTS:
17 cards

     10 Potless Plant Cards
     6 Goal Cards
     1 Rules Card (back of Title Card)

description:
With dozens of playing options, this classic-looking set of 8 wooden nesting boxes and 8 bouncy balls is awesome at home or away.

Beginners might want to start with the suggested games printed on the sides of each box. But once you've figured out the basic play possibilities, there's no end to the play configurations!

Let's look at the challenge from Box 2: the Rolling Target Game. Simply set up the boxes in descending order, with the smallest upside down as a target basket. Looks simple, huh? But it takes a steady hand to succeed! Get the ball bouncing down each successive box and you'll know you won. The acoustic design of Box & Balls sounds like a wooden xylophone!

Select a bounce game and use the numbers on the boxes for scoring; the smaller the box, the more difficult the shot, the higher the number. As skills grow, simply increase the level of difficulty just by decreasing target box size.

—description from the publisher

Zaraba Twenty-Two is one of the finalists for the 2020 Trick Taking Party Awards.  It is a three-player only trick-taking game using a subset of a standard deck of cards.

In the game, the lead player will play 2 cards to each trick, and the first one that is "followed" becomes the lead suit; the other card is moved to a stock chart area.

At the end of a hand, suits without any cards are worth 0 points; suits with cards in the area whose ranks sum to 22 or more are worth -1; and suits whose ranks sum 1 to 21 will be worth positive points.

Players score points for the cards won in tricks and the player with the most points after a number of rounds wins.

Sharing a Table is one of the finalists for the 2020 Trick Taking Party Awards. It is a six to eight-player trick-taking game using two standard decks of cards and one joker.  Players will also need two tables.

In an eight-player game, 13 cards are dealt to each player, and the remaining card is flipped face up.  The suit of the revealed card will set the specific scoring of the hand at each table; the scorings will be related, such as -2 points for each trick taken at one table, and -1 point for each taken at the other table.

The first 4 tricks are played to determine which players from the 8 will move to the second table to play; the results of these tricks do not otherwise bear on the scoring.  Each table then plays out the remaining 9 tricks, and the players score.

The player with the most points after a number of rounds wins.

In 9-card Dungeon Quest you are an adventurous thief who has entered the Dragon’s dungeon seeking your fortune and fame. You’ve been told the dungeon is under an illusion spell and all the room seem to shift and move. You must make your way through a random dungeon path to reach the Dragon's Chamber all the while dealing with dangerous traps that are impossible to avoid. You will earn gold as you move through the dungeon and once you reach the Dragon's Chamber you can try to steal as much treasure as you can before he awakens. If you tire from stealing or don't die from the Dragon’s fire breath, you will make your way back to the exit along a new random path and escape the dungeon for good. Along the way you may find treasure chests that will earn you extra gold or lifesaving potions to heal you as you escape.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel HeroClix: X-Men Rise and Fall
description:
The X-Men are no stranger to struggle, but some foes will rock the entire GALAXY!

Based on the critically acclaimed story Rise & Fall of the Shi’ar Empire, this HeroClix release has everything! Vulcan is out for revenge against the X-Men, the Shi’ar, and their allies!

—description from the publisher

Grimpath is a collectable trading card game where two or more players face off against each other with a deck building combat system designed around tactical miniature wargaming.

Set in a dark and expansive fantasy universe, Grimpath brings all of your favorite fantasy heroes, villains, and tropes to life with unique artwork and abilities designed to maximize the compatibility of your favorite fantasy tabletop collectibles.

In a typical game players draw cards from constructed decks, recruiting fantasy miniatures, moving them around custom battlegrounds, and attacking each other.

Your deck of cards is made up of a variety of minions, spells and magical items that give your miniatures their powers. As you draw cards and put them into play you'll attack your opponents on their home turf, collecting points along the way. The first to reach a designated number of “kill points” by destroying the other players' minions is crowned the winner of that game.

The world of Grimpath is divided into different realms of power: Shadow, Fury, Nature, Honor and the Arcane. These realms make up a broad array of strange creatures, spells, and magical items that you can combine to bring your own unique flavor to the battleground. Your minion’s mastery in these realms and the construction of your deck will determine how well it can perform during play. Players are encouraged to get creative by mix and matching cards as much as they like. But be mindful when constructing decks so that your minions will be able to perform the abilities you give them with the cards you chose.

Grimpath is a game designed to bring your fantasy miniatures to life by accompanying them with colorful artwork and abilities provided by your cards during play. The base game will provide you with everything you need for a match of Grimpath including miniatures, but players are encouraged to dive into the hobby and to bring their own custom creations to the table to face off against each other.

It’s up to you to choose to fight alongside the citizens of Grimpath in the endless war against the darkness that plagues their world… or to help it spread… The choice is up to you and your deck of cards and mini’s!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Murder Mystery Party Case Files: Mile High Murder
description:
INVESTIGATE A MURDER IN THE SKIES - Solve a murder mystery with friends. Open the Mile-High Murder Case File to start the crime scene investigation. Marisa Hightower was murdered on an overnight transatlantic flight from San Francisco to London. The hunt is on to find a killer and it’s up to you to solve this mile-high murder. This Case File includes all the evidence you need to solve the murder. Review every clue and piece of evidence in this unsolved mystery case file.

IMMERSIVE STORY AND REALISTIC EVIDENCE - This Case File includes crime scene photos, suspect interviews, newspaper clippings, and much more to help you determine who committed murder. All the evidence has been curated to present the most realistic experience possible (no fake handwriting). This murder mystery game in a box is the perfect gift for friends and family who love mystery board games and is perfect for family game night, an evening with friends or for date night for couples who love cold case murder mysteries. Get your Sherlock on to solve this murder mystery.

DETERMINE MOTIVE, MEANS AND OPPORTUNITY - Murder Mystery Party Case Files is the only game where players solve the murder the way a real detective would. Players need to hunt through the evidence to find the killer and determine the motive, means and opportunity of each suspect. During a murder investigation, a detective must answer the following 3 questions: 1) Motive: Who had a reason to kill the victim? 2) Means: Who had access to the murder weapon and victim? 3) Opportunity: Who had a chance to kill the victim? Is any particular person unaccounted for during the time the victim was killed?

ONLINE HINTS AND SOLUTIONS If you get stuck anytime during your investigation, you can access misplaced case files online for hints to keep you moving. Once you think you know the solution, access complete solutions online to confirm your suspicions and make an arrest.

Case File Contents:
· 12 Suspect and Evidence Photos
· 1 Air Marshal Notebook
· 37 Pieces of Case Evidence and Instructions
· Online Access to Hints and Solutions

Crime Location: International Flight

Background: Marisa Hightower was murdered on an overnight transatlantic flight from San Francisco to London (with emergency landing in Iceland).
family: Series: Murder Mystery Party Case Files (University Games)

description:
In Dead'n Slaaash every players control a survivor crew and has to take the right decisions in order to ensure a safe future to their owns. Strategy, bluff, alliance ant treason are on the schedule of each game, because in this cruel world your survival will have to pass before that of the others.

—description from the publisher

The game takes place over 4 Years (cycles of play) in the City of Athens 400 BC. 12 dice are rolled in each round of play. Players take sets of dice to position citizens. When all Citizens have been placed, players resolve the various spaces they occupy. Each Citizen brings an empowerment so that players can earn gold, defend the city, remove decay and gain culture. These empowerments are enhanced year on year. Socrates himself is a 'wild card' Citizen who is 'Immortalised' in the fourth and final year. The ultimate aim is to have the most culture and thereby win the game.

—description from the publisher

The battles for control of the Nine Worlds become more dangerous as monsters that haunt the darkest of our legends are unleashed. No longer can the gods and leaders of the realms merely focus on domination of the worlds. They must join forces to defeat these horrors or else suffer losses and destruction at their hands. For those who find the courage and resourcefulness to take on these enemies, honour and rewards beckon, some of which may help tip the balance in the wars between the worlds.

The Nine Worlds: Creatures and Monsters expansion introduces a new dimension where players divert some of their player stones to the fight against the monsters.

Defeat of the monsters will yield rewards. Failure to tackle the monsters means that various players will suffer penalties with the least courageous, who have done the least to fight the monsters, treated harshly by the norns who govern the fates of all.
 The key to this expansion is balancing the division of the action points between actions in the Nine Worlds and engaging the monsters.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fire in the Lake: Sovereign of Discord
description:
Building off of Mark Herman and Volko Runke’s legendary game Fire in the Lake, the Sovereign of Discord Expansion provides a prequel to the events depicted in the base game, covering the years 1961-1963. The expansion allows players to see how the war escalated from an insurgency between ARVN and Viet Cong, assisted by the US and North Vietnam, to the eventual conventional interventions by North Vietnam and the United States at the start of the base game. New scenarios allow players to extend the game backward to 1961 right after the formation of the Viet Cong, or to 1963 during the tumultuous last year of the Diem regime. Players owning both expansions, Sovereign of Discord and Fall of Saigon, will be able to experience the full Indochina War from 1961 to 1975. This allows players to see how the war evolved from the lower intensity insurgency of the Diem period all the way to the massive conventional offensives of the last years of the war. A copy of Fire in the Lake will be necessary to play the Sovereign of Discord expansion.

New mechanics are introduced to reflect the war prior to escalation by the US and North Vietnam and the unique aspects of the Diem Regime:

     1961 and 1963 Scenarios with a Fall of Diem Sequence allow players to continue play into the Fire in the Lake Campaign Scenario (1964-1972), or even through to Fall of Saigon (1973-1975) if players also possess the sequel expansion.
     New ARVN Strategic Hamlet pieces and mechanics reflect the Diem regime’s strategy of relocating the population into fortified villages.
     Hanoi and Washington Tracks control the number of available pieces and some abilities of the Viet Cong and ARVN factions. In addition, rather than possessing Troops, the US and NVA each use Advisor pieces, which allow them to enhance and control the units of their South Vietnamese allies.
     A Regime Stability Track affects the cost of ARVN Operations to model the rising urban instability and coup plotting against the Ngo Dinh Diem regime.
     A head-to-head 2 player scenario uses the Initiative system from Brian Train’s Colonial Twilight for players wanting to focus on the internal clash between the Viet Cong and ARVN, and might also be suitable as an introductory scenario for players new to Fire in the Lake.
     72 new Events cover the 1961-1963 period, and four new Pivotal Events represent debates in Washington and Hanoi over their level of aid, the Strategic Hamlet Program by the Diem regime, and the major summer offensive by the Viet Cong. In addition, a new ARVN leader Card, Ngo Dinh Diem, is in play throughout the early 1960s scenarios and new Coup! Events reflect threats to Diem’s hold on power.
     A solitaire system using the card-based Non-player system, similar to those provided with the Tr’ung Bot Update Pack and Fall of Saigon. 


Fire in the Lake Bonus Content
In addition to extending Fire in the Lake back to the early 1960s, Sovereign of Discord also includes additional bonus content for the base game: 

     40 new Events (8 new 1964 Events, 16 new 1965 Events, 16 new 1968 Events) covering themes such as women’s role in the war, civilian victimization, the effect of the war on South Vietnamese society, North Vietnamese strategies, and the 1964 escalation debate. Among the new Events is also one new Capability Event for each faction.
     Two new short scenarios:
         Turning Point (1967 to 1969), covering the period of fiercest offensives by both communist and allied forces to win control of the countryside.
         Long 1964 (Mid 1963 to Early 1965), covering the period that historian Frederick Logevall refers to as the “long 1964,” representing the crucial escalation debates in North Vietnam and the United States.
    


—description from the publisher

description: Align Me is a party game for role players. Give clues based on the mundane category and have other players guess your secret alignment! Players take turns giving clues and guessing the correct alignment. Each correct guess gains you 1 point. Each clue you give that was guessed correctly gains you 1 point. The first player(s) to 10 points wins!

Duelo, juego de cartas is a fantasy themed card game in which you are going to embody a hero, equip legendary items, manifest powerful auras, casts infernal curses and trigger events that lead you to victory.

In Duelo, juego de cartas you will choose a pre-constructed hero's deck to quickly start playing. On each turn you will have to equip items to your hero to increase the attack or defense; use item abilities; play auras to gain a temporal boost and cast curses onto your opponents to damage them and control their actions; play events to get powerful effects on the board and attack your opponents to death or damage them from distance.

Your goal is to defeat all your opponents by reducing they life points to 0 or by emptying their deck. This game can be played on free for all mode or 2 vs 2.

Duelo, juego de cartas is designed to bring you the Trading Card Game experience within a cheap box.

boardgame: Thakhi: la senda de los dioses
description:
You help a brave archaeologist to collect Aymara objects of great historical and cultural value. This objects are hidden inside an Andes labyrinth wish is about to collapse.

Explore the labyrinth and rescue the objects before a collapse buries them forever in time.

Objective of the game
Go through the Chakanas inside the labyrinth to earn points and reach the exit.

Nein Nimmt! is a trick-taking card game for two players. Players attempt to score lowest by capturing 9s and avoiding as many other cards as possible.

Nein Nimmt! is played with a 36 card "stripped deck", any traditional 52-card deck that is pared down to Six through Ace in four suits.

Card play is traditional trick-taking must-follow, with cards played to the trick then played to a central display following strict placement rules. When certain conditions are met, rows in the central display "bust" and players are forced to collect cards, which give players points.

The player with the least points at the end wins the game.

—description from the publisher

23 learning games to introduce kids to things that go. Games start with things that go on wheels and then introduce water transportation and air travel.

The games range from domino-like picture cards, to 3-dimensional vehicles, to card games based on vehicle types.

This is a card game from the 1950-60s. Players set a line-up and draw cards one at a time. Each card shows a result based on the player in the respective line-up slot. Normal basketball rules apply.

Part of line of card games by Built-Rite that included baseball and football.

Quiz game about London landmarks.

The board is a map of the city, with a track wandering through it. If a player is the first to land on one of the numbered spaces, he draws the card from the deck of 30 cards and reads out the information on the green side of the card. Then he turns the card over to see what instructions he has to follow. This are fairly predictable; Miss a turn perhaps, roll the die and move backwards, and so on. The player then takes the card and keeps it in front of him where all the players can see it. The card will have a value ranging from 5 to 30, with the value being commensurate with the penalty value of the instruction on it. If a subsequent player lands on a numbered field that has already been taken, then he treats the space as a normal space without number.

When the numbered space 30 has been reached, that player has finished. The others play on. When all the players have reached the 30 space, the 'won' cards are added up and the winner is the one with the most points.

—user summary

Jumping Jacks is a trick-taking game for 4 or 5 players in which the Jacks are all-important. The object is to have the most points after eight hands.

Jumping Jacks is played with a stripped deck of 20 cards, 10-A in four suits. Play is standard trick-taking must-follow with no trump suit.

Only the Jacks are worth points and must be avoided or sought after depending on the phase of the game. The game has two phases. The Jacks are worth negative points in the first four hands. They are worth positive points in the last four hands.

The winner is the player with the most points after 8 hands.

Players try to navigate through a circuitous path while blindfolded. Players can play as a team or individually. The goal is to complete the path from start to finish in the fastest time which includes penalties for going off the path.

When playing individually, the player studies the map, then puts blindfold on and uses a wipeable crayon to mark their path on the wipeable board. They can stop at any point and take a peek to see how they are doing, but this incurs penalty time.

When playing as a team, a navigator gives directions to the blindfolded player.

Family game that aims to help improve driving habits. Players drive along one of four routes on the board encountering various obstacles and requirements along the way. The players deal with driving school, accidents, and insurance to deal with random events that occur along the route.

Players earn money along the route. The player with the most money when all players have reached their destination wins.

—user summary

"What does it get a ballpoint pen? "
"What does a helmet do? "
Poi is a cooperative quiz game that is fun to consider sorting waste by Mr. 326 who designed the most popular ito.

"What does a helmet do? " and let everyone answer the answer.
Plastic and burning debris? "Oh there are metal fittings? "Can't be recycled? " and the debate is very excessive.
What's more, if you have a place to throw it overseas here?

The back of the card also has a reason for sorting garbage and is excited to laugh and admire the unexpected differences in each city.
It also includes Japanese, Chinese, English, and German. It also stimulates a little intellectual curiosity.

Do you feel free to use the game to start with the garbage problem that has become a huge topic around the world?

This is a cooperative game. A player draws a location card, and reveals a "garbage" card, and all players have to cooperatively guess which trash can this will go into. If they are correct, they score a point, if they fail, they cover half of the earth card. They win after scoring 10 points, and lose after covering the complete earth card (2 fails).

Players use a spinner to move along a path from start to finish. Along the way, the players follow instructions on the board or draw cards which they must follow. They will sometimes have to spend money or other times they may collect and save money.  Some cards may send the player on a detour and slow them up.

The players try to get to the finish line with the most money, but also quickly as each player who finishes also collects money from all the remaining players.

—user summary

Crescendo is a 3 player trick-taking game.

It is played using a short deck with cards A-5 in four suits plus one joker.

Each hand is divided into an Auction Phase and a Play phase. Only one player will be eligible to score points in a hand. The auction determines who will be the "soloist" and choose the trump suit in the play phase. The other two players will play against the soloist.

Play is standard must-follow trick-taking. Tricks are won by the highest card in the lead suit, or the highest trump card if any trump cards are played.

The soloist wins points for tricks, attempting to win the amount that they had bid in the auction phase.

The player with the most points after several hands wins the game.

Japanese baseball card game. Each game package contains cards representing the players on a Japanese baseball team. The package also includes a paper representation of a baseball field and rules for play.

boardgame: Marvel Zombies: A Zombicide Game – Fantastic 4: Under Siege
description:
Featuring Super Heroes, Zombie Heroes, and Bystanders tied to Marvel’s First Family, this expansion takes the action from the ground floor all the way to the roof of the Baxter building, in a unique floor-by-floor board setup that allows players to run this deadly gauntlet in order to secure Reed Richard’s fantastic technology! This unique location will be explored over several new scenarios for both Hero Mode and Zombie Mode.

—description from the publisher

Magicalligraphy is a social deduction game where players attempt to guess which, if any, of the players were attempting to draw a different symbol than the others.

To draw, each of the players puts a ring on one of their fingers that is connected with a thin wire to a feather quill.  The quill is dipped into a container of water and the players attempt to, without verbally communicating, draw a certain symbol on a special sheet of paper where the water appears as black ink, but will dry without effect after a short period of time.

After several drawing strokes, the players choose who, if anyone, to accuse of having been trying to draw a different symbol, and then points are awarded.
designers: Masakazu Takizawa (たきざわ まさかず)
artists: Masakazu Takizawa (たきざわ まさかず)

description: Move your characters - the most famous characters from the popular series about the two Gauls Asterix and Obelix - across the field. You bring the most important moments of the comic classic to life. Build as many huts and hostels as possible on the fields to win. The instructions on the Potions and Community Cauldron cards will get you there quickly. But beware: they also have one or two surprises in store.
family: Versions & Editions: Versions with IP after the original release

description:
In Rabauken ("Bullies"), you need to keep things moving because if your ball stops, everyone else gets rewarded.

To set up, place the round wooden board on the table. Each player receives a flat block of wood, a wooden tower with a bowl carved in the top of it, and a glass ball. Each player in turn places their block on the board, with their tower fitting into a hole in this block and their ball placed on top of their tower.

Once everyone is ready, play begins, with each player in turn using a finger to push their block toward the blocks of other players. If you knock someone else's block off the board or dislodge their ball without yours falling out, then you receive a boar token as a reward. If you dislodge your own ball or you take too long and your ball comes to rest inside your tower, each other player receives a pig token as a punishment to you.

When someone scores, reset the board, then start a new round with the player to the left of the previous starting player.

Whoever collects ten tokens first wins!

Pop, the little dinosaur, has lost all his colors! Be the first to feed Pop so that he becomes multicolored again.
Use your memory skills to match food cards with Pop’s color cards and unlock the corresponding stripe color stripe. The first to get all 7 colored stripes wins the game.

This family game requires memory and association skills.
Can be played alone or with the family.

boardgame: Unlock!: Les Escape Geeks – Échappez-vous de la Tour Eiffel
description:
Unlock! is a series of escape adventures for up to six players. With one hour on the clock, players work through a deck of sixty cards as a team, searching for clues, combining objects, and solving puzzles. The free Unlock! companion app runs the timer while also providing clues, offering hints, and confirming successes. Once the team has reached a solution and entered the correct code into the app, they will escape and win the game!

Unlock!: Les Escape Geeks – Échappez-vous de la Tour Eiffel ("Escape from the Eiffel Tower") is a level 1 adventure featuring Alex and other characters from the Unlock! novels from author Fabien Clavel and publisher Rageot, but no knowledge of these novels is required to solve this challenge.

You and up to three friends are airport luggage porters delivering luggage during the golden age of aviation. Each turn you’ll take Luggage off of the ever-changing Carousel and sort it into your three luggage stacks. Then stack and sort your luggage based on its size and destination, and score points for delivering complete sets to each airplane on time! The cards you take from the Carousel will manipulate the value of completed luggage stacks, so choose carefully!
The game ends when there’s no luggage left to take from the Luggage Carousel. The player with the most points wins!

Card Drafting:
Choose one of the first three cards from the front of the Carousel. Any cards in front of the chosen card will move to the back of the Carousel and add a Boarding Call to their destination(s), which can help or hinder you AND your opponents’ scoring opportunities!

Push Your Luck:
Boarding Calls and Lost Luggage increase the scoring value of each Luggage Card for that destination by 1. Do you deliver your luggage now, or hold out for the highest possible score? Choose wisely, for when a destination receives a fourth Boarding Call, its value resets to 0!

Stacking/Sorting:
Sort your Luggage Cards from smallest to largest to form complete stacks. Keep your Luggage moving! Any cards that can’t fit into your stacks may become Lost Luggage!
Set Collection:
Score completed stacks of luggage heading to the same destination. The more Boarding Calls played, the more points your Luggage Cards are worth! Extra Trolleys will give you one more stack to work with, but will cost you points to use.

—description from the designer

Theme
Color Life is a meta-monopoly family board game. The player will take on the role of little-ü. In the trip he/she will buy some colorful lands, select the abilities and earn money to win the game.

Gameplay
 On player's turn, the player rolls one dice and moves that number of spaces around the board. If the player lands on an as-yet-unowned property, he/she has the opportunity to buy it. The land is not exclusive so that all players can buy the same land as if he/she does not own it. The player can use ability card or item card to move instead of rolling a dice. 
 If a player owns all 3 lands within a color group, he/she may then build building and enable its functionality. Then all players can benefit from that function once they own that land.                           
 There are other lands on the board which can't be bought, they provide other functions such as drawing a random event card, guess other player dice rolling, buy an item card, get new ability cards and earn salary.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Denk mich: Wer tickt so wie ich?
description:
This is an association game with 55 cards with 6 different questions each. 
One player reads the first question and all players write the first word which comes to their mind. All players read their words and the players who selected the same word score 1 point. Now it's the next player's turn who reads the second qustion.  
After 3 cards, the game ends and the player with the most points wins.

—user summary

Below each cup, a token is placed. Each child then lifts three cups in order to find at least 2 tokens which are the same. If that's the case, the child gets the same tokens and the cups are  put back on the table. 
The child with the most tokens wins.

—user summary

Help! The mad MICROMINDS crashed with their spaceship and only you can save them!

Look with your smartphone into the funny world of the Microminds und help them to repair their damaged spaceship. Alot of exiting and informative tasks await you. With varied playful experiments you and the Microminds repair the spaceship part for part. Do you find the best combination for an exiting rocket drive?

A new interactive gaming system from Ravensburger with complex smartphone app and high-quality gaming materials.

—description from the publisher

Mining Colony Duel is a 1-2 player "BYOP" (bring your own pencil) adaptation of Mining Colony, a print-and-play game that requires no components other than the printed sheets and pencils.

In the game, you build a mining colony on a distant planet. At the start of each round, players bid for resources, which include polyomino tiles, domes, landing pads, and science stations. After players reveal their bids, the players place (draw) the resources onto their sheets, trying to accomplish various things, such as having multiple items of the same type in a row or column.

After eight rounds, players score based on the development of their colonies.
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
The children blow a little rocket from one planet to the other planet. On every planet, they have to solve different tasks which involve some gymnastics, puzzle games or quizzes.
The kid who solves the most tasks wins at the end.

—user summary

Every player is a visitor on a fairground and tries to drive all the four different wagons of the merry-go-round. But the funny clown tries his best to distract the players and changes the places of the wagons!
The child who first manages to drive all wagons wins.

—user summary

Two racing games in a single box

Car-racing:
Objective: Be the first ending the car race by passing target space the 3rd time (or more if agreed by the players)
- moving by turns -
roll the die, move your meeple & and follow instructions for some fields.
On each field only 2 cars are allowed to stay.

Space-racing:
Racing with the Alien "Spacy" through the space in a round course starting from the earth to moon and mars and back.
Objective: Be the first ending the space race by passing field 70. 
- moving by turns -
roll the die, move your meeple & and follow instructions for some fields.

—user summary

A tactical dice game where each player tries to run as fast as possible. At the same time, each one needs to stop at least seven times, but the more you stop, the more points you'll score. What a dilemma...

The winner is the player with the most stops.

—user summary
This is a funny jump & run game for children. The child who is the first one in putting all his animals and Noah in the ark wins the game.

The players try to catch the dogs to put them in their corresponding dog kennels. 
Each play gets the same amount of dog kennels. All dog cards are put in the middle of the table. The starting player roles the dice three times. If the dice shows at least 2 times the same colour, the player can put one dog in the kennel of this colour. 
The player who is the first one in putting the dogs of the correct colours in his dog kennels wins.

—user summary

Fix and Foxi make a picnic.
Each player has a board which shows a picnic blanket.
Each picnic blanket has the same 9 elements (picnic theme) printed on it.

Objective:
Complete your picnic as first, but don't pick the mice. 
On players turn he selects one memory tile and turns it open.
If it's an element he still needs (not covered by an equal tile or a mouse tile) he takes and places it. Otherwise he turns it on the back side at the old position.
If the player finds a mouse he can choose if he place it on 1 free field on his blanket or if he puts it back.
The game ends, when 1 player has placed 1 tile on each of the 9 fields on his blanket.
Every player removes all mice and 1 good tile for each mouse tile from his blanket. For every remaining tile he gets 1 point. The player who ends the game gets 2 additional points. The player with the highest amount of points wins.

—user summary

The player are employees in a bank who want to become CEO. Therefore, each player needs to answer questions and if he knows the right answer, he moves up the corporate ladder. 
The fastest and most skilled player will become CEO and win the game.

—user summary

This is a collection of word games where children can easily learn the different senses of various word pairs.
It contains 48 tokens which form 24 pairs.

Puzzle game for 2-6 children
One half of the pairs is put face up on the table, whereas the other half is put face down. Then one of hidden tokens is put face up as well and the children have to find the corresponding partner. The fastest child gets the pair. In the end, the child with the most pairs wins.

Memory game for 2-6 children
All tokens are put face down on the table and the children try to find pairs according to the actual memory rules. The one with the most pairs wins.

Homonym game for 4-6 children
2 kids secretly take one word pair and both of them explain their word to the other children. The child who is the first one to guess the word wins.

—user summary

Chapter IV sees you infiltrating a small desert camp to locate and decipher a secret message left for you.

You must figure out your next steps by examining the items left in the camp, deconstructing them and reforming them into new tools.

---

Recommended for 12+; younger players may require guidance from an adult. Designed to be played either solo or collaboratively, and takes an average of 60-90 minutes to solve, though players can work through them at their own pace.

Each Chapter of The CCC is supported by a dedicated digital hint library, allowing you to choose how many additional clues you receive. From subtle nudges all the way to complete solutions for each puzzle, it's up to you to choose your level of difficulty.

A simple quiz card game with 107 cards. Each card covers a specific topic about world records in sports, culture etc. with 4 questions. The answers are on the backside of the cards. Players try to answer the questions and score points.

—user summary

This game encourage children to remember and explain easy stories. It contains 50 tokens with images of some terms, such as apple, bike, street. These tokens are put face down on the 8 fields of the game board. 
In the first round, the starting player turns the tokens of one field face up and tells a fairy tale inclduing all the terms of the tokens on his field. He puts the tokens face down again and the next player starts to tell a story about the next field.   
In the second round, the children have to remember the terms. Therefore, a field is selected randomly and the player mentions all the terms of this field. For every correct one, he gets the token. 
The child with the most tokens wins.

—user summary

boardgame: Die Simpsons: Drunter und Drüber in Springfield
description:
Each player is one of 8 "The Simpsons" characters.
There are 5 Springfield locations (Kwik-E-Market, School, Moe's bar, nuclear power plant, TV channel). On each location the player can gat a typical thing (e.g. beer at Moe's), a new order card or fullfill an order card he has on his hand.
The player who fullfills 3 order cards first, wins the game.
On players turn he can choose 1 of 4 moving possibilities (monorail, car, skate board, feet). There are differences in cost, speed and precision.
2 additional rules: moving by feet allows to move the barrier (drunken Barnie), which blocks the monorail and the street (for all except skate board).
On each order card there is a "being mean" possibility. This can be done for free if you fullfill the order. The victim can be choosen. It can also be executed by giving back one order card and an additional thing.

The cards with half funny pictures of animals are shuffled and dealt evenly to the players. The players now play cards in turn. If the next player can complete an animal, he may take the cards. If not, then he must place another card in front of him.
Whoever has collected the most complete animals at the end wins.

—user summary

boardgame: Blood Bowl: (Second Season Edition) – Glorious Gifts Promo Card
description:
Promo card found in Issue 470 of the magazine White Dwarf.

"Every year, gifts are exchanged at the end of the season. Will our players get what they wished for this year? Or will it be more patterned socks and spare underwear!"

TIMING

Play this card during the pre-game sequence, after step 5 but before step 6.

DURATION

This card expires at the end of this game.

EFFECT

When you play this card, select a player on your team, roll a D6 and apply the corresponding effect until this card expires:

On a 1, the selected player has opened a brand new pair of socks Woo. This card has no additional effect. 
On a 2-3, the selected player gains a Primary Skill of your choice.
On a 4-5, the selected player gains a Primary Skill, Secondary Skill or a Trait of your choice. 
On a 6, the selected player may improve their MA, ST, AG or PA by 1.

You enter the sewer system underneath the looming tower. Will you be able to navigate the winding tunnels with what you have on you?

boardgame: Necromunda: Underhive – A Swig Of Wild Snake Tactics Card
description:
Card was available in issue 470 of the magazine White Dwarf.

Play this gang tactic when activating a friendly fighter.

Place the thin end of the Flame template in contact with the active friendly fighter. All fighters touched by the Flame template (both friend and foe) gain the Intoxicated condition and the Doused in Alcohol condition. Playing this card counts as the chosen fighter's activation.

Intoxicated fighters are marked with an Intoxicated token and suffer a -1 penalty to BS and WS checks for the remainder of the battle. For the remainder of the battle, fighters Doused In Alcohol are automatically set on fire the first time they are hit by a weapon with the Blaze trait.

boardgame: Adeptus Titanicus: The Horus Heresy – Ordnance Delivery Promo Card
description:
Card found in Issue 470 of the magazine White Dwarf.

As two battlegroups clash, low-orbit aircraft brave the blizzard conditions to unleash blind bombing runs upon the two sides.

If all players agree, the card can be added to an existing Open Engine War deck or used on its own. At the start of each Strategy phase, the First Player rolls a D10. Until the end of the round, the long range characteristic of all weapons is reduced by a number of inches equal to the number rolled (to a minimum of the weapon's short range). In addition, each player, starting with the player who is not the First Player, may place a 5" Blast marker anywhere on the battlefield and Scatter it D10 inches. Any model at least partially under the template suffers D3 S7 Hits, as if hit by a weapon with the Ordnance trait.
family: Game: Adeptus Titanicus – The Horus Heresy (Games Workshop)

description:
It's a bowling for kids.
Additional the kids should use the mages for building pyramids with them to train their mechanical abilities.

—user summary

boardgame: Legend of the Galactic Heroes: Side Stories 2
description:
Science fiction wargame set in the universe of the Legend of the Galactic Heroes. This set covering battle of Starzone Vanfleet and 6th siege of Iserlhon.

Two scenarios are contained in the game, and the fictional space battle that each was drawn in the story is reproduced. These are being called a fleet battle, but when either is said, it is close to the land battle of Napoleonic age.

Each player control one or more star fleets.
Each fleet is commanded by one admiral, and organized by 6 to 14 ships units.

Each turn, a fleet are activated in the unintentional order by chit-pull system, and it resolves a movement and a combat.
An average admiral has only one or two chits. But the heroes of the story has four or more chits, and it is activated many times during one turn.
A battle is resolved with adjacent hex on the Odds CRT.

The winner determined by the number which made a fleet collapse. A fleet collapses when a fixed unit is eliminate or admiral's mental agitation.

description: Family friendly escape game in a viking age setting. The story driven game includes solving puzzles as well as some deductive skills and offers a scoring at the end based in the age of the group and the used hints.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Pékin Express the board game is a race in which the players will move across Europe or Asia and collect points. The game ends when a player reaches the finish line (Lisbon or Beijing) and the player with the most Amulet points wins.

Once the board chosen (Asia for 2 to 4 players ; Europe for 4 to 6 players) and the setup complete, the first round begins. A round is structured as follows:

     The players draft their 5 Travel cards.
     The player with the Red Flag card in hand must play it and take the Red Flag token. Then they take their turn by playing a Travel card or by passing. When a player reaches first a city with no Stage Token, they gain a face down Amulet card and place a Stage Token on that city.
     The players continue to take their turn in order in a similar fashion until every player has passed consecutively.


Such rounds keep going until the end of the game is triggered immediately as soon as a player reaches the finishing line (and by doing so, they gain 3 Amulet cards).

Each player counts their Amulet values, the player with the most points wins.

Vrahode - The Calteeryn Acension is an adventure/dungeon crawl board game, the second in a series of five games, designed by Jeffrey Irving. Following the initial game Age of Prophecy (2024) this massive "core box" is a solo/co-op game for 1-6 players where players can choose to journey through huge multi-quest campaign that sprawls over the heights and depths of the mysterious world of Vrahode or choose one of five other modes of play as well as four difficulty levels!

The core box campaign victory condition: "Calteeryn" (the chosen one) must learn the ruhl (magic) of each crafted race in the game while facing mounting threats. This is done by journeying through the eight unique biomes of the Overlands, Underlands, and Oceans of the world of Vrahode.

Dice-based combat is enhanced by powerful skill deck building and racial abilities unique to each race. Every enemy encounter is different, with the enemy behavior deck threatening to change how the heroes must fight them each battle!

The world of Vrahode is based on a narrative first imagined in 1990 by designer Jeffrey Irving. The Vrahode series of games includes a introductory game Vrahode - Age of Prophecy and three additional expansions: The Enlightened & The Enslaved, Facing The Storm, and The False Gods' Deceit. All are part of the Vrahode Game System which invites players to generate their own content, adding to the expanding lore of Vrahode! This game is compatible with our proprietary 3D terrain system called Harbinger, players can create dynamic, multi-level dungeons up to five levels deep.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Holly Jolly: Dice Tower 2022 Promo Card Set
description:
Available as part of the Dice Tower 2022 Kickstarter campaign.

This set of Present cards consists of three total cards, one each of the following items: Dice Tower, Dice, and Board Games.

These three cards can be shuffled into your presents/stockings deck. At the end of the game if a player manages to collect one card from this set, they score 1 point. If they can get two, they score 2 total points. But if they can manage to collect all three, then they will earn a big ol 18 points!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Just One: Dice Tower 2021 Promo Pack
description:
Originally available as part of Pack A from the Dice Tower 2021 Kickstarter campaign.

A pack of 5 new words for Just One, chosen by Dice Tower fans! (ADDED TO DECK 6-8-24)

Originally available as part of Pack A from the Dice Tower 2021 Kickstarter campaign.

A pack of 8 new cards (and 1 title card) for Wavelength, created by the Dice Tower team!

Cards include:
Good Game/Bad Game
Cooperative Game/Competitive Game
Best Pie/Worst Pie
Tiny Game/Huge Game
Short Game/Long Game

Miniatures agnostic skirmish game of mech on mech combat.

This is a game of mech combat in a science fantasy setting of battle and ambition. Of pirates and those of royal blood.

Any faction can fight any other faction it is assumed that the pirates will fight pirates when their ambitions do not align. And the noble's family’s will fight in petty conflicts for similar reasons.

You can design your own mechs and carrier then send them off to war. You need something to represent your forces. Model kits or LEGO are what I recommend. You will also need two sets of polyhedral dice and some paper for your character sheets. Last but not least: you will need a flat surface to play on. I also would recommend some books to use as terrain.

The game is won when you destroy or demobilize the opposing player's carrier or they do the same to yours.

In the future, I hope to introduce solo play and a campaign but for now let’s get to the game.

—description from the designer

Legend has it that long ago, Gah'dir the supreme mage forged a weapon known as the Scarlet Sword in order to form an alliance with Bashok the great orc king. By wielding the sword together, they created the most powerful alliance ever built among enemies and gained a power like no other. Since then, the sword has disappeared and reappeared many times over the years and it has determined the destiny of all nations time and time again.
Recently, the legendary Scarlet Sword was rediscovered, causing the most powerful leaders in all the land to seek out an ally and once again prepare for war.

In Allies, you will create and control a fantasy army by uniting two different factions of your choice. You can either use your cards to command your troops or deploy them onto the battlefield. The unique abilities of each faction can be combined to create many different synergies, so choose wisely.

Allies is a competitive two-player card game in which every move matters. It includes tactical and strategic components. Prepare your strategies well and don’t get distracted! Remember that your plans need to change constantly to anticipate your enemies’ movements as well as to disrupt them.

In the end, the army that gains control of the battlefield first claims victory!

—description from the designer

description:
In Eco Neighborhood the most important thing is building a clean environment, so moving through the streets you will have the opportunity to use different means of transport and find out their pros and cons, but be careful, you can get stuck in a traffic jam! On your way to the finish line, every time you step on the Eco Fact field you will find out new and interesting information about renewable energy sources, and why it’s important to wisely spend the electrical energy! Eco Neighborhood will show you where to move to find out the importance of protecting our environment and how you can contribute in protection of the Planet.

On the way to the finish line you will find out new things and challenges. Some of the fields will move you forward, but some will move you back. Also you will have the opportunity to invest you hard earned points in order to save energy through energy efficient light bulbs, energy efficient windows, solar panels and isolation for your home. The road to your goal is field with lots of interaction and fun.

Eco Neighborhood was created as a didactic material as part of the project “Education on energy”. The project was realized by Eco Logic, and was supported by the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC).

The board game is intended for pupils from 5th to 9th grade, and it comprises of a platform with 11 examples of everyday locations toward which the children/teams have to go. On the way to the location, they resolve exercises and tasks for improving of the English language skills, through cards with activities, questions, fun facts – given by the mentor/teacher/parent that has the role of Game Guide/Maser.

Likes is a party game in which we will discover the tastes of others.

Do you think you know the people around you? Are you one of those people who see other people at first? Put it to the test!

In the Likes card game you will be able to see first-hand that people are never known as well as we like to believe. Try to guess the tastes and hobbies of other people through the images on the cards. At first everything will depend on your intuition, but little by little all people will become an open book for you. Of course the feeling will be reciprocal.

Likes is fun while we get to know each other!

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Skinchangers
description:
Skinchangers have the rare ability to project their consciousness into nearby creatures, altering their behavior. This can be used simply, perhaps having them perform tricks. But it can also be used in combat. It’s bad enough when an enemy unit is advancing on your position. It’s worse when that unit is flanked by giant wolves or bears.

The Skinchangers unit box for the A Song of Ice and Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game gives Free Folk commanders a new option for their armies. The Skinchanger Unit Attachment can be placed in any unit, ignoring usual Unit Attachment rules (so they can join a unit with another attachment already in it). When being added, the player can also add their animal unit to the table. When the Skinchanger’s unit activates, the accompanying animal can activate immediately afterward, creating perfect “one-two punch” combinations. Just be sure that the two are never further than Long Range from one-another, or the Skinchanger is killed. Either situation will cause the animal to also be destroyed.

A game for ages 5 to 105
2 to 6 persons may play

HOW TO PLAY: Shuffle deck. Deal four cards face up to each player. Turn one card face up in the center of the playing area and place the remaining pile face down next to it. The player to the left of the dealer begins by putting a card with the next numerical value on top of the card that was turned up. Only one card can be played. If the player does not have the right card, he picks a card from the face down pile. If he picks the right card, he must play it. If he picks the wrong card, he places it face up with his other cards. The player to his left then takes a turn. If the pile you pick from gets used up, play continues. Each player, in turn, must play the right card if he has it, or, pass if he does not have it. The first one to play all of his cards is the Game Winner. The deal is passed clockwise for succeeding games.

boardgame: Variations on a Theme: Supplementary Rules for Monopoly
description:
A collection of Monopoly variants, intended (except for the last) to be used separately:


     The Investment Fund allows players to invest their money. The principal can never be taken out but every time a player passes GO they receive 10% interest. This can radically change the nature of the game, among other things speeding it up significantly.



     The Auction Game requires all properties to be auctioned when landed on for the first time. This tends to slow the game down and reduces reliance on luck somewhat.



     Building Past Hotels allows further property development. This makes the cheaper monopolies more interesting. A set of extended property cards is provided which show the additional costs and rents.



     Ruffles and Flourishes, a set of small tweaks which can be used together or separately. One is a new set of "wahoo" cards which can all be kept until a player wants to use them; a set is provided.

Morning Dew allows you to play Momiji with up to 5 players. Morning Dew cards can also be added to a 1-4 player game in place of another leaf type. The blue Morning Dew leaf cards can be collected in their own pile in a player’s personal area as usual, or they can be played as Wild cards/Jokers. When played as Wild you can add it to the top of any color of leaf card following the usual rules. You can also play any color of leaf on top of a blue leaf. You cannot have two or more leaf cards of the same color visible on top of your piled at the end of your “play leaf cards” action, and you can never play a blue leaf onto a closed pole showing the Torii symbol. If used as a Wild, immediately take the blue leaf token (-3 victory points at the end of the game).

Modern Royalty is a trick-taking game for exactly six players using a standard 52-card deck plus two jokers.

In each hand the deck is dealt out and the players then bid; bids can be 1-36 in a suit or 1-25 in "anarchy."

If the winning bid is in a suit, that suit is trump and cards are ranked normally, except that aces are special and jokers are the lowest members of the trump suit. Play is fairly standard (must follow suit, not required to trump) except that the aces in the non-trump suits are assassins which kill trump cards, and the trump ace is a guard which stops one assassin.

If the winning bid is in anarchy, there is no trump suit and cards are ranked ace high to ten low, with the face cards being equal below the ten; the jokers form their own "pariah" suit. If a pariah is led to a trick no one wins it.

Scoring depends on whether the play was in a suit or in anarchy, and if in a suit each player's score depends in part on what is taken by three of the other five players. The player who won the bid must always achieve their bid in points or be penalized; if the bid was in a suit then other players who last bid that suit must also do this.

Play is to 626 points.

There is an optional rule for an extra "knife" bid which basically means that the knifing player(s) work deliberately against the winning bidder.

Solitaire 34 is played with a standard 52-card deck. It assigns court cards a value of 10 points and all other cards their face values. This gives the deck a total of 340 points; the goal of the game is to divide the deck into 10 stacks of 34 points each.

At the start of the game the player deals a row of three cards and a row of four cards. They then find any subset of those seven cards that total exactly 34, set them aside, and deal cards to fill the empty slots.

If there is no way to make 34, the player chooses one of the two rows of cards to set aside and deals a new row. They can bring the set-aside row back in later by completely emptying the row of matching size. Several rows can be set aside at any given time.

Eagles is a Diplomacy variant which is set in an alternate history Roman Empire; the game starts in Spring 118 CE shortly after the assassination of Hadrian. The game can accommodate 2-12 players but is recommended for 4-6.

Each player starts in one of a group of given supply centers and has two more from a different group as allies; which ones each player gets is determined randomly.

The basic rules are modified in three ways:


     Reinforcements are handled differently.



     Many spaces on the board are held by barbarians. A force entering one of these must roll a die and may be destroyed or repelled.



     Naval movement and combat is very different, with multiple fleets being able to coexist in the same space, die-rolling, etc.

description:
Home Run Derby is a simple baseball game. Each player creates a nine-member team either by rolling on a table or by choosing a real team's current starting lineup; in each case only the batting averages matter.

When a baseball player comes up to bat two dice are rolled to determine if they get a hit, sacrifice, walk, or are out. If they get a hit a roll is made on a second table to determine the outcome. Bases can be stolen, which is also resolved via dice roll.

The defending player's only decision is whether to issue an intentional walk.

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Stark Attachments I
description:
House Stark is known for extolling integrity, justice, and loyalty. Those that flock to the Direwolf’s Banner feel that what they are fighting for is right. This feeling is instilled in the officers that are in charge of the units within the Stark army. House Stark might not have the deepest coffers, but what they lack in raw materials they make up for in determination.

The Stark Unit Attachments box set gives House Stark players in the A Song of Ice and Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game 11 new Unit Attachments that they can use to customize their forces. They can enhance their troops with the likes of Sworn Sword Captain, Umber Champion, Crannogman Warden, and more. Each one will allow Stark players to build an army that fits exactly the type of battle they want to fight.

description:
Fantasy monster pet deck builder.  Create the ultimate team of monsters, beasts, and cryptic to compete in pet competitions. Collect pets, companions, and abilities of different elements and combine their powers to win the most trophies.

Each player starts with a deck of ten starter cards, and a starting pet. Your pet stays out on your player mat, ready to compete using its Beauty, Brains, and Brawn stats! Each turn, draw 5 cards from your deck. Some cards will add points to your pets' Beauty, Brains, or Brawn statistics.  Others have money that let you buy more powerful cards or pets from the marketplace.  Cards can also contain abilities that let you improve your hand, improve your pets, or sabotage your competitors! All cards also align to one of four elements. By pairing cards of the same element, you can activate even better abilities and add extra pets to your game board to help win competitions. After buying cards, placing pets on your mat, activating abilities, or any combination of those things, place your hand in the discard pile, and draw a new hand.  As the game progresses, your deck will grow more and more powerful!

Once the pets on your player mat, with the assistance of any cards in your hand that turn, have enough Beauty, Brains, and Brawn, go claim your prize! Each trophy has a unique combination of statistics required to claim, and once you've claimed a prize no one else can claim the same one. Trophies also give you victory points to win the game.  But be careful.  Once your pets win a trophy, they retire and you will need to purchase new ones for the next competition.

Play until all the competitions are claimed, then add up victory points from your trophies. Be strategic: some cards, including pets, also contain victory points that count towards victory. So don't assume the person with the most trophies is always the winner!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Song of Ice & Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game – Dothraki Screamers
description:
The warrior ways of the Dothraki might make them appear as simple barbarian savages, screaming into the sky as they charge into battle. And many commanders have underestimated them because of those stereotypes. A Dothraki will spend more time on their horse than they will off of it, and with that experience comes skill that regular training will never achieve. Despite being unarmored, it is still difficult for a trained swordsman to land a significant hit on a mounted Dothraki.

The Dothraki Screamers unit box for the A Song of Ice and Fire: Tabletop Miniatures Game gives Targaryen commanders a solid cavalry unit for their forces. These fearsome fighters can charge quickly into battle, dealing out deadly blows. Skilled commanders will use them as a lightning-fast strike force, avoiding getting them into protracted combats, lest their fewer numbers start to catch up with them.

However, the company has been made aware of the potential of an inside job and users will be alert and observant for any suspicious behaviour.  If their plans are exposed, there will be drastic consequences."

Play as a ring caster, in the attempt to reach the center of the board first. On your turn choose one of four available actions:

Whole title: "Durch Kampf zum Sieg, Original-Hakenkreuz-Gesellschaftsspiel“ (eng. "Through struggle to victory, Original swastika parlor game") made by Verlag August Haug, Berlin.

Game rules similar to game "Mensch ärgere dich nicht!", which is German version of Pachisi. Each player has a set of pawns that start in his or her corner of the board (this game is unique because the game boards layout is similar to swastika). The goal is to move the pawns around the board to the "home" section. Movement is controlled by dice. All players move around the same board, so they may capture each others pawns. Captured pawns are returned to their player's corner and must start their journey over. The winner is the first player to move all pawns "home".

These are 'Shelfie' promo tokens for the game Dream Home. The first player to complete a three-card Living Room receives the 'board games bookshelf' promo token, which is worth 2 points.
